note: Steuben County park on Almond Lake (USACE reservoir). 40 electric (30/50A) + 30 non-electric + 2 cabins, dump, boat launch; some lake-view sites. Phone reservations, Apr 15-Dec 1. RV Life 5*. --Claude

name: Montour Falls Marina & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 42.3548,-76.8528: front row of RV sites faces the Seneca Lake canal across the internal road/dock margin (~20-30m, open view); pads not at the waterline so riverfront not earned

name: Havana Glen Park
note: Town of Montour municipal park. 14 RV sites (30A electric + water) + 10 tent, dump; Eagle Cliff Falls swimming area. Phone reservations, May-Oct. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Myers Park

note: Town of Lansing park on Cayuga Lake. ~19-20 RV sites, 30A electric + water, dump, marina/boat launch/beach; some sites have water views. Phone/online reservations. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Chautauqua County Fairgrounds Campground
note: Chautauqua County Fairgrounds, Dunkirk. 80+ sites; year-round/non-fair-week public camping via separate application (NOT event-only). Reserve via Fair Office. RV Life 5*. --Claude

name: Greenwood County Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 42.2940,-76.0935: campground loops sit in dense forest ~150-200m east of the lake with a full tree buffer; county page itself says walk/bike/drive to beach — no RV site overlooks water

name: Upper Lisle Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 42.3974,-75.9695: no developed pads resolvable — camp area is scrub old-field set back from the reservoir arm behind brush; no official per-site map exists and a 2017 The Dyrt report says "there is nothing here" — defaults down (low confidence)
note: Broome County (Whitney Point Lake area). ~25 drive-in tent & RV sites, first-come-first-served (no formal reservations), no electric, restrooms/showers/dump; rustic. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Smith Point County Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 40.7375,-72.8530: front RV rows directly along Narrow/Moriches Bay shoreline, pads within ~1 pad-depth of water

name: Nickerson Beach Campground
note: Nassau County oceanfront park (run by Dover Group concessionaire). 74 RV sites: water + 30/50A electric, 66 with sewer, max 40 ft; open paved lot set back behind the dunes. Non-residents may book. Apr-Nov. RV Life 5*. --Claude

name: Indian Island County Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 40.9245,-72.6285: campsite row along shore road overlooking Peconic River/Flanders Bay across open reed marsh ~40-80m; no pads at waterline
note: Suffolk County park at the Peconic River/Flanders Bay. ~100-150 tent & trailer sites, some electric, showers, dump; some water-view sites. Green Key required (non-resident available). RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: General Clinton Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 42.3014,-75.4594: RV rows on open grass ~50m from Susquehanna with scattered bank trees, open sightline but no pads at waterline
note: Town of Bainbridge park on the Susquehanna River. 50 W/E sites (30/50A), 38 pull-thru, showers; first-come-first-served, no reservations. May-Oct. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Southaven County Park
note: Suffolk County park on the Carmans River. Tent & RV sites, ~51 with electric, showers; loops set back in pine-oak woods. Green Key required (non-resident available). RV Life 3*. --Claude

name: Cathedral Pines County Park

name: Blue Licks Battlefield State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Carlisle). Single loop ~49 modern sites, electric + water (no sewer), level, back-ins ~32-50 ft, dump, showers, laundry; RV loop uphill off US-68 (only 6 primitive tent sites along the Licking River). Year-round (water off & office closed mid-Nov-Mar). Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Greenbo Lake State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Greenup). ~58 equipped sites (electric-only + full-hookup w/ sewer, 20/30/50A), back-in + pull-through, gravel pads, RVs to ~50 ft, 3 dump stations, showers, pool, grocery, + ~36 primitive; wooded, lake frontage is by the lodge. Year-round, reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Natural Bridge State Resort Park - Whittleton Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.7815,-83.6735: pads/RVs strung single-file up the narrow Whittleton Branch hollow whose floor carries the creek; canopy hides the stream, firsthand reports (campsitephotos/thedyrt) confirm creek runs behind camper sites
note: Kentucky State Parks (Slade). ~40 single-family sites; RV/electric sites 1-3 and 24-37 (30/50A, water at most), dump, showers, camp store; Whittleton Branch creek runs behind many camper/tent sites. Tight loop best for rigs under ~30 ft (a 23-ft fits). Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Yatesville Lake State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 38.0968,-82.6824: pads strung along ridge above lake arm ~20-60m away behind tree band and bank drop; no pads at waterline
note: Kentucky State Parks (Louisa). Pleasant Ridge Campground: 27 RV sites, water + 15/30/50A electric (some double-wide), max ~35 ft, dump, showers, laundry; on a ridge above Yatesville Lake (separate boat-in/hike-in primitive sites). Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Kentucky Dam Village State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Gilbertsville). ~221 paved back-in sites, electric throughout w/ water at shared pedestals (bring long cord/hose), 20/30/50A, two dump stations, grocery, four bathhouses; set back behind the resort/marina on Kentucky Lake. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. Some short pads. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Barren River Lake State Resort Park
waterfront_evidence: old pin on park grounds ~500m NE of campground; satellite at 36.8521,-86.0637: loops with RVs on ridge, SW pads overlook open grass slope down to lake arm ~100-150m with open line of sight; no pads at waterline
note: Kentucky State Parks (Lucas). ~99-101 sites; sites 1-9 full hookup (W/E/S), rest water+electric (30/50A), pull-throughs, max ~60 ft, dump, two playgrounds; wooded peninsula on Barren River Lake (some sites have lake views). Sloped - leveling needed. ~late Apr-mid Nov. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: My Old Kentucky Home State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Bardstown). 39 sites (8 pull-through), 5 full hookup, rest water+electric (30/50A), max site length 24-55 ft, dump, bathhouse; golf-course/historic-home setting. Underwent a $4M rebuild (closed 2025, reopening ~spring 2026 - verify open). Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Natural Bridge State Resort Park - Middle Fork Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.7670,-83.6765: RV loop sits in Middle Fork Red River bend with the tree-lined channel along the loop's west edge within ~1 pad-depth; firsthand reports confirm B-section sites back directly to the creek
note: Kentucky State Parks (Slade). ~36 water+electric RV sites (20/30/50A, 8 pull-through, max ~45 ft) + ~11 tent-only; B-section sites back to Middle Fork Creek. Dump, showers, camp store. ~Mar-Oct. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Cumberland Falls State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Corbin). Ridgeline loop ~49 sites with 21 paved RV pads, water + 10/30/50A electric, dump, grocery, laundry, playground; max RV ~30 ft (a 23-ft fits). Clifty section (~9 sites) is tent-only. On a ridge set back from the Cumberland River. ~mid-Mar-mid-Nov. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3.5*. NOTE: the campground is closed for the 2026 season for renovations (reopens 2027); park/reservations otherwise via ReserveAmerica. --Claude

name: Grayson Lake State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Grayson). 71 sites, 30/50A electric + shared water spigots (bring 35-50 ft hose), no full hookups, max RV ~45-60 ft, single dump, showers; on a ridge set back from Grayson Lake (lake access via trail), adjacent to the golf course. Sloped - leveling needed. ~Apr-Oct. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Kincaid Lake State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Falmouth). ~84 standard-electric RV sites (+ 75 tent), 20A electric + water, gravel pads, back-in & some pull-through, large rigs OK, bathhouse, dump; RV loops set back in woods (only some tent sites near the 183-acre lake). ~Mar-Nov 15. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Carr Creek State Park
waterfront_evidence: old pin at bridge/marina day-use area; satellite at 37.2334,-83.0023: peninsula loop pads set back ~25-50m from waterline behind thin shoreline fringe - view yes, not on-water
note: Kentucky State Parks (Sassafras). 39 sites, electric (30A) + water, back-in, max RV ~30-60 ft, bathhouse, dump; adjacent to the longest sand beach in the KY park system on 750-acre Carr Fork Lake (some RV sites have lake views). ~Mar-Nov. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Lake Barkley State Resort Park
waterfront_evidence: old pin in forest ~600m E of campground; satellite at 36.8568,-87.9234: parallel camp rows on open grass ~30-60m from summer-pool shoreline (drawn-down beach in between) with clear view; no pads at waterline
note: Kentucky State Parks (Cadiz). ~78 sites (73 standard electric 30A + water, 6 no-hookup), paved back-in (some steep/unlevel), max RV ~32-58 ft, two bathhouses, dump, campers' boat ramp; wooded ravine with many sites overlooking Lake Barkley fingers. ~Apr-Oct. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3*. --Claude

name: Kenlake State Resort Park
waterfront_evidence: ReserveAmerica per-site data: 60+ of 63 sites 'Near Water: no'; satellite at 36.7812,-88.1451: campground loop in dense forest ~300-400m from Kentucky Lake shore, no line of sight (pin was at US-68/KY-94 junction ~1km south)
note: Kentucky State Parks (Hardin), on the W shore of Kentucky Lake. Reopened Mar 2026 after a $9.3M renovation: 63 full-service sites (sewer), pull-throughs, new electric/water, new bathhouse, more ADA sites; many sites have Kentucky Lake views (RV pads on a wooded bluff). Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3* (pre-renovation). --Claude

name: Lake Cumberland State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Jamestown). Campground on a wooded peninsula/bluff between Pumpkin Creek and Lake Cumberland: ~87 sites (11 full-service + electric, plus tent/primitive), back-in, max RV ~30-35 ft, dump; water off Nov 1-Mar 15. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3*. --Claude

name: Fort Boonesborough State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.9015,-84.2670: NE loop pads within ~20-40m of Kentucky River bank behind a thin single tree row; no pads at waterline so no upgrade
note: Kentucky State Parks (Richmond), on the Kentucky River. 166 sites, electric/water (18 full hookup), paved/level, back-in + pull-through, big-rig friendly, 3 dump stations, showers, laundry, WiFi; a few sites have river views, none directly on the water. CLOSED for flood-damage repairs through 12/31/2026 - scheduled to reopen Jan 2027; verify before relying on it. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Jenny Wiley State Resort Park
waterfront_evidence: ReserveAmerica per-site data: all 118 sites 'Near Water: no'; satellite at 37.7158,-82.7406: loops in forested hollow ~150-250m from Dewey Lake arm behind woods, no open sightline
note: Kentucky State Parks (Prestonsburg), on Dewey Lake. ~121 sites, electric/water/sewer (20-50A), site lengths ~19-95 ft (most 40-50), boat ramp nearby; some sites overlook the lake. CLOSED for a major renovation, scheduled to reopen ~March 2027; verify before relying on it. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 2*. --Claude

name: Kendall Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.8757,-85.1497: riverside row (sites 45-54 per rec.gov coords) on bench ~25-35m above Cumberland River with partial tree screen; main loops set back; no pads at waterline
note: USACE Nashville District, Lake Cumberland. Tailwater campground on the Cumberland River just below Wolf Creek Dam (Jamestown), next to Wolf Creek NFH. 116 sites, water+electric (20/30/50A), two heavily wooded loops, 2 bathhouses, 2 dump stations; back-ins + some pull-throughs, pads to ~50 ft (some listed longer). Sites overlook the river (scenic tailwater); not directly on the water. Reserve online. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Waitsboro Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site flag 'Proximity to water: Lakefront' incl. RV electric site 01; satellite at 37.0146,-84.6424: site lane at top of drawdown band (summer-pool shoreline); pin was 1.6km SE in Burnside residential area
note: USACE Nashville District, Lake Cumberland (Somerset). Small: ~17 RV sites (electric) + 5 tent, 8 sites on the waterfront; dump, showers, laundry, boat ramp, playground. Tight exit turnaround - 5th-wheels over 32 ft are warned off, but a 23-ft rig is fine. Reserve online. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Fishing Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.0713,-84.6870: pad row directly along Fishing Creek arm shoreline, nearest pads ~15-30m from waterline even at drawdown
note: USACE Nashville District, Lake Cumberland (W of Somerset). 26 RV sites + 20 tent, water+electric; boat ramp, dump, showers, laundry. Wooded; many sites sit near/on the water at summer pool (steep inclines down to the lake between areas, sharp entrance). Reserve online. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Fall Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site coords put all 10 sites on ~100m-wide peninsula in Lake Cumberland (pin was 1.6km NE in a subdivision); satellite at 36.9227,-84.8470: pads on open peninsula spine ~30-50m from drawdown waterline with water visible both sides - view confirmed, pads not at waterline; waterbody is the lake, not a river
note: USACE Nashville District, Lake Cumberland (Monticello). Small lakeside loop: 10 RV sites (water+electric, ~29-70 ft), all waterfront, + dump, boat ramp, playground, showers. Reserve online. ~mid-May to mid-Oct. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Baileys Point Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.8910,-86.0970 (leaf-off, winter drawdown): point-loop pads sit at the top edge of the exposed shoreline band (= summer-pool waterline) with water on three sides of the peninsula; rec.gov overview lists several waterfront campsites
note: USACE Nashville District, Barren River Lake (Lucas/Scottsville). Largest of the lake's Corps campgrounds: 215 sites across 5 loops, most water+electric, bathhouses, dump, laundry, two playgrounds, sand volleyball, amphitheater, boat ramp; loops B & C have many sites facing the lake (some on private coves for mooring). Reserve online. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: The Narrows Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.9046,-86.0680: east-loop spur pads ~15-25m from Barren River Lake waterline through thin leaf-off band; peninsula campground surrounded by water
note: USACE Nashville District, Barren River Lake (Lucas), near the marina/boat ramp. 92 sites in two loops, water+electric (electric ~May-Sept); Loop B sites let you pull a boat right up to the site (on the water). Sand beach, amphitheater, nature trail. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Tailwater Campground (Barren River Lake)
note: USACE Nashville District, Barren River Lake (Glasgow). 48 spread-out sites along the Barren River just below the dam; water + 50A electric in peak season (~May-Sept), primitive off-season (dump, potable water, pit toilets). Boat ramp and riverside paths, but no sites have direct water access. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Canal Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.9972,-88.2112: NE-loop RV pads at beach/waterline on Lake Barkley cove; shoreline sites also visible along east shore at 36.997,-88.210
note: USACE Nashville District, Lake Barkley (Grand Rivers), on the canal connecting Lake Barkley and Kentucky Lake at the north end of LBL. ~115 sites, water+electric (some full hookup), densely wooded; 3 dump stations, showers, laundry, playground, beach, boat ramps. Many sites are waterfront on one of the two lakes. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Hurricane Creek Campground (Lake Barkley)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.9170,-87.9763: RV pads ~15-25m from cove waterline along shore road; multiple RVs visible at shoreline sites
note: USACE Nashville District, Lake Barkley (Eddyville). 51 water+electric sites (6 walk-to), lakefront camping with direct lake access; showers, laundry, playground, boat ramp. Max site ~40 ft; a 23-ft rig is fine. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Eureka Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.0217,-88.1950: RVs on shoreline loop road ~10-30m from Lake Barkley waterline; pin is at campground's north edge (rec.gov sites span to 37.0245), within facility footprint
note: USACE Nashville District, Lake Barkley (Kuttawa/Eddyville). Small: 26 sites, all water+electric, some with direct lake access; showers, playground, boat launch, swimming area, picnic shelter. Chestnut Oaks Trail starts here. Peak ~late Apr-mid Oct. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Dog Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.3228,-86.1320: spur pads ring the peninsula with shoreline at the loop edge on Nolin River Lake (drawdown beach visible)
note: USACE Louisville District, Nolin River Lake (Cub Run). 70 sites (50 water+electric to 50A), well-spaced; many sites along the shoreline (waterfront), modern bathhouse, dump station, showers, beach. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Moutardier Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.3138,-86.2348: spur pads on shoreline lobes ringed by Nolin River Lake, pads at the water's edge
note: USACE Louisville District, Nolin River Lake (Leitchfield/Sweeden). Large: 167 sites, water+electric (50A, some pads to ~75 ft), flush toilets, showers, dump, picnic shelters, boat ramp, marina; sites along the shoreline (waterfront). Reserve online. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Axtel Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site data: 63 RV sites flagged Proximity to Water=Lakefront (A023-A026, B027+); satellite at 37.6212,-86.4575 shows RVs at drawn-down Rough River Lake shoreline
note: USACE Louisville District, Rough River Lake (McDaniels/Falls of Rough). Large: 158 sites, most water+electric, sits directly on the lakeshore with lake views from nearly all sites (waterfront sites book fast); swim beach, boat ramp, dump, showers, playground, fish-cleaning station. Reserve online. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Laurel Branch Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site data: 31 RV sites flagged Proximity to Water=Lakefront (B21-B24, C31+); satellite at 37.6066,-86.4608 shows campers ~20-40m from waterline on C-loop point (lake drawn down); pin sits in A loop
note: USACE Louisville District, Rough River Lake (McDaniels). 62 sites (several electric), most waterfront (rest a short walk to the lake); flush toilets, showers, playground, boat ramp, beach, fish-cleaning, nature trail. ~Apr 14-Oct 30. Reserve online. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Cave Creek Campground (Rough River Lake)
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site data: sites 49-64 flagged Proximity to Water: Lakefront at 37.572,-86.493; satellite shows that SE loop on the cove (lake drawn down for dam work, waterline ambiguous, official per-site flag governs)
note: USACE Louisville District, Rough River Lake (Falls of Rough); Corps land run for the 2026 season by concessionaire BrightSky Campgrounds. 65 sites (36 electric/water; RV section tiered down the hillside, pads to big-rig length); dump, showers, boat ramp, fishing pier, disc golf, playground. Hilltop basic sites have no water access; lower RV sites overlook the cove (most not directly on the water). ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: North Fork Campground (Rough River Lake)
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site data: 10 sites flagged Lakefront (e.g. E063/E064); satellite at 37.630,-86.436 shows east loops adjoining the drawn-down lake arm
note: USACE Louisville District, Rough River Lake (McDaniels); Corps land run for the 2026 season by concessionaire BrightSky Campgrounds. 81 sites (50 electric); several sites on the waterfront, others a short walk to the lake; dump, showers, beach, boat ramp, fishing pier, two group shelters, playground. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 3.5*/$. --Claude

name: Pikes Ridge Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site data: 31 of 60 sites flagged Lakefront; satellite at 37.2849,-85.2936 shows peninsula spurs at the Green River Lake waterline; old pin (rec.gov facility coord) was 550m SE in unbroken forest
note: USACE Louisville District, Green River Lake (Campbellsville); Corps land run for the 2026 season by concessionaire BrightSky Campgrounds. Shoreline campground: 60 sites (20 water+electric), several waterfront; boat ramp, courtesy/tie-up docks, fishing pier, swim beach, playground, showers, hiking trail. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Smith Ridge Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.2944,-85.2921 (leaf-off): all loops on ridge top ~250-400m from lake behind continuous forest; rec.gov: no per-site proximity flags, beach/ramp 'nearby/within 1 mile'; old pin was 700m W in forest
note: USACE Louisville District, Green River Lake (Campbellsville). 80 sites, most with electric (many water+electric), set along the lake perimeter with many waterfront-access sites; flush toilets, showers, playground, hiking trail; marina swim beach/disc golf within ~1 mi. Big, well-spaced sites; weak cell signal. Reserve online. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Holmes Bend Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.213,-85.265 (leaf-off): loops wind along wooded ridge, nearest spurs ~100-150m from shore behind forest belt, no pads at waterline; rec.gov: no per-site lakefront flags (125 sites), waterfront amenities listed as 'within 1 mile' at the marina
note: USACE Louisville District, Green River Lake (Columbia/Cane Valley). 125 sites (40 electric-only, 62 water+electric), pads to ~70 ft, set along the lake perimeter with many waterfront-access sites; flush toilets, showers, two playgrounds, interpretive trail, paved roads good for biking; swim beach/boat ramp/fishing pier marina-managed within ~1 mi. Reserve online. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Buckhorn Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site data: 28 of 37 sites flagged Riverfront; satellite at 37.3449,-83.4697 shows loop pads along the Middle Fork KY River bank in the bend below Buckhorn Dam (tailwater river, not the lake); old pin was on Buckhorn village 700m N
note: USACE Louisville District, Buckhorn Lake (Buckhorn), eastern KY Cumberland Plateau. ~30 paved standard-electric sites + walk-in tent and boat-in; 14 waterfront sites at the water's edge. Flush toilets, hot showers, dump, laundry, WiFi, playground, swim area, boat ramp. RVs to ~50 ft. Open ~May 1-Sep 30. Reserve online. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Littcarr Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.237,-82.948: spur pads strung along east bank within ~15-30m of the Carr Fork arm of Carr Creek Lake (winter-drawdown channel visible, fluctuation-zone mud flats confirm lake arm)
note: USACE Louisville District, Carr Creek (Carr Fork) Lake (Littcarr/Vicco), SE KY mountains. 45 water+electric sites, waterfront sites with direct lake access; flush toilets, showers, laundry, dump, two playgrounds, picnic shelter, horseshoe/basketball courts. RVs to ~54 ft. Reserve online. Distinct from Carr Creek State Park on the same lake. RV Life 2.5*/$. --Claude

name: Trace Branch Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.2727,-83.3695: angled RV pads in a row at the shoreline of Buckhorn Lake's upper Middle Fork arm; rec.gov flags 18 of 29 sites Riverfront (same on-water tier); old pin was 3.4km S in a residential creek valley (KY F&W ramp listing located the real site)
note: USACE Louisville District, Buckhorn Lake (near Hazard), eastern KY. Mostly-waterfront sites with electric/water (some 50A, some sewer), pads to ~70 ft; shower house, dump, playground, fishing area. Mix of reservable and first-come sites. Open ~May-Sept. --Claude  RV Life 3.5*/$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Blue Heron Campground
note: NPS, Big South Fork National River & Recreation Area (Stearns), on the plateau near the Blue Heron historic mining community. 45 sites, all water+electric (30A), back-in + pull-through, pads to ~61 ft; flush toilets, hot showers, dump station. Wooded (not on the water; the river gorge is below the rim). ~Apr-Nov. Reserve online. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Twin Knobs Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site data: 13 standard RV sites (A030, D009-D013, F019-F023, J008-J014, driveways 44-96 ft) flagged Proximity to Water: Lakefront on Cave Run Lake; satellite canopy-blind at shoreline loops
note: USFS Daniel Boone NF, Cave Run Lake (Salt Lick/Morehead). Large: 200+ sites over 10 loops, electric to full-hookup (premium), paved spurs to ~70 ft, dump, bathhouses, camp store, boat ramp, sandy swim beach; outer-loop sites sit on the lakeshore (waterfront sites book early). ~late Apr-early Nov. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Clear Creek Campground
note: USFS Daniel Boone NF, Clear Creek Recreation Area (Salt Lick), E of Cave Run Lake. Small primitive ~20-site campground along Clear Creek (tent/RV pads, no hookups), vault toilets, drinking water; boat ramp onto Clear Creek Lake + picnic area nearby. Max RV ~30 ft (a 23-ft rig fits); first-come, first-served, no reservations. ~Apr 15-Nov 29. RV Life 3.5*/$. --Claude

name: Holly Bay Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site data: 51 of 70 STANDARD ELECTRIC sites flagged Proximity to Water: Lakefront on Laurel River Lake (e.g. sites 3-7, D01-D10, E1-E7); canopy-blind satellite cannot veto - upgrade earned by per-site reservation data
note: USFS Daniel Boone NF, Laurel River Lake (Keavy/London), ~2 mi N of Laurel River Dam. ~75 drive-in sites (single + double family), 20/30/50A electric + water, paved pads to ~50 ft, flush toilets, showers; near Holly Bay Marina/boat ramp. Many sites have water views but none directly on the shore. Run by concessionaire American Land & Leisure. ~early Apr-mid Oct. Reserve online. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Grove Campground (Laurel River Lake)
note: USFS Daniel Boone NF, Laurel River Lake (Corbin). Drive-in campground on a wooded ridge above the lake: single + double family sites, 30A electric (some full-hookup), paved pads to ~50 ft, flush toilets, showers, drinking water. Reservable ~Memorial Day-Labor Day (else first-come). Separate from the Grove boat-in campground. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Bee Rock Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.0287,-84.3205: small campsite clearings with vehicles on open Rockcastle River bank ~30-60m from water with line of sight; no pads confirmed at waterline so riverview, not riverfront
note: USFS Daniel Boone NF, Bee Rock Recreation Area (off KY 192, ~18 mi W of London), along the Rockcastle River; renovated 2022. 25 wooded first-come sites, no hookups, vault toilets, water spigots. North loop is the RV-friendly side; best for compact rigs - a 23-ft EKKO-sized motorhome fits, but call the London Ranger District before bringing anything bigger (access road has narrow/bumpy stretches near the river). Pulaski-side loop ~mid-Apr-mid-Oct, Laurel-side year-round. RV Life lists it under 'state' but it is USFS. RV Life 3*/$. --Claude

name: Energy Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.8560,-88.0190: campground loops occupy peninsula on Energy Lake with RV pads ~30-80m from shore under partial canopy overlooking water (swim boom at shore); no pads resolved at waterline
note: USFS Land Between the Lakes NRA (Golden Pond), on Energy Lake (a quiet Lake Barkley embayment). 35 wooded sites, 33 with electric (30/50A), water along the loop road, dump station, restrooms/showers, swim beach, playground, kayak rentals. Some sites have lake views with trails down to the water (none directly on the shore); rigs over ~30 ft fit only some sites - call ahead. Reserve via LBL's reservation system, not Recreation.gov. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Redd Hollow Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.7136,-88.0727: self-service camp track and cleared sites sit at the Lake Barkley shoreline vegetation line (waterline at summer pool; imagery shows winter drawdown flats)
note: USFS Land Between the Lakes NRA (Kentucky Lake / west side). Primitive self-service campground: pea-gravel pads, fire rings, picnic tables, vault toilet, boat ramp, lake access; no hookups or potable water (dump/water at Golden Pond). Good-condition gravel access road (no 4WD). Some sites sit right on the water, others higher with lake views; a few pads are big enough for a small RV (best for compact rigs like a 23-ft EKKO). First-come, self-pay ($10). RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Taylor Bay Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.8838,-88.0203: open camping loop on a point with bay wrapping E/S, RV on site, gravel spurs on open grassy bank ~20-40m from drawn-down waterline with zero tree buffer
note: USFS Land Between the Lakes NRA, on Taylor Bay of Lake Barkley (east side, by the waterfowl refuge). Primitive self-service campground: ~8-10 lakefront sites (boats can tie up) + ~8-10 wooded sites, designated pads, picnic tables, grills, fire rings, vault toilets, boat ramp; no hookups or potable water. Accommodates tents and RVs. First-come, self-pay ($10). RV Life 2.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Unhitched Paducah RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.9730,-88.5598: RV rows immediately flank rectangular stocked pond (~55x26m) on both long sides within ~10m; official site confirms 'fully stocked fishing pond'
note: Private (Paducah). Full-hookup RV park (20/30/50A), pull-through + back-in, daily/weekly/monthly; clubhouse, 2 bathhouses, laundry, propane, dog parks. Catch-and-release fishing pond with dock - some sites sit on the pond. Real transient park. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Duck Creek RV Park
note: Private (Paducah). 89 oversized level full-hookup sites (30/50A), pull-throughs, free WiFi, seasonal pool, stocked fishing pond, dog park/wash, walking trails. Easy in/out for overnighters; some highway noise near the front sites. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Birdsville Riverside RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.2147,-88.4471: front row of RV pads runs along open Ohio River bank ~20-40m from waterline with no buffer, boat ramp adjacent; original pin sat in empty field ~250m NE of the rows
note: Private (Smithland), on the Ohio River near the Cumberland confluence. 34 full-hookup riverfront sites (30/50A), big-rig friendly, shade trees, small dock; Harvest Hosts. Seasonal operation, transient. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Stagecoach Station Campground
note: Private (Benton), near Kentucky Lake / LBL. Water+electric (50A) sites, pull-through + back-in to ~70 ft, dump station (no individual sewer; weekend pump-out), bathhouse, game room, WiFi. Transient, friendly owners. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Tween the Lakes Campground
note: Private (Grand Rivers), easy on/off I-24/I-69 between Kentucky Lake and Lake Barkley. Full-hookup (50A) sites, concrete pads; a straightforward overnight stop. No on-site bathhouse/showers as of recent reports (permit pending). RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Tanglewood Campground & RV Resort
note: Private (Hardin/Aurora), ~1 mi from LBL, walking distance to Kentucky Lake. 60 full-hookup sites (30/50A), tents to Class A; laundry, showers, fish-cleaning station, WiFi, pavilion, fishing pond, playground. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Birmingham Point Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.9078,-88.2052: pin was in forest 360m SE; campground occupies a Kentucky Lake peninsula with multiple RV pads within ~10-20m of the waterline on the west and north shores
note: Private (Benton), on Kentucky Lake. 76 sites, water+electric (20/30/50A), boat ramp, fishing pier, swim beach, playground; lake-view sites. Mostly seasonal campers with ~10 sites kept for short-term/transient. Tight exit - rigs over ~38 ft struggle (a 23-ft rig is fine). RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Kamptown RV Resort
note: Private (Cadiz), near Lake Barkley. 93 full-hookup gravel sites, swimming pool, playground; short- and long-term stays. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Lake Cumberland RV Park
note: Private (Bronston), ~5 min to a Lake Cumberland boat ramp with free shuttle to State Dock. 102 sites, electric/full hookups, wooded rolling terrain; reservations + monthly options. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Somerset KY RV Park
note: Private (Somerset). Small, well-kept full-hookup park across from a golf course; friendly, quiet, well-run. Transient. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Wolf River Resort & Marina
note: Private (Albany), on Dale Hollow Lake (end of Hwy 738), operating since 1946. ~30 RV sites with full hookups + cabins, motel, houseboats, marina, restaurant, pool, WiFi. Seasonal ~May-Sept. RV camping is one part of a marina resort. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: 829 RV Park
note: Private (Albany), ~5 mi from Marina Rowena (Lake Cumberland). 32 full-hookup gravel sites (30/50A, septic, WiFi), 24 kept for transient overnight/weekly/monthly; new, quiet, hands-on owners. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Pinecrest RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.0026,-84.9337: RV rows sit on a plateau ~125m from the nearest Lake Cumberland cove behind a dense unbroken forest band; no line of sight to water
note: Private (Russell Springs), on a peninsula overlooking Lake Cumberland, ~1 mi from Alligator boat launches. 50+ full-hookup sites, year-round, + seasonal cabins. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (some recent reviews note upkeep slipping under newer owners). --Claude

name: Singing Hills RV Park & Campground
note: Private (Cave City), 2 mi from Mammoth Cave NP. 29 full-hookup RV sites + tent, bathhouse, dump, catch-and-release fishing pond; year-round, friendly owners, Passport America/Good Sam. No-frills, well-rated transient park. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Rock Cabin Camping
note: Private (Cave City), closest campground to the Mammoth Cave NP visitor center. Restored 1928 National Register cabins + full-hookup RV sites (50A) + tent sites; restrooms/showers. Variable upkeep per recent reviews. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Cedar Creek Camp
note: Private (Crab Orchard), ~1 mi from Cedar Creek Lake. Family-owned: 10 shady water+electric sites on gravel pads, modern bathhouse, laundry/washroom; reservations. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: OH! Kentucky RV Park & Campground
note: Private (Berea), off I-75 exit 76. Year-round; spacious pull-through 30/50A full-hookup sites, free WiFi, pool, laundry, propane, dump, bathhouse, camp store. Popular, well-kept I-75 overnight stop. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Grandma's RV Camping
note: Private (Shepherdsville), off I-65 south of Louisville. Big-rig-friendly pull-throughs to ~70 ft, full hookups, free WiFi; easy quick-overnight stop (interstate noise; not dog-friendly). RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Guist Creek Marina & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 38.2101,-85.1449: main RV loop set ~150-200m back from Guist Creek Lake across open grass with line of sight; waterline units on the west shore appear to be seasonal trailers, so on-water not credited to RV sites
note: Private (Shelbyville), on Guist Creek Lake (Shelbyville's water-supply lake). Pull-through water+electric (30/50A) + primitive sites, shower house, dump, marina/boat ramp; lake-view sites. ~Mar-Nov. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Green River Stables
note: Private (Campbellsville), ~1 mi from Green River Lake (boat ramp/beach). 93 full-hookup RV sites + 50 horse stables - equestrian-oriented but open to all RVers (general FHU sites, camper lounge, events); 4 bathhouses, laundry; year-round, 30+ mi of trails. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Cedars RV Park
note: Private (Campbellsville), <1 mi from Green River Lake. Family-owned (since 2011): 25 spacious full-hookup sites (20/30/50A, city water, sewer), WiFi, community pavilion; year-round. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Creekside Cabins & RV Park
note: Private (Louisa/Fallsburg, E KY), near Yatesville Lake SP. Full-hookup sites (50A, water/sewer) + cabins, on-site restaurant, WiFi; family-run, nightly stays. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: The Falls Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 38.1779,-82.6744: nearest RV row sits ~50m above the falls swim-hole pool with open line of sight across the sand beach; NE loop RVs ~40-70m from the creek behind a tree band; no pads at the bank
note: Private (Louisa/Fallsburg), on Blaine Creek at the Falls of Blaine Creek. RV hookups + tent sites, bathhouses, laundry; some waterfront sites along the creek (book ahead). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Wild Cat Campground
note: Private (Flatgap, ~11 mi from Paintsville). 10 full-hookup gravel sites (30/50A), fits 40+ ft rigs; catfish paylake, bait/store, game room, pavilion, playground; restrooms but no showers/laundry. Call to reserve. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Valley Breeze RV Campground
note: Private (Grayson), off I-64. 16 long level full-hookup pull-through sites; an overnight-only stop (no bathhouse - self-contained RVs), cash/check. Friendly hosts. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Grayson Getaways
note: Private (Grayson), 84 wooded acres near Grayson Lake. RV sites + cabins/glamping, bathhouse, laundry, 4+ mi of private trails, kayak/SUP rentals. Clean, quiet. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Harlan County Campground
note: Private (Putney, near Harlan); family-owned (Foster family) - despite the name, not a county park. 19 RV/tent sites + cabins at the Black Mountain Adventure Area off-road trailhead; bathhouses; nightly/weekly/monthly. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Hopewell RV Park
note: Private (Drakesboro, Muhlenberg Co). 40 full-hookup sites (city water/sewer, upgraded electric), WiFi; level large pads, big-rig friendly. Mixed clientele - vacation overnighters plus some longer-term contract workers (TVA Paradise / Logan Aluminum). RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Timeless Days Campground
note: Private (Lucas), 0.5 mi from Barren River Lake SP (formerly Spring Hill RV Park, new management). 40 full-hookup gravel sites (30/50A), nightly to seasonal; quiet, helpful owner. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: County Line Campground & Storage
note: Private (Dover, ~8 mi W of Maysville on the AA Highway). 20 full-hookup sites (20/30/50A, water/sewer), DSL; also offers RV/boat storage. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Paintsville Lake State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.8427,-82.8787: RV row strung along hillside road ~45-100m above the lake; lower sites overlook open lawn/beach down to shore, no pads at waterline
note: Kentucky State Parks (Staffordsville), on Paintsville Lake. 32 full-hookup sites (W/E/S, level gravel pads, RVs to ~48 ft, some pull-through) + 10 primitive + hike-in tent; restrooms, showers, laundry. Full-hookup loop within walking distance of the shoreline; several sites have direct lake views (e.g. site 18). Year-round. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks (ReserveAmerica). (RV Life mislabels it commercial; it is a state park.) RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Kentucky Horse Park Campground
note: State-owned (Kentucky Horse Park, Lexington; run by the Commonwealth, not the state-parks system). Large: 260+ paved sites, water + 20/30/50A electric, fire rings, + primitive area; bathhouses. Open to all campers (not horse-only). Year-round (B & D loops in off-season). Reserve via ReserveAmerica. (RV Life mislabels it commercial.) RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Pikeville RV Park
note: Local (City of Pikeville). Level concrete full-hookup pads (30/50A, continuous sewer), free cable + WiFi, daily-serviced trash; $25. In the city's rec corridor (dog park, courts, trails). RV Life 5*. --Claude

name: Levi Jackson Wilderness Road Park
note: Local (City of London; a state park until 2019, now city-owned). Large wooded campground ~136-146 sites, water+electric at most (sewer at some), 3 bathhouse/laundry buildings, camp store; historic mill & museum on site. Some sites year-round. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Riverfront Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: Firsthand manual audit by owner (--AWH note); waterfront 'riverview' per owner: Riverfront Park on the Ohio River. 16 sites across a field from the river, W/E, dump, free WiFi; two boat ramps, dock, river views. ~Apr 1-Nov 30, 10-day limit, $25. RV Life 4*.
note: Riverfront Park on the Ohio River. 16 sites across a field from the river, W/E, dump, free WiFi; two boat ramps, dock, river views. ~Apr 1-Nov 30, 10-day limit, $25. RV Life 4*. --AWH

name: Pulaski County Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.0661,-84.6991: pin was in woods ~500m W; campground spur pads sit at the vegetation line (summer-pool shoreline) of the Fishing Creek cove, ~20-40m from winter-drawdown water with open ground
note: Local (Pulaski County), 804-acre park on Lake Cumberland (W Pulaski Co). RV sites (water+electric, upgrading to 50A) + primitive, near the lake; beach, boat ramps, courtesy dock, bathhouse, cabins, disc golf, trails. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Lake Jericho Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 38.4426,-85.2790: west and south loop camper pads within ~15-30m of the shoreline across mowed bank, multiple trailers at the lake edge with dock below
note: Local (Henry County), around 137-acre Lake Jericho (est. 1969). 62 RV sites (water+electric, ~half with sewer), dump, camp store; lakeside. ~Apr-Oct, first-come (nightly/weekly/monthly). RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Moffit Lake Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.5792,-87.8465: pin was in woods ~285m S; camper pads line the NE shore road within ~10-20m of the lake edge
note: Local (Union County), on 64-acre Moffit Lake (~10 mi SE of Morganfield). 48 sites (30/50A + water), waterfront lake sites, 2 dump stations, 3 bathhouses + unlimited primitive. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: 2 Rivers Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 38.6780,-85.1849: legible - RV rows set back ~30-45m from Kentucky River behind a continuous riparian treeline (boat ramp only at waterline); no pads on shore, no open sightline through summer canopy
note: Local (City of Carrollton), in Point Park at the Kentucky/Ohio river confluence. 33 full-hookup sites on level concrete pads (pull-through + back-in), bathhouse, playground, riverfront River Walk & pier; exceptionally clean. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Portal 31 RV Park
note: Local (Harlan County Fiscal Court / Tourist & Convention Commission), in Lynch next to the Portal 31 Exhibition Mine. 12 full-hookup sites + 8 primitive, big-rig friendly gravel, laundry, on-site coffee shop; year-round, $15. RV Life 5*. --Claude

name: Ohio County Park and Campground
note: Local (Ohio County), a 500-acre county park ~2 mi from Hartford. 84 sites (27 full-hookup, 5 pull-through; 50A water/electric/sewer); park lake, ball fields, ample rec. RV Life 2*. --Claude

name: Logan-Hubble County Park
note: Local (Garrard County), a 400-acre park/nature preserve between Stanford and Lancaster with a 12-acre lake. 13 gravel water+electric sites; disc golf, trails, shelters. ~Apr-Oct. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Marion Fair RV Park
note: Local (Marion County fairgrounds, Lebanon). 20 full-hookup sites (20/30/50A, water/sewer, gravel, max ~50 ft); open year-round for transient camping (not events-only). RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Kentucky Splash Waterpark and Campground
note: Local (City of Williamsburg). Campground at the city's waterpark: 25 full-hookup sites (20/30/50A, water/electric/sewer, cable, WiFi) + 6 tent; year-round. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Mine Made Paradise Adventure Park & Campground
note: Local (Knott County), an ATV/horse adventure park on reclaimed coal land (Leburn/Vest). RV sites (water+electric) + cabins/tent, camp store, shower house; gateway to an extensive marked trail system. Open to all campers. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Sandhill RV Park & Camp
note: Local (McCreary County, Whitley City). 80 water+electric sites + primitive, dump station, showers, playground, shelter, community building. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Governor's Campground
note: Local (City of Manchester, Clay County), adjacent to Beech Creek Lake. ~75 sites (24 full-hookup concrete pads + 8 water/electric, rest primitive), dump, laundry, store, playground, courts, pavilions; year-round. RV Life 2.5*. --Claude

name: Myer Creek Park Campground
note: Local (McLean County, Calhoun). ~30 sites (12 with 50A + 12 with 30A water/electric, some water-only), day-use shelters; county park, RV sites being expanded. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Fish Pond Lake RV Park
waterfront_evidence: campendium firsthand review (reviewer GPS 37.1562,-82.6771): 'cg sits up above the lake and there is no direct water access', overlook decks' view 'obstructed by trees' - sites have no real lake view; old pin was lake day-use shore ~900m N; Esri tiles predate the new 15-site park
note: Local (Letcher County), in 895-acre Little Laurel Park above 45-acre Fish Pond Lake (~1 mi W of Payne Gap, Jenkins). Graveled RV sites (water/sewer/30A), WiFi, restrooms (no showers); set above the lake (lake views, no direct water access), electric-motor/paddle boating. ~Mar-Oct. RV Life 3*. --Claude

name: Fox Valley Recreation Park
note: Local (Fleming County, Wallingford). 85 gravel sites, RVs to ~45 ft, 20/30A water/electric (some sewer), flush toilets, hot showers, dump, store; many seasonal/permanent sites plus a couple nightly-rental sites and a large primitive area. ~Apr-Oct, 14-day max. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Chain O'Lakes State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 42.4506,-88.1880: campground loops on open knoll east of Turner Lake, nearest pads ~60-120m back with tree/marsh band at shore, no pads at waterline; original pin 42.4585,-88.2113 was farmland/subdivision at park's west boundary
note: Illinois DNR (Spring Grove), at the Fox River chain of lakes. ~238 sites: Class A electric (Honeysuckle Hollow, Fox Den) + Class B-premium areas (Turner Lake South, Prairie View, Mud Lake East); showers, dump. Some sites by the water. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Kankakee River State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Bourbonnais), on the Kankakee River. Potawatomi Campground: 110 Class A electric sites (showers, dump, flush toilets), RVs to ~35 ft; + Chippewa (Class BE/C). Wooded, set back from the river. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Rock Cut State Park
waterfront_evidence: IL DNR site map: Campground marked inland by Plum Grove Nature Preserve, lakeshore peninsula is the Youth Camp; satellite at 42.358,-88.980: camp loops 350-650m north of Pierce Lake in forest
note: Illinois DNR (Loves Park/Rockford), at Pierce Lake. ~270 sites (199 electric across Prairie View/White Oak/Plum Grove/Stag Horn + 55 non-electric Hickory Hills); showers, dump, boat launch, playground. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL May-Oct (walk-in Nov-Mar). RV Life 3.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Lowden State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 42.035,-89.325 (leaf-off): camp area on wooded bluff top ~250-400m from Rock River, no pads at bluff edge; no official per-site map found confirming river-view RV sites — default down
note: Illinois DNR (Oregon), on a bluff above the Rock River (Black Hawk statue). Sun Valley Area: 80 Class A electric (to 50A, shower, dump) + 38 Class B/S + hike-in. RVs/trailers over 40 ft not permitted. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Mississippi Palisades State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Savanna), bluffs above the Mississippi River. 241 sites, 110 electric (30/50A), max RV ~35 ft; showers May-Oct, dump. Campground set back atop the bluffs. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Lake Le-Aqua-Na State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 42.4252,-89.8368: Hilltop campground loops ~300m NW of lake behind unbroken forest; lakeshore east end is beach/day-use only; original pin sat at park entrance road
note: Illinois DNR (Lena), on 45-acre Lake Le-Aqua-Na. 132 electric sites (some 50A) in Hickory Hill (paved, RV-friendly) + Pine Ridge; swim beach, boat ramp. Campground year-round; showers/dump May-Nov. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: White Pines Forest State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Mount Morris), along Pine Creek (fords/white pines). ~103 Class B/S drive-up sites (vehicular access, showers, dump) + only 2-3 non-reservable electric; $10. Small wooded park - best for compact rigs (a 23-ft fits; verify larger sites). FCFS + ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 3.5*/$. --Claude

name: Apple River Canyon State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Apple River, NW IL). Canyon Ridge Campground: 49 Class C primitive sites (no hookups), pit toilets, water spigots, dump station, no showers; $8. ~mid-Apr to Oct, reservable + FCFS via ExploreMoreIL. Winding canyon access. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Morrison-Rockwood State Park
waterfront_evidence: official IDNR brochure map: campground loops well north of Lake Carlton; 'Lakeview' and 'Cedar Ridge' overlooking the lake are picnic areas, camping is in heavily wooded interior; satellite shows no loops near shore
note: Illinois DNR (Morrison), on Lake Carlton. 92 electric sites (15/30A, grill, table; 59 reservable), shower May-Oct, dump. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Johnson-Sauk Trail State Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 41.3263,-89.8879: easternmost Chief Keokuk pads ~50-70m above Johnson Lake on partly open slope with water line of sight; no pads at waterline; original pin was park road ~400m north of loops
note: Illinois DNR (Kewanee/Mineral), on Johnson Lake. Chief Keokuk Campground: 70 Class A electric pads + 25 tent; showers, dump, boat ramp, marina. Lakeside wooded setting. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL May-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Shabbona Lake State Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 41.7520,-88.8664: SE row of Arrowhead campground pads with RVs on open grass ~25-45m from Shabbona Lake shoreline, clear water view but not within one pad-depth; original pin was interior pond/golf area ~600m NW
note: Illinois DNR (Shabbona), on Shabbona Lake (muskie fishing). 150 Class A electric sites, showers, boat launch, store, playground. Many sites are small (not for big rigs; a 23-ft fits). Reserve via ExploreMoreIL May-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Prophetstown State Recreation Area
note: Illinois DNR (Prophetstown), near the Rock River. 105 sites in two loops, all electric (some 50A); about half are full-hookup; dump, water hydrants, privies. Open/shaded. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Giant City State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Makanda), Shawnee hill country. 85 Class A electric sites (9 50A, rest 30A), water, showers, dump, privies; ~half reservable, rest FCFS via silent-host. $20. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL (closed Dec-Mar). RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Ferne Clyffe State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Goreville). Turkey Ridge Campground: North Loop (24-59) reservable electric (some 50A), South Loop (1-23) FCFS; + Deer Ridge (4 Class A, RVs to 70 ft). Showers, flush toilets, dump. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Fort Massac State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.1465,-88.7085: campground loops ~250-400m from Ohio River behind wooded floodplain (flooded in imagery); fort/day-use occupy the riverbank, no camp pads near or overlooking the river
note: Illinois DNR (Metropolis), on the Ohio River (Illinois' first state park; reconstructed 1757 fort). 50 electric sites (30/50A, no water/sewer at sites), new bathhouse; boat dock/launch. Year-round (walk-in off-season). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Cave-in-Rock State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Cave-In-Rock), on a bluff above the Ohio River. 34 Class A electric sites (30/50A, units to ~60 ft), water, showers, flush toilets, dump. Campground set back - no river view from sites. First-come, first-served (no reservations). RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Dixon Springs State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Golconda), within Shawnee NF. 39 electric sites (30/50A; 2 pull-through), max RV ~40 ft, water, showers, dump, courts. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Lake Murphysboro State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.7793,-89.3830 (Big Oak loop, matches ExploreMoreIL facility coord): RV pads with campers on peninsula within ~1 pad-depth of waterline; original pin was 430m W at a picnic loop in forest
note: Illinois DNR (Murphysboro), on Lake Murphysboro. 54 electric trailer sites (30/50A, electric-only), dump, showers; some back-in sites are right on the lake (mostly shorter rigs; max ~40 ft). + 20 tent. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 3.5*/$. --Claude

name: Trail of Tears State Forest
note: Illinois DNR state forest (Jonesboro, 5,000 acres). 14 standard drive-in sites, no hookups, pads 25-130 ft (RVs of varying sizes fit), tables/fire rings, shelters with toilets/water; $8, year-round, reservable + self-register. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Fort Kaskaskia State Historic Site
waterfront_evidence: official site page: 'the campground itself doesn't directly overlook the Mississippi' (overlook is a separate day-use bluff); satellite at 37.9724,-89.9103 (ExploreMoreIL Main Loop coord): loop in bluff-top clearing ~300m from river behind forest; pin was 470m S
note: Illinois DNR historic site (Ellis Grove), on a bluff overlooking the Mississippi/Kaskaskia. 32 electric-hookup sites (some walk-in) + primitive, water at select. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Randolph County State Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: ExploreMoreIL: RV campground is 'Pine Ridge' (51 B-E sites) at 37.9692,-89.8014; satellite: loop on wooded ridge ~150-300m inland of lake, full forest between; no unit waterfront flags; pin was at a road junction
note: Illinois DNR (Chester), on a 65-acre lake. Pine Ridge Campground: 146 sites - Class BE electric + some Class C full-hookup (water/sewer); RV limit ~40 ft, dump, restrooms, boat ramp; sites walk directly to the lake. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Pyramid State Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: DNR brochure map + OSM: North Campground at 38.0322,-89.3996; leaf-off satellite: camp clearing/loop ~60-120m from strip-pit lakes adjoining the boat-launch clearing at shore; pads not at waterline; pin was 480m NW on a pit road
note: Illinois DNR (Pinckneyville), reclaimed strip-mine with 500+ acres of lakes. 54 sites across 3 areas (Pyramid North, Boulder Lake, Jim Batson Trailer Area); no hookups (boondocking), max RV ~30 ft, pit toilets, tables/fire pits. First-come, first-served (no online reservations; not in the ExploreMoreIL system - verified 6/2026). RV Life 2*/$. --Claude

name: Sam Dale Lake State Fish and Wildlife Area
waterfront_evidence: ExploreMoreIL: B/E loops are H Hollow and L View (Sassafras Point is Class D primitive); satellite at 38.5435,-88.5918: L View loop in woods ~100m+ upslope of shore, no pads at waterline; DNR camp page says 'shady, secluded section of the park'; pin was 600m SE near office
note: Illinois DNR (Johnsonville), on 194-acre Sam Dale Lake. Two lakefront electric campgrounds - Hickory Hollow (33 sites, pads 25-58 ft) and Lake View (33 sites, pads 40-64 ft), both a few feet from the water; boat dock, playground, dump. Year-round. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Saline County State Fish and Wildlife Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.6917,-88.3796: open-grass camping area on slope across the shore road from Glen O. Jones Lake NE corner (~80-120m, sparse trees, open sightline); pads not at waterline
note: Illinois DNR (Equality), overlooking Glen O Jones Lake. 45 sites, no electric/water hookups (water spigot near #31), trailer dump, pads 40-45 ft; boat ramp, swimming. First-come, first-served, ~Apr-Sept. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Fort Defiance State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.9846,-89.1442: camping is on the open park lawn inside the levee ring at the Ohio/Mississippi confluence, ~60-150m from banks behind levee/tree fringe — rivers visible from open lawn but no pads at waterline; DNR page documents no campground, camping area informal
note: Illinois DNR (Cairo), at the Ohio/Mississippi confluence. Small non-modern campground: 16 sites, 8 with electric, no flush toilets/showers, self-register; camp in the grassy area or on pavement down by the water. FLOOD-PRONE - the road closes during high water; call ahead (managed by Horseshoe Lake SFWA). RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Eagle Creek State Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 39.4991,-88.7088 (Hawk Lane; legible summer imagery, pads/RVs visible): all RV loops (Campfire/Hawk/Hickory/Quail/Squirrel) sit 100-250m from Lake Shelbyville coves behind solid forest; only Eagle Point tent area nears the shore; pin was 500m NW at day-use parking
note: Illinois DNR (Findlay), on a bluff overlooking Lake Shelbyville. 75 Class A reservable electric sites (water hydrants, showers, dump, fire blocks, tables); playground, boat ramp, visitor center. Year-round. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. (State-park campground on the Corps lake.) RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Wolf Creek State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 39.476,-88.687: all Class A loops 100-300m inland behind dense tree band, no shoreline or overlook pads; firsthand reviews put RV loops in woods, lakefront sites are tent/boat-in only; old pin was at entrance junction ~300m N
note: Illinois DNR (Windsor), on Lake Shelbyville. 304 Class A electric sites (30A, gravel pads ~20-60 ft) + 78 Class C + cabin; showers, dump, potable water. Year-round; reserve May-Oct via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 3.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Eldon Hazlet State Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 38.6595,-89.3205 and 38.6489,-89.3315: Illini campground loop pads within one pad-depth of Carlyle Lake shoreline on coves and main shore
note: Illinois DNR (Carlyle), western shore of Carlyle Lake (largest manmade lake in IL). Illini Campground: 328 Class A electric sites (30/50A) + 36 Class C + cabins; ~1.5 mi of shoreline, many loops within reach of the water (several with direct lake views and trails to sandy beach). Showers, dump, store, pool. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Ramsey Lake State Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: ExploreMoreIL facility pin + satellite at 39.1592,-89.1306: White Oak loops in woods 60-200m back from lake's south tip, no shoreline pads; reviewer notes lake views 'kinda limited'; old pin was ~1 km E in farm fields
note: Illinois DNR (Ramsey), alongside Ramsey Lake. ~91 sites, electric (30/50A; some water), pull-throughs; flush toilets, showers, dump. Sites 1-30 FCFS, rest reservable. Year-round. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Stephen A. Forbes State Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: official DNR text credits 'scenic view of the lake' to the 10 walk-in sites only, RV sites reach lake via stairways below; canopy satellite shows Oak Ridge ridge-top loops 100-250m from coves; old pin was at park office ~700m SW
note: Illinois DNR (Kinmundy), on Forbes Lake. 115 electric sites (water + electric), max RV ~40 ft; showers, dump. Stairways + a floating walkway connect the campground to the marina/lake. Only one of three loops reservable, rest FCFS. (Separate equestrian campground too.) Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Hidden Springs State Forest
note: Illinois DNR state forest (Strasburg/Mode). Possum Creek Campground (Class C): no electric, flat drive-in sites (separate tent/RV sections), drinking water, privies, dump, playground, pavilion. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Sam Parr State Fish and Wildlife Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 39.0224,-88.1196: Lakeview CG pads along narrow peninsula spine with lake arms 40-100m on both sides; directory descriptions confirm some sites overlook the lake; old pin was across the west arm at day-use area
note: Illinois DNR (Newton), on 183-acre Sam Parr Lake. Lakeview Campground: paved back-in sites with 50A electric (water spigots, no hookups), RVs to ~45 ft; dump, restrooms; some sites overlook the lake. Year-round. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Red Hills State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 38.7236,-87.8351: RV pads on open ridge ~90-130m above lake arm; 'Lake view loop' 50-amp sites plus dated firsthand review of lake view from site; old pin ~400m SW in woods
note: Illinois DNR (Sumner, between Olney and Lawrenceville). 100+ Class A electric sites (water, dump, showers; some 50A pull-through), RVs to ~45 ft, big-rig friendly; wooded/private. 40-acre lake - some sites have lake views. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Beaver Dam State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Plainview, S of Carlinville). 59 electric-only sites (30/50A) under oak/hickory, 5 pull-throughs; shower building, dump, playground, pavilions; on-site vineyard. 59-acre lake (electric motors only) set below the campground. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Washington County State Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 38.2855,-89.3536: Shady Rest peninsula pads 40-100m from shore with water on three sides and a campground dock below sites; no pads at waterline so not lakefront; old pin ~500m SW off the campground
note: Illinois DNR (Nashville), around a 248-acre lake. Shady Rest: 37 RV sites with 30/50A electric (+ tent areas); dump, water points, shower (Apr-Oct), toilet blocks; lake-loop trail. Cash only. Year-round. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Horseshoe Lake State Park (Madison County)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 38.6831,-90.0807: 26-site loop at Walker's Island south end with pads in woods ~90-200m from shore, no shoreline or overlook pads; old pin was ~2 km NE at the Hwy 111 day-use/office strip
note: Illinois DNR (Granite City), on a 2,400-acre Mississippi oxbow lake (Walker's Island). Semi-primitive: 26 sites, no electric/showers, water hydrants, dump, outhouses; $8. ~May-Sept. Distinct from Horseshoe Lake in Alexander County. RV Life 2.5*/$. --Claude

name: Argyle Lake State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at all three loops (Twisted Oak 40.4542,-90.7985; Big Oak 40.4531,-90.7821; Twin Oaks 40.4621,-90.7883): pads on wooded ridges 50-250m from lake behind dense canopy, no open sightline; pin was on open parkland W of campground
note: Illinois DNR (Colchester). 159 sites: Twisted Oak (86 Class A, showers/electric, some full hookup), Big Oaks (24 Class B/E + 18 Class C), Twin Oaks (31 primitive); max RV ~35 ft, dump. On Argyle Lake (boat launch, dock, rentals). Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Delabar State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 40.9655,-90.9407 (leafless, legible): campsite pads/structures between camp road and Mississippi side-channel bank ~20-30m from waterline
note: Illinois DNR (Oquawka), on the banks of the Mississippi River (89-acre park). 50+ RV sites with 30A electric (40 also tent), dump, water, restrooms, jet-pump fill; nearby marina/boat ramps. Year-round, permit from staff. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Big River State Forest
note: Illinois DNR state forest (Keithsburg, 3,000+ acres along the Mississippi). Shady Pines Campground: drive-in RV/trailer sites, no hookups, max RV ~40 ft, dump, water (Riverview area is tent-only by the river). First-come, first-served (not in ExploreMoreIL). RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Nauvoo State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 40.5439,-91.3802 (ExploreMoreIL facility coord): campground loop ~100m south of Horton Lake behind a wooded band, no open sightline; pin was on picnic drives W of lake
note: Illinois DNR (Nauvoo), near the Mississippi River; campground around 13-acre Lake Horton. 105 sites (35 Class A, 70 Class B), 30A electric (some water/sewer), showers, RVs to ~60 ft. ~Apr-Oct. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 3.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Siloam Springs State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 39.8836,-90.9368 (Oak Ridge loop per ExploreMoreIL facility data): pads on narrow peninsula ridge 40-100m directly above lake arms on both sides; heavy canopy limits per-pad confirmation, other loops inland
note: Illinois DNR (Clayton/Liberty). 182 sites, 98 electric (30/50A, some full hookup), max RV ~45 ft. Pine Grove (49 Class A) + Hickory Hill (Class A + Class B); on a small lake with 6 fishing piers, camp store, boat rentals. Year-round. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Starved Rock State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Oglesby/Utica), famous canyons above the Illinois River. 129 Class A Premium electric sites (30/50A), RVs ~15-45 ft; showers, dump. Campground set inland from the canyons/river. Reservation-only via ExploreMoreIL (fills fast). RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Illini State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 41.3206,-88.7065: herringbone RV pads in open grass 20-60m from Illinois River bank with open sightlines; pin was at bridge ~300m west of campground
note: Illinois DNR (Marseilles), on the Illinois River. Great Falls + Whitetail campgrounds, electric (mostly 30A, shared boxes - bring an extension cord; no water at sites), dump; some sites have river views. Boat ramp. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Moraine View State Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: official DNR moraineviewcampgroundmap.pdf: perimeter trailer sites P18-P23 drawn on Dawson Lake shore; satellite at 40.4159,-88.7210 confirms SW-loop pads ~20-30m from waterline; pin was at maintenance/farm area SW of lake
note: Illinois DNR (Le Roy), on 158-acre Dawson Lake. Gander Bay Campground: 137 Class A electric sites (large flat pads, RVs under ~35 ft, mostly back-in), showers, dump, water; some waterfront sites, boat ramp. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Kickapoo State Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 40.1353,-87.7407 (Erie Loop per ExploreMoreIL facility data): drive-in non-electric sites in open meadow 20-60m from strip-mine pond with open sightline; electric loops wooded inland; pin moved onto main campground loops
note: Illinois DNR (Oakwood), a reclaimed mine with 22 deep-water ponds. 184 sites, ~half electric (sites 1-101, 30/50A; 50A sites on a ridge overlooking a lake); 2 shower buildings, dump, water. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL May-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Weldon Springs State Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 40.1209,-88.9268 (leafless, legible): campground loops ring a peninsula 30-60m above Weldon Springs Lake on east and south sides; no pads at waterline; pin was at road junction ~400m west
note: Illinois DNR (Clinton), on a 29-acre spring-fed lake. 75 Class A electric sites (30/50A, 5 pull-through) + 5 non-powered + 15 primitive; showers, dump, water. Year-round (reserve May-Sept). RV Life 3.5*/$. --Claude

name: Fox Ridge State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Charleston), wooded ravines above the Embarras River. 43 electric sites (20/30/50A, no water/sewer), RVs to ~40 ft, back-in only; dump, showers (closed winter), water. Year-round, $20. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Lincoln Trail State Park
waterfront_evidence: ExploreMoreIL per-unit GPS (facility 277): Lakeside Campground Class A sites 079L-089L on narrow lake peninsula ~25-50m from shore; canopy-limited satellite at 39.3394,-87.7200 shows peninsula site clearings surrounded by water; old pin was park service area 700m NE of lake
note: Illinois DNR (Marshall), on 146-acre Lincoln Trail Lake. Two Class A campgrounds: Plainview (105 electric) + Lakeside (72 electric, many back-ins overlooking the lake), 30/50A; showers, double dump, water. RVs to ~44 ft. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Walnut Point State Park
waterfront_evidence: ExploreMoreIL per-unit GPS (facility 377): Grey Squirrel Class A sites 015-021 line the east rim of a peninsula at the shoreline; leaf-off satellite at 39.7011,-88.0357 shows campers on peninsula flanked by lake arms; old pin was woods/lake arm 500m NE of campground
note: Illinois DNR (Oakland), on 59-acre Walnut Point Lake. 34 electric sites (30/50A) in Grey/Fox Squirrel areas, many with lake views/close to the water; restrooms, showers, dump. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Weinberg-King State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Augusta). 29 Class B/E electric sites for general campers (+ 19 in a separate equestrian area), RVs to ~70 ft; tables, grills, water, toilets, dump, playground. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Jubilee College State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Brimfield, 3,200 acres). Class A electric + Class C sites; Coyote Cove loop has Class B electric open for winter camping. Showers, dump, graveled pads, water. Main season ~Apr 15-Nov 1. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Lincoln's New Salem State Historic Site
note: Illinois DNR historic site (Petersburg), by the reconstructed New Salem village near the Sangamon River. ~100 electric sites (30/50A) + ~100 non-electric, RVs to ~40 ft; hydrants (no water/sewer at sites), dump, two shower buildings. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Deer Run Campground (Sangchris Lake State Park)
note: Illinois DNR (Rochester), north side of Sangchris Lake. Deer Run Campground: 80 Class A electric sites (showers, dump) + 40 primitive + tent + equestrian; set in woods (not directly waterfront - Hickory Point is the lakefront campground, separately in DB). Closed mid-Jan to Mar. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Jim Edgar Panther Creek State Fish and Wildlife Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 39.9910,-90.0652: pads on open grass slope above Prairie Lake with clear sightlines; ExploreMoreIL per-unit GPS puts nearest units (Class A 057-062) ~50m back from waterline, none at shore; old pin was empty fields 700m west
note: Illinois DNR (Chandlerville). Prairie Lake Campground (general): 84 electric sites incl. 19 full-hookup, some 50A; hot showers, restrooms. (Questing Hills is a separate horses-only campground.) Cabins, multiple fishing lakes. Reserve via ExploreMoreIL. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Sand Ridge State Forest
note: Illinois DNR state forest (Forest City). Pine Camp: 24 drive-in sites in a pine plantation, no hookups; fire pads, tables, water spigots, vault toilets, dump across the road. Largest sites ~52-ft pull-through (most 25-45 ft). Reservable. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Woodford State Fish and Wildlife Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 40.8815,-89.4565: campsite clearings in open bottomland 20-50m east of backwater channel near boat ramps/check station (matches DNR 'sites near the boat ramps'); Goose Lake proper screened by ~250m forest; FCFS so no per-site map exists and exact pads unresolvable
note: Illinois DNR (Low Point), on Illinois River backwater lakes (Goose Lake / Upper Peoria Lake). 12 seasonal drive-in sites near the boat ramps, no hookups, max RV ~30 ft; sheltered tables, pit toilets, fire pits. First-come, ~Apr-Sept. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Spring Lake State Fish and Wildlife Area
waterfront_evidence: IL DNR: Pine/Oak campgrounds in north park; allstays pins north campground at 40.4660,-89.8588 in pine woods east of shore road; satellite shows continuous tree band plus private cabins occupying lake frontage and candidate camp loop ~150m inland behind trees - no confirmed water sightline from any RV site; old pin was a residential subdivision
note: Illinois DNR (Manito), on Spring Lake (Illinois River backwater). Pine + Oak campgrounds (Class C, no electric/showers), ~70 tent/trailer sites, trailer dump; + a walk-in tent area. First-come, first-served (not in ExploreMoreIL). RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Anderson Lake State Fish and Wildlife Area
waterfront_evidence: IL DNR camp page: Class C campgrounds 'on the west bank of the lake'; satellite at 40.1950,-90.2030: camp strip between road and open shoreline with loop spurs in scattered-tree grass reaching within ~25m of waterline, no tree wall
note: Illinois DNR (Astoria), on Anderson Lake (Illinois River backwater). North + South campgrounds, ~100 sites with waterfront access, tables/grills/water/toilets, dump; mostly no electric hookups, RVs to ~64 ft. First-come, first-served (not in ExploreMoreIL). RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Forrest W. "Bo" Wood Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site data: 39 of 135 sites flagged Proximity to Water=Lakefront (005-008, 016-019, 025-033, 043-047, 060-067...); satellite at 39.5495,-88.6215 shows loop pads at shoreline point on Lake Shelbyville
note: USACE St. Louis District, Lake Shelbyville (Sullivan, northern shore). 141 sites, all electric (pads to ~150 ft), + tent-only sites; flush toilets, showers, dump, playground, dog park, fish-cleaning station, high-water boat ramp. Reserve online. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Opossum Creek Campground (Lake Shelbyville)
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site data: 16 of 64 sites flagged Proximity to Water=Lakefront (11-17, 25-29, 40-43); satellite canopy-blind over loops, defers to per-site reservation flags
note: USACE St. Louis District, Lake Shelbyville (western shore). 70 sites, most electric (RVs + tents, some tent-only non-electric); flush toilets, showers, playground, dump. Quiet. Reserve online. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Lone Point Campground (Lake Shelbyville)
waterfront_evidence: Firsthand manual audit by owner (--AWH note); waterfront 'lakefront' per owner: USACE campground on Lake Shelbyville. ~80 electric sites, some waterfont. Reserve online. RV Life 5*/$.
note: USACE campground on Lake Shelbyville. ~80 electric sites, some waterfont. Reserve online. RV Life 5*/$. --AWH

name: Coon Creek Campground (Lake Shelbyville)
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site coords overlaid on satellite at 39.4516,-88.7615: many sites on cove shorelines; zoom at 39.448,-88.765 shows loop pads at waterline
note: USACE St. Louis District, Lake Shelbyville (western shore). Large: 181+ sites, all electric (pads to ~90 ft), ~half reservable; flush toilets, showers, swim beach, playground, bike pump track, basketball/volleyball courts, nature trail, dump, boat ramp. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Dam West Campground (Carlyle Lake)
waterfront_evidence: pin was on marina parking lot; rec.gov 108-site coords overlaid on satellite: arc of sites along east shoreline at waterline, campground loop centroid 38.6313,-89.3581
note: USACE St. Louis District, Carlyle Lake (southern shore, by the dam; Illinois' largest man-made lake). 109 reservable electric sites (pads to ~138 ft); marina, boat storage; walk/bike across the dam to Dam East. Reserve online. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Dam East - McNair Campground (Carlyle Lake)
waterfront_evidence: pin was ~1 km east of campground; rec.gov North/South loop site coords on satellite show sites along Carlyle Lake shore at 38.613,-89.336
note: USACE St. Louis District, Carlyle Lake (east side of the dam). 47 individual sites (North/South loops 1-32 by the lake; East Spillway loop 33-47 below the dam) + a group shelter; swim beach, bathhouses, laundry. Reserve online. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Gun Creek Campground (Rend Lake)
waterfront_evidence: satellite zoom at 38.0842,-88.9322: north loop pads with RV on riprap shoreline point, ~15-25 m from waterline; rec.gov site dots adjacent
note: USACE St. Louis District, Rend Lake (Whittington, near Wayne Fitzgerrell SRA). 98+ reservable electric sites (pads to ~85 ft); boating, fishing, swimming. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: South Marcum Campground (Rend Lake)
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov 160-site coords overlaid on satellite at 38.0375,-88.9378: peninsula loops with sites at cove shorelines on west and north edges
note: USACE St. Louis District, Rend Lake (SE shore). 158 reservable sites, electric (pads to ~94 ft) + tent sites; dump, hot showers, flush toilets, drinking water. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: South Sandusky Campground (Rend Lake)
waterfront_evidence: pin was ~2.8 km south of campground (sites span 38.055-38.063); rec.gov site coords on satellite: PTRE/POAK loop sites at eastern point shoreline, pads ~20-40 m from water
note: USACE St. Louis District, Rend Lake (western shore, Sandusky cove). 124 sites: 119 electric (some full-hookup, pads to ~100 ft) + 8 tent; dump, hot showers, flush toilets; on the 20+ mi Rend Lake Bike Trail. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: North Sandusky Campground (Rend Lake)
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov site coords on satellite zoom at 38.0665,-89.0010: east rows end at cove shore with RV pads ~20-30 m from waterline
note: USACE St. Louis District, Rend Lake (western shore). 95 reservable electric sites (pads to ~66 ft); dump, hot showers, flush toilets, drinking water. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Fishermans Corner Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite zoom at 41.5694,-90.3917: pads with RVs within ~20 m of Mississippi River backwater shoreline along west row; rec.gov site dots at shore
note: USACE Rock Island District, Mississippi River (Hampton, Pool 14). 51 reservable sites: 46 electric (pads to ~80 ft) + 5 tent; amphitheater, canoe ramp. The Mississippi River Trail runs right past the campground. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Blanding Landing Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 42.2845,-90.4030: pads ~55 m from Mississippi backwater across open grass/marsh strip; rec.gov per-site data flags only 1 of 38 sites 'Riverfront'; open sightline supports view
note: USACE Rock Island District, Mississippi River backwaters (Hanover, NW IL). 38 sites, most electric (pads to ~50 ft); boat ramp, great fishing/birding. Reserve online. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Blanchard Island Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 41.3478,-91.0563: linear camp road hugging Blanchard Chute bank, pads/camper ~15-30 m from waterline; old pin sat ~250 m east at a cropland crossroads
note: USACE Rock Island District, Mississippi River (New Boston). Small/primitive: 15 sites (table/fire ring, vault toilets; a couple of host sites have water/electric), boat ramp, view of Blanchard Chute. First-come 'Scan & Pay', ~May 15-Oct 15. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Andalusia Slough Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite (leafless) at 41.4384,-90.7688: angled pad row along Hwy 92 set ~100 m back from Mississippi shore behind a continuous floodplain-forest strip; no shoreline pads
note: USACE Rock Island District, Mississippi River (Andalusia). Roadside park: campground + boat ramp + day use; vault toilets, water hydrants, group shelter, no electric. First-come, self-register $10, ~May-Oct. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Bear Creek Recreation Area (Mississippi River)
waterfront_evidence: Firsthand manual audit by owner (--AWH note); waterfront 'riverfront' per owner: USACE campground on offshoot of Mississippi River. FREE, first-come-first-served, no reservation system. Primitive riverside sites: vault toilets, NO showers, NO fresh water (bring your own), fire rings, trash service, cement boat ramp; no hookups. Level, shaded gravel sites usable by RVs. On the riverside of the levee and flood-prone -- in spring call to confirm it isn't underwater.  Warning: Google Maps misroutes ~4 mi south). RV Life unrated, $.
note: USACE campground on offshoot of Mississippi River. FREE, first-come-first-served, no reservation system.
