Primitive riverside sites: vault toilets, NO showers, NO fresh water (bring your own), fire rings, trash service, cement boat ramp; no hookups. Level, shaded gravel sites usable by RVs. On the riverside of the levee and flood-prone -- in spring call to confirm it isn't underwater.  Warning: Google Maps misroutes ~4 mi south). RV Life unrated, $. --AWH

name: Oak Point Campground (Lake Glendale)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.4100,-88.6610: loops on wooded point with pads ~30-100 m above shore, none at waterline; firsthand reviews name sites 20-24 for lake views; USFS 'along the lakeshore' is prose only
note: USFS Shawnee NF, Lake Glendale Recreation Area (Glendale). 35 electric/water sites + 24 non-electric, RVs to ~40 ft; showers, dump station; swim beach on Lake Glendale. First-come, first-served, 14-day max. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Pine Ridge Campground (Pounds Hollow)
waterfront_evidence: USFS page: campground 'located on the ridgetop above Pounds Hollow Lake'; satellite: ~350 m of unbroken forest canopy between camp loops at 37.6146,-88.2682 and the lake — no line of sight
note: USFS Shawnee NF, on the ridgetop above Pounds Hollow Lake (Karbers Ridge). 13 electric (20/30/50A) + 22 non-electric sites, water spigot, $10 cash/check. Narrow undulating roads limit rigs to UNDER ~25 ft - a 23-ft fits but big rigs don't. FCFS, ~Mar 15-Dec 15. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Camp Cadiz Campground
note: USFS Shawnee NF (Elizabethtown), a former CCC work camp. Drive-in sites accommodating RVs to ~40 ft, no hookups; tables, fire grills, toilets, drinking water. Allows equestrians (general campground, not horse-only). FCFS, $10. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Redbud Campground (Bell Smith Springs)
note: USFS Shawnee NF, Bell Smith Springs Recreation Area (Ozark/Eddyville). 21 sites, no hookups, vault toilets, one potable-water source; accommodates pull-behinds/motorhomes to ~24 ft (a 23-ft fits; best for compact rigs). FCFS, $10, ~Mar 15-Dec 15. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Crab Orchard Campground (Crab Orchard NWR)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.7400,-89.1160: RV pads and parked rigs along shoreline road within ~20 m of Crab Orchard Lake waterline on east loop and south peninsula
note: USFWS Crab Orchard National Wildlife Refuge (Marion), on Crab Orchard Lake; concessionaire-run (Bluegill Bay). 62 RV + 7 tent sites, full-hookup / electric-water / non-electric, showers, dump, flush toilets, small camper beach; many waterfront sites, RVs to ~38 ft. First-come, ~Apr 1-mid Nov. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Johnson Creek Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.8335,-89.5190 (matches USFS-listed coords): camp loops on forested ridge ~1 km from nearest Kinkaid Lake arm; no water adjacent to any pad
note: USFS Shawnee NF, on a NW finger of Kinkaid Lake (Ava). 20 large drive-in sites, no hookups; boat ramp, toilets, drinking water. Allows equestrians (hitching posts/highline poles) but open to all; group sites take RVs to ~40 ft. FCFS, $10. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Pine Hills Campground (Shawnee NF)
note: USFS Shawnee NF (Wolf Lake), by the LaRue-Pine Hills bottoms. 13 level primitive sites, no hookups, vault toilets, no potable water (bring your own); max RV length ~80 ft but no pull-throughs. FCFS, $10, ~Mar 15-Dec 15. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Tower Rock Campground (Shawnee NF)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.4583,-88.2335: camp loop on open grassy bench with nearest sites ~40-50 m from Ohio River waterline (high water) behind thin bank tree line; USFS 'along the banks' prose only
note: USFS Shawnee NF (Elizabethtown), on the bank of the Ohio River. Free primitive campground, pit toilet, no water/electric; improved sites take small trailers/RVs (~25 ft; a 23-ft fits). Flood-prone with river levels. ~May 1-Dec 15, 14-day max. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: World Shooting & Recreational Complex
note: Illinois DNR (Sparta), a 1,600-acre reclaimed-strip-mine shooting/event complex. ~1,001 campsites, all with 50A electric (premium + standard), showers, restrooms; open to general camping (busy during major shoots). Reserve ~Apr-Oct via ExploreMoreIL. (RV Life mislabels it commercial; it is IDNR.) RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Illinois State Fairgrounds Campground
note: Illinois Dept. of Agriculture (Springfield). ~300 sites with electric/water and limited sewer, showers, permanent restrooms; accommodates motorhomes/RVs/fifth-wheels/tents. Year-round (very busy during the State Fair and horse shows). (RV Life mislabels it commercial; it is state-owned fairgrounds.) RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Marion Campground & RV Park
note: Private (Marion), one block E of I-57 exit 53. 58 full-hookup sites, swimming pool, playground, game room, camp store, laundry; year-round. Easy on/off the interstate (some sites close together; mix of short-term and long-term). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Whittington Woods Campground
note: Private (Whittington), off I-57 exit 77, ~6 mi N of Benton, near Rend Lake. Pull-through full-hookup sites (50A, water/sewer), RVs to ~70 ft; wooded, quiet, level. Year-round. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: EZ Camp Now
note: Private (Benton), off I-57 near Rend Lake. 10 full-hookup sites (50A), WiFi, cable, well-lit/cameras, self-registration; geared to quick overnight stops (~$25). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Circle O Campground
note: Private (Fairfield). Large sites (power hookups + primitive), WiFi, fishing pond, zipline, event/group area, OHV trails; owners help park larger rigs. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Taylor Lake Campground
note: Private (Olney), just off IL-130. 10 full-hookup sites (15/20/30A, water/sewer), clean and well-kept; close to town amenities. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Arrowhead Campground (Robinson)
note: Private (Robinson), small park. 30/50A sites with water + septic hookups (no central toilets - self-contained rigs); clean, quiet, affordable overnight stop. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Hickory Holler Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 39.1492,-87.7641: RV pads/campers at the west edge of the fishing pond (within ~1 pad-depth of water) and a rig at the main lake's west shore
note: Private (West York), family RV park with two lakes/ponds. 80 sites (30/50A) + tent + cabins; swimming pool, playground, mini golf, fishing, camp store, trails. Year-round. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Pine View Campground
note: Private (Amboy), family-owned 36-acre wooded park. Full/partial-hookup + primitive sites (RVs to 45 ft), dump station, laundry, fishing/swimming ponds; reservations via RoverPass. Open ~Apr 15-Oct 15. Skews seasonal but keeps a dedicated nightly/transient section. Ponds isolated from RV loops (waterfront none). RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Geneseo Campground
note: Private (Geneseo), 59 pull-thru + 12 back-in sites (RVs to 45 ft) plus cabins/yurt/store, 30/50A full & W/E hookups, 3 mi off I-80. On the Hennepin Canal trail (biking/fishing/paddling) but RV sites set back from the canal (waterfront none). Online reservations. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Camp Hauberg
note: Private (Port Byron), former-YMCA camp on a 40-acre wooded parcel along the Mississippi 2 mi N of town. 30A electric+water sites, pull-throughs, shower house, river fishing/boating; also rents lodges. River is an amenity, RV pads are wooded interior (waterfront none). Reservations by phone. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Leisure Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 41.7535,-89.6535: RVs parked on the lake-edge perimeter road at the waterline on north and east shores
note: Private (Rock Falls), small RV-only lakeside campground (not the membership Leisure Lake Resort in Joliet). Full hookups (sewer at every site), controlled access, fishing/kayak/canoe on Leisure Lake; explicit lakeside RV sites front the water. Daily ($30)/weekly stays. Open Apr 1-Oct 15. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Ruffit Park
note: Private (Sterling, est. 1965), 120-site family campground: 95 electric/water 30/50A sites, 50 pull-throughs (RVs to 65 ft), 5 tent-only sites, swimming pool, laundry, dump station, store. Markets creek fishing but no confirmed creekside RV sites (waterfront none). Reservations via RoverPass. Open May 1-Oct 1. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Kentuckiana Kampground
note: Private (Mackinaw), family campground with full-hookup sites (30/50A, sewer, concrete patio; rigs to ~50 ft), two stocked fishing ponds, laundry, live country-music concerts. Lakeside sites named are tent sites, RV sites not confirmed on the ponds (waterfront none). Reservations. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Princess Wenona RV Oasis
note: Private (Wenona), 6 full-hookup (50A, water, sewer, WiFi) concrete pull-through pads at a truck-stop/mini-mart 1/4 mi off I-39 Exit 41; Burger King & Subway on site. Convenient overnight stop. First-come-first-served; no dedicated website (operating per consistent recent reviews). RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: D & W Lake Camping and RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 40.1832,-88.2530: southernmost RV row sits across an internal perimeter drive from the lake's north shore (~15-20 m, open line of sight); no pads at the waterline
note: Private (Champaign), year-round 45-site full-hookup park (20/30/50A, pull-thru, big-rig to 70 ft) 5 mi from U of Illinois, with fishing lake, dog park, WiFi. Reviews confirm RV sites bordering/viewing the lake (waterfront lake). Water shut off at sites in early Nov. Online + phone reservations. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Evening Star Camping Resort
note: Private (Topeka), ~300 sites on 35 acres (20/30/50A, pull-thru/back-in) with pool, mini-golf, fishing ponds, planned activities. Has a seasonal contingent but books public nightly via Campspot with no membership requirement. Ponds not confirmed RV-site-adjacent (waterfront none). Open Apr 1-Oct 31. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Camp A While
note: Private (Lincoln), small ~11-site Route 66 transient park, open year-round, full + partial hookups (30/50A) $30-40/night, big-rig friendly (no on-site bathrooms). Reservations by call/email. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Prairie Pines Campground
note: Private (Rantoul), ~95-site 21-acre full-hookup park (water/sewer/electric, pull-thru, big-rig, laundry, clubhouse) on former Chanute AFB land, rates from $27. Mixed daily/weekly/monthly/seasonal use; takes daily transients. Reservations via RoverPass + phone. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Kamp Komfort RV Park & Campground
note: Private (Carlock), all full-hookup pull-thru sites (30/50A, up to 90 ft) near I-74 Exit 120, with pool, playground, covered group picnic area, 8 mi from Normal. Some highway noise. Reservations (call/RoverPass). Open Apr 1-Oct 31. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Countryside Campground
note: Private (Gibson City), year-round 52 full-hookup gravel sites (30/50A, leveled, big-rig to 60 ft) plus tent sites, with swimming pond/beach; $40/night. Pond is a rec amenity, no confirmed pond-side RV sites (waterfront none). Reservations by phone. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Deer Run Campground
note: Private (Golconda), small country campground in Shawnee NF; 30/50A full-hookup sites, tent sites, 4 primitive electric cabins, 3 bathhouses. Ohio River/swimming holes are nearby off-site attractions (waterfront none). Bookable (Hipcamp/phone). No standalone website (Facebook/Hipcamp), offset by strong reviews (Google 4.8). (Distinct from Deer Run at Sangchris Lake SP.) RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Bay Creek Ranch Campground
note: Private (Simpson), family-owned year-round Shawnee NF trail-riding ranch that also welcomes RVers/hikers (not equestrian-only); ~45 gravel sites with electric+water, dump station, bath house, horse stalls. No confirmed creekside RV sites (waterfront none). Online reservations. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Cedar Lake Campground
note: Private (Vienna), large ~148-acre campground (200+ sites) on a 25-acre private lake bordering Shawnee NF; RV electric/water hookups, big-rig friendly, cabins, clubhouse, fishing; popular with horse riders (not equestrian-only). RV-site-to-lake relationship unconfirmed (waterfront none). Reservations via Campspot. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Four Seasons Campground
note: Private (Herrin), 38-site full-hookup town campground behind the city park; 19 pull-throughs handle any size RV, 20/30/50A, dump station, LP, laundry, storm shelter, 7 cabins. Small stocked pond is an amenity, not RV-site-adjacent (waterfront none). Reservations (RoverPass). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Hilltop Campground
note: Private (Goreville), year-round interstate-convenient campground at I-57 exit 40 (8 mi S of I-24); 35 sites (15 pull-thru/20 back-in) with W/E/sewer, big-rig friendly (~75 ft), WiFi, laundry. No water on site (waterfront none). Reservations required via ResNexus. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Robin Hood Woods
note: Private (Shelbyville), ~200 wooded full-hookup sites (W/S/E, 20/30/50A), big-rig pull-throughs, pool, store, laundry, cabins; accepts nightly + seasonal. Set back from Lake Shelbyville; on-site fishing pond is an amenity (waterfront none). Open Apr 1-Oct 31. Reservations. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Spring Creek Campground
note: Private (Montrose), small family-owned gravel park ~1 mi N of Montrose just off I-70; 25 sites (18 electric-only + 6 elec/water, 30/50A, big-rig to 40 ft), dump station, showers, playground, fishing pond. Convenient I-70 overnight stop; pond is an amenity (waterfront none). No standalone website (Facebook); reservations via RoverPass. Open Apr-Nov. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Tomahawk Campground
note: Private (Pocahontas), ~140 partial/full-hookup sites (15 pull-thru, 50A, up to 50 ft), showers, laundry, dump, 7-acre fishing lake; mix of seasonal + transient (confirmed overnight $25-30). No site-level RV-on-water confirmation (waterfront none). No standalone website (Facebook); reservations + walk-up. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Kaskaskia Dragon RV Park
note: Private (Vandalia), small 12-site gravel park just off I-70; 30/50A electric + water, laundry, fits up to ~60 ft, novelty fire-breathing dragon attraction. In-town lot, no water frontage (waterfront none). Transient overnight stop; reservations by call. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Timber Trails Campground
note: Private (Mulberry Grove), family park 2 mi N of I-70 Exit 52; full hookups + 100-ft pull-thrus (up to 50 ft), pool, bathhouse, 3 fishing ponds, playgrounds; mix of seasonal + nightly (cash). Ponds dotted through park but no confirmed RV-on-water sites (waterfront none). Reservations. Open Apr 1-Nov 1. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Lazy Days RV Park & Campground
waterfront_evidence: official park map (lazydaysrvparkil.com/map, 2025): elite sites 24-25/56-57/94/99-100 adjacent to swimming lake; Esri satellite tiles predate the park (pin matches operator's own coordinate)
note: Private (Litchfield, off old Route 66), 139 sites (standard 30A + premium/elite full-hookup), clubhouse, bathhouse, store, swimming lake + fishing ponds, kayaking. Official site markets 'elite' RV sites with waterfront views (waterfront lakeview). ~Half permanent residents but genuinely sells nightly stays. Reservations. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Mt. CarMerriel RV Park
note: Private (Mount Carmel), small full-hookup park (30/50A), laundry/showers, near the local airport/golf; nightly/weekly/monthly (confirmed transient), open 24/7. Inland lot, no water frontage (waterfront none). Near Beall Woods SP. Reservations. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Percival Springs Airport & RV Park
note: Private (Watson), 24 full-hookup (30/50A) pull-thru grassy sites adjoining a private airstrip off US-45 (~8 min from I-57), laundry/showers, small on-site restaurant; fits long rigs. General drive-in RV park (not aviation-only); no water on site (waterfront none). Reservations. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Country Bend Campground
note: Private (Litchfield, formerly Honey Bend Resort), 150+ sites + 13 cabins; pool, jumping pillow, putt-putt, four fishing ponds, pedal boats, game room. Family park near Route 66 / I-55. Ponds not confirmed RV-site-adjacent (waterfront none). Online booking via CampLife. Open Apr-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Driftwood Campground and RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 39.9598,-91.4150: RV pads east of road, across it from a Mississippi backwater pond with open sightline; no pads at waterline
note: Private (Quincy, former KOA), ~51 sites, 50+ full-hookup (30/50A) mostly concrete pads, max ~50 ft, pool, laundry, showers, playground; on the Great River Road. Overlooks the Mississippi (river access across Bonansinga Dr; some sites have a distant river view, not on-water; waterfront riverview). Year-round; reservations via Campspot, 3-night min. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Timberview Lakes Campground
note: Private (Bushnell), ~60 full-hookup RV sites (30/50A), 11 big-rig pull-throughs (~60 ft), restrooms/showers/laundry, swimming lake + fishing ponds; public ('open to all', accepts Passport America). Water set back from RV sites (waterfront none). No clear official website (Good Sam/RoverPass); reservations by phone. Open Apr 15-Oct 15. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Timbered Meadows Campground
note: Private (Milford), ~95 W/E sites (no full hookups; dump station), bathhouses, fire pits, swimming, 3 fishing lakes, playground/courts; nightly overnight ($30-31) open to public plus weekly/monthly/seasonal. Lakes offer views but no confirmed RV-on-water sites (waterfront none). Open Apr 1-Oct 31. Reservations by call. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Sandy Oaks Campground
note: Private (Keithsburg), ~100 sites, E/W 20/30A, dump, laundry, cable, pool, playground, store, max RV ~50 ft; general campground that also offers equestrian sites (not horse-only) with a separate members-only ATV/motocross park. Direct access to Big River State Forest trails; no on-property water for RV sites (waterfront none). Reservations via Campspot. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Camp Reinberg
note: Local (Forest Preserves of Cook County, in Deer Grove FP near Palatine). 5 individual drive-in RV/tent sites with 20/30/50A electric + water, gravel pads, fire rings; also cabins/tent/group sites. Year-round; reservations required. RV Life listed. --Claude

name: Camp Sullivan
note: Local (Forest Preserves of Cook County, Oak Forest — closest campground to downtown Chicago). 10 individual drive-in RV/tent sites, 30/50A electric, gravel pads ~32-38 ft (no water/dump). Year-round; reservations required. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Big Rock Campground
note: Local (Forest Preserve District of Kane County). 96 improved drive-in electric/water sites (handles 50-ft rigs), 2 dump stations, no showers; also tent + a few equestrian sites (general campground). Ponds/creek in preserve but not RV-site-adjacent (waterfront none). Seasonal May 1-mid Oct; reservations. --Claude  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Paul Wolff Campground
note: Local (Forest Preserve District of Kane County, in Burnidge FP near Elgin). 89 improved drive-in electric/water sites (handles 50-ft rigs), plus 5 equestrian + 10 primitive tent sites. Seasonal May 1-mid Oct; reservations required. RV Life mislabels commercial; it is county FP. --Claude

name: Blackwell Family Campground
note: Local (Forest Preserve District of DuPage County, in Blackwell FP, Warrenville). 60+ drive-in electric sites (tents/trailers/motorhomes), crushed-limestone pads, showers, dump + water-fill (no hookups). Silver Lake in preserve but not RV-site-adjacent (waterfront none). Weekends-only May-Columbus Day; reservations. --Claude  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Illiniwek Forest Preserve
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 41.5642,-90.4030: camp loops ~40-60 m from the Mississippi across open mowed lawn with clear sightline; shoreline strip at 41.5663,-90.4008 is boat ramp/parking, not sites
note: Local (Rock Island County Forest Preserve District, Hampton) on the Mississippi. 60 water/electric RV pads + dump station + 2 shower houses; 25 primitive sites near the shore are tent-only. RV loops set back with river views (waterfront riverview). Seasonal Apr-Oct; first-come-first-served (permit at camp office). --Claude  RV Life 4.5*/$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Hononegah Forest Preserve
note: Local (Winnebago County Forest Preserve District, Rockton) on the north bank of the Rock River. ~57 electric sites (20/30/50A), dump station, restrooms (no showers), playground; camp host registers on arrival. RV loop set back from the river (waterfront none). Year-round (water off in freezing temps); first-come-first-served. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Seward Bluffs Forest Preserve
note: Local (Winnebago County Forest Preserve District, Seward). 47 electric sites, dump station, ADA pit toilets (no showers), creek + limestone bluffs; separate equestrian loop (general campground). 30-ft max RV length (fits a 23-ft rig). Year-round; reservations required 7+ days out. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Sugar River Forest Preserve
note: Local (Winnebago County Forest Preserve District, Durand). ~70 electric sites in pine-shaded main loop + primitive river-side tent loop, showerhouse, 5.9-mi trails, fishing/paddling on the Sugar River. RV loop set back; on-river sites are tent-only (waterfront none). Year-round; main loop reservable, primitive FCFS. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Loud Thunder Forest Preserve
note: Local (Rock Island County Forest Preserve District, Illinois City). RV camping in White Oak (26 water/electric sites) + Deer Haven (35 full-hookup 50A pull-throughs); showers, marina/boat rental on 167-acre Lake George, 12+ mi trails; separate equestrian loop. RV loops not on the water (waterfront none). Reservable, 2-night min, Apr-Oct. RV Life ~9/10. RV Life mislabels state; it is county FP. --Claude

name: Forest Glen Preserve
note: Local (Vermilion County Conservation District, Georgetown). Family campground: ~34 electric sites (20/30/50A, no water/sewer at site) + 8 non-electric, water + 2 dump stations. Separate Vermilion River canoe/backpack camp is not RV. First-come-first-served. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Middle Fork Forest Preserve - Harry L. Swartz Campground
note: Local (Champaign County Forest Preserve District, Penfield). 65 sites (53 electric, 48 electric+water), ~$16/night, on-site host, flush toilets/showers, summer swim beach, 11 dark-sky sites; small ponds not RV-site-adjacent (waterfront none). Reservations required. --Claude  RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Friends Creek Conservation Area
note: Local (Macon County Conservation District, near Decatur). ~35-40 sites (mostly 30/50A electric, 3 non-electric), showers/flush toilets/dump station; some back-in sites are short but 50A service handles larger rigs. Season ~May-Nov; reservations. --Claude  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Comlara Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 40.6449,-89.0323: peninsula loop with west-side spurs ~10-25 m from Evergreen Lake waterline (within ~1 pad-depth); original pin was in woods ~200 m SE of the loop
note: Local (McLean County Parks & Recreation, Hudson) on 925-acre Evergreen Lake. ~94 electric (mostly 30A, some 50A) + 23 non-electric + primitive walk-in; showers, dump station. Several RV sites sit directly on the lake with kayak-launch access (waterfront lake). Sites 1-33 year-round, rest Apr-Nov; reservations. RV Life mislabels commercial; it is county. --Claude

name: Sherwood Forest Campground
note: Local (City of Hillsboro), between Old Lake Hillsboro and Glenn Shoals Lake. Mostly seasonal (~113 permanent sites) but with 16 dedicated short-term/transient electric+water sites, dump station, online booking; $25-45/night. Transient sites not confirmed on the water (waterfront none). Apr 1-Oct 31; reservations via Firefly. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: South Park Municipal Campground
note: Local (City of Gibson City, in South Park at IL-9/Lott Blvd; some directories list this as 'Arrowsmith Park & Campground' — same place). 9 asphalt sites incl. 1 ADA pull-through, 30A electric + water, fire rings, restroom/showers, dump station. Apr-Oct, 7-night limit, ~$10-20; first-come-first-served, self-pay (shower keys at Police Dept). --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Twin Lakes Park
note: Local (City of Paris) on the Twin Lakes reservoirs (directory listings call it 'Paris City Park'). ~85 stoned sites, asphalt roads, 20/30/50A electric, dump station, showers/flush toilets, shelter. RV sites not confirmed on the water (waterfront none). Open all year (limited winter services); reservations incl. online via CampLife. --Claude

name: Fairview Park
note: Local (City of Casey). 40-acre municipal park, ~30 sites most with water + electric, dump station, fire pits, restroom/showers; $10-25/night. Twin fishing ponds not RV-site-adjacent (waterfront none). First-come-first-served, self-pay. --Claude  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Jurgens Park
note: Local (Village of Arthur), south edge of town. 7 electric (30A) + water sites, bath house within walking distance, accommodates rigs to ~75 ft; $20/night. First-come-first-served (call Park Administrator). --Claude  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Robinson City Park
note: Local (City of Robinson Parks & Rec). 8 sites (first 3-4 with concrete pads), 20/50A electric + water spigot + dump station, pool/showers in park; $25/night. Open May-Sept; first-come-first-served, 3-night limit, pay at the pool. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Burrell Park and Campgrounds
note: Local (City of Carmi). 25 shaded concrete-pad sites, full hookups (30/50A water/sewer), modern bathhouse, WiFi; full amenities Apr 15-Oct 15 (winter camping allowed, showerhouse closed). Little Wabash River nearby but sites are park-interior (waterfront none). First-come-first-served; ~$16-25. RV Life ~9.9/10. --Claude

name: Charley Brown Memorial Park
note: Local (City of Flora), large 100-acre park ~3 mi W of Flora. ~85 grassy water/electric sites, modern restrooms/showers, pool, 9-hole golf, disc golf, small fishing lake (not RV-site-adjacent; waterfront none). First-come-first-served. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Main Brothers Campground
note: Local (Village of Karnak), near the Tunnel Hill State Trail / Cache River. 19 gravel sites all water/electric (50A on most, else 30A), 7 sewer sites + dump station, tent sites; ~$25/night RV. Reservations via Village Hall ($20 deposit). --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Arrowhead Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 37.8293,-88.9113: all RV spurs sit west of the shore road behind a ~20-40 m tree strip; open lake views near the swim/ramp area but no pads at waterline; original pin was ~350 m south at the day-use/ballfield area
note: Local (City of Johnston City), Arrowhead Recreation & Wildlife Area on a 30-acre lake off I-57 Exit 59. ~55-65 sites (some lakefront) all 30/50A electric + water, shower house, laundry, 2 cabins, nature trail; 40-ft max. A dozen RV sites sit directly on the lake (waterfront lake). Reservations recommended. RV Life mislabels commercial; it is municipal. --Claude

name: Bicentennial Campground - Lake Lou Yaeger
note: Local (City of Litchfield) at Lake Lou Yaeger. 18 wooded sites (1 ADA) with 30/50A + water spigot, dump station, playground, pavilion; nestled in woods ~1/2 mi from the shore (waterfront none). Open Apr 1-Oct 31; reservations. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Lake Jacksonville Campground
note: Local (City of Jacksonville Parks Dept) on ~500-acre Lake Jacksonville (directories misname it 'Lake Jackson'). Electric (20/50A) + water sites, dump station, fire rings, boat launch, marina, camp store. Seasonal-heavy mix but runs a real transient overnight program. RV sites not confirmed on the water (waterfront none). Apr 1-mid Oct; reservations by phone. --Claude  RV Life 3.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Pittsfield City Lake
note: Local (City of Pittsfield) on a ~200-acre city lake. ~33 sites, 30/50A electric + water, gravel pads, fire pits, shower house with flush toilets; fits up to 40-ft RVs. RV sites not confirmed on the water (waterfront none). ~$15 full-service. First-come-first-served, cash drop box. RV Life 4.0*. --Claude

name: Carthage Park District Campground
note: Local (Carthage Park District), next to the golf course / Carthage Lake. 15 sites all with water + electric, shelter house, near boat launch; lake is a short walk away, sites not on the water (waterfront none). ~$15 hookup. First-come-first-served. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Francis Park
note: Local (City of Kewanee). 60 RV sites (30x 30A, 30x 20A), central water fill + dump station, restrooms (no showers); $13/night. No water body at the park (waterfront none). First-come-first-served for electric sites; season closes ~end of August. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Allison Campground
note: Local (City of Galesburg) at Lake Storey Recreation Area. 142 sites (21 full-hookup, 101 electric/some water, 41 primitive/tent), concrete pads, dump station, adjacent water park/beach/golf. Official site map shows RV sites behind a wooded buffer, none on the shoreline (waterfront none). Season ~Apr-mid Oct; reservations by phone/in-person. RV Life mislabels commercial; it is municipal. --Claude

name: Keithsburg Riverside Campground
waterfront_evidence: operator page prices front-row 'riverfront' sites separately from rows 2-4 ('fish from your campsite'); satellite at 41.0966,-90.9437: front-row pads directly atop the Mississippi bank at the beach edge, open sand to waterline
note: Local (City of Keithsburg) on the Mississippi. ~70 water/electric RV sites + 15 full-hookup, restrooms/showers, two dump stations, boat ramps; front-row sites sit on the river bank (waterfront river; riverfront row priced higher). Open May 1-Sep 30 (flood-dependent); first-come-first-served. --Claude  RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Sturgeon Bay Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 41.1697,-91.0020: westernmost RV row ~50m from Sturgeon Bay across an open sand/shore strip with clear sightline; eastern rows set back into town grid — view yes, not on-water
note: Local (City of New Boston) on a Mississippi backwater (Sturgeon Bay). ~70 water/electric/sewer sites (30/50A), shower house, splash park, boat ramps; limited waterfront sites mostly held by seasonals so transient RV sites front-of-water not assured (waterfront riverview). Open Apr 1-Nov 1; reservations via City Hall. --Claude  RV Life 1*/$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Warsaw Riverfront Park
waterfront_evidence: operator page (warsawillinois.org): 10 water/electric camping pads 'located on either side of the Warsaw ramp' at the riverfront; satellite at 40.3546,-91.4458: ramp flanked by open bank-top pad areas directly on the Mississippi
note: Local (City of Warsaw), Riverfront Park (a.k.a. Goose Landing / D&R Camp) on the Mississippi (Pool 20). 10 camping pads directly on the river bank, each with power + water, picnic table, fire ring; two boat ramps; dump station offsite. RV sites on the water (waterfront river). First-come-first-served, self-pay. --Claude  RV Life 3*/$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Village of Kampsville Campground
waterfront_evidence: pin matches directory coords for 210 W Oak St (enjoyillinois 39.2979,-90.6110); satellite: campground parcel sits ~250m inland of the Illinois River behind IL-100 and village blocks/trees — no pads at or overlooking water
note: Local (Village of Kampsville) on the Illinois River. Full-hookup (E/W/S) and pull-through sites, shower house; ~$25/night RV. Riverside park but front-row RV placement not site-confirmed (waterfront riverview). Pay at village hall. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Great River Road Campground
note: Local (Pleasant Hill area, Pike County; adjoins the Pike County Fairgrounds but operates as a standalone public campground, not event-only). 59 full-hookup sites (E/W/S 30/50A), 40 pull-throughs, 60-ft max, dump station; inland by the fairgrounds (waterfront none). Open Apr-Oct; reservations by phone. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Roodhouse Community Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 39.4648,-90.3637: west-row pads 20-40m above the reservoir across open mown grass with clear view; not consistently within one pad-depth of waterline, so view not on-water
note: Local (City of Roodhouse). ~20 full-hookup gravel sites (W/E/50A/sewer) + tent, shower house, laundry, dump station, boat ramp, stocked fishing lake, shelters/courts. Some sites near the lake but on-water RV placement unconfirmed (waterfront lakeview). Reservations by phone. --Claude  RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Fulton County Camping & Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 40.4693,-90.0695: campers line the north side of the camp lane directly on the strip-mine lake bank, ~10-25m to waterline; original pin was on the entrance road ~1km SW amid fields
note: Local (Fulton County, on MWRD land leased & managed by the county, near Canton) on IDNR-stocked lakes. Electric (30A) and non-electric sites, shower house, dump, fresh-water pump; $30/night electric. Some seasonal sites on the lake; transient RV row on-water unconfirmed (waterfront lakeview). Open Apr 1-Oct 31; reservations. Coords approximate (office); camping loop is on the lakeshore. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Carl Spindler Campground & Marina
waterfront_evidence: official campground layout (fondulacpark.com 2026 JPG): 'Unpaved Beach Camper Sites 1-5' drawn on the Illinois River beach and sites 49/53-55 on Marina Harbor; satellite confirms west-row pads at bank/beach edge; original pin was in an industrial lot ~350m SE
note: Local (Fon du Lac Park District, East Peoria) on the Illinois River (Upper Lake Peoria). 55 paved 50A sites (varying W/S) + ~27 primitive electric RV sites; electric-only/overflow sites sit on the river (waterfront river), marina, boat launch, pool, showers/laundry; some sites tight + road noise. Reservations via RoverPass. RV Life mislabels commercial; it is park-district. --Claude

name: Riverfront Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 40.3041,-90.0656: campground loop set back ~70-90m from the Illinois River across open park lawn with clear sightline; no pads at the bank — boat ramp/lawn between
note: Local (Havana Park District) on the Illinois River next to the Havana Nature Center. 12 water/electric riverfront sites, firepits, playground, pavilions, dump station nearby; shore/dock fishing on site (waterfront river); $35/night. Reservations. RV Life mislabels commercial; it is park-district. --Claude

name: Pere Marquette State Park
note: Illinois DNR (Grafton), bluff country off Park Rd along the Great River Road near the Illinois River. Class A campground: 80 electric sites (20/30/50A), dump station, showers, drinking water; fits 25-68 ft rigs; year-round. Sites 2-30 reservable May-Oct via ExploreMoreIL, 31-80 FCFS; campground set inland behind trees (no water view from RV sites). --Claude  RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Hamilton County State Fish & Wildlife Area
waterfront_evidence: ExploreMoreIL per-site coords (Standard facility, 59 units) overlaid on legible satellite: sites 006/007/016/034/037/038/041 at Dolan Lake waterline on the Piney Wood Campground peninsula; IDNR site map confirms campground location — old pin was ~700 m SE in trailless woods
note: Illinois DNR (near Enfield/McLeansboro), on L.P. Dolan Lake. ~61 Class B electric sites + 10 primitive (on the lakeshore) + 5 cabins; showers, water, dump station. Some electric RV sites + the primitive sites sit on the north shore (waterfront lake); roads/sites narrow (large rigs cautioned, 23-ft fine). Concession Apr 1-Oct 31, area year-round. Reservable via ExploreMoreIL or walk-in. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Henderson County Conservation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 40.8518,-90.9816 (leaf-off, legible): gravel drive-in camping loop on open grass 10-50 m above Gladstone Lake SW tail with open line of sight; IDNR site map places camping+parking+restrooms here; no discrete pads visible at waterline so no upgrade to lakefront — old pin was on the east-shore boat-launch road ~450 m NE
note: Illinois DNR (Gladstone), 87-acre area with a 27-acre fishing lake. Primitive drive-in tent/trailer sites on gravel pads by the lake (limited/older electric per reviews), pit toilets, tables. Camping area is lake-adjacent on a hill; on-water RV placement not confirmed (waterfront lakeview). Open year-round; first-come-first-served (permit on arrival). --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Green River State Wildlife Area
note: Illinois DNR (Harmon), rustic primitive vehicular camping in an open grassy/gravel area among grasslands/timber; no electric, no showers; dump station, water pump, vault toilets; gravel/blacktop access passable by a normal vehicle. RV/tent/van OK, very remote/dark-sky; self-pay ~$8/night, first-come-first-served. Camping area not on the river (waterfront none). Open year-round. --Claude  RV Life listed but unrated $ (auto 6/2026).

name: Allaire State Park

name: Boyd Lake State Park (Cottonwood)

Loops sit back from the shore behind the marina/swim beach. Open year-round. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). Loops sit ~100yd back behind a shoreline treeline and the paved lake-loop path; edge/perimeter sites have open reservoir views (lakeview); the sandy swim beach is a separate facility N of the campground. --Claude

name: Jackson Lake State Park (Pelican & Cove)

name: Jackson Lake State Park (Lakeside)

Sandpiper's east-side sites (esp. 124-132, with 128/130 closest) sit on the wide sandy beach apron on the lake side = lakefront; Fox Hills and Northview loops are set back from the shore. --Claude

note: 20 sites on the paved Turquoise Lake loop by the boat ramp, vault toilet, no water in 2026, ~9,900 ft; Leadville RD. FCFS. --Claude

note: 10 sites, vault toilets, hand-pump water, on graded gravel FR-211 (Matukat Rd) near the Lost Creek Wilderness; South Platte RD. FCFS. --Claude

note: 15 sites, vault toilets, no water (pack out), 10,500 ft near Como off Boreas Pass Rd (narrow/bumpy 2WD); South Park RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Cottonwood Lake Campground
note: 25 sites, vault toilets, no potable water, ~9,600 ft on gravel CR-344 SW of Buena Vista; lake/creek trout fishing; Salida RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Elbert Creek Campground
note: 17 sites at ~10,000 ft by the Mt. Elbert & Mt. Massive trailheads on graded Halfmoon Rd (FR-110, 2WD to the CG); vault toilets; Leadville RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Meridian Campground
note: 18 sites with parking spurs, vault toilets, hand-pump water, ~9,000 ft on gravel Park CR-43 near Bailey/Harris Park; South Platte RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Springdale Campground
note: 13 sites, vault toilets, no potable water, ~9,200 ft on gravel Rampart Range Rd near Rampart Reservoir E of Woodland Park; Pikes Peak RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Twin Peaks Campground
note: 35 sites at 9,600 ft above Lake Creek W of Twin Lakes on paved CO-82 (below the 35-ft limit), vault toilets, no water; Leadville RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Jumbo Campground
note: USFS campground at ~9,800 ft in the Mesa Lakes area on the north slope of Grand Mesa (Hwy 65, paved access). About 26 single/double sites; rec.gov lists RVs/trailers from 20 up to ~50+ ft, most sites with electric hookups, vault toilets, drinking water. Mix of reservable (online) and first-come-first-served (booked in person). Open roughly late June to late September, weather permitting. --Claude

name: Matterhorn Campground
note: USFS campground at ~9,500 ft along Hwy 145 (paved access) about 7 mi south of Telluride. 28 single-family sites, max spur ~45 ft; some sites have full hookups, others non-electric. Flush and vault toilets, showers, and drinking water. Mostly reservable online with some first-come-first-served sites booked in person. Open summer through early fall, weather permitting. --Claude

name: Sunshine Campground
note: Small single-loop, paved USFS campground at ~9,500 ft on Hwy 145 about 8 mi south of Telluride. 15 sites with max spur ~35 ft; metal tables and fire grates, vault toilets, hand-pump drinking water, no hookups. First-come-first-served only (pay on-site via the rec.gov app; cell service can be limited). --Claude

name: Rosy Lane Campground
note: USFS campground at ~8,600 ft in Taylor River Canyon (paved access off CR 742), about 17 mi NE of Gunnison. 20 single-family sites, RVs to ~30 ft; one site (008) has 15-amp electric, the rest non-electric. Vault toilets and potable water. River reached by a trail from the campground (sites are set back, not on the bank). Mix of reservable online and first-come-first-served sites booked in person. --Claude

name: Lake Irwin Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 0e40be5 (Add 23 CO GMUG-area USFS campgrounds (Grand Mesa/Uncompahgre/Gunnison)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USFS campground at ~10,300 ft on the west shore of Lake Irwin, about 9 mi from Crested Butte via a gravel/dirt road that is dusty and washboarded the last 2 miles but passable by a normal RV (campers report 27-34 ft rigs fitting). ~25-32 non-electric sites with gravel spurs, tables, fire rings; 3 vault toilets, no potable water (bring your own). Mix of reservable online and first-come-first-served. Open roughly mid-June to early October. --Claude

name: Amphitheater Campground
note: USFS campground at ~8,400 ft on a steep hillside about 2 mi south of Ouray off Hwy 550. 35 sites total; designated RV sites (roughly 3-15) cap at ~35 ft, but the access road and loops are steep with tight turns and narrow, generally small sites - fine for a 23-ft rig, not for large motorhomes or long trailers. Vault toilets, drinking water, no hookups. Mix of reservable online and first-come-first-served sites booked in person. --Claude

name: Woods Lake Campground
note: USFS campground at ~9,400 ft reached via Fall Creek Rd (FR 618), a ~7-8 mi dirt/washboard road that is dusty with some rough rocks but passable by a normal RV. 41 sites across 3 loops including some pull-throughs; vault toilets and potable water, no hookups. First-come-first-served only. Woods Lake is a short walk from the sites (sites are set back from the shore). Open roughly late spring to early fall. --Claude

name: Deer Lakes Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 0e40be5 (Add 23 CO GMUG-area USFS campgrounds (Grand Mesa/Uncompahgre/Gunnison)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Small USFS campground at ~10,400 ft near Lake City, reached via FR 788 then FR 458 (dirt forest roads, a bit tight to maneuver but passable by ~30-ft rigs per campers). 12 sites, longest reported vehicle 30 ft; vault toilets, no hookups. Lower sites overlook the small Deer Lakes. First-come-first-served (pay on-site via the rec.gov app). Open roughly June through September. --Claude

name: Dinner Station Campground
note: Gunnison NF (GMUG) campground 32 miles up the Taylor River canyon NE of Gunnison, ~9,000 ft. 22 single sites, vault toilets, potable water, no hookups; reservable on recreation.gov. Late spring to early fall, ~$18/night. Some sites accommodate larger rigs; improved dirt access road suitable for a 23-ft RV. --Claude

name: Cottonwood Lake Campground (Grand Mesa)
note: Grand Mesa NF (GMUG) campground on the Grand Mesa near Collbran at ~10,100 ft. 36 mostly-shaded sites, 42-ft max spur, vault toilets, NO potable water, no hookups. First-come, first-served (scan-and-pay on-site), ~$18/night, open roughly late June to late September. --Claude

name: Cobbett Lake Campground
note: Grand Mesa NF (GMUG) campground along the Grand Mesa Scenic Byway near the visitor center, ~10,300 ft. 20 shaded standard non-electric sites, 42-ft max spur, vault toilets, drinking water, no hookups. Mix of reservable (recreation.gov) and in-person FCFS sites; ~$22/night. Cobbett Lake allows non-motorized boats. --Claude

name: Silver Jack Campground
note: Uncompahgre NF (GMUG, Ouray RD) campground near Silver Jack Reservoir, ~18 miles up Cimarron Rd (CR 858) from US-50, ~8,900 ft. 60 heavily-wooded sites in three loops, vehicles up to 45 ft, vault toilets, drinking water; some sites have electric. Mix of reservable and FCFS via recreation.gov; ~$20 non-electric / $30 electric. --Claude

name: Soap Creek Campground
note: Gunnison NF (GMUG) campground in the Soap Creek drainage north of Blue Mesa Reservoir, reached via FR 721/824 off CO-92, ~7,700 ft. 21 sites, vault toilets, potable water, no hookups. First-come, first-served (scan-and-pay), ~$18/night. Dirt access road is passable to RVs when dry but can become impassable after heavy rain. --Claude

name: Slumgullion Campground
note: Gunnison NF (GMUG) campground near Slumgullion Pass, ~9 miles south of Lake City off CO-149 via 0.1 mi of FR 788 (dirt), ~11,300 ft. 21 sites, vehicles up to 30 ft, vault toilets, potable water, no hookups. First-come, first-served. High-elevation, short summer season. --Claude

name: Almont Campground
note: Gunnison NF (GMUG) campground 1 mile SW of Almont on CO-135 along the Gunnison River, ~8,000 ft. 10 primitive sites but 5 are tent-only; the ~5 RV-capable sites are small and rigs over ~35 ft may struggle in the turnaround, so a 23-ft EKKO fits the larger ones. 2 vault toilets, hand-pump potable water, no hookups. First-come, first-served, ~$18/night, open ~late May to early October. --Claude

name: Cement Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'creekside' assessed in commit 0e40be5 (Add 23 CO GMUG-area USFS campgrounds (Grand Mesa/Uncompahgre/Gunnison)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: GMUG (Gunnison RD) campground ~3.8 mi up Cement Creek Rd (FR 740, fairly wide dirt with some washboard) off CO-135 near Crested Butte South. 13 FCFS sites, no hookups, pit toilets, no potable water (creek only); $18/night. Max RV ~28 ft but loop is narrow with tight turns, so a 23-ft rig is comfortable while 30+ ft is discouraged. Sites 3-9 sit along the creek. Open ~May 23-Oct 1, weather dependent. --Claude

name: Dorchester Campground
note: GMUG (Gunnison RD) campground at ~9,800 ft at the upper end of Taylor Park, reached via the maintained dirt Taylor Canyon Rd / FR 742 (CR 742) past Taylor Park Reservoir - long (~39 mi from Almont) but a graded improved-dirt road passable by normal vehicles, not 4WD-only. 10 FCFS sites with dirt/gravel pads (max RV ~35 ft), vault toilets, no potable water, free. Popular OHV/fishing area. Open ~late May-Oct 1, weather dependent. --Claude

name: Buckeye Recreation Area (Pioneer Campground)
note: Manti-La Sal NF (Moab RD) campground at Buckeye Reservoir, ~7,600 ft, ~13 mi of winding gravel road (FR 0371) from Paradox. Pioneer Campground on the reservoir's north side has 30 reservable single-family sites with graveled spurs, vault toilets, no potable water; $15/night, plus 18 FCFS dispersed sites around the lake. Reservable online. Open ~mid-May to September. Reservable Pioneer sites sit back from the water (dispersed sites have the across-water views). --Claude

name: Erickson Springs Campground
note: GMUG (Gunnison NF, Paonia RD) campground on Anthracite Creek off Kebler Pass Rd (CR 12). 18 primitive sites in spruce/fir forest, RVs up to 35 ft, some pull-throughs; vault toilets, no potable water, no hookups. First-come, first-served; $14/night. Elevation ~2,065 m (6,780 ft); late spring to early fall. --Claude

name: Gold Creek Campground
note: Small, remote GMUG (Gunnison NF) campground 7 mi north of Ohio City on FR 771. 6 dirt-pad sites, 25 ft max length (small rigs only - a 23-ft Class C fits the larger spots); vault toilet, no potable water, no hookups. Free, first-come, first-served. Access is well-maintained dirt but narrows to 1-1.5 lanes past the gold mines; elevation ~3,050 m (10,000 ft), open ~late May-Sept. --Claude

name: Gothic Campground
note: GMUG designated camping area on Gothic Rd (NFSR 317) past Gothic townsite above Mt. Crested Butte; sites G1-G14, vault toilet, no water, picnic tables, $12/night, first-come first-served (claim in person). Only the lower sites are RV-suitable - the road beyond about site 4 turns rough/narrow and high-clearance 4x4 territory, so a 23-ft rig should stay to the first few sites. Elevation ~2,937 m (9,640 ft); late May-Sept, weather dependent. --Claude

name: Iron Springs Campground (Uncompahgre Plateau)
note: GMUG (Uncompahgre NF, Ouray RD) campground on the Uncompahgre Plateau near Divide Rd along the Rimrocker Trail. 8 sites for tents and small/medium trailers (campers report rigs up to ~32 ft fitting), vault toilet, no potable water, no hookups. First-come, first-served; $16/night. Access is via wide, smoothly graded gravel forest road; elevation ~2,919 m (9,580 ft), late spring to early fall. --Claude

name: Lodgepole Campground (Taylor River)
note: GMUG (Gunnison NF) campground in Taylor River Canyon, 12 mi up Taylor Canyon Rd (CR 742) from Almont. 16 sites (max ~30 ft), drinking water (hand pump) and vault toilets, no hookups; $18/night, mix of reservable (recreation.gov) and first-come first-served sites. Sits across the road from the Taylor River - good trout fishing/rafting access but sites are not on the bank. Elevation ~2,694 m (8,840 ft); open ~May 23-Oct 1. --Claude

name: Junction Creek Campground
note: USFS campground about 5 mi NW of Durango near the Colorado Trailhead, set a quarter-mile above Junction Creek. ~34 sites; 14 have electric hookups and some accommodate RVs up to ~60 ft. Vault toilets and drinking water; open roughly May-September. Mostly reservable online with some first-come, first-served sites. --Claude

name: McPhee Campground
note: Large USFS campground on a mesa about 500 ft above McPhee Reservoir near Dolores, ~7,400 ft. 71 sites on two paved loops, 24 with electric hookups; vault toilets and drinking water. Boat ramp and fish-cleaning station in the complex. Open roughly May-September; ~50 sites reservable online plus walk-ins. --Claude

name: Mavreeso Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 724327d (Add 21 CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Vallecito/McPhee/Dolores/Pagosa)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USFS campground along the West Dolores River on West Dolores Rd (FR 535), ~20 mi NE of Dolores. ~18-19 sites, several right along the river; about 7 have electric hookups and there are RV pull-throughs (sites max ~30-42 ft). Vault toilets and drinking water; open roughly mid-May to September. Mix of reservable and first-come sites. --Claude

name: West Fork Campground
note: Wooded USFS campground near the West Fork of the San Juan River off US-160 between Pagosa Springs and Wolf Creek Pass (FR 648). 28 sites, max RV ~35 ft; some sites are near the river but reviews report no sites directly on the bank. Vault toilets and drinking water; open mid-May through September. 15 reservable sites plus first-come, first-served. Access road is gravel with some rough patches but passable for a 23-ft rig. --Claude

name: Target Tree Campground
note: USFS campground in pinon-juniper/ponderosa about 7 mi east of Mancos off US-160, ~7,640 ft. ~28-30 sites, max RV ~45 ft with a couple of pull-throughs; composting toilets and drinking water (trucked in, no RV fill). No water body on site. Open roughly May-September; reservable online. Well-maintained access road suitable for a 23-ft RV. --Claude

name: Williams Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'creekside' assessed in commit 724327d (Add 21 CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Vallecito/McPhee/Dolores/Pagosa)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USFS campground on Williams Creek about 23 mi N of Pagosa Springs via Piedra Rd (FR 600/631/640), ~1 mi below Williams Creek Reservoir. ~61 sites across several loops, max RV ~45 ft; some sites sit on the creek bank and a few have water/sewer hookups. Vault toilets and drinking water; no dump on site. Open May-September; mix of reservable and first-come sites. Washboard forest-road access. --Claude

name: Ute Campground
note: First-come, first-served USFS campground on a gentle south-facing slope just off US-160 across from Chimney Rock, ~15 mi W of Pagosa Springs. 26 sites (max RV ~35 ft; many sites on a slope, larger rigs limited); potable water and vault toilets. No water body on site. Open May-September; not reservable online. --Claude

name: Teal Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 724327d (Add 21 CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Vallecito/McPhee/Dolores/Pagosa)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: First-come, first-served USFS campground on FR 640 overlooking Williams Creek Reservoir, ~1 mi N of Williams Creek Campground. 16 sites set above the water (short walk to shore); open and grassy with some ponderosa shade. Max RV ~35 ft but only a few sites suit larger rigs-best for a 23-ft rig and tight; potable water and vault toilets. Open May-September; not reservable online. --Claude

name: West Dolores Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 724327d (Add 21 CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Vallecito/McPhee/Dolores/Pagosa)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USFS campground on the West Dolores River, ~21 mi north of Dolores via Hwy 145 and FR 535 (the campground is reached before the steep/narrow upper-road section, so it's fine for a 23-ft rig; max RV 35 ft, some sites larger). 18 sites, 7 with electric, 10 reservable plus FCFS overflow. Potable water, composting toilet, no dump. Open roughly May-Sep; reservable online. --Claude

name: Golden Horn Designated Camping Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'creekside' assessed in commit 724327d (Add 21 CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Vallecito/McPhee/Dolores/Pagosa)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Free designated dispersed camping area in San Juan NF along FR 585 (South Mineral Creek Rd), ~5 mi from Silverton off US-550, ~9,600 ft. Bumpy but all-vehicle-accessible road; sites large enough for most rigs including a 23-ft RV. Vault toilets and fire rings, no water/hookups; FCFS, 14-day limit, not on recreation.gov. --Claude

name: Little Molas Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 724327d (Add 21 CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Vallecito/McPhee/Dolores/Pagosa)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Free FCFS USFS campground at ~10,900 ft beside Little Molas Lake, reached by ~1 mi of dirt road (FR 584) off US-550 just north of Molas Pass; road is washboarded but 2WD/passenger-car passable. 10 sites, 5 of which fit RVs. Pit toilets, no water, no hookups; 14-day limit, not on recreation.gov. --Claude

name: House Creek Campground
note: USFS campground above McPhee Reservoir near Dolores, set in a gently sloping meadow near the high-water line (closest sites ~300 ft from water; the reservoir draws down well below the campground, so pads are set back). 22 renovated sites in several loops, 12 reservable and 12 with electric, plus FCFS sites; vault toilets, drinking water, boat ramp on site. Open roughly May-Sep; reservable online. --Claude

name: South Mineral Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'creekside' assessed in commit 724327d (Add 21 CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Vallecito/McPhee/Dolores/Pagosa)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USFS campground at ~9,800 ft along South Mineral Creek, reached via ~4 mi of FR 585 off US-550 west of Silverton; the road to the campground is 2WD-drivable (it only turns 4WD beyond the campground), fine for a 23-ft rig. 26 mostly level FCFS sites (no reservations), potable water, vault toilets, no hookups. Trailhead for the Ice Lake Basin hike is across the road. --Claude

name: Pine Point Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 724327d (Add 21 CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Vallecito/McPhee/Dolores/Pagosa)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USFS campground on the shore of Vallecito Reservoir, ~13 mi north of Bayfield via CR 501/501A; midway up the lakeshore with several lakefront sites. 28 sites (about half reservable, ~13 FCFS), max RV length around 30-45 ft depending on site; vault toilets, drinking water, no hookups. Open roughly May-Sep; reservable online. --Claude

name: Bradfield Campground
note: San Juan NF (Dolores RD), in the Dolores River Canyon below McPhee Dam ~30 mi NW of Dolores via Hwy 184/US 491/FR 505. 22 sites, large and open, FCFS only (not on recreation.gov). Vault toilets, potable water, picnic tables, fire grates; limited/no services Sept-May. Boat ramp busy during the short Dolores rafting release (around Memorial Day). No hookups; sites set back from the brushy river bank. --Claude

name: Burro Bridge Campground
note: San Juan NF (Dolores RD), on a sloping hillside above the West Dolores River ~2 mi NE of Dunton. 14 sites, FCFS only, max RV 35 ft. Vault toilets, potable water, picnic tables, fire grates; two sites have horse corrals. Access via West Dolores Rd (FR 535) gravel; approach from the Dolores/Hwy 145 side - the steep, narrow last 6 miles 'not recommended for trailers' are the upper segment toward Rico, beyond the campground. Mid-May through Sept. No hookups. --Claude

name: Cimarrona Campground
note: San Juan NF (Pagosa RD), beside Cimarrona Creek near the head of the road above Williams Creek Reservoir, ~27 mi NW of Pagosa Springs on FR 640. 21 sites, FCFS only; a few sites fit large RVs (max 35 ft). Vault toilets, potable water, picnic tables, fire grates. Adjacent Cimarrona Trailhead; short walk to creek/reservoir fishing. Open roughly May-Sept. No hookups. --Claude

name: Ferris Canyon Campground
note: San Juan NF (Dolores RD), on the Lower Dolores River ~3.5 mi below McPhee Dam on FR 504, ~6,500 ft. Small primitive barrier-free CG, 7 graveled easy-access sites, FCFS; max RV ~30 ft. Popular with catch-and-release fly fishers. No potable water - bring your own; no hookups. Lightly used. --Claude

name: Florida Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 724327d (Add 21 CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Vallecito/McPhee/Dolores/Pagosa)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: San Juan NF (Columbine RD), along the Florida ('Floor-ee-da') River above Lemon Reservoir, reached via CR 240/243. 20 shaded individual sites, all FCFS; a reservable group site exists. Max RV 25 ft, only a few short pull-thru/back-in sites take larger rigs (not big-rig friendly); a 23-ft fits. Vault toilets, potable water, picnic tables. Open roughly May-Sept. No hookups. --Claude

name: Graham Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 724327d (Add 21 CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Vallecito/McPhee/Dolores/Pagosa)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: San Juan NF (Columbine RD) on the east side of Vallecito Reservoir, reached via CR 240/CR 501/FR 603. 25 sites (15 reservable on recreation.gov + 10 FCFS), max RV 35 ft; entrance/exit roads are steep but pads take large rigs. Hillside sites overlook the lake ~100 yd above the high-water line. Drinking water, vault toilets, trash. Mid-May to mid-Sept. No hookups. --Claude

name: Kroeger Campground
note: San Juan NF (Columbine RD) in La Plata Canyon near the La Plata River, ~5 mi up CR 124 from Hesperus (US 160). 10 shaded sites, FCFS, max RV 25 ft - most sites suit smaller rigs but at least one long-RV site and one pull-thru exist, so a 23-ft fits. The campground is reached by 2WD gravel; CR 124 turns to 4WD only farther up-canyon past the CG. Potable water, vault toilet, picnic tables, host; pack out trash, bear-safe storage required. Late May-Sept. No hookups. --Claude

name: Mogote Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit ad1da0d (Add 8 CO Rio Grande NF campgrounds (Conejos/South Fork/Saguache)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USFS campground on the Conejos River off Hwy 17, ~13 mi W of Antonito; 40 sites in Upper and Lower loops (21 pull-through), no hookups, vault toilets, drinking-water hydrants. Per-site max length is 25 ft, so a 23-ft rig fits but larger rigs won't. Open Memorial Day weekend through the second Monday of September; reservable on recreation.gov with some first-come, first-served sites. Lower loop sits along the river. --Claude

name: Lower Beaver Creek Campground
note: USFS campground in the Beaver Creek drainage, 1 mi south of South Fork then 4.5 mi south on gravel Forest Rd 360. 18 sites (15 dead-end spurs, 3 pull-through), max spur 45 ft though not all sites fit that. No hookups, vault toilets, seasonal hand-pump drinking water. Open Memorial Day-Labor Day (5 sites stay open year-round, no services). FCFS, not reservable; pay at site. --Claude

name: Cross Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'creekside' assessed in commit ad1da0d (Add 8 CO Rio Grande NF campgrounds (Conejos/South Fork/Saguache)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Small USFS campground straddling Cross Creek just above the Beaver Reservoir inlet, ~6.4 mi up Beaver Creek Rd (FR 20, gravel after 4 mi) from US 160. 8 sites, max spur 25 ft and not all sites fit that length. No hookups, vault toilet, no drinking water. Concessionaire season Memorial Day-Labor Day; accessible year-round if snow allows. FCFS, not reservable. --Claude

name: Highway Springs Campground
note: USFS campground in two loops just off US 160, 4.1 mi west of South Fork toward Wolf Creek Pass. 13 sites (3 pull-through) with adequate RV turnaround/parking; no posted max length, no hookups, 2 vault toilets, no drinking water. Open Memorial Day-Labor Day. FCFS, not reservable; pay by envelope. --Claude

name: Bristol Head Campground
note: USFS campground ~21 mi from Creede off Hwy 149 (Silver Thread Scenic Byway) via a short maintained gravel road. 15 sites (11 pull-through), site lengths 35-55 ft so big rigs fit easily. Vault toilets, potable water, no hookups. Sits in a meadow on a bench above South Clear Creek. Open Memorial Day-Labor Day, 14-day limit. FCFS, not reservable (not on recreation.gov). --Claude

name: Buffalo Pass Campground
note: 19 large, well-spaced sites (4 pull-through) in a single loop in a ponderosa grove/grass meadow at ~9,100 ft; max vehicle 30 ft (a 32-ft fifth wheel has fit per a review). Reached via 27.6 mi west on Hwy 114 from Saguache then 1.7 mi gravel road. Vault toilets, no potable water, no hookups. FCFS, not reservable; $10/night. Open mid-May to mid-November. Sites 16-18 on a no-turnaround dead-end spur. --Claude

name: North Crestone Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'creekside' assessed in commit ad1da0d (Add 8 CO Rio Grande NF campgrounds (Conejos/South Fork/Saguache)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: 13 FCFS sites (one pull-through) strung 0.7 mi up the canyon along North Crestone Creek at ~8,300 ft near Crestone; no posted length limit but described as best for smaller rigs, so a 23-ft Class C should aim for the pull-through. No reservations; $10/night, vault toilets, bear lockers, no potable water. Reached via unpaved county road / Alder St north of Crestone. Open mid-May to mid-November. --Claude

name: Comstock Campground
note: Small, remote 7-site FCFS campground (remodeled 2010) on the South Fork of Rock Creek SW of Monte Vista at ~9,600 ft; dead-end spur sites run 35-40 ft (max vehicle 30 ft) so a 23-ft rig fits, though maneuvering room for large trailers is limited. Reached via ~12 mi of County Road R then ~6 mi of graded dirt Forest Road 28 (passenger-car passable, 4x4 not required). Vault toilet, no water, no posted fee. Thinly reviewed - verify season/road with the Divide Ranger District. --Claude

name: Big Creek Lakes Campground
note: Routt NF campground near Big Creek Lake at ~9,000 ft; 54 sites (24 reservable online, 30 FCFS). Mostly large gravel pull-throughs; site-level max RV length ranges from 20 ft up to 40 ft, so a 23-ft rig fits the larger sites. Vault toilets; NO potable water currently (spigots capped, bring your own). Short season ~early June to mid/late October. Reach via FR 600 (gravel). --Claude

name: Hahns Peak Lake Campground
note: Routt NF campground in spruce/fir forest just uphill of 160-acre Hahns Peak Lake at ~8,500 ft; 26 sites (23 single + 3 double), reservable on recreation.gov. Sites are spacious and wooded, set back from the shore (~5-min walk to the lake, no waterfront sites). Confirmed RV lengths to 25 ft with larger sites longer, fitting a 23-ft rig. Vault toilets, NO potable water on site. Season mid-June to mid-October; non-motorized/electric-motor boats only. --Claude

name: Blacktail Creek Campground
note: Small Routt NF campground of 8 first-come, first-served sites right off paved CO Hwy 134 between Kremmling and Yampa; $20/night, cash or check only (no credit cards), not on recreation.gov. Vault toilet, potable water (June-Sep, weather dependent), no hookups. Open roughly June-October. Open-meadow sites along Blacktail Creek; a reviewer fit a 33-ft rig, so a 23-ft RV is fine (sites are unlevel). --Claude

name: Chapman Reservoir Campground
note: Primitive Routt NF campground at Chapman Reservoir off the Flat Tops Trail Scenic Byway west of Yampa, reached via FR 16/FR 940 (2WD, typically open Memorial Day to mid-October). 12 first-come, first-served sites, $20/night cash or check only; 22-ft max trailer length (a 23-ft Class C fits marginally), vault toilet, camp host on site in summer, NO potable water (bring your own). Elevation ~9,300 ft. --Claude

name: Dry Lake Campground
note: Routt NF campground on lower Buffalo Pass Rd about 8 mi NE of Steamboat Springs; rebuilt and expanded in 2024 from 8 to 23 sites (reopened 2025). For 2026 a majority of sites are reservable on Recreation.gov (~June 15) with the rest first-come, first-served; ~$26 standard/$50 double. Vault toilets, bear boxes, no potable water; open late June into October. The access road is good up to the campground (the rough/4WD stretch is past it toward Buffalo Pass). 'Dry Lake' is a shallow seasonal puddle, not a real water feature. --Claude

name: Freeman Reservoir Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit ce15c1c (Add 7 CO Routt NF campgrounds (North Park/Steamboat/Yampa)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: 18 sites (pull-through + back-in), max RV ~25 ft so a 23-ft rig fits but sites run small; vault toilets, no hookups. FCFS, exact cash/check only, NOT on recreation.gov; open ~mid-June through Oct (weather dependent). Access is 8.5 mi of maintained gravel/dirt off Hwy 13 / Moffat CR 11 (steep/washboard in spots, fine for a careful Class C). Reservoir (non-motorized boating only) and boat ramp/picnic area are a separate facility at the lake; most sites are set back in meadow/forest, one loop has lake views. Forest Service resumes management in June 2026 (formerly run by Moffat County). General campground that also offers horse corrals. --Claude

name: Hidden Lakes Campground
note: Small 9-site campground at ~9,100 ft on a small lake near Buffalo Pass; 3 sites are RV-capable. FCFS, $20/night cash or check; vault toilet, no potable water, no trash (pack it out). Open ~mid-June to mid-Oct (weather). Official FS page lists trailers up to 50 ft and gives no high-clearance warning; a reviewer reached it in a 2WD car. Access: CR 24 west of Hebron to Forest Road 60, then FR 20 south ~6 mi (forest gravel). Satellite shows the loop set back ~30-50 m from the lake in thinned forest, not on the shoreline. --Claude

name: Deer Creek Campground (Bailey)
note: Heavily wooded Pike NF campground at ~9,170 ft near Bailey where Deer Creek and Camp Creek meet. 13 single sites, RVs up to 30 ft; no hookups, no potable water, vault toilets. First-come, first-served only (pay on-site, including Scan & Pay via the Recreation.gov app) - not reservable. --Claude

name: Kenosha Pass Campground
note: Small Pike NF campground at Kenosha Pass on US-285, in lodgepole and aspen near the Colorado Trail. 25 sites with parking spurs 16-30 ft (some fit a 23-ft rig); no hookups, hand-pump water, vault toilets. Reservable on Recreation.gov; season roughly Memorial Day into the fall. --Claude

name: Jefferson Creek Campground
note: Pike NF campground at ~10,100 ft in the Jefferson Lake Recreation Area, on a ridge overlooking Jefferson Creek. 17 sites, parking spurs 25-40 ft but max RV length 25 ft; no hookups, potable water, vault toilets. Reservable on Recreation.gov (unreserved sites go walk-up); $11 one-time rec-area entrance fee; mid-May to mid-October. Access via graded but bumpy dirt CR-37 (passenger-car/RV passable, not 4WD). --Claude

name: Lodgepole Campground (Jefferson)
note: Largest of the Jefferson Lake Recreation Area campgrounds (~9,900 ft) in a valley along Jefferson Creek, 4 miles north of Jefferson. 34 sites, parking spurs 20-40 ft, max RV length 30 ft; no hookups, drinking water, vault toilets. Reservable on Recreation.gov; $11 one-time rec-area entrance fee; mid-May to mid-October. Access via graded dirt CR-37 (bumpy but passenger-car/RV passable, not 4WD); tight maneuvering reported for larger rigs. --Claude

name: Timberline Campground (Kenosha)
note: Pike NF campground at ~9,700 ft near Kenosha Pass, about 7 miles southwest of Grant, in an aspen-forested area within a mile of the Colorado Trail. 30 individual sites in two loops (RVs to ~36 ft reported); no hookups, no potable water (available at nearby Kenosha Pass CG), vault toilets. Reservable on Recreation.gov; access is rutted gravel but passable in a passenger car, not 4WD. --Claude

name: Michigan Creek Campground
note: 12-site drive-in USFS campground about 4 miles NW of Jefferson off CR 35/CR 54 at 10,100 ft, max trailer length 25 ft. FCFS only ($22/night) - no reservations; pay on-site via Recreation.gov Scan and Pay. Vault toilets, tables, fire rings; no potable water or trash service. Open mid-May to mid-October, weather dependent. --Claude

name: Aspen Campground (Jefferson)
note: 12-site single-loop USFS campground in the Jefferson Lake Recreation Area, along Jefferson Creek at 9,900 ft, reached via gravel CR 35/CR 37 from Jefferson. Gravel parking aprons fit two vehicles, max trailer length 25 ft. Reservable on recreation.gov (up to 5 days out; unreserved sites become FCFS walk-ups); $11 rec-area entry fee per stay applies. Vault toilets and a hand-pump water well; open mid-May to mid-October. --Claude

name: Devil's Head Campground
note: 21-site drive-in USFS campground in two loops at ~8,800 ft on the Rampart Range, at the base of Devil's Head and its fire-tower trail. Sites take RVs up to 30 ft; vault toilets and a hand water pump (no potable hookups, no dump). FCFS only -- no advance reservations; pay on-site via the rec.gov Scan-and-Pay QR. Reached via ~20 mi of graded gravel Rampart Range Rd (2WD-passable, no 4WD needed), open ~late May to late September, road closed by Dec 1. The creekside lower loop near Jackson Creek has been closed pending sediment removal; the upper loop is a dry forested ridge. --Claude

name: Coffee Pot Spring Campground
note: Small USFS campground (~10 sites) on a south-facing aspen/spruce slope at about 10,100 ft, roughly 16 miles up Coffee Pot Road (FDR 600) from the Dotsero exit off I-70. Sites have level gravel driveways that accommodate RVs/trailers up to about 30 ft; picnic tables, fire rings, vault toilets, and no potable water. FCFS, no reservations; access is via a long, narrow one-lane gravel road (drive slowly), but it is passable for a 23-ft rig. Season roughly mid-summer through early fall. --Claude

name: Homestake Valley Campground
note: Designated-site Forest Service campground (44 single + 2 group sites, formerly the informal Blodgett/Homestake established-dispersed sites) spread along the lower gravel of Homestake Road (FSR 703) and Missouri Creek Road southwest of Red Cliff, overlooking Homestake Creek at ~8,900 ft. Per the USFS some sites are RV-accessible while others fit personal vehicles only, so a 23-ft rig works at some sites but not all; no on-site restrooms or potable water, fire rings and tables. FCFS for the 2026 season (June 15-Oct 3, $20/single); about half the singles plus the group sites become reservable on recreation.gov starting 2027. The graded lower road is passable for most vehicles (the rough 4WD pitch is the last mile to the reservoir, well past the campground), but turnarounds are limited so pick an RV-capable site. --Claude

name: Marshall Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 34be9ad (Add 20 more CO Rio Grande NF campgrounds (Creede/South Fork/Conejos)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Rio Grande NF, ~7 mi SW of Creede on Hwy 149 at ~8,800 ft. 16 sites (7 paved pull-throughs, 9 gravel spurs) in a meadow on the Rio Grande; sites 1-10 reservable on recreation.gov, 11-17 FCFS. Vault toilet, no drinking water (bring your own). No hookups. Memorial Day-Labor Day. --Claude

name: River Hill Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 34be9ad (Add 20 more CO Rio Grande NF campgrounds (Creede/South Fork/Conejos)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Rio Grande NF, ~9.5 mi west on FR 520 (Rio Grande Reservoir Rd) off Hwy 149, ~9,250 ft, on the Rio Grande just below Rio Grande Reservoir. 20 sites (13 reservable on recreation.gov, 7 FCFS); max spur 55 ft but not all fit it, most take up to ~30 ft. Two vault toilets, well hand-pump water, a few sites have electric hookup. Memorial Day-Labor Day. --Claude

name: Thirty Mile Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit 34be9ad (Add 20 more CO Rio Grande NF campgrounds (Creede/South Fork/Conejos)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Rio Grande NF, ~11 mi west on FR 520 off Hwy 149, ~9,300 ft, ~1 mi below Rio Grande Reservoir in a wooded meadow between the Rio Grande and Grizzly Creek. 35 sites (28 reservable on recreation.gov, 7 FCFS); max spur 40 ft, not all sites fit it, 9 pull-throughs. Vault toilets, seasonal potable water (Memorial Day-Labor Day). No hookups. --Claude

name: North Clear Creek Campground
note: Rio Grande NF, ~2.1 mi north on FR 510 off Hwy 149 (~24.5 mi west of Creede), ~9,900 ft, adjacent to North Clear Creek (firsthand reviews put sites ~50 yd from the creek, not on the bank). 21 sites; max spur 40 ft, not all fit it. Two vault toilets, well potable water, camp host. No hookups. FCFS - not on recreation.gov. Memorial Day-Labor Day. --Claude

name: Road Canyon Campground
note: Rio Grande NF, ~6 mi west on FR 520 off Hwy 149 (~20 mi SW of Creede), ~9,300 ft, in an open treeless meadow at the head of Road Canyon Reservoir (sites not at the shoreline; boat launch nearby). 6 units plus an undeveloped overflow area; max spur 50 ft, not all fit it. Vault toilet, no drinking water, no hookups. FCFS - cannot be reserved. Peak Memorial Day-Labor Day. --Claude

name: Rito Hondo Campground
note: Rio Grande NF dispersed camping area (signed 'Rito Hondo Campground') near Rito Hondo Reservoir, ~3 mi northwest on FR 513 off Hwy 149, ~10,200 ft. Designated drive-in gravel pads set back from the lake; pit toilet, no water, no hookups, no fee, FCFS. Access is ~2-3 mi of washboard gravel passable to a normal vehicle; RVs to ~31 ft reported by campers. Open range (cattle). --Claude

name: Rock Creek Campground
note: Rio Grande NF (Monte Vista RD) on Rock Creek, ~9,200 ft. 10 sites (7 dead-end spurs, 3 pull-through), spurs 20-40 ft, RVs up to ~30-35 ft though not all sites fit. Vault toilets, no potable water. FCFS, not reservable; $10/night. Access via CR 28, dirt and single-lane after 3.1 mi but passable by passenger vehicle. Some sites sit next to the creek but no per-site map/satellite confirmed a pad at the waterline. --Claude

name: Mix Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 34be9ad (Add 20 more CO Rio Grande NF campgrounds (Creede/South Fork/Conejos)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Rio Grande NF (Conejos Peak RD), 1 mi west of Platoro at ~10,000 ft overlooking Mix Lake. 22 sites with trailer spaces (RVs to ~40 ft), 3 vault toilets, potable water. FCFS, not reservable. Season roughly Memorial Day-Labor Day; forest road snow-closed Mar 15-May 15. Access point for South San Juan Wilderness. --Claude

name: Poso Campground
note: Rio Grande NF (Saguache RD) on a hillside above the South Fork of Carnero Creek. 8 sites, max trailer length 25 ft, two vault toilets, no potable water. FCFS, not reservable. Season ~May 19-Nov 18. Access via gravel Carnero Creek Rd (FR 690) then FR 675; passenger-vehicle passable. No horses/pack animals in the campground. --Claude

name: Spectacle Lake Campground
note: Rio Grande NF (Conejos Peak RD) in the Conejos River canyon at ~8,700 ft. 24 sites (13 pull-through), tables/fire rings, two vault toilets, developed water with 2 hand pumps. Open roughly Memorial Day-Labor Day. Access via FR 17 then 6.3 mi on dirt FR 250; passable by passenger vehicle. Adjacent to the Conejos River (a short walk to the bank) and a small Spectacle Lake across the road, but no per-site map/satellite confirmed RV pads at a waterline. FCFS. --Claude

name: Storm King Campground
note: Rio Grande NF (Saguache RD) at the base of Storm King Mtn on the Middle Fork of Carnero Creek, ~9,400-9,500 ft. 6 campsites (plus 2 picnic sites), max trailer length 25 ft, vault toilet, no potable water. FCFS, not reservable; $10/night. Season ~mid-May to mid-Nov. Access via CR 41G/FR 690 over Carnero Pass - dirt, single-lane with turnouts but passable by a standard vehicle. --Claude

name: Stunner Campground
note: Rio Grande NF (Conejos Peak RD) beside the Alamosa River, ~9,700 ft, about 2 mi below Stunner Pass. 5 sites with trailer spaces, vault toilet, no potable water; free, FCFS. Season ~late May to mid-Oct. Access via dirt FR 250/255; narrow and rough in spots but reported drivable in 2WD. Alamosa River fishing here is poor due to naturally acidic water. A riverside open site exists but no per-site map/satellite confirmed a pad at the waterline. --Claude

name: Big Meadows Campground
note: Rio Grande NF, ~11 mi W of South Fork off FR 410, ~9,200 ft, in 3 loops above Big Meadows Reservoir. 42 sites (23 reservable on recreation.gov, 19 FCFS), no hookups, vault toilets, drinking water, max spur 60 ft (not all sites fit). Open Memorial Day-Labor Day; 14-day limit. Sites sit up the hillside above the reservoir, not on the shore. --Claude

name: Palisade Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 34be9ad (Add 20 more CO Rio Grande NF campgrounds (Creede/South Fork/Conejos)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
