note: Rio Grande NF, small campground on the Rio Grande in the canyon on Hwy 149 between South Fork and Creede, ~8,300 ft. 12 sites, FCFS only (not reservable), vault toilet, water Memorial Day-Labor Day only, no hookups, max spur 35 ft but most sites suit rigs under ~30 ft. Active railroad tracks and Hwy 149 border the campground (traffic/train noise). --Claude

name: Park Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 34be9ad (Add 20 more CO Rio Grande NF campgrounds (Creede/South Fork/Conejos)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Rio Grande NF, small campground along the South Fork of the Rio Grande just off US-160 ~8 mi W of South Fork, ~8,500 ft. 13 gravel sites, FCFS only (not reservable), 2 vault toilets, hand-pump water in season, no hookups, max spur 35 ft but only a few sites take rigs over 30 ft; tight loop turnaround. Open Memorial Day-Labor Day; 14-day limit. Some highway noise. --Claude

name: Tucker Ponds Campground
note: Rio Grande NF, high-elevation (~9,600 ft) campground SW of South Fork via US-160 then ~2.7 mi up gravel FR 390 (single-lane with turnouts). 15 sites in one loop, FCFS only (not reservable), vault toilets, hand-pump water, no hookups, max spur 40 ft (not all sites fit). Open Memorial Day-Labor Day; 14-day limit. The two Tucker Ponds are across the road from the entrance, not in the campground. --Claude

name: Upper Beaver Creek Campground
note: Rio Grande NF, campground on Beaver Creek Rd ~4.75 mi from US-160 W of South Fork, ~8,500 ft, in two sections under ponderosa pine. 14 sites, FCFS only (not reservable), vault toilets, seasonal water, no hookups, max spur 35 ft (not all sites fit); 4 sites accessible year-round weather permitting. Open Memorial Day-Labor Day. Beaver Creek runs along the campground and Beaver Creek Reservoir (boat ramp) is nearby, but firsthand RV-site-on-water proof wasn't confirmed. --Claude

name: Aspen Glade Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 34be9ad (Add 20 more CO Rio Grande NF campgrounds (Creede/South Fork/Conejos)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Rio Grande NF, 32 sites in 3 loops (A/B lightly wooded ponderosa, C along the Conejos River), 7 pull-through; max RV length 45 ft; vault toilets, potable water, no hookups; 8,500 ft, Memorial Day-Labor Day. Reservable on recreation.gov. On CO-17 ~15 mi west of Antonito; Gold Medal trout water 1/4 mi upstream. --Claude

name: Trujillo Meadows Campground
note: Rio Grande NF, 50 sites (Upper/Lower loops) near Cumbres Pass; max site length 40 ft (inner-loop sites 34-39 not RV-friendly); vault toilets, potable water, no hookups; ~10,000 ft, Memorial Day-Labor Day. FCFS. Reached via paved CO-17 then ~3 mi of gravel FR118. Sits above Trujillo Meadows Reservoir (steep climb/short trail down to the water; sites are set back from the shoreline). --Claude

name: Luders Creek Campground
note: Rio Grande NF, small 5-site campground (3 pull-through) near Cochetopa Pass; official max trailer length 25 ft; vault toilet, NO potable water (treat creek/spring water); no hookups; ~10,000 ft, mid-May to mid-Nov. FCFS, not on recreation.gov. Reached via CO-114 then ~11 mi of gravel FR750/CR NN14. Has 3 horse corrals but is a general drive-in campground. --Claude

name: Lakeview Campground (Taylor Park)
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 14b2e1e (Add 11 more CO GMUG campgrounds (Taylor Park/Grand Mesa/Pitkin)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Largest Taylor Park Reservoir USFS campground, ~65 sites on a bench above the reservoir; some sites have 30/50-amp electric, no sewer/water hookups. Vault toilets, potable water, dump station nearby. Mix of reservable and first-come-first-served sites; reservable online. Open late spring to early fall, ~9,400 ft. --Claude

name: Lottis Creek Campground
note: ~47 sites in Taylor River Canyon, renovated 2012 with 30/50-amp electric at most sites (no sewer/water hookups); back-in gravel pads, larger sites fit 40+ ft. Vault toilets, drinking water. Mix of reservable and first-come-first-served (FCFS booked in person); reservable online. Open ~June to late September, ~9,070 ft. --Claude

name: Rivers End Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 14b2e1e (Add 11 more CO GMUG campgrounds (Taylor Park/Grand Mesa/Pitkin)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: 15 sites at the north end of Taylor Park Reservoir where the Taylor River enters, ~28 miles up graded Taylor Canyon Rd (FR742) from Almont. No hookups, vault toilets, hand-pump water, fire rings and tables. First-come-first-served only - arrive in person to claim a site; not reservable. ~9,300 ft. --Claude

name: Spring Creek Campground (Taylor Canyon)
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'creekside' assessed in commit 14b2e1e (Add 11 more CO GMUG campgrounds (Taylor Park/Grand Mesa/Pitkin)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Small 12-site loop along Spring Creek off Taylor River Rd, ~35 ft max RV (not for big rigs, but fine for a 23-ft rig). No hookups, vault toilets, dump available. First-come-first-served only - arrive in person; not reservable (on-site Scan-and-Pay via the rec.gov app). Near the Taylor River gold-medal waters, ~8,800 ft. --Claude

name: Mosca Campground
note: 16 spacious tent/RV sites at Spring Creek Reservoir, ~12 miles up graded gravel Spring Creek Rd (FR744); a boat ramp and fishing site are within the campground. No hookups, no potable water, vault toilets, fire rings and tables. First-come-first-served only - arrive in person; not reservable. Open ~May 23 to Oct 1, ~9,990 ft. --Claude

name: Pitkin Campground
note: GMUG/Gunnison RD campground ~0.5 mi east of Pitkin along Quartz Creek (Cumberland Pass Rd/FR765), ~9,400 ft. 22-23 sites, mostly pull-throughs; allstays/rec.gov list 30-ft max RV and reviewers report 23-27 ft rigs fitting. Hand water pump, vault/portable toilets, gray-water dump (no hookups), no cell service. Open ~May-Sep. FCFS only (Scan & Pay on the rec.gov app on-site); not reservable. --Claude

name: Island Lake Campground
note: Grand Mesa NF, ~10,200 ft. 41 sites, max spur 60 ft; 7 electric sites plus non-electric. Vault toilets, potable water. Mix of reservable (recreation.gov) and FCFS. Open ~late June to late Sept. Set back ~0.1 mi from Island Lake (not on shore); access via paved CO-65 then gravel FR-116, last climb a bit rough. --Claude

name: Big Creek Campground
note: Grand Mesa NF near Big Creek Reservoir. Max spur 33 ft, mostly shady sites. Vault toilet, hand-pump water; FCFS only (scan-and-pay on site). Open ~late June to late Sept. Reached by a long gravel road (FR-121 / FR-521). Reservoir is drawn down in summer and recent reviews call the grounds run-down but operating; sites set back from the water. --Claude

name: Crag Crest Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 14b2e1e (Add 11 more CO GMUG campgrounds (Taylor Park/Grand Mesa/Pitkin)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Grand Mesa NF, ~10,300 ft, above Eggleston Lake. Only 11 small sites, max spur 27 ft (sites 5-6 tent-only, no turnaround) - fits a 23-ft rig at some sites but tight; FCFS. Vault toilet, NO potable water. Open ~mid-June to mid-Sept. Gravel access (FR-121) then a rutted/steep spur to the pay station - best for shorter, higher-clearance rigs. --Claude

name: Weir and Johnson Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 14b2e1e (Add 11 more CO GMUG campgrounds (Taylor Park/Grand Mesa/Pitkin)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Grand Mesa NF, ~10,500 ft, single loop between Weir & Johnson and Sackett reservoirs. 11 sites, max spur 33 ft; vault toilet, NO potable water; FCFS only. Open ~late June to late Sept. Access is a long, dusty, bumpy gravel road and a firsthand reviewer warns only a few sites fit rigs over 25 ft - doable for a 23-ft rig but tight; not 4WD-required. --Claude

name: Lost Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 14b2e1e (Add 11 more CO GMUG campgrounds (Taylor Park/Grand Mesa/Pitkin)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Gunnison NF (GMUG), Paonia RD, on Lost Lake Slough at ~9,600 ft. 18 RV sites (plus tent and a few horse sites), gravel parking aprons; RV sites capped ~28 ft so a 23-ft rig fits, but the access is FR706 - 2 miles of dirt, narrow with tight turns past 9+ mi of gravel County Rd 12; passable by passenger cars and RVs to ~28-30 ft, larger trailers discouraged. Vault toilets; potable water availability is inconsistent across sources so bring water. FCFS, no reservations; season ~mid-June to early October. --Claude

name: Haviland Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 2466e2c (Add 10 more CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Durango/Vallecito/Pagosa/Rico)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: 43 sites in 4 loops on Haviland Lake (an 80-acre no-wake reservoir) ~18 mi N of Durango at ~8,000 ft; 17 sites have electric hookups, max RV 40-45 ft, vault toilets, drinking water on site. Open ~May-Sep. 26 sites reservable online, remainder FCFS (claim in person). --Claude

name: Lower Hermosa Campground
note: Small first-come-first-served campground (~18-19 sites, RVs up to 30 ft) above Hermosa Creek ~18 mi N of Durango; vault toilet (at adjacent Hermosa Creek Trailhead lot), NO potable water - bring your own. Narrow dirt access road, passable for a 23-ft rig. Sits above the creek with no creek access from sites. Open ~May-Sep, FCFS only. --Claude

name: Miller Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 2466e2c (Add 10 more CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Durango/Vallecito/Pagosa/Rico)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: 12 first-come-first-served sites on the east shore of Lemon Reservoir where Miller Creek enters, ~18 mi N of Durango at ~8,150 ft; RVs up to ~35-45 ft, vault toilets, potable water, concrete boat ramp. Shoreline recedes at low pool (reservoir drawdown). Open late May-Sep, FCFS (scan-and-pay), not reservable. --Claude

name: Sig Creek Campground
note: 9 reservable drive-in sites (listed max RV 35 ft) ~1/4 mi from the East Fork of Hermosa Creek, ~6 mi W of Purgatory on Hermosa Park Rd / FR 578 at ~9,000 ft; single vault toilet, water seasonal/intermittent (bring your own). Access road is 2WD-rated but has rough switchbacks from Purgatory - fine for a 23-ft Class C, tight for larger rigs (the upper section past the campground over Bolam Pass is 4WD-only). Reservable online. --Claude

name: Transfer Park Campground
note: 25 sites in two loops on 11 forested acres above the Florida River, ~21 mi NE of Durango via CR 240/243 (1 mi past Florida Campground) at ~8,600 ft; 12 individual sites (5 reservable), a group site, and barrier-free sites; pit toilets, water in season (no services Sep-May). Most sites fit RVs. River is a walk downhill from the loop - sites are above it, not on it. Reservable online; some sites FCFS. --Claude

name: Vallecito Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'creekside' assessed in commit 2466e2c (Add 10 more CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Durango/Vallecito/Pagosa/Rico)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: 80 single-family sites in 4 loops (B/C/D/W) on Vallecito Creek ~4 mi north of Vallecito Reservoir, ~8,000 ft; 33 reservable + 47 FCFS, 3 electric sites (rest no hookups), vault toilets, potable water; max RV ~45 ft; open May-Sep; concessionaire-run (Rocky Mountain Recreation), reservable online. --Claude

name: East Fork Campground (San Juan)
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit 2466e2c (Add 10 more CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Durango/Vallecito/Pagosa/Rico)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USFS campground on a ridge above the East Fork San Juan River, ~11 mi NE of Pagosa Springs off Hwy 160 then 0.75 mi on FR 667. 26 sites (17 reservable, 9 FCFS), max RV ~35 ft, a few pull-throughs; vault toilets, potable water, no hookups. Elev ~7,700 ft, open May-Sept. Reservable online; some sites FCFS. --Claude

name: Bridge Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit 2466e2c (Add 10 more CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Durango/Vallecito/Pagosa/Rico)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USFS campground in a flat open meadow beside Williams Creek, ~18 mi NW of Pagosa Springs via Piedra Road (FR 631, gravel, suitable for most vehicles). 19 large sites, all FCFS — must arrive in person to claim a site; drinking water, vault toilets, no hookups. Open Memorial Day through September. FCFS, not reservable online. --Claude

name: Transfer Campground
note: USFS campground in a level aspen grove ~11 mi N of Mancos via West Mancos Road (FR 561), elev ~8,500 ft, above the West Mancos River canyon. 12 individual sites (5 reservable, rest FCFS) plus a group site and 4 horse sites across FR 561; most accommodate RVs, all sites barrier-free; vault toilets, no hookups. Reservable online; some sites FCFS. --Claude

name: Cayton Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 2466e2c (Add 10 more CO San Juan NF campgrounds (Durango/Vallecito/Pagosa/Rico)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USFS campground along the Dolores River, ~6 mi NE of Rico on Hwy 145 (San Juan Skyway, paved access) then 0.5 mi on Barlow Creek Rd (FR 578). 27 sites in two loops; riverside Loop A has 30-amp electric sites (some with water/sewer), Loop B is non-electric and set back; max RV ~50 ft, pull-throughs available; vault toilets, potable water, dump station (fee). Elev ~9,400 ft, open mid-May through September. Reservable online; some sites FCFS. --Claude

name: Snowblind Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'creekside' assessed in commit dded2df (Add 9 more CO USFS campgrounds (Gunnison/Routt/White River/Arapaho)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: 23-site FCFS Forest Service campground on Tomichi Creek off US-50 via FR 888 (Gunnison Ranger District), ~9,300 ft, open roughly late May to early Oct. Vault toilets, hand-pump non-potable water, no hookups; about $14/night. Access is ~6 mi paved plus ~2 mi dirt and is RV-passable (no 4WD); max RV length ~30 ft with a few longer sites, so a 23-ft rig fits easily. --Claude

name: Ophir Creek Campground
note: 31-site San Carlos Ranger District campground along Ophir Creek on FSR 361 west of Wetmore; sites 1-19 take trailers/RVs while 20-31 are tent-only. No hookups, vault toilets, drinking water available, ~8,800 ft. Reservable on recreation.gov (~$23/night), typically full service Memorial Day through Labor Day with reduced service into mid-Oct. --Claude

name: Dumont Lake Campground
note: 22 sites (~15-17 reservable online + 6 FCFS) at ~9,400 ft, ~1 mile up FR 315 off US-40/Rabbit Ears Pass. Mix of pull-through and back-in; rec.gov driveways run 50-70 ft so a 23-ft rig fits easily (Allstays lists 40-ft max RV). Vault toilets, no potable water, no hookups. Late June through Oct 1. Campground sits back across the alpine meadow from 50-acre Dumont Lake (lake is at the separate day-use/picnic area). --Claude

name: Meadows Campground
note: 30 sites at 9,300 ft on Rabbit Ears Pass off US-40; pull-through, back-in, and some walk-in tent sites. Max RV 40 ft (about a third take large rigs; shorter motorhomes fine, some tight interior turns). Vault toilets, no potable water, no hookups. Reservable online; $26/site, cash or check only. Late June to mid-October. --Claude

name: Bear Lake Campground
note: Routt NF (Yampa RD) campground 11-14 mi SW of Yampa via CR 7/FR 900, ~9,700 ft. 43 sites (incl. 3 doubles, 1 triple), trailers up to 30 ft, vault toilets, potable water mid-June to early/mid-Oct. FCFS, no reservations; $20 single / $30 double / $40 triple, cash or check only. Last several miles are rough washboard dirt but passable by a normal vehicle. Bear Lake (30 ac) and Yamcolo Reservoir are a short walk outside the entrance; no campsites are on the shoreline. --Claude

name: Teal Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit dded2df (Add 9 more CO USFS campgrounds (Gunnison/Routt/White River/Arapaho)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: 17 individual FCFS sites (sites 3 & 9 reservable online; rest first-come, first-served) plus a separate tent/car group site, at ~9,000 ft in Routt NF's North Park / Mount Zirkel area on 50-acre Teal Lake. Vault toilets, potable water, $20/night cash or check; season early June to mid/late October, weather dependent. Reviews report best for rigs up to ~25 ft (a 23-ft rig fits); spaces aren't large but workable. Access via Hwy 14 to Hebron, CR 24 west to FR 60, then FR 615 ~3 mi (RV-passable improved gravel); do NOT use the Buffalo Road alternate, which is rough/4WD, and GPS/map apps misroute here. --Claude

name: McClure Campground
note: GMUG (Grand Mesa, Uncompahgre & Gunnison NF), Paonia RD; on CO-133 just south of McClure Pass at ~8,200 ft. ~8-10 forested sites, ~35-ft average spurs plus 2 pull-throughs for larger rigs; vault toilets, no drinking water, no hookups. $14/night, FCFS - not on recreation.gov. Paved highway access. --Claude

name: Sunset Point Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit dded2df (Add 9 more CO USFS campgrounds (Gunnison/Routt/White River/Arapaho)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: 25 non-electric sites on a point in Lake Granby (Arapaho NRA), elev ~8,300 ft; max vehicle length 46 ft, sites 12-24 are the longer ones. Potable water, vault toilets, trash service, boat ramp; no hookups. First-come, first-served (not reservable on recreation.gov). $33/night single. --Claude

name: Tom Bennett Campground
note: Small primitive 10-site campground near Pingree Park along the South Fork Cache la Poudre in lodgepole pine, elev ~9,000 ft; reached via well-maintained gravel Pingree Park Rd (CR 63E), passable by passenger cars. Older facility geared to smaller rigs (parking spurs 15-25 ft) so only the longer spurs fit a 23-ft rig; no potable water, vault toilets, $19/night. First-come, first-served (not reservable on recreation.gov). --Claude

name: Salida East Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit e59012c (Add 4 CO non-Forest-Service federal campgrounds (BLM/USFWS)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: BLM land in the Arkansas Headwaters Recreation Area, operated by Colorado Parks & Wildlife. ~29 drive-in basic sites (+4 walk-in, 1 yurt) on the south bank of the Arkansas River; vault toilets, no hookups, no potable water (pack in). Renovated ~2023 with raised gravel pads. Reservation-only via CPW/ReserveAmerica; year-round, reservations Apr-Oct. Site fee plus AHRA daily parks pass required. Adjacent to US-50 (highway noise); RVs to ~44 ft reported. --Claude

name: Swinging Bridge Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit e59012c (Add 4 CO non-Forest-Service federal campgrounds (BLM/USFWS)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Browns Park National Wildlife Refuge (USFWS, not BLM). 7 primitive drive-in sites at the historic Swinging Bridge over the Green River; free, FCFS, no reservations, 14-day limit per 28 days. Fire rings, pit toilets, gravel boat ramp, livestock corral; no hookups, no potable water, pack out trash. Year-round. Drive-in via long gravel Browns Park Rd (passable by normal vehicle); access crosses the single-lane historic Swinging Bridge (20-ton limit, narrow). --Claude

name: Crook Campground
note: Browns Park National Wildlife Refuge (USFWS, not BLM). 10 primitive drive-in sites under cottonwoods off Wildlife Drive; free, FCFS, no reservations, 14-day limit per 28 days. Pit toilets, fire rings, gravel boat ramp; no hookups, no potable water, pack out trash. Year-round. Drive-in via long gravel Browns Park Rd (passable by normal vehicle). Aggregators list 'small RVs only / ~20 ft' (unverified by FWS); a 23-ft rig should fit the shaded pull-through sites - confirm the specific site on arrival. --Claude

name: Cochetopa Creek Rec Site
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'creekside' assessed in commit e59012c (Add 4 CO non-Forest-Service federal campgrounds (BLM/USFWS)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: BLM Gunnison Field Office developed/dispersed sites along CO-114 near mile marker 113; ~10-15 dirt pull-offs over a ~4-mi stretch on both sides of the highway along Cochetopa Creek. Free, FCFS, no reservations, 14-day limit. Vault toilets, no water, no hookups; fits rigs to ~30 ft. High elevation (~8,700 ft), practically a summer/fall site. --Claude

name: Arkansas River Rim Campground & RV Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit 1ca1bea (Add 60 CO private campgrounds (commercial RV parks + 1 Hipcamp)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Riverside-bluff RV park ~4.5 mi north of Buena Vista, overlooking the Arkansas River with Collegiate Peaks views and walk-down river access (fishing/gold panning). ~46 sites; large level full-hookup 20/30/50-amp sites, many pull-through (big-rig friendly). Also camper cabins and a tent area. Year-round. Main drawback is US-24 highway noise. RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Aspen Ridge RV Park
note: 43 full-hookup sites, all upgraded to 30/50-amp water/sewer; mix of pull-through and back-in, big-rig friendly. Open all year. On South Fork's Silver Spur ATV trail (ride out of the park); walkable to Gold Medal Rio Grande fishing. Clubhouse w/ showers, laundry, kitchen; fish-cleaning station, dog run, WiFi. RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: Blue Spruce RV Park & Cabins
note: Full-service RV park at ~7,800 ft on CR 500 N of Bayfield, on the road up to Vallecito Reservoir (~25 mi from Durango). ~66-80 RV sites plus cabins; full hookups (30/50 amp), back-in and pull-through, max length ~60 ft. Heated pool, hot tub, laundry, store, game room. Primarily transient (some monthly returnees); open seasonally ~April-Oct. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Burlington RV Park
note: No-frills I-70 overnight stop at 630 Old US Hwy 24, Burlington (far E plains). Level full-hookup pull-throughs (30/50 amp, water, sewer, cable, free WiFi); easy in/out for big rigs. No bathhouse/showers (hence low price). Geared to overnight travelers; open year-round. RV Life 4 stars, $$ (~$35/night). --Claude

name: Campland RV Park
note: Small (~10-11 site) private RV park on US Hwy 24 in Burlington, ~2 min off I-70 — a convenient eastern-CO plains overnight stop. Full-hookup level sites (30/50 amp, water, sewer), mostly long pull-throughs; big-rig friendly (reviewers parked 34-37 ft, so a 23-ft rig fits easily). Year-round. Cash-only (pay by envelope), ~$30-35/night; on-site host. A few sites occupied by monthly/long-term tenants but it operates as a transient overnight park. No official website. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Castle Lakes Campground and Cabins
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 1ca1bea (Add 60 CO private campgrounds (commercial RV parks + 1 Hipcamp)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Private campground at 9,200 ft, 9 mi south of Lake City on Cinnamon Pass Rd (CR-30), last ~4 mi graded gravel county road -- passable for a 23-ft rig (the 4WD-only Alpine Loop is further up-pass). ~45 RV sites, 39 full hookup + 6 water/electric, 50-amp, 20 sites fit 45 ft+, ~30 wide enough for slideouts. Two private stocked fishing lakes (no state license, day-pass fee), general store, showers, laundry. Open ~late May to early Oct (seasonal). RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: Cat Creek RV Park & Event Center
note: Small RV park ~2 miles from downtown Springfield, adjacent to US-287/385 (some truck and train noise). Open since 2021. 18 back-in + 2 pull-through gravel sites, all minimum 30x60 ft with 30/50-amp electric and sewer; on-site event space and coin laundry. From ~$39/night. No standalone official website (book by phone; active Facebook page). RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Cañon Bonito RV & Fisherman's Club
note: 14 RV sites, full hookups (50/30/20-amp), big-rig friendly w/ pull-throughs (rigs to ~50 ft), dump station, free WiFi, hot showers, laundry. Open to all (not a members-only club despite the name) -- reserve by phone, owner prefers phone over online. Private 1.5-mi catch-and-release fly-fishing-only stretch of the Conejos River for guests. Open Apr 1-Nov 17; near Cumbres & Toltec RR / Rio Grande NF. RV Life 5★, $$. --Claude

name: Centennial RV Park
note: Full-hookup RV park 10 mi south of Montrose on Hwy 550 with San Juan Mountain views. 60 large grassy sites, all pull-through full hookup (20/30/50-amp), wide alternating-direction sites with grass/trees between; spotless restrooms, on-site propane, laundry. Not adjacent to any river or lake. Highly rated transient park (RV Life ~9.2/10, 4.5★), $$. --Claude

name: Conejos River Campground
note: 56 pull-through sites in Conejos Canyon, ~12 mi W of Antonito on CO-17, bordered by Rio Grande NF at ~8,400 ft. Full hookups (30/50 amp); accepts big rigs / max length ~60 ft. Showers, laundry, dump, playground. Open mid-May to Oct 31. The Conejos River (premier trout fishery) is ~100 yards behind the campground with full river access, but RV sites are not on the water. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Cottonwood Cove Guest Ranch & RV Park
note: Historic guest ranch (operating since 1944) with a real RV park: ~21–36 full-hookup sites (8 are 50-amp, 15 pull-through; one back-in <25 ft). Nightly transient bookings; season ~May–early Oct. On Hwy 149 between South Fork and Creede; on-site restaurant, gift shop, jeep/horse rentals, Gold Medal Rio Grande fishing. RV sites front cliffs/pasture, not the river (riverbank is the cabin/lodge side). RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: Cripple Creek Hospitality House & RV Park
note: 58 full-hookup RV sites (50-amp) on 18 acres at a restored 1901 hospital (National Register) at 600 N B St, <1 mi from historic Cripple Creek's casino district at ~9,500 ft. Hotel rooms also on site. Transient daily plus seasonal/extended sites; main RV season ~May-Oct. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$ (~$52-63/night). --Claude

name: Dark Sky River Lodge
note: Former Rocky Top River Ranch, rebranded Dark Sky River Lodge under new ownership in 2024; reopened year-round. ~18 RV sites (16 w/ hookups), full hookups (30/50 amp, water, sewer), back-in sites bookable nightly via Guesty; big-rig friendly, off paved US-50 on the Arkansas River. Cabins, motel rooms, cafe, general store, community fire pit on site. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Drake Campground
note: ~20 sites total (~12 with 30-amp electric; some full hookups, others dry/primitive), riverside tent + RV camping at the confluence of the Big Thompson River and its North Fork in the Big Thompson Canyon on US-34, gateway to Estes Park / RMNP. Drive-in gravel sites; dump station on site; pet-friendly; fits a 23-ft rig easily (a reviewer fit a 45' toy hauler). Open year-round (peak summer/fall). Reservations by phone only -- no online booking; reviewers note quoted price can differ on arrival. RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: Echo Canyon Campground and RV Park
note: Full-service campground ~8 mi W of Canon City in Royal Gorge Country at 45044 W US Hwy 50. 47 sites (46 RV pull-throughs); electric (20/30/50 amp) and water, extra-large sites fit motorcoaches. On-site rafting outfitter, store, cabins/glamping. Set up Echo Canyon away from the Arkansas River (not riverside). RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Elk Valley RV Park and Fly Shop
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit 1ca1bea (Add 60 CO private campgrounds (commercial RV parks + 1 Hipcamp)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Small private park (~23-24 sites) on Hwy 12 across from Grandote Peaks Golf Course at ~7,300 ft, with an on-site fly shop. All full hookups, gravel, 4 pull-throughs, big rigs to 45 ft. Open Apr 1-Oct 31 (seasonal). RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Elkhorn RV Resort
note: 23 full-hookup RV sites (water/sewer, 30 & 50-amp) in three size classes, pull-throughs fitting rigs to ~60 ft; also cabins/cottages. Walking distance to downtown Lake City, near the Alpine Loop. Seasonal (roughly June 1–Oct 1); nightly bookable via Campspot, 2-night minimum in peak. RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Five Branches Camper Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 1ca1bea (Add 60 CO private campgrounds (commercial RV parks + 1 Hipcamp)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Family-owned private park (since 1995) on the south end of Vallecito Lake where the Pine/Los Pinos River enters, ~15 mi N of Bayfield. 90+ RV sites plus tent sites, cabins and 9 park models; full hookups, 30-amp only (no 50-amp), no concrete pads and no pull-throughs (back-in 'wilderness' sites). Max RV length ~40 ft. Riverside Drive sites along the Pine River; Lakefront Drive sites face Vallecito Lake but sit behind a drawdown flat at low water. Seasonal: ~mid-May to early September (2026 season 5/16-9/8). 4-night minimum stay. Nightly transient camping, reservable online. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Floyd's RV Park
note: 40 full-hookup pull-through sites (water/electric/sewer), 36 sites 45 ft+ for large rigs; soft city water, laundry, group pavilion; convenience store/gas next door. Open all year; daily and monthly rates. On Hwy 50 in Penrose between Pueblo and Cañon City, ~15 min to Pueblo Reservoir/Royal Gorge. RV Life 5★, $$. --Claude

name: Four Seasons River Inn & RV Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 1ca1bea (Add 60 CO private campgrounds (commercial RV parks + 1 Hipcamp)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: 30 RV sites (17 back-in, 9 pull-through) plus 4 tent sites, all full hookups with 20/30/50-amp. 1,285 ft of Gunnison River frontage with a boat ramp; laundry, showers, dump station, propane, small store. Year-round, nightly bookable online. Riverside sites are popular and sell out. RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Gears RV Park
note: ~20 full-hookup sites (30/50-amp, water, sewer), mostly pull-through and big-rig friendly, max length ~44 ft; year-round. 45-day max stay, reservations accepted; a few long-term residents but operates as an overnight/way-stop park. On-site Exit 30 BBQ & Cafe and convenience store at I-25 Exit 30. Official domain gearsrvpark.com is dead (parked redirect) -- use the Facebook page or phone. RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: Golden Bell RV Park (Camp & Conference Center)
note: Church-run (Nazarene) camp at base of Pikes Peak, ~9,180 ft, but operates a genuine public RV park bookable nightly online. ~30 sites, 2 pull-through. Full hookup, partial water+electric, and tent options. Open year-round. No smoking/alcohol (church camp rules). Heated indoor pool, disc golf, fishing. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Greeley RV Park & Campground
note: Year-round private park on the SE edge of Greeley, 179 sites across two lots split by the highway (501 E 27th St / 810 E 28th St). Full-hookup 60-ft pull-throughs, 30/50-amp, big-rig friendly; max ~45 ft. Laundry, showers, dog runs, propane refill, WiFi. Predominantly a long-term RV community but takes weekly and nightly transient stays (~$36/night). RV Life 4 stars, $$. In-town plains location, no water on site. --Claude

name: Gypsy HideAway RV Park
note: Small in-town RV park in Stratton just off I-70 (exit 419), an easy overnight stop on the eastern plains. ~40 sites with 30/50-amp full hookups; pull-throughs for large rigs; laundry, showers, free wifi. No staffed office - call to reserve. No working official website, but consistently well-reviewed and currently operating. RV Life ~4 stars (8.4/10), $$. --Claude

name: Haggard's RV Campground
note: Transient RV park on US-50 between Pueblo and Canon City (~mile marker 298). ~50 sites, 40 full-hookup (water/electric/sewer) with 30/50-amp, pull-throughs available; some no-hookup sites. Seasonal pool (Memorial Day-Labor Day), gameroom, playground, mountain views. On-site well water is high in iron/minerals - bring a filter and bottled drinking water. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: High Country RV Park
note: In-town full-service RV park at 218 W Main St, Naturita; 37 full-hookup sites (25 pull-through), 30/50 amp, water/laundry/showers/dump. Open year-round; described as the only full-service RV park for miles. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Highlander RV Campground
note: Family-run (est. 1993) at 1245 County Rd 30, Lake City, in the San Juans on a bench above the Lake Fork of the Gunnison. 34 level RV sites (7 dual RV/tent), full hookups 20/30/50 amp, water/sewer each site; pavilion, laundry, showers. Seasonal ~June 1-Oct 1. On-site Jeep rental. Nightly ~$48-$50. RV Life 5 stars, $$. Sites overlook the river valley but are set back from the water. --Claude

name: Indian Springs Ranch & Campground
note: Family-run RV park outside Penrose at the base of Copper Mountain, near Beaver Creek State Wildlife Area (not on the creek). ~27-30 RV sites, 30-amp full hookups (water/electric/sewer), 7 pull-through. Max RV length 33 ft; no Class A, diesel pushers, or toy haulers (a 23-ft rig fits fine). Year-round; weekly/monthly rates offered but operates as a transient park. Swimming pool, showers, group pavilion; a registered National Natural Landmark for fossils. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: La Mesa RV Park
note: Small in-town RV park (under 40 sites) on E. Main St in Cortez, ~10 mi from Mesa Verde NP and near Four Corners/Hovenweep. Full-hookup, big-rig-friendly 20/30/50-amp sites, back-in and pull-through. Free WiFi, dump station, hot showers, laundry; EV/Tesla charging on request. Year-round; nightly/weekly/monthly. Harvest Hosts member. RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Main Street Manor RV Park
note: 18 RV sites (no tents), full hookups with 30/50-amp, pull-throughs for big rigs, max ~45 ft. Year-round (winterized), nightly/weekly/monthly, bookable online via Campspot. Near historic downtown Florence; mix of transient and some long-term residents. RV Life 5★, $$. --Claude

name: Moon Dance RV Park
note: Off I-70 exit 28 near Grand Junction at 774 23 Rd. 27 concrete-pad RV sites (mostly pull-through, 30/50 amp full hookups) plus a separate tent area; 13 luxury sites fit 45+ ft with patios. Big-rig friendly. Laundry/shower comfort station. Views of Colorado National Monument/Grand Mesa/Bookcliffs. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Mother Lode RV Park
note: 16 full-hookup pull-through sites (water/electric/50-amp sewer), all rated 45 ft+ — a 23-ft rig fits easily. Access via paved CR 76 (the rough 4WD road starts past Pitkin, miles beyond). Seasonal, ~May 1-Nov 30 (winter by arrangement). Elev ~8,560 ft in the historic Quartz Creek mining valley, 20 mi NE of Gunnison near Pitkin; on-site ghost town, ATV/Jeep access to the Alpine Tunnel, creek fishing nearby. No standalone official website; book by phone. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Mountain Valley Meadows RV Park
note: On CO-133 (West Elk Loop) in the North Fork Valley at 35145 CO-133, Hotchkiss. Full hookup (30/50 amp) and partial-hookup sites; big-rig accessible, grassy sites with mature trees. Shower house, laundry, dump station, propane. Open year-round. Near Paonia wine country. Nightly $30-$45. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Observer Ranch Campground & Learning Center
note: 22 full-hookup RV sites (20/30/50 amp, level concrete pads, ~1/10 acre, fire pit). Year-round; book nightly online via Newbook. New 2026 commercial campground with an on-site astronomy/nature learning center; also planet-themed camper + cabin rentals (not RV-relevant). Eightmile Creek crosses the property but RV pads sit ~130 m back across CR 123 -- not waterfront. RV Life 5★, $$. --Claude

name: Outback RV
note: 8 level gravel pull-through sites, all full hookup (W/E/S, 30/50/110-amp); big-rig friendly at 45'x75' (max ~75 ft). No restrooms/showers; WiFi. Open Apr-Oct; owner-operated. Near BLM lands and Phil's World mountain-bike trails, ~5 mi from Cortez and a gateway to Mesa Verde NP; dark-sky stargazing. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Pleasant Valley Cabins & Campground
note: 10 RV sites: 8 full-hookup (30-amp) + 2 water/electric; all back-in, level gravel pads with fire pit & picnic table each -- no pull-throughs or big-rig sites (a 24-ft rig fits per a firsthand review). General store, cafe, showers, laundry, free WiFi; also tent sites & cabins. Seasonal: open daily ~May 1 to just before Thanksgiving. Family-run on US-50 near Black Canyon NP and Curecanti/Blue Mesa; gold-medal fishing on the Little Cimarron River; some highway road noise. Reservable online (RoverPass) or by phone. RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Rancho Del Rio
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit 1ca1bea (Add 60 CO private campgrounds (commercial RV parks + 1 Hipcamp)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Private rafting outpost on the Upper Colorado River; ~20 acres of riverside camping with ~12 RV sites + tent sites + cabins. Dry camping only -- no hookups, no dump station, potable water for limited use (not RV fill). Max RV length ~35 ft (23-ft rig fits easily); reviewers note level, spacious RV spots. Boat ramp/raft put-in, gear rental, store, restaurant, liquor store on site. Access via Trough Road (CR1), a maintained gravel scenic byway -- passable by 2WD when dry (rutted/slick after heavy rain). No cell service; 2-night weekend minimum. Reservable online. RV Life 5★, $. --Claude

name: Red Mountain RV Park
note: In-town Kremmling at 2201 Central Ave (~1 mi W of US-40/SR-9 junction). Family-owned, ~65 sites incl. ~45 pull-through full-hookup (30/50 amp), accommodates rigs to 45 ft. Restrooms/hot showers, laundry, dump, playground, horseshoes. Open year-round; takes nightly/weekly/extended stays. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: River Fork RV Park and Campground
note: 26 sites with 30/50-amp full hookups (water/sewer), free WiFi, laundry, showers; mix of pull-through and back-in, RVs to 44 ft. Seasonal (roughly June 1–Oct 1), 8,661 ft in downtown Lake City near the Lake Fork of the Gunnison. Also tent sites, a bunkhouse, and a cabin. Reservable online. RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: San Luis Valley Campground
note: Family-run private RV park on US-285 south of Villa Grove, ~7,950 ft. 15 full-hookup pull-through sites, 30/50 amp; fits rigs to ~27 ft. Showers, laundry, free WiFi, dump station, dog run. Open approx mid-May to mid-November (closed winter). FCFS -- call to reserve, no online booking. RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Santa Fe RV Park and Horse Motel
note: Now operating as Lamar Sportsman RV Park (same site, 5385 US-50, 3 mi west of Lamar on the north side). General transient RV park that also offers horse stalls -- not equestrian-only; takes motorhomes/trailers/tents. Pull-through sites, full hookups (30/50-amp, water, sewer), swimming pool, dump station, showers, laundry; ~10 acres, year-round, paved access. Mixed-use: some long-term/full-time residents alongside nightly/weekly travelers. RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Shady Creek RV Park
note: Small family-run in-town RV park (~15-21 level sites) on N Grand Mesa Dr in Cedaredge, at the base of the Grand Mesa (~20 min to the top). 30/50-amp full-hookup sites, big-rig friendly (fits 20-50 ft rigs plus a towed vehicle), WiFi and laundry; gravel sites, lots of mature shade trees. Year-round, nightly (~$32-55) with monthly available; walkable to downtown restaurants and shops. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Shady Grove Campground
note: 24 full-hookup pull-through sites (30/50-amp, water, sewer), some over 100 ft — big-rig friendly. Year-round (winterized). Just off I-70 exit 405 in Seibert; marketed as a clean overnight alternative to truck stops. Private bathhouse (4 individual restroom/shower suites), commercial laundry, fenced dog park, WiFi. RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Silver Sage RV Park
note: Big-rig-friendly in-town park (~57 gravel sites, 90-ft pads) with full hookups 20/30/50-amp, pull-through and back-in; restrooms but no showers/laundry; free WiFi, pet-friendly. Walkable to downtown; near Dinosaur NM, Canyon Pintado, BLM OHV trails. Reservable online (nightly ~$38-43). Mixed transient/long-term park in an oil town (about half long-term/pipeline workers) but openly books overnight travelers. RV Life 4.4★, $$. --Claude

name: Silver Thread Basecamp
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 1ca1bea (Add 60 CO private campgrounds (commercial RV parks + 1 Hipcamp)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: ~48 full-hookup RV sites (30/50A) plus tent sites and glamping covered wagons, on the Rio Grande Gold Medal trout water 4 mi E of South Fork; pull-throughs, big-rig friendly (sites to 65 ft), hot showers, laundry, playground, free WiFi. 11 designated riverfront FHU/50A sites. Seasonal, ~May 1-Oct 31 (elev ~8,100 ft). Reservable online; walk-ups taken, 2-night minimum. RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Sky Ute Casino RV Park
note: 24 paved full-hookup sites (3 ADA), 20/30/50-amp, all pull-through and big-rig friendly, adjacent to the casino. Guests get casino-resort amenities: pool, fitness center, bowling, arcade, showers, laundry, WiFi. Open ~May 1 to mid-November. Restriction: RVs must be ≤15 years old; no pop-up/tent campers. RV Life 5★, $$. --Claude

name: Sleeping Ute RV Park
note: Fully developed RV park next to Ute Mountain Casino Hotel (not a casino overnight lot), about 20 min from Mesa Verde NP. All full-hookup pull-through sites with 30/50-amp; amenities include indoor pool, wading pool, sauna, laundry, showers, dump station, store with RV supplies, cable/WiFi. Office 7am-10pm (after-hours check-in at the casino hotel). Full hookups from ~$35/night. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Springfield RV Park
note: 10 full-hookup gravel sites (30/50-amp, water, sewer); big-rig accessible; pets OK; no restrooms/showers/wifi. In-town private park in SE Colorado near the KS/OK line; nightly, weekly, and monthly rates, reservations accepted. Aggregators flag a few full-time residents, but it operates as a transient overnight stop. Official Wix site appears dead/unpublished -- reserve by phone. RV Life 5★, $$. --Claude

name: Starlite Vintage Resort
note: Retro 1960s-themed resort near the Royal Gorge with its own RV/van/tent sites (not just vintage-trailer rentals): 30-amp water-and-electric back-in sites plus full-hookup options, dump station, heated pool, upgraded bathhouses; walking distance to River Runners rafting. Year-round. Bookable via Campspot. RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: Sundance High Plains RV Park
note: In-town highway stopover on US-287 at the south edge of Lamar. ~22 pull-through full-hookup sites (30/50-amp, water, sewer) plus a few deluxe pull-throughs with patios/fire pits; cabins and tent sites also available. Gravel sites ~50 ft long x 18 ft wide; big-rig friendly (rigs to 50 ft). Free WiFi, hot showers. Year-round; nightly/weekly/monthly. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: The Campers Hub
note: New park with spacious ~70-ft full-hookup pull-through sites (20/30/50-amp, water, sewer), fiber internet, clean restrooms, camp store, dog park; 14-day max stay (short-term only). ~17 sites open now, expanding toward 135 as build-out continues. On US-550 near Black Canyon of the Gunnison NP. RV Life 5★, $$. --Claude

name: Trailing Edge Park
note: 37 large powered sites with 20/30/50-amp electric, water, sewer, trash, WiFi; heated water lines for year-round operation; long pull-through-style spaces (~100 ft) handle big rigs. No on-site office (self-service online check-in); no restrooms/showers on site per reviews. Daily/weekly/monthly; no-frills lot near I-70 exit 361 in Limon. RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: Travel Port Campground
note: Small full-hookup RV/tent campground on US-24 at Lake George, ~20 wooded sites, 30/50 amp water/sewer, pull-through, big-rig friendly. Near Eleven Mile and Spinney Mtn reservoirs (good fishing base). RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Twin Rivers Cabins and RV Park
note: Private RV park 5 mi west of Antonito on CO Hwy 17 at the base of Conejos Canyon, ~8,000 ft, in a grove of old cottonwoods. 39 full-hookup RV sites, most 30/50 amp, both back-in and pull-through (14 pull-throughs), sites accommodate large rigs incl. 40+ ft with slideouts; also 8 cabins and tent sites. Nightly rates ~$39-49 (monthly available). Year-round. Camp store, propane, dump station, dog park, playground. A small branch of the Conejos River runs through the property (trout fishing); near the Cumbres & Toltec railroad. RV Life 4 stars, $$ (~22 reviews). --Claude

name: Ute Bluff Lodge, Cabins & RV Park
note: Year-round private park at 27680 US Hwy 160 in South Fork on 17 acres adjacent to the Rio Grande NF and Rio Grande River; ~minutes from Wolf Creek ski area. 30 full-hookup RV sites, big-rig friendly: 8 50-amp pull-throughs, 11 50-amp back-ins, 11 30-amp back-ins. Laundry, dump station, fish-cleaning station; also lodge rooms and cabins. River access is across the highway, not riverside. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Valley Sunset RV Ranch
note: Year-round private RV park just north of Hwy 92, ~1.25 mi east of US 50/92 junction in Delta (adjacent to Safeway). ~66 sites, full hookups, 30/50 amp, ~25 pull-throughs, big-rig friendly (long sites 45+ ft) so a 23-ft rig fits easily. Pool, hot tub, game room, laundry, leash-free dog park; takes both overnight/transient and monthly stays. Near the Gunnison/Uncompahgre confluence but not on the water (Confluence Park ~2 mi). RV Life 4.5 star, $$ (~$42/night). --Claude

name: Wolford Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 1ca1bea (Add 60 CO private campgrounds (commercial RV parks + 1 Hipcamp)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: ~75 sites in 4 loops: A/B/C electric (30/50-amp), D non-electric; central water + dump station, no full hookups at site. Big-rig friendly (40-43 ft rigs reported, some pull-throughs). Reservable online; 2-night min weekends, 3-night holidays. Open year-round; Dec 1-Apr 30 limited to Loop A, FCFS, cash/check only. Boat ramp, marina, swim beach on Wolford Mountain Reservoir. Private concessionaire on Colorado River Water Conservation District land. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Woodlake Park
note: ~50 full-hookup RV sites (30/50 amp), 18 pull-throughs; 34 luxury sites 45+ ft with room for slideouts, accepts rigs to 60 ft, so a 23-ft rig fits easily. Also cabins, bunkhouses, tent areas; shower house, laundry, WiFi. Family-run since 1978, 2.5 mi south of Lake City on Hwy 149 at the base of Slumgullion Pass; near Lake Fork of the Gunnison. Seasonal (roughly June-Sept). Reservable online. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Wray Hitch'n Post RV Park & Campground
note: Family-run RV park just south of Wray on US-385 (~1 mile south of the US-34/385 junction, east side of 385). Year-round, transient and long-term. 12 large pull-through full-hookup sites (water/electric/sewer) plus back-in sites with electric or water+electric; covered picnic tables, restrooms, dump station, 24/7 coin laundry, fiber WiFi. Online booking via Firefly. Near Stalker Lake and Sandsage Wildlife Area (not on the water). RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Sage View Ranch
note: Small 11.5-acre agritourism ranch ~1 mi from Paonia (also a wedding/glamping venue). 3 bookable drive-in RV sites with electric (no water/sewer at site; potable water in sunroom); Joy and Bliss sites fit rigs under 35 ft, so a 23-ft Class C fits. Hwy 133 noise on some sites; no pets. RV Life 5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Bear Creek Lake Park Campground (Indian Paintbrush)
note: City of Lakewood park campground (Indian Paintbrush) in Bear Creek Lake Park, ~15 mi from downtown Denver. 47 sites with 30/50-amp electric, water fill stations (no individual water/sewer), dump station, restrooms, coin showers; big-rig friendly, open layout. Season Apr-Dec, reservable through Lakewood. Campground sits north of and set back from Bear Creek Lake. RV Life 4.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Boulder County Fairgrounds Campground
note: Boulder County in-town fairgrounds campground in Longmont, open year-round to general travelers (not event-only). 16 RV sites with full water/electric/sewer hookups (plus additional electric/water spaces). FCFS, no reservations; $35/night, dump $15, 14-day max per calendar year. Restroom/showers Mar-Oct. RV Life 4 stars, price $$. --Claude

name: Brush Memorial Park Campground
note: City of Brush in-town park campground near 500 S Clayton St; 18 gravel sites, 12 with electric+water hookups, restrooms/showers and a dump station. Max rig ~30 ft (fits a 23-ft rig). $25/night at a self-pay kiosk, FCFS (no reservations; 7-day limit, longer by City Hall approval). RV Life 4 star / $. --Claude

name: Campo City Park Campground
note: Free city park RV stop in Campo (6th St); ~3 RV sites with water and toilets, no hookups, open year-round, FCFS. Realistically fits only 2-3 rigs; low shade trees so watch overhead clearance for a tall rig. RV Life 4.5 star / free. --Claude

name: Carter Lake - Eagle Campground
note: Larimer County-operated, on the north end of Carter Lake west of Loveland. Eagle loop has ~19 standard 50-amp electric sites (no full hookups); site lengths 36-70 ft, big-rig friendly; shower house. Open year-round (off-season dump station closed). Reservable online. Eagle sites set back from the lake. RV Life 2.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Carter Lake South Shore Campground
note: Larimer County Natural Resources campground on the south end of Carter Lake reservoir. Drive-in electric RV sites S1-S12 in an interior loop plus a non-electric loop (S31-S51); the shoreline sites (S17-S23) are walk-in tent only per the county site map, so RV pads sit back from the water. Sites fit large RVs (county lists up to ~40 ft). Reservable online (also same-day reservations). RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Chief Hosa Campground
note: Denver Mountain Parks campground in Genesee Park, 20 min west of Denver off I-70 exit 253. 48 sites: 24 RV sites with water+power (2 full-hookup) plus 24 tent sites; dump station, coin showers, WiFi. Open May through late September. Reservation required (online only). RV Life 4.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Clear Creek RV Park
note: City of Golden municipal RV park along Clear Creek at 1401 10th St, walking distance to downtown Golden. Full hookups (electric/water/sewer); accommodates rigs up to ~45 ft. Open year-round with daily reservations plus seasonal extended stays (Nov 1-Mar 31); reservable online. Pricier in-town park ($68-85/night). RV Life 4.5 stars, price $$$. --Claude

name: Del Norte Town Park
note: Town of Del Norte park with ~4-5 free overnight RV/self-contained spots (no tents), 1-night max, FCFS. Paved access, fits rigs up to ~35 ft. Free RV dump station and water fill on site; restrooms, picnic tables. Near the Rio Grande in town. RV Life 4 stars, price $. --Claude

name: Eads Roadside Park
note: Town of Eads roadside park on US-287; ~4 paved/asphalt overnight RV spots, free FCFS (donation box), 1-night max. Fits 23 ft+ rigs (reviewers report 24-ft Class C and fifth-wheels parking fine). Restroom, grassy area, picnic table; no hookups. Adjacent to rail line/highway, so expect noise. RV Life 3 stars, price $. --Claude

name: Elbert County Fairgrounds Campground
note: Elbert County-operated fairgrounds campground in Kiowa (Hwy 86 SE of Denver). ~32 RV sites with 30-amp electric + water and ~30 dry sites; restrooms, showers, laundry, dump station. Open all year for general transient camping (not event-only); 14-day max. $25/night hookup, $15 dry. RV Life 3.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Flatiron Reservoir
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 55d14b2 (Add 32 CO local-government campgrounds (county/city/regional)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Larimer County (local government) park, NOT private. ~34 electric sites (F1-F34) plus 3 tipis, ~5,470 ft. Electric only; no on-site water/sewer (frost-free spigots; dump station at Carter Lake S entrance). No swimming/boating; shore/pier fishing. Reservable online or 800-397-7795. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Gateway RV Park (Carbondale)
note: Town of Carbondale Recreation Dept park (formerly private, taken over by the town) along the Roaring Fork River. 18 RV sites with 50/30/20-amp service, fire rings, picnic tables, free WiFi. Open seasonally May 1-Oct 1, reservable online via the town's WebReserv portal. RV Life 4.5 stars, price $$$. Riverside but no confirmed RV pads at the waterline. --Claude

name: Grant McCormick Memorial RV Park
note: Town of Holly municipal RV park on W. Highway 50, an overnight stop in far eastern CO. 5 sites, all full hookups (electric/water/sewer); $20/night. Restrooms with showers, picnic tables, shade trees, enclosed play yard. Reserve via town clerk's office or FCFS. RV Life 3.5 stars, price $$. --Claude

name: Hermit Park Open Space
note: Larimer County Hermit Park Open Space near Estes Park; ~108 sites across several campgrounds (Hermit's Hollow, Bobcat, Granite Gulch, plus cabins), no hookups. Open March through mid-December; reservable online up to 180 days out (2-night weekend minimum in season). Mountain forest park, not on water. RV Life 4 star / $$$. --Claude

name: Horsetooth Reservoir - South Bay Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 55d14b2 (Add 32 CO local-government campgrounds (county/city/regional)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Larimer County-operated campground at the south end of Horsetooth Reservoir near Fort Collins. ~150 sites: 17 full-hookup (set back on an adjacent loop), ~110 standard electric (30/50-amp) in shoreline clusters, plus boat-in/walk-to sites and cabins. Big-rig friendly (some pull-throughs up to ~70 ft). Showers, dump station, vault toilets; no individual water hookups. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica/larimercamping. Fees ~$25-65. RV Life 4.5 stars, price $$$. --Claude

name: Inlet Bay Campground (Horsetooth Reservoir)
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 55d14b2 (Add 32 CO local-government campgrounds (county/city/regional)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Larimer County campground on the SW shore of Horsetooth Reservoir near Fort Collins; sites strung along the lake edge in two loops with a marina between them. ~115 electric sites plus 17 full-hookup; accommodates large rigs (well over 23 ft). Open ~April-October, reservable online up to 6 months out (14-day max stay), ~$30-45/night plus vehicle fee. RV Life 4 star / $$. --Claude

name: La Plata County Fairgrounds
note: La Plata County Fairgrounds in Durango (2500 Main Ave); 6 back-in gravel RV sites with 30/50-amp electric and shared water, no sewer (dump stations in town), big-rig friendly. Open mid-April through September, $28/night, reservations by phone in advance (availability limited by fairground events). Animas River Trail within walking distance. RV Life 4 star / $$. --Claude

name: LaVern M. Johnson Park
note: Town of Lyons municipal park (formerly Meadow Park) on North St. Vrain Creek at 432 5th Ave. ~15 full-hookup RV sites (year-round) and ~15 50-amp electric sites plus tent sites (Apr-Oct); RV sites up to 45-55 ft, so a 23-ft rig fits easily. Reservable online up to 12 months ahead via Lyons Parks & Rec; summer weekends fill. RV ~$50-60/night. RV Life 5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Marble Campground - Meri Daes
note: Town-run campground in Marble (operator states proceeds fund the community). Small park, RVs 30 ft or less, sites with 15/20/30-amp electric and water, dump station, showers. Reservable online. RV Life 5 star, $$$. --Claude

name: Meeker Town Park
note: Town of Meeker municipal park on the White River in downtown Meeker. RV sites with electric hookups ($30/night), tent sites ($10); a bridge links the RV side to the tent side across the river. FCFS - no reservation system; self-pay permit at the pay station. Sites are small and close together; a 23-ft rig fits. RV Life 4.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Molas Lake Park & Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 55d14b2 (Add 32 CO local-government campgrounds (county/city/regional)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Town-of-Silverton-run high-alpine park at ~10,508 ft on US-550 (Million Dollar Hwy) at a stocked 25-acre lake; 62 sites, no hookups (water fill + $5 showers + general store on site, dump 10 min away in Silverton). 4 'Ultra RV' sites take rigs of any size; a 23-ft rig fits easily. Open ~June 1-Sept 30, reservable online (opens Jan 1); sells out most of summer. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Pinewood Reservoir Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 55d14b2 (Add 32 CO local-government campgrounds (county/city/regional)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Larimer County-operated, on Pinewood Reservoir west of Carter Lake (~6,580 ft). 16 standard drive-in electric sites (lengths 20-55 ft; a 23-ft rig fits, but the access road is narrow with 8-10% grades and 40+ ft motorhomes are discouraged) plus 12 walk-in tent sites; water shut off Sep-Apr. Open year-round, reservable online. RV Life 0 star (unrated) / $. --Claude

name: Rangely Camper Park
note: Town of Rangely municipal camper park at 940 E. Rangely Ave. 26 spaces, some with electric hookups ($20 electric / $15 non-electric), restrooms, showers, dump station; stocked fishing pond on site. 14-day limit. FCFS / first-come basis (no online booking system). RV Life 4 star, $$. --Claude

name: Rifle Mountain Park
note: City of Rifle municipal park (NOT the adjacent Rifle Falls State Park) in a limestone canyon along East Rifle Creek, north of the fish hatchery. Primitive sites along the creek; mix of FCFS and online-reservable via RoverPass, with the 14 Huffman Gulch sites staying FCFS (some car-camping-only). $30/night incl. day pass ($20 with annual pass); pass required. Drive-in gravel canyon road; a 23-ft rig fits the larger drive-in spots. RV Life 0 star (unrated) / $. --Claude

name: Riverdale Regional Park & Fairgrounds
note: Adams County regional park/fairgrounds (operated by the Adams County Agricultural Society) at 9755 Henderson Rd, Brighton - a real reservable RV campground, not event-only: ~396 hookup sites (20 full-service 50/30-amp, 86 water+electric, 290 electric). Big open lot, easily fits a 23-ft rig and larger. Open ~Apr 15-Oct 15; reservable online via Campspot; periodic closures to public camping during fairgrounds events (incl. the Adams County Fair). 3-week RV max stay. RV Life $$. --Claude

name: Riverdale Regional Park & Fairgrounds Campground
note: Adams County regional park & fairgrounds in Brighton with a seasonal campground (open ~Apr 15-Oct 15). Camping by walk-in/appointment reservation, not just fair events. RV-accessible; near the South Platte River. RV Life 3.5 stars, price $. Call to confirm hookups/site length before relying on it. --Claude

name: Routt County Fairgrounds RV Park
note: Routt County-operated fairgrounds RV park in Hayden. 9 full-hookup sites (20/30/50 amp) open year-round, plus 12 primitive electric-only (20 amp) sites open Apr 15-Nov 30; dump station, showers. RVs to 50 ft. 14-day max per 30 days; general transient camping (not event-only), closed to the public only during the August fair. Reservation required (online + ReserveAmerica). RV Life 4.5 star / $$$. --Claude

name: Standley Lake Regional Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 81b6600 (Re-sweep CO reservoir waterfront under the sandy-beach rule (18 fixes)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Westminster regional park on Standley Lake reservoir. 66 reservable campsites (plus 6 deluxe bell tents); only 3 sites have electric (52, 53, ADA 16), rest no-hookup; large open dirt sites that fit RVs/trailers. Season May 1-Sep 30, reservation-only (booking opens each January), basic site ~$35/night, hookup ~$45, includes a daily park pass. Showers, flush restrooms, water fill spigots. RV Life 4 star / $$. The southern 'waterfront loop' (e.g. site 28) overlooks Standley Lake from a low bench set back behind an open grassy/drawdown apron - open lake view, no pads on the shore; interior loops have no water view - lakeview. --Claude

name: Telluride Town Park Campground
note: Town of Telluride municipal campground between the San Miguel River and Bear Creek, walkable to downtown. 28 vehicle campsites (10 fit larger RVs); max unit length 30 ft incl. tow vehicle, so a 23-ft rig fits the larger sites. NO electric hookups or on-site dump (dump at Society Turn). Season ~May 15-Oct 4; online reservation only, festival blackouts; 7 nights max per 30 days. ~$32-55/night. RV Life 4.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Williams Fork Reservoir - East Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 81b6600 (Re-sweep CO reservoir waterfront under the sandy-beach rule (18 fixes)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Owned and operated by Denver Water (Denver's municipal water utility); reservations via Campspot. ~56 designated sites around the reservoir, majority RV/trailer-capable (sites cited 30-50 ft, so a 23-ft rig fits); fire ring, picnic table, vault toilets, boat launch; NO drinking water (haul your own) and no hookups. Season ~Apr 1-Nov 30; reservations required via Campspot, $25/night + fee. RV Life 5 star / $$. East Campground: many designated RV sites sit on the open, exposed reservoir shoreline/beach apron (low tree cover); not all sites are on the water. --Claude

name: Wupperman Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 55d14b2 (Add 32 CO local-government campgrounds (county/city/regional)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Hinsdale County campground on the east shore of Lake San Cristobal, ~4 mi south of Lake City at ~9,000 ft via gravel County Road 33 (narrow with some steep dropoffs; CR30 is a paved/wider alternative). 31 sites, no hookups; max length ~25 ft but lower lakeshore sites reportedly take bigger rigs (EKKO's 23 ft fits). Vault toilets, water, dump station ($20). FCFS, no reservations; $15-20/night. RV Life 4 stars, price $. --Claude

name: Alexandria State Recreation Area

note: NGPC state rec area on sandpit lakes 4 mi E of Alexandria. ~53 sites: 40 w/30-amp, 5 w/50-amp, 8 basic; shaded. Water, modern restrooms, dump station, playground; camp host Apr-Oct. name: Ballards Marsh State Wildlife Area

FCFS, no reservations. State park entry permit required. May be closed during hunting season. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Bassway Strip State Wildlife Management Area
waterfront_evidence: riverview -> not waterfront satellite at 40.6864,-98.9491: primitive Platte-River WMA, only dirt pull-offs/parking by a sandpit lake near I-80; the river is ~300m+ south and no defined RV sites overlook it; riverv
note: Platte River WMA adjacent to I-80 SE of Kearney. Drive-in primitive camping: flat pull-offs along a gravel road, self-contained rigs fit, no facilities and no fires; free, no permit, FCFS, 3-night limit per 6 days. Riverside fishing access. Note: access bridge has weight limits (10-ton dual-axle / 23-ton trailer) — fine for a 23-ft Class C. Highway noise. RV Life unrated/$. --Claude

About 30 free FCFS sites (no reservations); some have electric. Reviewers note leveled gravel big-rig-friendly sites to the right of the boat ramp plus a sandy dispersed area to the left. Vault toilets, picnic tables/grills, water spout/laundry house near entrance, boat ramp; no dump station. As a WMA this is drive-in vehicle camping (not walk-in/hunt-only). RV Life unrated/$. --Claude

name: Bloomfield - Lewis and Clark SRA

Half of reservable-loop sites are reservable via NGPC system, rest FCFS (register at Iron Ranger). Showers/dump/fill at the main Weigand-Burbach complex. Park entry permit required. RV Life 0*/$ (unrated). --Claude

RV Life unrated/$. --Claude

name: Bridgeport State Recreation Area

name: Cedar View Campground - Lake McConaughy SRA
waterfront_evidence: audited 2026-06 (NE state evidence-gate sweep, commit 3126a2b); waterfront 'lakefront' set by that satellite/per-site-map sweep (per-entry bucket detail in the commit message)

Peak season (May 20-Sep 10) is advance-reservation only via NGPC system; beach camping books 30 days out. State park entry permit required. RV Life 3.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Champion Lake State Recreation Area

name: Conestoga State Recreation Area

name: Cottonwood Lake State Recreation Area
