Picnic tables, fire rings, drinking water, dump-and-fill, modern restrooms, showers, laundry; 8-mi limestone hike/bike trail, disc golf. Half the sites reservable, rest FCFS. Park entry permit required. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Windmill State Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: audited 2026-06 (NE state evidence-gate sweep, commit 3126a2b); waterfront 'pond' set by that satellite/per-site-map sweep (per-entry bucket detail in the commit message)
note: NGPC recreation area at the Gibbon I-80 interchange (Exit 285), built around five small ponds (14 water acres). 69 electric sites (20/30/50-amp, accepts large rigs) plus ~20 basic sites; about half reservable up to 180 days out in peak season (May 1-Oct 15), the rest first-come first-served. Two modern restrooms, two shower houses, dump/fill station, laundry, swim beach. Nebraska park entry permit required. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Bessey Recreation Complex
note: Nebraska NF (Bessey RD) at Halsey, in the historic hand-planted Bessey forest. ~50 sites: 31 electric, 19 non-electric, plus a group site and several tent-only sites. Flush toilets, free showers (mid-May to mid-Oct), drinking water, swimming pool nearby. $15 non-electric / $20 electric. Roughly half the sites reservable on recreation.gov mid-May to mid-Oct, FCFS otherwise (pay online via app). Adjacent to the shallow Middle Loup River (tubing/canoeing) and a 7-acre stocked pond, but the camping loops sit back behind a treeline so pads are not on the water. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Cottonwood
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit ced93f3 (Add 11 NE federal campgrounds (USACE + Nebraska NF/Grasslands)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USACE Lewis & Clark Lake / Gavins Point Dam, on the Lake Yankton peninsula just below the dam near Crofton. 77 sites, all electric, all reservable on recreation.gov. Two modern shower houses w/ flush toilets, RV dump station nearby, paved drive-in sites that fit a 23-ft rig. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Gremlin Cove
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit ced93f3 (Add 11 NE federal campgrounds (USACE + Nebraska NF/Grasslands)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USACE Harlan County Lake, eastern end near Republican City, by the marina cove. Large drive-in camping area; water, vault toilets, some electric hookups available, ~$6/night primitive sites. FCFS (the rec.gov 'Gremlin Cove Shelter' listing is a day-use group shelter, not the campground). May-Sept. RV Life unrated/$. --Claude

name: Hunter Cove
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit ced93f3 (Add 11 NE federal campgrounds (USACE + Nebraska NF/Grasslands)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USACE Harlan County Lake, eastern end near Republican City. ~150 sites, standard electric and non-electric drive-in (plus some tent-only), hard-surfaced main roads. Showers/toilets, laundry, two playgrounds, boat ramp, fish-cleaning station, marinas adjacent. Reservable on recreation.gov; attendant May-Sept. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Methodist Cove
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit ced93f3 (Add 11 NE federal campgrounds (USACE + Nebraska NF/Grasslands)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USACE Harlan County Lake, west side near Republican City/Alma. 133 sites, mix of standard electric and non-electric, drive-in with hard-surfaced main roads. One large heated shower house w/ flush toilets, several vault toilets, dump station, drinking water, boat ramp. Reservable on recreation.gov; attendant on duty May-Sept. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Nebraska Tailwaters
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit ced93f3 (Add 11 NE federal campgrounds (USACE + Nebraska NF/Grasslands)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USACE Gavins Point Dam tailwaters on the south bank of the Missouri River below the dam near Crofton. 36 sites total: 34 electric drive-in RV sites plus 2 tent-only non-electric. Modern showers/flush toilets, RV dump station, boat ramp, fish-cleaning station. Reservable on recreation.gov. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Red Cloud Campground (Nebraska NF)
note: Nebraska NF Pine Ridge unit, ~8-9 mi south of Chadron on the west side of US-385 (1 mi south of Chadron State Park). 13 graveled non-electric sites with picnic tables, pedestal grills, fire rings, vault toilet; access to the Pine Ridge trail system. Mostly short back-in pads but RV-capable - reviewers with 24-ft and 26-ft trailers fit fine; two pull-throughs. $10/night, reservable on recreation.gov. Drawback: heavy US-385 truck noise. No water feature nearby. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Soldier Creek Campground (Nebraska NF)
note: Nebraska NF Pine Ridge unit adjacent to Soldier Creek Wilderness and Fort Robinson SP. From Crawford, US-20 to Fort Robinson then ~6 mi north on Soldier Creek Rd (gravel, with a paved creek crossing that can be dicey after heavy rain). 8 spacious sites large enough for RVs, picnic tables, fire rings, hand-pump well, vault toilets; 15-paddock corral/round pen and wilderness trailheads (general campground that also serves horse users). FCFS, $15/night. RV Life unrated/$. --Claude

name: South Outlet
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit ced93f3 (Add 11 NE federal campgrounds (USACE + Nebraska NF/Grasslands)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USACE Harlan County Lake, primitive grass camping below the dam outlet near Republican City. ~60 undesignated grass sites, no hookups; picnic tables, fire rings, pit/vault toilets, dump station. FCFS, ~$10-12/night, May-Sept, 14-day limit. Drive-in via washboard gravel; access road is narrow if meeting boat trailers, but RV-usable (firsthand RV reviews). RV Life unrated/$. --Claude

name: Steer Creek Campground
note: Samuel R. McKelvie NF, SE corner, in shade of ponderosa pines; ~18 mi south of Nenzel on State Spur 16F. 23 non-electric sites, picnic tables, fire rings, vault toilets, hand water pump (not potable); 8-stall horse corral and the 1-mi Blue Jay nature loop. FCFS only (not on recreation.gov), self-pay $15/night, open year-round. RV Life 1*/$. --Claude

name: Toadstool Geological Park Campground
note: Oglala National Grassland (Nebraska NF & Grasslands), NW of Crawford in the badlands. 6 primitive drive-in gravel sites, FCFS, honor-system $15/night; vault toilets, picnic tables, fire rings, NO water on site (bring your own). Reached via ~12-14 mi of maintained gravel/dirt washboard road - passable by a normal vehicle and a 23-ft rig at slow speed (a 25-ft trailer reportedly fit, just barely); road washes out and gets greasy when wet, so avoid in/after heavy rain. Open year-round. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Adams County Fairgrounds
note: County fairgrounds RV camping in gravel lots on the grounds: 20 full-hookup (50/30A water/sewer/electric), 86 water+electric, and 290 electric-only sites. Big-rig friendly (rigs to ~60 ft; easily fits a 23-ft rig). Open seasonally Apr 15-Oct 15; closed during major fairground events/fair week. Reservable online via Campspot or by phone, not FCFS. RV stays capped at 3 weeks. Free dump station (donations accepted), shower house/restrooms. RV Life 3.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Ag Park (Platte County Fairgrounds)
note: Platte County Agricultural Society fairgrounds with a real ~40-50 full-hookup RV campground (~85 total sites incl. dry), 30/50-amp electric + water + sewer, pull-throughs that fit big rigs (longest reported rig 28 ft; a 23-ft rig fits easily); horse stalls on site. Open seasonally (roughly Oct-June outside fair week, plus fair). Reservable online via Campspot; reviewers also report walk-up/FCFS availability. Dump station, showers, restrooms; ~$20/night. Reviewers note frequent passing trains and a somewhat run-down feel. RV Life 2.5 stars, price $. --Claude

name: Annevar Park
note: City of Ravenna-run campground in Annevar Park; ~10 RV sites with electric and water hookups (some 30A, some 50A; ~every other site has water). Dump station between the park and Public Works building. FCFS via donation box, up to 7 days; longer stays case-by-case through City Hall. Drive-in, fits a 23-ft rig. No showers noted. Same grounds host the annual Annevar festival/demo derby, but the campground operates year-round and independently. RV Life 4 stars, $. --Claude

name: Arnold Lake Recreation Area
note: Village of Arnold municipal recreation area on a 22-acre lake; ~29 campsites, a few with electric/water hookups ($25/night), rest primitive ($10/night). Open drive-in grassy loop fits a 23-ft rig. FCFS, no reservations. RV dump station north of park entrance on west side of Hwy 40. NGPC only stocks the lake -- not a state rec area despite some directory labels. RV Life 5 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Bader Memorial Park
note: Merrick County park run by the nonprofit Platte PEER Group, between the Platte and Wood Rivers. ~36 RV sites (electric, 30/50-amp) plus 15 tent sites; max site length ~35 ft, fits a 23-ft rig. Showers and dump station on site; seven fishing lakes with a swim beach. RV sites with electric ~$20/night plus $6/day vehicle pass; reservable online via Firefly. RV pads sit in an open loop set back from the lakes/rivers, not at a waterline. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Bancroft Municipal Park
note: Village of Bancroft municipal park on the village's SW/NW edge; ~5 drive-in electric+water sites (camping NW of the park off Public Rd) on open flat grass -- ample room for a 23-ft Class C. RV dump and flush toilets on site; no showers noted. Open May-Oct, roughly $6/night, very cheap. Not on recreation.gov -- book/inquire by phone via Ru-Des Mini Mart; effectively FCFS/call-ahead. RV Life unrated (0 star, price $). --Claude

name: Bassett City Park
note: City of Bassett municipal RV camping spread through the City Park & Fairgrounds areas south of US-20. Electric hookups (mix of 20/30/50-amp receptacles), water hydrants, sewage/dump disposal, showers, playground. Open year-round; FCFS (no online booking, donation/low fee). Largest sites fit a 23-ft rig (listings cite up to 30 ft). Availability tight during major events (Sandhills Ranch Expo in June, Rock County Fair and HS Rodeo in Aug). RV Life 3 stars / $ (price_level 1). --Claude

name: Battle Creek Municipal Park
note: City-run 55-acre municipal park & arboretum (ball fields, pool, playground) on the south edge of Battle Creek. ~18 RV sites with electric (20/30/50 amp) and water; sanitary dump on site. FCFS, pay on site. No water feature at the park (Battle Creek/Elkhorn River not adjacent). RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Bayard City Park
note: City of Bayard runs 3 gravel RV sites at the Swimming Pool Park (104 E 11th St). Each has 30/50-amp electric + water, no sewer; dump station ~6 blocks away by the library. FCFS, free first 2 nights then $10/night, 10-day max; pay at city office (445 Main St). Sites are close-set parking spots on gravel, fine for a 23-ft rig. Backs to farmland, no water feature. RV Life 5-star, free/$. --Claude

name: Beck Memorial Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Decatur park on the Missouri River at the edge of town. ~50 sites; some full-hookup, others water/electric with 30/50-amp service (one source notes 20-amp); dump station, showers, restrooms, boat ramp/fish-cleaning station. FCFS ($25 full / $22 W-E per one source). Open ~Apr-Oct. A reviewer noted many long-term campers can fill it, so call ahead in season. Big-rig friendly pull-throughs. RV Life 5-star, $. --Claude

name: Big Indian Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Lower Big Blue NRD recreation area (NRD-operated = local; RV Life mis-tags it 'dnr'/state) on a 77-acre no-wake lake 6 mi E of Odell / SW of Wymore. 20 electric + 14 non-electric gravel camper pads, swim beach/cove, boat ramp, ADA restroom; max RV ~40 ft (a 42-ft rig reportedly fit). FCFS, ~$10 electric / $5 non-electric. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Bloomfield City Park Campground
note: City of Bloomfield municipal park. 6 RV-only sites (no tents) with cement pads, full hookups (30-amp electric + water), fire pits, grills, picnic tables; flush toilets, swimming pool/playground in park. $20/night, open year-round. FCFS, pay at City Office or police drop box. RV Life unrated (0 stars). --Claude

name: Blue Valley Camping Area
note: City of Seward municipal campground at 730 S 6th St. 16 crushed-rock pads with 30-amp electric; pads fit large rigs (reviewer at 34 ft). Dump station, water hydrant, portable ADA restroom (no showers), playground. $25/night ($15 senior 65+, $10 tent), FCFS via drop-box envelopes, open Apr 1-Nov 1, 7-day stay limit (48-hr interim). Sits in a city park west of the Big Blue River, not on the water. RV Life 3 stars. --Claude

name: Bruce L. Anderson Recreation Area (Recharge Lake)
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Operated by Upper Big Blue Natural Resources District at Recharge Lake just off Hwy 34 near York. Level concrete pads with 20/30/50-amp electric, big-rig friendly (reviews report up to 43 ft); shared water source (bring a long hose), dump station near entrance, restrooms, archery range. $15/night RV, FCFS (pay on-site), open mid-April to end of October. RV Life 5 stars. --Claude

name: Bruce Park Campground
note: City of Creighton municipal campground on the east side of 30-acre Bruce Park (arboretum + ball complex). 7 numbered camper pads: #1-4 full hookup (electric/water/sewer), #5-7 electric+water 30-amp, plus a free primitive/tent area. FCFS, no reservations; pay at the City Office/Police Dept on Main St. Dump station, restrooms, showers on site; swimming pool nearby. A 29-ft Class C has fit, so a 23-ft RV is fine. RV Life 3 stars, $. --Claude

name: Bruning Dam Recreation Area
note: Little Blue NRD reservoir rec area NE of Bruning (~250 acres water). 19 electrical RV camper pads; FCFS - no online reservation system for Little Blue NRD areas. Boat ramp, floating dock, fishing pier, swimming beach, picnic shelters, restrooms, trails. Drive-in via gravel; pads sit near the boat ramp on the south shore. Primitive/rustic but developed pads. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Buffalo County Fairgrounds
note: Buffalo County fairgrounds (Kearney) with year-round by-the-night RV camping, not fair-week-only. Electric hookups 20/30/50A, water, dump station on grounds; FCFS, ~$20/night; 5-day max, no long-term camping. No bathhouse/showers. Office M-F 8-5, after-hours drop box. Open lots with ample room for a 23-ft rig. RV Life 4.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Buffalo County Recreation Area - Ravenna Lake
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Buffalo County-run (former Ravenna Lake SRA, turned over to the county ~20 yrs ago; NOT a Nebraska Game & Parks SRA despite the old name). 41 RV/tent sites: 8 primitive, 9 electric-only, 16 electric+water, 8 premium electric+water (pull-throughs); 30/50-amp pedestals. Premium pull-throughs fit big rigs. Bathhouse/showers opened spring 2022; dump station on site. Reservable online via Campspot, or drive-up self-pay via iron ranger at the entrance (FCFS sites can fill quickly). No Nebraska state park entry permit required (county area). RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Bussell Park
note: City of Ord municipal park campground (sometimes listed as Brussel Park). 12 concrete sites: 7 water/electric (30A), 5 full hookups; FCFS (no reservations). Dump station, showers/restrooms, shade. Within a large park with Aubles Pond, frisbee golf, tennis/basketball, paved trail; pads set back from the pond behind a perimeter path/treeline. RV Life 4 stars, $. --Claude

name: Cambridge City RV Park
note: City of Cambridge RV park in McKinley Park on US-6/34. 11 sites, 30/50-amp electric + water, dump station, showers. Free (donations encouraged; donation requested past 3 nights). FCFS, no reservations. Max rig ~32 ft - fine for a 23-ft RV. Some road/train noise from adjacent Hwy 34. Year-round. RV Life 4.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Carney City Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'pond' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of O'Neill municipal park with 3-acre fishing pond. ~16-18 back-in sites, water + electric (20/30 amp, some 30/50). Restrooms/showers with hot water (seasonal). FCFS, self-serve payment envelopes; pre-register at city offices for stays over 7 days. Back-in sites fit rigs to ~40 ft - fine for a 23-ft RV. Playground, horseshoes. RV Life 4 stars, $. --Claude

name: Chilvers Park
note: City of Plainview park. 4 paved, level (narrow) drive-in sites on the north side of the park; each pedestal has 50/30/20-amp electric plus water (no dump on-site). FCFS, free with a suggested donation; 3-night max. Reviewers report easy maneuvering and sites taking ~30-ft rigs. Restrooms, pool, playground, ballcourts; one block off US-20 (some road noise). RV Life 4.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Christensen Field - Fremont Parks & Recreation
note: City of Fremont municipal campground at the Christensen Field complex (West Campground, outside the Main Arena) — a real season-long public campground, not event-only. 26 sites with 30-amp and 26 with 50-amp electric, level pull-through gravel pads with water; year-round trailer dump station. Fits large rigs (sites listed to ~50 ft). FCFS, no reservations; $25/night; 14-day limit. Open roughly April through Nov 1, closed winter. RV Life 3.0 stars. --Claude

name: City of Sutton RV Park
note: City of Sutton RV park by the city park; check in / pay at City Hall (after-hours via police dept). Sites 1-14: full hookups (electric/water/sewer), 20/30/50-amp, pull-through, $10/night or $300/mo; sites A/B/C: electric+water 20/30-amp, $5/night up to 14 days. Dump/dumpster on site; no showers (restrooms at nearby park). 24-ft rigs reported fitting. RV Life 4 stars / $. --Claude

name: Cody City Park
note: Village of Cody municipal park; small grassy camping area near the old grain elevator (shields US-20 noise). ~4-10 full-hookup back-in sites, 30/50-amp electric + sewer + water, on-site dump station; sites unmarked (back onto grass next to pedestals), max RV ~29 ft. Restrooms; reports vary on showers. FCFS, $20/night honor-system (cash/check). Open seasonally ~May 1-Oct 15. Official villageofcody.com domain is dead; operation confirmed via campendium/campgroundviews and village clerk phone. RV Life 4.5 star, $. --Claude

name: Cody Park
note: City of North Platte municipal park on the North Platte River; large day-use park (lake, mini-zoo, railroad museum, pool). Campground has ~40 pads, lengths 18-28 ft (smaller rigs only at the long end). NO electric hookups (quiet generators allowed); restrooms + dump station. FCFS, $15/night, no reservations. Open ~Apr 15-Nov 1, limited to 7 nights per month. Camping loop sits back from the river behind the perimeter road and a treeline (satellite at 41.1533,-100.7573). RV Life 3 star, $. --Claude

name: Crawford City Park
note: City of Crawford municipal park along the White River. 4 hookup sites with water + 20/30/50-amp electric near the Wacipi arbor, plus primitive grass/dispersed sites throughout. Dump station, spigots, restrooms, playground, walking trail. FCFS, $20 hookup / $10 primitive, 14-night max. The main entrance bridge is posted 5-ton, but firsthand reviews report 29-34 ft RVs (incl. a 32 ft Class C) at the electric sites and an alternate dirt-road back entrance bypasses the bridge; big-rig friendly once inside (EKKO's 23 ft fits easily). Frequent BNSF train noise. RV Life 4 star, $. --Claude

name: Crystal Springs Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Fairbury park ~5 mi south of town with two spring-fed lakes. 42 sites total. Lakeside sites 1-20, 22-24 reservable online ($20/night, 14-day limit), 30 & 50 amp; upper sites are FCFS, 30 amp. Water at each site Apr 15-Oct 15; restroom/shower house. Dump station is free but located at Fairbury City Park (not on-site). Big-rig friendly. RV Life 3.5 star, $. --Claude

name: David City Park Campground
note: City of David City municipal park campground, RV-only (no tents). 12 electric sites (20 amp), 4 pull-throughs, max length ~40 ft. Open Apr 1-Oct 31. Water at sites, dump station, pit toilets, playground; adjacent aquatic center/walking trail/golf. Small ornamental ponds in the park but RV sites are not waterfront. Reserve via the city (402-367-3135). RV Life 1.5 star, $. --Claude

name: Dawson County Fairgrounds Campground
note: Dawson County Fairgrounds in Lexington with a real public RV campground open year-round (not event-only). 48 elec/water sites, 30 & 50 amp, $25/night ($350/30 days). Reserve by calling the office Mon-Fri 8-4; after-hours drop box south of campsites. Dump station east of the outdoor arena; showers. Access lane is one-lane but sites fit big rigs. RV Life 3.5 star (7.5/10), $. --Claude

name: Day Memorial Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Weeping Water park campground on a fishing lake. ~22 sites, all upgraded to 50-amp electric with level concrete pads; no per-site water but fill faucets throughout the park; dump station at the park entrance. FCFS (no reservations); pay nightly. Camp host (402) 297-2878. Paved walk/bike path along the lake. RV Life 4.5 star, $. --Claude

name: Deshler City Park Campground
note: City of Deshler park campground (599 Park St) next to the municipal pool and ballfields. ~10-12 back-in/pull-in sites with 20/30-amp electric and water; no onsite showers; dump station in town. First night free, then ~$15/night, FCFS (QR-code pay, no reservation system). Longest reported rig 29 ft; one reviewer noted the parallel-park layout is a little tight/confusing for big rigs but fits a 23-ft Class C. Swimming pool, horseshoes, sand volleyball, paved walking path. RV Life 5 star, $. --Claude

name: Diller Village Park Campground
note: Village of Diller park on the north edge of town (Commercial St). 12 full-hookup camper pads with water, electric and sewer; 30-amp $20/night, 50-amp $25/night. Shaded; playground, shelter, restrooms. Reserve via the village clerk (phone/email). Not on water. RV Life 5/5, $. --Claude

name: Duck Creek Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Nemaha NRD lake rec area (opened 2018; 62-acre no-wake lake). 28 paved RV pads, all 50-amp electric, FCFS, $16/night cash or check. Drinking-water hydrants but no dump station and no water hookups. Big-rig-friendly modern loops (fits a 23-ft rig easily). Nemaha NRD park permit required. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: East City Park Campground
note: City of Ainsworth park on the Cowboy Trail. ~7-8 grass sites with 30-amp electric pedestals plus water; FCFS, $10/night, dump station $5 (tents $7). Open April-October. Restrooms; showers reported. Big-rig friendly per reviews. RV Life 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: Elkhorn Crossing Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Operated by the Papio-Missouri River NRD (a Natural Resources District, not NGPC) on the Elkhorn River near Valley. 10 RV sites with 30/50-amp electric and water hookups plus a tent/group area; new bathrooms, playground, disc golf, river/canoe access. No dump station. FCFS - register at entrance kiosk, max 7 consecutive days. Open Apr 1 - Nov 1. RV Life 3*/$. --Claude

name: Fuller Park
note: City of Albion municipal campground in SW Albion (near the Family Aquatic Center). 26 sites: 14 full hookups and 12 electric/water, 30-amp; bath house/showers and dump station. Open year-round. FCFS; camping permit required from Albion City Hall (Mon-Fri 7-4) or after-hours via police non-emergency 402-395-2154. RV Life 4.5 stars (8.9/10, 3 reviews). --Claude

name: Genoa City Park Campground
note: City of Genoa park (Nance County). Camping loop on the south side of the 32-acre park; gravel/grass sites with 30-amp electric + water. Year-round, FCFS, $10/night paid at City offices. Dump station, restrooms, adjacent pool/ball fields. Little Loup River runs along the south edge but sites sit well back behind a wooded buffer. RV Life 4 stars (thin reviews). --Claude

name: George Mitchell Park RV Park
note: Village of Oxford park (Furnas County). 10 RV sites, water + electric (20/30/50-amp, varies by site), pull-throughs available. Year-round, FCFS, $10/night (+$20 after-hours hookup), 14-day limit. Sewer dump station, laundry; disc golf and a stocked fishing pond on the 37.7-acre grounds. RV sites are by the ball field/entrance, not on the pond or river. RV Life 3.5 stars (thin reviews). --Claude

name: Glenn Cunningham Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Omaha lake (flood-control lake the City owns; operated by the Lake Cunningham Development Trust). Two RV campgrounds: East C (42 full-hookup concrete sites, 20/30/50-amp) and West C (41 electric-only concrete sites, 60'x16', 20/30/50-amp) - fits a 23-ft rig easily. Showers, flush restrooms, dump station, snack bar. Open mid-April through October; 14-night max, 2-night weekend minimum. Reservable online (not FCFS). RV Life 5 stars, $$$. --Claude

name: Hall County Park (George H. Clayton Campground)
note: Hall County park ~3.5 mi east of Grand Island (I-80 exit 312). 19 paved RV sites with 30/50-amp electric; wide angled back-ins long enough for a 23-ft rig. Dump/fill station, modern bathhouse showers. FCFS self-pay $30/night (no reservations), 7-night max stay. Season ~April-October, gate hours 7:30am-8pm. Wooded park. RV Life 4.0 stars. --Claude

name: Harbine Park
note: City of Nelson park. 4-5 back-in concrete-pad sites with full hookups (30/50A electric, water, sewer); a 36-ft Class A has fit, so easy for a 23-ft rig. FCFS - no reservation system; pay cash at city hall (6th & Main) or after-hours mail slot. RV dump station on West 8th St. No showers. Restrooms, playground, disc golf, pool on site. ~$15/night. RV Life 4.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Holdrege City Campground
note: City of Holdrege park, south of Burlington St across the railroad tracks. 7 concrete-pad sites with electric hookups, ~$10/night. Dump station and water on site; good showers. Open year-round. FCFS - no online reservation system found. Sits right next to active rail tracks; frequent night trains. Fits a 23-ft rig on the concrete pads. RV Life 4.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Humboldt Lake Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Humboldt lake park with two fishing ponds. ~11-12 drive-in electric sites (reported 20-30A); max vehicle length 25 ft, so a 23-ft rig fits with little margin. Sanitary dump station on site; no showers confirmed. Seasonal, roughly April-September. No dedicated online reservation system found (treat as FCFS / call ahead). RV Life 4 stars / $. --Claude

name: Island Park Campground
note: City of Lyons park on an island in Logan Creek's oxbow. 14 sites with full hookups - electric (30 & 50 amp), water, sewer, plus a dump station and an on-site bathhouse. Open Apr 15-Oct 15, FCFS (no advance reservations). $25/night, $150/week, $500/30 days. Pool and fishing within walking distance. RV Life 0 stars (unrated) / $. --Claude

name: Karrer Park
note: City of McCook municipal campground at the east edge of town on US-6/34. 7 RV spots, 50-amp electric, on-site fill/dump. Was closed 2024-25 for misuse; reopened for the 2026 season under a new permit ordinance - now RESERVATION-required (book via the city), $20/night, max 5 nights per 20-day period. Bathrooms/showers reported out of order as of the 2026 reopening. Accommodates large rigs (38-40 ft reported); entry has a tight turn. RV Life 3.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Kirkman's Cove Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Nemaha NRD recreation area on 160-acre Kirkman's Cove Lake, ~2.5 mi W and 0.5 mi N of Humboldt. Electric RV and tent camping in lakeside loops (East/West) with level shaded sites, a sand swimming beach, boat ramp, and kayak launch (new 2026); 9-hole golf course adjacent. NNRD park permit required. Big-rig-capable level sites. RV Life 3.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Lafayette Park Campground
note: City of Gothenburg municipal park campground. ~30 RV sites with 30/50-amp electric + water, pull-through and back-in on grass/gravel, big-rig friendly. Showers, restrooms, dump station at entrance. ~$25/night, reservable online (RoverPass) or by phone. Open year-round; Apr 1-Oct 31 full service, Nov-Mar electric-only/no water for self-contained rigs. Lake Helen (fishing/kayaking) across the canal via footbridge. RV Life 4 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Lake North Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Free FCFS campground on the north shore of Lake North, operated by Loup Power District (public power district = local operator). Electric hookups plus primitive sites, open big-rig-friendly layout. No reservations (FCFS); 7-day limit per 30-day period across the Loup park system. Open May 1-Nov 1 (weather permitting). Potable water May-Sept; no dump station and no direct water hookups. Two beaches, two boat ramps on the ~200-acre lake. RV Life 3.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Lions RV Park
note: City of Wayne municipal RV park (maintained by the Wayne Lions Club) on Hwy 35 east of town at the airport. ~20 sites with 30/50-amp electric and water hookups; on-site sewer/dump discharge area; picnic shelter, grill, no showers. Open Apr 1-Oct 31. $15/night, FCFS, no stay limit. Big-rig-friendly small lot. RV Life 5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Lone Star Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Little Blue NRD recreation area on a 75-acre spring-fed lake, 4 mi east + 1/4 mi south of Ohiowa. 19 electric RV camper pads (lakeside), plus free tent camping anywhere; primitive restrooms, no showers, no on-site dump (nearest ~6 mi north at Milligan). Swimming beach, boat ramp (no-wake lake), floating dock, fishing pier, trails. Power/water Apr 1-Dec 1. $20/night electric. Big-rig friendly. Contact NRD for booking; otherwise FCFS. RV Life unrated (0 stars) / $. --Claude

name: Lynch RV Park
note: City of Lynch municipal RV park on the SW side of town (4th St & Railroad Ave), inland from Ponca Creek. ~6-10 back-in/pull-in sites, electric (reviews cite 30/50-amp), tables/grills, restrooms, dump station; reviewer reports rigs up to ~45 ft accommodated, fine for a 23-ft Class C. Open roughly Mar-Nov; FCFS (no online reservation system, ~$20-25/night honor pay). RV Life 4 star / $. --Claude

name: Mel's Landing Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Village of Cedar Rapids riverside park on the Cedar River at the north edge of town (Hwy 52). ~10 RV sites with electric (city water; some water/electric/sewer combos), tables and fire pits, cement bathhouse with 2 showers and 2 restrooms, canoe landing, playground. Open, level grassy sites suit a 23-ft Class C. FCFS — payment by drop box, no online reservations (~$15/night). RV Life 3 star / $. --Claude

name: Mill Race Park & Campground (Atkinson)
note: City of Atkinson park on the Elkhorn River with a 14-acre fishing lake; mislabeled 'Atkinson Lake State Rec Area' by some directories but it is a city park, not NGPC. ~24 RV spots, all with electric (20/30 amp); little site demarcation, level grassy pull-ins under shade trees -- a 40-ft fifth-wheel fit easily so a 23-ft rig is no problem. Open year-round, FCFS (no reservations), $20/night RV. Free hot showers + flush toilets; no on-site dump (a $3-donation dump is at the Community Center on Hwy 20). RV Life 4.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Muny Park
note: City of Cozad municipal park campground. ~12-18 back-in RV sites, concrete or dirt pads, 20/30/50-amp electric + city water (partial hookups). Accepts big rigs (sites to ~50 ft) so a 23-ft Class C fits easily. On-site dump station, flush-toilet restrooms, seasonal swimming pool/water park, playground, walking trail. Open year-round. FCFS via self-pay kiosk (no reservations); pay station ~$10-25/night. In-town park, not on any water. RV Life 4.5 star / $. --Claude

name: Neligh Park
note: City of West Point municipal campground in Neligh Park (despite the name, it's in West Point, not the town of Neligh). 17 concrete pads, 20/30/50-amp electric + water hookups, RVs up to ~50 ft so a 23-ft rig fits easily; $15/night. Sites 1-8 reservable online or by phone, sites 9-17 FCFS; tent camping also available. Bathhouse with showers across from the tent area; off-site sewer dump ($2 fee) near the swimming pool; WiFi. Pads sit by a small city pond/ballfields, not on the Elkhorn River (river is ~0.3 mi west). RV Life 4.5 star / $. --Claude

name: Neligh Riverside Park
note: City of Neligh municipal campground in Riverside Park, along the Elkhorn River in town. ~32 grass sites with 15/30/50-amp electric + water; FCFS, $20/night RV / $5 tent (primitive). Pull-through options; grass pads, some need leveling; reviews report rigs to 36+ ft so a 23-ft rig fits. Restrooms with showers; sewage dump at the park exit; WiFi. Despite the name and 'along the river' marketing, the RV loop sits inland behind a continuous wooded riparian buffer screening it from the Elkhorn (verified on satellite) — not riverfront. RV Life 4.5 star / $. --Claude

name: Newman Grove City Park
note: City-run municipal park with ~8-9 drive-in RV sites on grass; full hookups available (50-amp electric, water, sewer) plus some partial-hookup sites. Big-rig friendly per reviews. On-site sanitary dump station, restrooms, pool, playground, golf course/walking trail adjacent. Open roughly early April-late October. FCFS, honor-system payment via dropbox at City Hall (no online reservations); recent rate ~$20/night. Shell Creek runs along the park's east edge but the campsite loop sits in the open park interior, buffered from the creek by lawn/trees. RV Life 2 stars (7.0/10), price $. --Claude

name: Osmond City Park
note: City-run park (Poolside Park) one block off Hwy 20 in Osmond. 5 RV pads (three 46'x9', two 50'x9') with ~32' usable length, 30 & 50 amp electric and water; dump station nearby. FCFS, suggested donation $10/night. Adjacent swimming pool, sand volleyball, horseshoes, picnic shelters. RV Life 4 stars, $. --Claude

name: Pawnderosa Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'pond' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Pawnee City-run campground on the city fishing pond (the 'Pawnderosa'). Concrete pads (all sites recently paved) ringing a loop around the pond; water + electric sites (30/50-amp confirmed by reviewers), $15/night W/E or $8/night tent. No host on site — reserve and pay at City Hall (402-852-2781). No dump station and no showers (single toilet/sink restroom in a concrete restroom/storm shelter). Reviewers have fit a 33-ft Class A and report a pull-through (site 6), so a 23-ft rig fits easily; some smaller sites won't take big rigs. Fishing pond/pier, picnic areas. RV Life 4 stars, $. --Claude

name: Pibel Lake Recreation Area
note: Operated by the Lower Loup NRD (a Natural Resources District, not NGPC), 9 mi south of Bartlett in Wheeler County on a 26-acre lake. 30 primitive sites - RVs permitted (reported up to ~50 ft) but no pads, water or electric hookups. Restrooms, picnic shelter, boat dock (small boats), pedestrian bridge, ADA fishing piers. Free, FCFS, open year-round. RV Life 0*/$ (unrated). --Claude

name: Prairie Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Wayne park opened 2025 on a reclaimed sewage-lagoon site; 17 full-service camper pads (electric/water/sewer) arrayed along a stocked 4-acre fishing lake with pier and kayak launch; large angled pull-in/back-in pads fit a 23-ft Class C and bigger rigs. Bathhouse with showers on site. Reservable online (City of Wayne Rec1 portal). RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Riverside Campground (Scottsbluff)
note: City of Scottsbluff park campground in Riverside Park (by the Riverside Discovery Center zoo), with Scotts Bluff National Monument views. 43 pull-through RV sites (vehicles up to 50 ft) plus 20 tent sites; full-hookup, water/electric, or primitive, with 30 and 50 amp service. Dump station, bathhouse with showers, free WiFi, fire ring + picnic table per site. Open seasonally (roughly May 1-Sept 30). Reservable online via Campspot; 14-day RV limit (7-day tents). FHU ~$25/night. RV Life 3.5 stars, $$. Pads sit well back from the North Platte River with open ground, an internal road, and a treeline buffer between sites and the river. --Claude

name: Riverside City Park
note: Burwell municipal city park campground (City of Burwell), distinct from the rodeo fairgrounds. ~4 gravel pad sites behind the tennis courts with 30A electric (one pedestal also has 50/30/15A); two back-in, two pull-through, easy turnaround. Reviewer with a 42-ft rig reported sites fit another 10-15 ft, so a 23-ft Class C is no problem. Water available (no site hookup); coin-operated shower and restrooms; dump station a few blocks away at the city sanitation facility. FCFS, no reservations; $10-20/night per recent reports. North Loup River runs past the park but the pads sit back behind a treeline (not riverfront). RV Life 4 stars / $. --Claude

name: Riverside Park Campground
note: City of Beatrice park on the Big Blue River. 8 concrete back-in pads (16'x60', no pull-thru) with full hookups: 30/50-amp electric, water, sewer; big rigs fit (60' pads). FCFS, no reservations; $25/night, 7-day max stay, 1pm checkout, pay by QR-code/envelope or by phone. Restrooms, hot showers, picnic shelters, playgrounds, lighted tennis. Pads sit by the ballfield/tennis courts, screened from the river by a continuous treeline (not waterfront). RV Life 5.0 star, $$. --Claude

name: Robidoux RV Park
note: City of Gering municipal RV park at the base of Scotts Bluff Nat'l Monument, 585 Five Rocks Rd. 42 concrete pads, most pull-through with full hookups (electric/water/sewer); pads accommodate large rigs (listed up to 65 ft) so a 23-ft Class C fits easily. Open year-round; monthly/28-day rates Nov 1-Mar 31. Showers and laundry in the multipurpose building; dump station (closed Nov 1-May 1). Reservable online via Campspot. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Schuyler City Campground
note: City of Schuyler municipal campground, ~1 mi S of US-30/Hwy-15 jct on E side of Hwy 15. 35 RV sites (RV-only, no tent), electric hookups (35-amp per most listings; a few 20-amp), water at most sites, dump station, flush toilets, pay showers, playground; adjacent day-use park, golf course, and stocked fishing ponds. Open Apr-Oct. FCFS, no online reservations; pay on site ($10 primitive / $15 electric / $20 full hookup). Loop layout fits a 23-ft Class C with room for larger rigs. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Seward County Fairgrounds Campground
note: ~39-site RV campground at the Seward County Fairgrounds, run by the Seward County Ag Society. 30-amp electric at most sites; no individual water hookups (a few water hydrants around the grounds); dump station on the south end; restrooms/showers not included with a campsite. $30/night (1 p.m.-1 p.m.); 5-day max stay (no long-term camping). Year-round and open to any traveler, not just fair attendees (closed July 1-3, 2026 for the Outlaw Tractor Pull and likely during fair week). Reservations with full pre-payment, or self-pay drop box/envelope at the picnic shelter for walk-ins. No generators. RV Life 5 stars, $. --Claude

name: South Park Campground
note: City of Franklin municipal RV park at the SW corner of NE Hwy 10 & J St. 5 full-hookup sites (20/30/50-amp electric, water, sewer), free dump station, restrooms/showers, picnic tables, adjacent town pool/playground. Big-rig friendly (a 41-ft rig fit per a review); easily fits a 23-ft rig. Year-round; ~$10-15/night. FCFS / pay the city clerk at the listed phone (no online reservation system). Adjacent to Hwy 10 so expect some road noise. RV Life 2.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: South Park RV Park
note: City of Arapahoe municipal RV park in South Park; 9 concrete pull-through pads, 7 with water+electric (30/50-amp) and 2 full-hookup, 2 ADA-accessible. Fits big rigs (34-ft reported). FCFS, no reservations; 10-night limit (longer by arrangement with city office). Dump station, restrooms with showers, free WiFi; walking trail with exercise stations, disc golf, playground. Adjacent to railroad tracks (train noise noted). $15-$20/night. RV Life 4.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Stanton Lake Park
note: City of Falls City park on the NW edge of town. ~11 full-hookup RV spots (30/50 amp, water, sewer); some sources count up to 15 with a mix of full-hookup and electric/water sites. Paved loop; big-rig friendly - reviewers parked 36-37 ft rigs. Bath house with showers, dump available. Reservable via the city office (402-245-2851) or the city's online Parks & Rec portal, not FCFS. ~$30/night camper, $5 tent. Fishing lake, walking path, disc golf, dog park, volleyball, playground. Active rail line nearby - reviewers note trains at night. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Stapleton City Park
note: Village of Stapleton municipal park on the north edge of town along NE Hwy 92. ~6 drive-in grass/gravel campsites, several pull-through; 20/30-amp electric + water (shared power poles). Big-rig friendly - reviews report rigs to ~36 ft, so a 23-ft Class C fits easily. FCFS, self-pay envelope (~$15/night donation; one recent report $10). Dump station, flush restrooms and showers at the park building (restrooms close in winter). Playground, sand volleyball, tennis court, shelter. RV Life 5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Streeter Park Campground
note: City of Aurora municipal campground in wooded Streeter Park, ~4 blocks from downtown. ~10-12 level back-in sites (some lists say 18) with 30-amp electric and water; free dump station; free water. FREE camping, donations requested via box at the shelter near the entrance. FCFS, no reservations; 4-night max stay. Showers are in the adjacent city pool building (seasonal). Most sites suit small-to-medium rigs; a 23-ft Class C fits easily and a few sites reportedly take rigs up to ~35 ft. A creek and walking paths run through the park but sites do not front water. Open year-round. Trains pass nearby at night. RV Life 4.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Stuart Municipal Park
note: City of Stuart municipal park on the Cowboy Trail. ~20 RV sites with electric + water, 30-amp; dump station, restrooms, showers, drinking water, playground. Rigs to ~35 ft. Season ~March-Nov. FCFS, no reservations; cheap town-park rate. Tent camping free. RV Life 3 stars / $. --Claude

name: Sunset RV Park (Hay Springs City Park)
note: City of Hay Springs municipal RV park (formerly Hay Springs City Park), on S. Post St across from City Hall. ~10 sites with full hookups (electric/water/sewer). FCFS, no reservable sites; honor-system payment. ~$25/night or $450/month. Close to city park, pool, ball fields. RV Life 4.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Tomahawk Park
note: City of Broken Bow municipal RV park (Streets/Parks dept); the two RV Life 'Tomahawk City/Municipal Park' listings are the same place. ~25 full-hookup sites (30/50-amp electric, water, sewer); mostly level pull-throughs on gravel with some concrete pads, big-rig friendly (to ~50 ft). Dump station, showers/restrooms on site. Reservable by phone; ~$20/night, monthly rates available. Open all year. Separate from Melham Park's fishing lake. RV Life 4.5 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Tuxedo Park
note: City of Crete Parks & Rec; 96-acre Tuxedo Park west of the Big Blue River (Saline County fairgrounds buildings on site but camping is city-run, not event-only). 20 RV sites, electric only: ~10 by the ball field 20/30-amp (~35 ft) and ~10 near the river 30/50-amp (~40 ft). FCFS, no reservations; $15/night, 10-night max (extensions in writing around fair week). Restrooms, showers, fresh water, dump station. Season ~April-Oct. RV Life 3.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Valentine City Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'creekside' assessed in commit 9af42bc (Add 83 NE local-government campgrounds (city/county/NRD)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Valentine 40-acre municipal park along Minnechaduza Creek. Primitive FCFS camping (no hookups); ~5 informal streamside RV pull-through pads plus tent space, $5-10/night self-pay dropbox. Restrooms with free hot showers; water/restrooms typically shut off in the off-season. Reported site length ~27 ft (a 23-ft rig fits). Open year-round. No dump station noted. RV Life 4 stars / $. --Claude

name: Verdigre City Park Camp
note: Village of Verdigre municipal campground at Wildwood Acres Park. 27 camper pads plus primitive sites and 2 cabins; 30-amp electric, communal water spigot (no per-site water), oak-shaded. Bathrooms with showers, swimming pool, playground. FCFS for camping (cabins reserved via Village Clerk 402-668-2621). $10 no-electric / $15 electric / $35 cabin nightly, weekly & monthly rates. Dump station uncertain. RV Life 4 stars / $. --Claude

name: Victor Park
note: City of Wayne municipal campground at the south edge of town on Hwy 15. All sites have electric (30 & 50 amp), no water/sewer at site but well water and a dump station on site; toilets. $15/night, 5-night max, FCFS only (no reservations). Maintained by Public Works; rigs to 40 ft fit. RV Life 4/5, $. --Claude

name: Walnut Creek Recreation Area Campground
note: City of Papillion rec area (450 acres, 105-acre reservoir) at 9902 Schram Rd, south of Hwy 370 west of 96th St. 44 electric sites (30/50 amp) on 55-ft concrete pads, paved roads, city water, dump station; FCFS (no reservations), $20/night (7 nights for 6), 2-week limit. Big-rig friendly. Camp host 402-679-9889. RV Life 4.5/5, $. --Claude

name: Wisner River Park
note: City of Wisner river park along the Elkhorn River (south side of town at the stoplight, 1111 Ave E). Camping area by the East Parking Lot has ~10 electric sites plus open camping; $15/night with or without hookups, FCFS (no reservations). Coin-op hot showers, restrooms, fishing pond. Camp/ball-field area sits behind a wooded river-bank buffer, not on the water. RV Life 4/5, $. --Claude

name: York County Fairgrounds Campground
note: York County fairgrounds (2345 Nebraska Ave) with year-round RV camping, not event-only. Electric/water sites: 30-amp $15, 50-amp $20; tent $5; dump station nearby. Gridded angled pull-in pads. Fair-week camping ($125/spot) is booked through the county extension office; non-fair stays are otherwise available. Not on water. RV Life 4.5/5, $. --Claude

name: 91 Pines Campground
note: Small private drive-in campground on NE Hwy 91 in the North Loup River valley between Burwell and Taylor; shower house, nearby fishing access, on-site camper rentals also available. Thinly reviewed (verify hookups/length before relying on it). Satellite at 41.75664,-99.26966 shows the site cluster set back from a small pond to the west and the river/treeline to the north — not waterfront. RV Life 5.0★ ($) but only ~1 review. --Claude

name: Blue Heron Campground (Gothenburg)
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 850a48c (Add 33 NE private campgrounds (commercial RV parks + 1 Hipcamp)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Sandpit-lake campground just off I-80 exit 211 at Gothenburg (check in at the Blue Heron gas station). 40 sites among the lakes, 17 full hookup and the rest water/electric, mix of pull-through and back-in (sites close together), 30/50 amp; pool, clean showers. Online reservable. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Blue Ox RV Park
note: Small 14-site RV park beside the Blue Ox towing-products factory in Pender (One Mill Rd industrial park), open to the general public. Full-hookup 50-amp pull-in/back-in sites plus dry-camping spots; welcome center and severe-weather shelter on site; factory tours available. Non-customers $15/night (free for Blue Ox service customers). RV Life 5★, $. --Claude

name: Cabela's RV Park & Campground
note: FCFS RV park beside the original Cabela's in Sidney (Walmart/restaurants in walking distance). 31 full-hookup sites (27 pull-through, 4 back-in, 50-amp) + 7 electric-only pull-throughs + tent sites; fits big rigs up to ~70 ft. Restrooms/showers, laundry, WiFi, playground. No reservations. RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: Chimney Rock Pioneer Crossing
note: Private campground in the shadow of Chimney Rock off Hwy 92 east of Bayard; ~16 sites, full hookups (30/50-amp, water, sewer), long pull-throughs that handle 45-ft rigs, clean restrooms/showers, dump station, on-site trading post. Stock pond sits ~400 ft south of the pads (not waterfront). RV Life 4.5 stars, $$ (Campendium 4.9 stars). --Claude

name: Creekside RV Park and Campground
note: Small private RV park on the south edge of Chappell, ~30 sites with 30/50-amp full hookups; 22 pull-through. First-come, first-served (no advance reservations) and cash only per the operator. Flat open grass, railroad runs alongside so expect train noise. RV Life 4.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Eagle Canyon Hideaway
note: Private full-hookup RV park in a canyon draw near Lake McConaughy's south shore; ~29 sites (30/50 amp, water, sewer), pull-through and back-in, max RV length ~30 ft, plus log cabins. Year-round, nightly reservations via Campspot. Marketed as near the lake but reviews note the water is well off and not walkable at low pool; satellite at 41.26106,-101.91947 shows pad rows set inland, no shoreline sites — not waterfront. RV Life 4.0★ ($$). --Claude

name: Eagle's Rest RV Park
note: Private RV park 1/2 mi west of Chadron on Hwys 20/385; ~48 shaded full-hookup pull-through sites (30/50 amp, water, sewer), big rigs to 60 ft, WiFi, laundry. Open year-round; daily/weekly/monthly rates with a mix of overnighters and some long-term residents, but nightly transient stays are available. In-town highway location, no water. RV Life 4.0★ ($$). --Claude

name: Fishberry Campground
note: Private RV park on US-83 about 5 mi north of Valentine (90440 US Hwy 83), in the Sandhills near the Niobrara. 22 full-hookup pull-through sites, most 50-amp, pull-throughs 80-100 ft; big-rig friendly. Showers, laundry, restrooms. Open May-October. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Hastings Campground & RV Park
note: Family-owned year-round RV park on the edge of Hastings, surrounded by cornfields. Full hookups (20/30/50 amp) on spaces 7-50, level gravel pull-through sites (no backing in). Laundry, game room (arcade/pool), WiFi; near Hastings Museum and Kool-Aid museum. Open all year; nightly/weekly/monthly. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: High Plains Homestead
note: Small RV park / glampground on a 40-acre homestead in the Pine Ridge badlands near Crawford and Fort Robinson, backing to the Oglala National Grassland. A handful of full-hookup back-in/pull-through sites (30/50 amp); call ahead, few spots. Seasonal (roughly mid-May to mid-October). Also listed on Hipcamp. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Highway 81 RV Park
note: RV-only park just off Hwy 81 south of Columbus, 22 level full-hookup sites (18 pull-through), 30/50 amp, laundry. Open roughly April 1-November. Right by the highway so road noise is a factor for light sleepers. RV Life 5 stars, $. --Claude

name: I-80 Lakeside Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 850a48c (Add 33 NE private campgrounds (commercial RV parks + 1 Hipcamp)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: 126-site campground on a private lake between I-80 (Exit 179) and the Platte River north of North Platte. Many full-hookup (30/50-amp W/E/S) sites plus pull-throughs that fit any size RV; primitive/tent areas too; swim/fish lake with beaches. Full season Apr 15-Sep 30 (reduced amenities off-season). RV $35 W/E, $41 full hookup. RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Jeske's Over The Hill Campground
note: Small private campground next to Carhenge north of Alliance; ~15 all-drive-through (pull-through) gravel sites, of which ~4 full-hookup (W/E/S, 50-amp) and the rest water/electric only. Free WiFi. Tents welcome too. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Lakeshore RV Park
note: Small family-run, all-ages park on the west side of Geneva by a small tree-lined pond, open year-round. About 10 full-hookup pull-through sites (~80 ft, 50 amp); no restrooms/showers. Nightly stays $30 (pay 5 nights, get 2 free); no monthly rate. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Land-N-Park RV Park
note: Small in-town RV park in Nemaha (506 4th St), ~1 mi west of the Missouri River near Indian Cave State Park. ~7-8 level gravel pull-through sites, 30/50-amp electric, water and sewer; handles rigs to ~45 ft. Year-round. No standalone website (active Facebook page, listed on directories). RV Life 5★, $$. --Claude

name: Lil' Village Campground
note: Small in-town RV park on a former empty lot in Platte Center, opened ~2020 by Joe & Deb Hanson. 9 full-hookup sites (50/30/110), water and sewer, on-site laundry; pads fit rigs up to ~43 ft. Open year-round; ~$35/night. RV Life 5★, $$. --Claude

name: Lincoln Highway RV Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 850a48c (Add 33 NE private campgrounds (commercial RV parks + 1 Hipcamp)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Family-owned RV park built around three small private lakes on West Hwy 30 west of North Platte; fishing, swimming and non-motorized boats. Extra-large pull-throughs with 30/50-amp electric, water, sewer and wifi; on-site store, laundry, propane. Year-round; nightly $30 (waterfront sites cost more). RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Long's Creek RV Park
note: Private RV park behind the Auburn Inn Motel on US-75 at the north edge of Auburn; ~37 sites with concrete pads, full hookups (30/50-amp), on-site laundromat. Near Auburn's Rotary Lake and walking trail (lake is across a developed strip, not waterfront). RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: McGreer Camper Park
note: Private RV park ~1 mi from Big Springs at the I-80 exit, set on a working farm. 28 full-hookup pull-through sites (30/50 amp, water, sewer) with big-rig access to 40 ft, plus non-hookup and tent sites; clean showers, laundry, dump station. Year-round. Satellite at 41.0468,-102.0683 shows the site cluster set inland by the interchange, no water nearby. RV Life 4.0★ ($$). --Claude

name: Niobrara Valley Vineyards Dark Sky RV Park
note: Vineyard RV park on a working cattle ranch in the Sandhills south of Nenzel, near Merritt Reservoir and a certified Dark Sky area. 12 pull-through sites with 20/30/50-amp electric and water (no sewer; dump station 7 mi away in Cody Park); pull-through rate $38/night includes 2 wine tastings. Seasonal May 1-Nov 22 (off-season by phone), 7-day stay limit. RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Patterson Harbor Marina Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 850a48c (Add 33 NE private campgrounds (commercial RV parks + 1 Hipcamp)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Privately-operated marina campground on the south side of Harlan County Lake (USACE reservoir). ~80 gravel sites with 30-amp ($40) and 50-amp ($45) water hookups + dump station; both transient and annual sites, but primarily FCFS overnight with a 2-week limit and weekly rates. Full-service marina (gas, bait, rentals). RV Life 4★, $. --Claude

name: Pavement Ends Campground
note: Small privately-owned ranch campground 1 mi east of Agate Fossil Beds NM, NW-corner Nebraska. 8 full-hookup pull-through (drive-thru) sites, 30/50 amp, water hydrants, dump station, tent sites, flush toilets; pads easily fit a 23-ft rig. ~$10-25/night. Book by phone or Facebook message (no online reservation system; reservations rarely needed outside Agate events). Remote dark-sky high-plains setting. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Pioneer Village Campground
note: Campground at Harold Warp Pioneer Village, a long-running tourist-attraction museum (open seasonally May-Oct). ~48 RV sites (a 2025 expansion adds 25 more: 14 pull-through, 11 back-in), grass/gravel with mature shade trees; 20/30/50-amp, partial and full hookups (8 full-hookup sites), pull-throughs, sites accommodate rigs up to 50 ft; restrooms, showers, dump station, laundry, Wi-Fi. Camping payment includes one free museum admission (camp tonight, re-enter tomorrow). Reservable by phone/website. RV Life 4 star, $$. --Claude

name: Point of Rocks Motel and RV Park
note: Long-running motel + full-service RV park on US-30 in Potter, quiet country setting (BNSF tracks nearby). Full hookups (electric/water/sewer), restrooms/showers, pets OK. Open to transient overnighters. RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: Prairie Oasis Campground & Cabins
note: 31 gravel pull-through sites, all full or partial hookups: all 50-amp sites are full-hookup (water/sewer/electric) up to 90 ft; 30-amp sites are 60 ft, 13 with full hookups and the rest water+electric. Big-rig friendly, 100% pull-through, just off I-80 exit 342 at Henderson. Updated restrooms/showers, dump station, free WiFi, underground storm shelter, camping cabin, small fishing pond with free paddleboats, playground, sand volleyball. Reservations recommended but not required; transient nightly park. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: R U Lost - RV Lots
note: Small owner-run (Jim & Colleen Pope) RV park in the village of Nemaha; 7 full-hookup sites (30/50A, water, sewer), 4 pull-thru, dump station. Despite the 'RV Lots' name it is nightly transient ($20/night; also weekly/monthly), year-round, reserve by phone. No restrooms; underground storm shelter, fire pits, on-site gardens guests may use. Fills with Cooper Nuclear Station outage workers on monthly stays but books nightly. RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: River Hideout Campground
note: Private campground on the former KOA site near Valentine (39761 River Drive), reopened 2024 along the Niobrara River. ~20 sites with 30-amp electric, water, limited sewer; tent sites. Dump and flush toilet; bathhouse under construction. Drive-in, open seasonally. Niobrara is ~200m off behind a continuous treeline, so sites are not on the water. RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Rosebud Casino RV Park
note: Small developed RV park at Rosebud Casino on US-83 at the NE/SD line north of Valentine. 10 designated drive-in sites with water and 50/30-amp/120V electric, free WiFi; no on-site dump (nearest in Valentine). Year-round; pay at the Fuel Plaza, stays over 3 days need approval. Real RV sites, not a casino parking lot. RV Life 4 star, $. --Claude

name: Sharp's Outfitters
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 850a48c (Add 33 NE private campgrounds (commercial RV parks + 1 Hipcamp)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Family-run river outfitter and campground on the Niobrara near Sparks, operating since 1974; popular tubing/kayaking put-in (has designated party areas plus quieter wooded/family sections). 140+ drive-in sites, electric hookups ($15 + camp fee), showers/flush toilets; some sites on the riverbank, others on a 10-ft embankment above. Seasonal ~May 1–Oct 15; reservations taken by phone. RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Sunset Motel & RV Park
note: In-town motel-plus-RV-park on E US Hwy 2 in Alliance; ~20 RV sites (about 14 full-hookup, 6 water/electric), 30/50-amp, WiFi, laundry, indoor pool shared with the motel. Open year-round. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: The 4 Seasons RV Park
note: Year-round big-rig-friendly park on US-183 just south of Elm Creek, a half mile off I-80. 43 full-hookup pull-through sites (30/50 amp, water, sewer), long enough to leave a tow attached; laundry, game room, dog park. Accepts Passport America. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Winsor Cove
note: Privately operated campground inside Haworth Park in Bellevue, along the Missouri River. ~130 sites with electric + water hookups (no sewer; dump station on site), nightly transient reservations ($30 50-amp / lower for 30-amp). Satellite at 41.1330,-95.8830 shows the RV loops set well back from the river behind an open field, boat-ramp lot and levee — no shoreline pads, so not waterfront. RV Life 4.0★ ($$). --Claude

name: Buckley Park Campground
note: Free (donation) city campground in Stromsburg's Buckley Park along the Big Blue River/Prairie Creek, just off Hwy 81. ~dozen RV spots with 30/50-amp electric (no water/sewer at site), flush toilets, can take big rigs; connected to city park (pool, disc golf, courts). BNSF tracks adjacent. RV Life 4.5★, $. --Claude

name: Lincoln Park (Superior)
note: City of Superior park on the west edge of town behind the high school (840 W. 4th St). ~20 level concrete RV pads with 30-amp electric, picnic table and grill; central water (not at each pad), restrooms, dump station, 14-day limit. Free/donation. Open seasonally. Drive-in, big-rig friendly. Reclassified to local (municipal park). RV Life 4.5 star, $. --Claude

name: Maskenthine Lake Recreation Area
note: Recreation area on a 100-acre lake managed by the Lower Elkhorn Natural Resources District, 10 mi east of Norfolk. 33 electric camper pads near the swim beach, reservable online ($25/night, 60 days out); shower house, plus FCFS tent area. Water on ~May 1–Oct 1. RV Life 5★, $$. --Claude

name: Riverside Park (Hebron)
note: City of Hebron municipal park. 15 utility hookup sites (50-amp; 10 cement pads + 6 grass pads), water, restrooms/showers, dump station at south end, picnic shelter. $25/night (3/2025), 2-week stay limit. Reservations by phone (402-768-6322); some directories list it as FCFS. Near the Little Blue River and Hebron Sports & Rec complex. RV Life 4.5 star, $. --Claude

name: Scenic Park Campground
note: City-owned (South Sioux City) year-round campground in a 133-acre riverfront park along the Missouri River, across from Sioux City IA. ~135 RV sites from electric to full-hookup, pull-throughs available, plus cabins/tent sites. Full park amenities (pool, YMCA, trails, ballfields). Reserve by phone, no online booking. RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: TaHaZouka Park Campground
note: City of Norfolk park campground in a heavily shaded area between the Elkhorn River and a lagoon, with direct access to the Cowboy Trail. 18 concrete pads (one ADA), each with 20/30/50-amp power; restrooms with showers, dump station, free wifi. Year-round (reduced services mid-Oct to mid-Apr). $28/night with pad+electric, $18 without. RV Life 4★, $. --Claude

name: Thedford City Park
note: Village of Thedford park at the US-83/NE-2 junction in the Sandhills. 4 drive-in RV spots with 30-amp electric (water nearby), plus tent sites; donation box on site. Restrooms, dump. Open seasonally, FCFS. Reclassified to local (municipal park). RV Life 5 star, $. --Claude

Sites set back in forested terrain, not on the lake. No daily park-entry fee in Iowa. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

About half the sites are FCFS self-registration, rest reservable on ReserveAmerica. Campground is in upland timber, not on the river. No daily park-entry fee in Iowa. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

waterfront_evidence: audited 2026-06 (IA state evidence-gate sweep, commit 5da9588); waterfront 'not waterfront' set by that satellite/per-site-map sweep (per-entry bucket detail in the commit message)

name: Brushy Creek State Recreation Area - Beach Campground

NOTE: per DNR, the North/Middle(Beach)/South loops are partly closed for 2026 renovation (Beach/South not expected open until ~Labor Day 2026) - verify before relying on it. waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'pond' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Malvern park a few blocks from Main Street on Boehner Pond, on the Wabash Trace Nature Trail. ~13 sites with 50-amp electric and water on many (sites 1-7 ~60-ft back-ins, 8-13 ~70-ft pull-throughs), picnic tables, fire rings, pit toilets; dump station a few blocks away at another city park. Reserve by calling Malvern City Hall. RV Life 4.5 star / $. --Claude

name: Botna Bend Park
note: Pottawattamie County Conservation Board park outside Hancock on Hwy G-30, beside the Nishnabotna River (camp loops set back behind a riverbank treeline) and known for its bison and elk herd. 40 sites with 30-amp electric, $20/night; modern shower house and dump station (close late Oct/early Nov), park otherwise open year round. RV Life 5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Brown's Lake - Bigelow Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Woodbury County Conservation park on 600-acre Brown's Lake, 1 mi W of Salix; rebuilt with concrete roads and 40 full-hookup (electric/water/sewer) sites sized for big rigs plus 10 primitive tent sites ($30 full hookup). Dump, showers, beach, boat ramp, fishing dock, WiFi. Open May 1 to Oct 31, FCFS only. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Clay County Fairgrounds
note: County fairgrounds RV camping in Spencer, run by the Clay County Fair Association on the Clay County Fairgrounds & Events Center grounds. Per the operator's own Non-Fair Time Camping page, camping is open to ordinary travelers April-October (not fair-only) on roughly 15 acres; FCFS, no reservations. Tiered nightly rates: $50 w/ utilities+sewer, $35 w/ utilities, $20 no utilities. Big-rig friendly (sites listed to ~35 ft; easily fits a 23-ft rig); back-in, no pull-throughs. Electric (30/50-amp) with water/sewer full-hookup sites available; dump station; restrooms with hot showers. Open campfires prohibited. RV Life 3.5 stars / $. Seasonal (April-October), not winter; year-round in-season transient camping, not fair-week-only. --Claude

name: Cold Springs Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Cass County Conservation Board park (105 acres) just S of Lewis on a no-wake lake. 26 electric sites + 20 primitive; self-register, FCFS (no reservations). Trailer dump station, modern showerhouse, swimming area, boat ramp. Open Apr 1-Oct 31 (weather permitting); loop sites fit a 23-ft rig. Lake renovated/drained Aug 2025 and refilling. RV Life 4 star / $. --Claude

name: Crawford Creek Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Ida County Conservation Board park 3 mi south of Battle Creek on a lake. 34 water+electric sites (upper loop 50 amp, lower loop 30 amp) plus 4 electric tent sites; modern restrooms/showers both loops, dump station. $17-18/night, FCFS (no online reservations), season April-October. RV Life 3.5 star / $. --Claude

name: Dickson Timber
note: Carroll County Conservation forest preserve, 155 acres of oak/hickory near Glidden. 12 regular electric drive-in sites plus 8 electric equestrian sites; pit toilet, shelter, hiking/horse trails. FCFS, no reservations. Open mid-Apr to mid-Oct. Upland wooded sites, no water. Lightly reviewed (RV Life unrated / $). --Claude

name: Douma Park Campground
note: O'Brien County Conservation Board. 14 full-hookup sites (water/sewer/electric), cement pads, in a former gravel-pit park with a 10-acre fishing pond. Modern shower building, dump station, resident host. Open May 1-Oct 15. Reservable online via mycountyparks; $20/night + fee. Campground loop is set back from the pond behind a treeline. RV Life 4 stars / $. --Claude

name: Elbert Park
note: City of Pocahontas park campground on N Main St, in town. Electric and full-hookup sites, modern restrooms, shelter, playground; walking distance to restaurants/pool/ball fields. Drive-in, fits a small Class C. Lightly reviewed (RV Life unrated / $). --Claude

name: Five Island Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Emmetsburg campground on the SE shore of Five Island Lake. 21 sites, all full hookup (30/50 amp electric, water, sewer); loop overlooks the lake across an open grass shelf. Self-pay at dropbox, FCFS (no reservations). RV Life 4 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Glenwood Lake Park
note: City of Glenwood lake park, ~20 sites: 12 full-hookup with 30/50A, ~8 electric-only 30A; big rigs fit. Open March-October, FCFS. Hot showers, restrooms. Pad rows sit west of Keg Creek Lake, set back across a road and vegetated buffer (not on the shore). RV Life 5 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Grant Park
note: Sac County Conservation Board park on the North Raccoon River, 1.5 mi N of Auburn (RV Life lists it as 'Grant County Park'; real name Grant Park). Area 1: 19 modern sites, all 50-amp — 9 full-hookup (water/sewer/electric), 10 water+electric; plus 2 primitive tent areas. FCFS, no reservations. Dump station, showers/restrooms, resident host; park closes 10:30 p.m. Season May 1 to mid-October. RV Life unrated (0 stars) / $. --Claude

name: Great Western Park
note: Carroll County Conservation Board park on the SW edge of Manning with a 12-acre lake. 16 electric/water sites (30/50 amp); FCFS, no reservations. Modern restroom/shower house, shelter, boat ramp, disc golf, shooting range. Camp loop sits back across grass from the lake (not on the water). Drive-in loop accommodates a 23-ft rig. RV Life unrated (0 star) / $. --Claude

name: Hagge Park
note: Sac County Conservation Board park (CCB HQ) on the North Raccoon River, 2 mi S of Sac City. 13 modern sites with water, sewer and 50-amp electric; also primitive sites and a seasonal cabin. Indoor restroom at shelterhouse. FCFS, no reservations. Open May 1 through 3rd weekend in October. River is tree-screened from the sites. RV Life 5 star / price 1. --Claude

name: Hawkeye Point Campground
note: Osceola County campground at Iowa's highest natural point (1,670 ft), opened 2011. 12 sites all with electric and water, 4 with concrete pads (added 2022). FCFS, $20/night, open year-round. Modern restrooms, shelter house, playground. RV Life 4.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Hills Park Campground
note: City of Sheldon park; 26 sites with electric + water hookups (20/30/40-amp), accommodates rigs to 45 ft, RV dump station, restrooms, disc golf. FCFS, no reservations. $20/night RV. RV Life 4.5 star, $. --Claude

name: Hillview Recreation Area
note: Plymouth County Conservation Board HQ park W of Hinton; Deer Ridge (27 sites, water + 50-amp electric) and Bur Oak (18 electric sites) campgrounds in an upland valley loop that fits a 23-ft rig (Beaver Creek is tent-only). Modern shower house, playground. Open May to mid-October, $15-$20. FCFS except the final Halloween-at-Hillview weekend (reservable the Tuesday after Labor Day). RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Koser Spring Lake Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Cherokee park on Spring Lake with a 1.1-mile paved lakeshore trail. ~48 sites with 50-amp electric, water and sewer hookups (full hookups), nearby showers/restrooms; accommodates rigs up to 60 ft. Playground, fishing, shelters. RV Life 4 star / $. --Claude

name: Lake Pahoja Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Lyon County Conservation park on Lake Pahoja, 4 mi S + 2 mi W of Larchwood; 71 modern electric sites across Lakeview (42) and Prairie View (29, 8 with water) campgrounds, modern comfort/shower stations, dump, swimming beach, boat ramp. FCFS with self-registration within an hour of arrival. Open seasonally. RV Life 4 star, $$. --Claude

name: Lake View Campground (Lake Icaria)
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Adams County Conservation Board, on the peninsula of Lake Icaria. Lake Icaria Rec Area has 153 graveled sites across Lake View + Timber Ridge loops (63 full-hookup, 23 water/electric, 43 electric, 24 tent); fire pit + picnic table per site. Beach, boat ramp/rental, dump station, showers. Big-rig sites available. Reservable online (mycountyparks.com). RV Life 4.5 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Laurens City Campground
note: City of Laurens municipal campground in Sportsman's Park (a.k.a. Time To Spare Campground). 5 sites with 30-amp electric, water, and cable; listed big-rig friendly, so a 23-ft Class C fits. Dump station on site; park also has restrooms, a pool, and stocked fishing ponds. ~$15/night. No online reservation portal — call the city (712-841-4526) to book/pay; small informally-managed park. Open dates not published (confirm by phone). RV Life 7.0/10, price $. --Claude

name: Little Sioux Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'pond' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Woodbury County Conservation Board; two loops - Bellamy (42 electric+water) and Riverside (53 full-hookup) plus 14 non-electric tent sites; 20/30/50A, dump station, showers. FCFS, no reservations. Open May 1-Oct 31. $30 full / $25 elec+water / $17 non-elec. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Littlefield Recreation Area
note: Audubon County Conservation Board park; ~100 modern sites across several loops (Old, A, B, C). All sites have electric + water; 15 full-hookup sites, Loop A is 50-amp full hookups, most loops 30-amp. Big-rig friendly — reviewers report 33-ft fifth wheels and 26-ft trailers on level sites; 23-ft Class C fits easily. Camping season Apr 15-Nov 15. FCFS only — ACCB does not take campsite reservations. Shower house on site; 70-acre lake with 400-ft swim beach. Water hookup is across the pad, bring extra hose; alt entrance via Quail Ave is easier for big rigs. RV Life 4.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Lost Island - Huston Park
note: Palo Alto County Conservation Board campground on the NE shore of Lost Island Lake (1,200 acres), N of Ruthven. 24 sites: most electric + water, some full hookup (electric/water/sewer). Hot showers, flush toilets, dump station ($3). Swimming beach, boat ramp, docks, nature center. Open ~May 15-Oct 15 (weather dependent). FCFS, no reservations taken ($25 elec/water, $30 full). RV Life unrated (0 star, $); RV Life geoloc was mis-pinned in farm fields ~0.7 km S, corrected to the lakeside camping loop. --Claude

name: Mike Macke Memorial Park
note: City of Lake City park west of town on County Rd N37 along Lake Creek. 5 RV sites with 30-amp electric + water (also usable as tent sites); a 23-ft Class C fits the small grassy loop. Fire pits, playground, well-maintained restroom with showers; no dump station noted. FCFS — fees/donations collected on site ($10/night RV, $5 tent). No published season or night limit. RV Life unrated (0 stars) / $. --Claude

name: Mill Creek Park
note: Despite the 'Mill Creek State Park' name, run by O'Brien County Conservation Board (since 1975) on Paullina Lake. 48 sites with 20/30/50A electric + water; dump station, showers, swimming beach, boat ramp. Open May 1-Oct 15. Reservable online via mycountyparks (some FCFS); $25/night + fees, 14-day limit. Campground sits back from the lake behind a treeline. RV Life 5 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Missouri Valley City Park Campground
note: City of Missouri Valley park campground; 25 sites with 30/50-amp electric + water, 12 pull-thrus, dump station. FCFS self-registration, no reservations. Open ~Apr 15-Oct 15; 14-day stay limit. RV Life 4 star, $. --Claude

name: Monona County Fairgrounds
note: Monona County fairgrounds. Off-season RV camping any date outside fair week (fair ~July 7-12); water + electric, no sewer. $25/night, reserve via online request form. Site count not published. RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Mormon Trail Park
