waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Adair County Conservation Board park 1.5 mi SE of Bridgewater, on 35-acre Mormon Trail Lake (170-acre park). Electric camping sites, modern shower house/restroom, shelter, playground, swimming, kayak/sailing, fishing. Camping loop sits on a bench above the lake. Reservable online (mycountyparks.com). Lightly reviewed (RV Life unrated / $). --Claude

name: Nelson Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Crawford County Conservation Board park 4 mi west of Dow City on 11-acre Stern Lake. 85 total sites across 4 areas: 55 electric on Modern Hill, 10 tent on Tent Hill, 10 at the Beach campground (swimming beach), 4 at the boat ramp; plus 4 primitive cabins. Modern hill has hot showers, flush toilets, dump station. $12-17/night, FCFS self-registration, season mid-April through October. RV Life 4 star / $. --Claude

name: Nodaway Valley County Park
note: Page County Conservation park on US-71 north of Clarinda, 164-acre timber. 13 sites: sites 1-2 primitive, 3-9 electric+water (50/30/20A), 10-13 electric+water pull-throughs (30/20A); plus a cabin. Shower-house/restroom and dump station on grounds. FCFS via self-registration kiosk (cabin requires advance booking). RV Life 4 star / price 1. --Claude

name: Oak Grove & Big Sioux Park
note: Sioux County Conservation campgrounds (Turkey Ridge and Deer Run) 7 mi N of Hawarden along the Big Sioux River; 20/30/50-amp electric and some full-hookup sites that fit a 23-ft rig ($22 electric, $26 full, $17 tent). Boat ramp to the Big Sioux River, but camp loops sit back in the wooded river bottom, not at the waterline. Open Apr 15 to second Friday of October; select sites (Turkey Ridge 12-15, Deer Run 19-22 and 51-53) reservable online, rest FCFS. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Pierce Creek Recreation Area
note: Page County Conservation Board park; north campground has 18 sites (sites 1-4 & 12-18 electric+water $15, sites 5-11 primitive $6), 50/30/20-amp receptacles, shower building with restrooms. FCFS via self-registration kiosk (cabin reservable by phone, 14-day notice). Drive-in teardrop loop, no posted max length; open spacing fits a 23-ft rig. Campground sits in woods ~0.7 km north of the 30-acre lake, not on the water. A separate equestrian campground (10 sites) is a distinct loop. RV Life 4 stars, $. --Claude

name: Pioneer Park
note: Page County Conservation Board park ~halfway between Shenandoah and Clarinda on Hwy 2. 12 sites: sites 3-12 electric/water (50/30/20-amp, $15), sites 1-2 primitive ($6); fire ring + picnic table each. Restroom with shower in campground. FCFS via self-registration kiosk. Open ~Apr 15-Nov 1. 3-acre pond on site but camping area set back behind a grass/treeline buffer (satellite at 40.7378,-95.2908), so not waterfront. RV Life 4 stars / $ (mislabeled 'city'; it's a county park). Max RV length not posted; open grass loop fits a 23-ft rig. --Claude

name: Pottawattamie County Fairgrounds Campground
note: County-owned fairgrounds campground in Avoca (Pottawattamie County), open year-round. ~200 electric sites plus 34 full-hookup (water/sewer) sites including 17 newer pull-through 50-amp sites for big rigs; level open grounds easily fit a 23-ft rig. Showers, restrooms, sewer dump available. First-come, first-served — no reservations; monthly rates offered. RV Life 4 stars, $ price level. Just off I-80. Camping is normal year-round transient, not event-only. --Claude

name: Rivers Bend Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'pond' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Rock Valley campground on 94 acres ~1 mi out of town near the Rock River; 81 sites total with 19 full-hookup and 55 electric-only (50-amp), plus tent sites, set among several small fishing ponds, big-rig friendly. Dump station, showers, playground. Open May through October; reservable by phone. RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Riverside Campground (Sac City)
note: City of Sac City campground at 302 Park Ave, by Chautauqua Park / Sac County Fairgrounds along the North Raccoon River. 20 electric+water sites (30/50A), level pull-through and back-in; tent sites too. Dump station, showers. FCFS honor-system pay, 14-day max stay. River channel is tree-screened from the loop. RV Life 5 star / price 1. --Claude

name: Riverside Park (Carroll County)
note: Carroll County Conservation Board park near Coon Rapids (rvlife mis-tags it 'state' - it is county). 20+ electric sites (30/50 amp) with water, shower house, dump station; paved trail along the Middle Raccoon River, kayak/canoe access. Pads sit well back from the river (woods/open ground buffer). Self-register, FCFS. Open mid-Apr to mid-Oct. RV Life 5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Sam Robinson Memorial Park
note: City of Sibley park by the municipal golf course and pool. 18 numbered sites: 8-14 are 50A, 1-7 and 15-18 are 30A; full-hookup $30 or electric+water $25. Season Apr 15-Oct 15. Reserve and pay online in advance (no on-site host). Restrooms, showers, laundry. RV Life 4 stars / $. --Claude

name: Schaben Park Campground
note: Harrison County Conservation Board, between Woodbine and Dunlap (2 mi off Hwy 30 on gravel). 12 electric RV sites (20/30A) with water hydrants, plus 3 tent sites, around a 6-acre electric-motor fishing lake with boat ramp. Dump station, shower house. Open Apr 1-Oct 31. FCFS, no reservations; $25/night RV. Pads sit on a bench above the pond behind the loop road. RV Life 5 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Scharnberg Park
note: Clay County Conservation Board park on a 15-acre pond near Everly. 61 electric drive-in sites (20/30/50 amp, rural water; 21 with sewer) plus 5 primitive sites; each site ~54 ft long, easily fits a 23-ft rig and big rigs. Beach, fishing, hiking/exercise trails, concession with boat rentals. Open ~May 1-Oct 15. 37 sites reservable online up to a year ahead via mycountyparks.com, rest FCFS; 3-night minimum on reservations (5 nights on Memorial Day/July 4/Labor Day). Camping fees $25/night electric, $30/night w/ sewer (eff. 7/1/2025). Satellite shows RV loops set back from the pond behind a treed shoreline buffer - not on-water. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Schildberg Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Atlantic park in a reclaimed quarry with 3 lakes; ~20 sites (expanding), full hookups, shower house, restrooms, dog park. Reservable online via Campspot. Open year-round. RV Life 4.5 star / price 2. --Claude

name: Silver Lake City Park - East
note: City of Lake Park-operated RV campground on the east shore of Silver Lake; 8 electric-only RV sites (RV-only, no tents), level gravel pads, 6 pull-throughs, ~35 ft max length (easily fits a 23-ft rig). Dump station, showers/toilets, pet-friendly; city pool and playground within walking distance. Season Apr 15-Oct 15; daily/weekly/monthly rates (~$25/night reported, cash). Reserve by calling the camp host (no online booking). Satellite at 43.44665,-95.32745 shows ~6-8 RVs parked on the gravel loop, set back behind a continuous shoreline treeline (no on-water pads confirmed). RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Snyder Bend Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Woodbury County Conservation park on Snyder Bend Lake near Salix; traditional sites with water + electric that fit a 23-ft rig ($25 electric/water, $17 non-electric). Pressurized water and campground open May 1 to Oct 31. FCFS with self-registration. RV Life 4 star, $. --Claude

name: Southwood Conservation Area
note: Woodbury County Conservation Board. 19 electric sites (20/30/50A) + 12 tent + 2 cabins; primitive-to-50A. FCFS only, self-registration (no reservations). Dump station, modern showers/restrooms, two stocked ponds, boat ramp, trails. Open May 1-Oct 31. $25 elec / $17 tent. Has equestrian trails but general RV camping. RV Life 5 star, $. --Claude

name: Straight Park Campground
note: City of Fonda park campground on the SW edge of town. Camper sites with electric + water hookups (picnic table + fire ring each); ~10 sites with full hookups (water/sewer). Also 3 rentable camper cabins. Park has a pond with lighted fountain, playground, nature trail, open-air shelter, restrooms/showers, accessible dock. RV/camper sites are FCFS (no reservations); cabins reserved via City Hall (712-288-4466). RV Life unrated (0 star, $). --Claude

name: Sunnyside Park
note: City of Atlantic municipal park (Sunnyside Park, on the Cass County Fairgrounds grounds). ~20 defined electric/water RV pads (20/30/50 amp); reservable (not fair-only camping). Sanitary dump, restrooms with showers; in-town park with pool, ball fields, disc golf. Open ~Apr-Nov; drive-in back-in/pull pads fit a 23-ft rig. Nishnabotna River is well west and screened by fields, so sites are not on the water. RV Life 4 star / $. --Claude

name: Swan Lake State Park
note: Carroll County Conservation Board park (county-run despite the 'State Park' name). 115+ sites: 45 full-hookup (water/electric/sewer), 52 electric+water 30/50-amp, 21 electric-only 30-amp, plus a large non-electric/tent area; reviewers report 28-35 ft rigs fitting (gravel, some grade — bring levelers), so a 23-ft Class C is fine. Open mid-April to late October. Campsites are FCFS (no reservations taken; only shelters are reservable). 14-night limit (designated long-term sites excepted). Two shower houses, restrooms, dump station ($3 non-registered). RV Life 8.0/10, price $. --Claude

name: Tjossem Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'pond' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: O'Brien County Conservation Board park 1 mi S + 0.5 mi E of Primghar. 10 full-hookup sites (water/sewer + 20/30/50A), upgraded 2023; sites 1-9 run 42'-50' long (fits a 23-ft rig easily). $20/night, reservable online via mycountyparks (by night, month, or season). Open May 1-Oct 15. Primitive restroom, shelter, fishing ponds, trail to Primghar. RV Life 5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Veteran's Memorial Park Campground
note: City of Orange City park. 7 RV sites (30x12 ft concrete pads) + 1 tent site: sites A-E full hookup 30/50A, F-G electric/water. Reservable online up to 14 days, non-refundable. Showers/restrooms on site. Open May 1-Nov 1. $20/night. Adjacent pickleball, skate park, ballfields. RV Life 5 star, $. --Claude

name: Westside Park
note: Small City of Rock Rapids park at the west edge of town (1200 1st Ave, Hwy 9/75 entrance); 8 camper pads with electric, restroom/shower house, picnic shelter, playground, sand volleyball and disc golf. Drive-in pads fit a 23-ft rig. Rock River nearby but not at the sites. Contact city for rates/reservations. RV Life 3 star, $. --Claude

name: Willow Creek Campground (Le Mars Municipal Park)
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'pond' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Le Mars municipal park. 62 RV sites (concrete pads) + 7 tent sites; ~half electric/water, half full-hookup 30/50A; dedicated beach sites on the 3.6-acre pond. Showers, laundry, renovated clubhouse. Open May-Sept. $25 (elec/water) / $30 (full hookup) / $35 (beach). RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Willow Lake Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Harrison County Conservation Board, 6 mi NW of Woodbine on a 27-acre fishing lake; 41 sites with electric + water (20/30/50A), shower houses, swimming beach, boat ramp; no on-site dump (use Woodbine/Pisgah). FCFS, no reservations. Open ~Apr 1-Oct 31. $25/night. RV Life 3.5 star, $. --Claude

name: Wolden Recreation Area Campground
note: Emmet County Conservation, on 467-acre High Lake 6 mi E of Wallingford. 90 electric sites with grills/picnic tables, water hydrants, shower house, dump station, playgrounds, lake access and beach nearby. Camping area is set back inland from the shore (treeline screen). Open Apr-Oct. Reservable online (mycountyparks.com). RV Life 4.5 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Yellow Smoke Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 292932f (Add 59 IA local campgrounds, NW Iowa (county conservation + city) [1/4); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Crawford County Conservation Board park on 40-acre Yellow Smoke Lake. ~76 electric/water sites total (61-site main campground + 15-site north loop); gravel pads, big-rig friendly with sites up to ~55 ft and some pull-throughs, so a 23-ft rig fits easily. Dump station, modern restrooms/showers, on-site host. Open mid-April through October, $20/night, 14-day max stay. FCFS — no reservations (not on mycountyparks booking). RV Life 4 stars / $. --Claude

name: Ashton Wildwood Park Campground
note: Jasper County Conservation, N of Mingo. 12 primitive drive-in sites in oak-hickory woods; dry camping (no plumbing/water, vault toilet), picnic tables + fire rings. RV/tent, $12/night, 2-night min on weekends, reservable on mycountyparks.com. On-site astronomical observatory; dark-sky park. RV Life unrated, $. Wooded primitive sites, no waterfront. --Claude

name: Bells Mill Park
note: Hamilton County Conservation Board park on the Boone River (~6 mi NE of Stratford), gravel access. ~45 electric (20/30-amp) sites plus 4 primitive in an open lawn inside a river-bend loop (a continuous treeline screens the sites from the riverbank), open-air shelter, modern shower/restroom, playground. Reservable via mycountyparks.com. RV Life unrated / $. --Claude

name: Bessman-Kemp Park
note: Hardin County Conservation park on 71 acres along the Iowa River, ~1/2 mi west of Alden. 12 electric drive-in campsites; vault restroom, shelter; no dump/showers noted. FCFS. Season roughly Apr-Sep. Carry-down river access for fishing/kayaking; camp spurs sit in the river-bend grass behind a continuous treeline. RV Life 2 star, $. --Claude

name: Bobwhite Park
note: Wayne County Conservation Board park (named "Bobwhite State Park" but county-run, NOT Iowa DNR) on ~680 acres beside Bobwhite Lake near Allerton. ~32 sites, 19 with 20-amp electric; drive-in. FCFS, no reservations; ~$20/night. Season roughly Apr 1-Dec 1. Modern shower house. Lakeside camp node sits back behind a treeline. RV Life 4 star, $ (lightly reviewed). --Claude

name: Boone County Fairgrounds Campground
note: Boone County Fairgrounds (1601 Industrial Park Rd, Boone) with a real year-round/by-reservation RV campground separate from fair week: 80+ electric sites (some full/partial hookup), shower house, dump station. Reservable online via Firefly; available outside scheduled events (gravel/open-field setting). RV Life 5 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Burt Lake Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Kossuth County Conservation Board park on Burt Lake, NW of Swea City / N of Armstrong. ~14 electric sites total (upper level ~8 at 30/50-amp, lower level ~6 electric); pit latrines/porta-potties, no dump station, shelter house. ~$15 electric/$10 tent, ~Apr 15 to end of Oct. Reservable online via mycountyparks. RV Life unrated (0 star), $. --Claude

name: Casey City Park
note: City of Casey park campground. ~25 electric sites on grassy/drive-through pads, flush toilets, free showers, drinking water, dump station, playground. Open May 1-Oct 1. FCFS, quiet overnight stop. RV Life unrated (0 star), $. --Claude

name: Chichaqua Bottoms Greenbelt
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Polk County Conservation greenbelt along the Skunk River bottoms near Maxwell. Drive-in electric (20/30-amp, some 50-amp) and non-electric campsites; $25 electric / $20 non-electric. FCFS (pay on-site; availability shown on mycountyparks.com). Season Apr 1-Nov 30. Sites set among restored oxbow channels; separate walk-in youth area is not this entry. RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Cinder Path RV Park
note: Small city-run RV park at the Humeston trailhead of the Cinder Path state trail (in-town municipal lot, not a Wayne County Conservation park). ~14 sites, 30-amp electric, potable water, dump station, restrooms/showers; $20/night. Call to reserve; book ahead (limited spots). Small in-town lot so big-rig fit is tight for a 23-ft rig but workable. RV Life 5 star, $. --Claude

name: Crystal Lake Park
note: Hancock County Conservation Board park on Crystal Lake (1045 320th St, mailing Forest City). 65 electric and 22 primitive sites, drinking water, restrooms, showers, dump station; public boat launch and swimming beach. RV pad rows sit back from the lake behind a road/treeline (beach and ramp are separate). Season May 1-Oct 15, 14-day max; all sites FCFS. $15 electric / $10 non-electric. RV Life 5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Daisy Long Memorial Park
note: Hardin County Conservation park on the Iowa River ~1 mi E of Union (site of the 1856 grist mill). 35 electric sites, 30 & 50-amp; dump station, shower house. First-come first-served (FCFS) with on-site camp hosts (only the cabin is reservable); season ~Apr-Oct; ~$15. Sites accommodate ~25 ft, so a 23-ft Class C fits. RV Life 5/5, $. --Claude

name: Dakins Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Story County Conservation, N of Zearing. ~29 sites: main loop 11 full-hookup + 18 elec/water; east loop 8 elec/water + 2 primitive (vault toilet). Reservable on mycountyparks.com or FCFS; W/E ~$20-21, full hookup ~$24-25. 20-acre lake, fishing. Sites overlook the lake across open grass but sit back from the waterline. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Dakota City Memorial Park & Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Dakota City park on the Des Moines River. 14 water+electric sites, plus tent area, restrooms/showers, beach access. Camp loop sits on the riverbank just N of the road bridge. RV Life 3.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Dallas County Fairgrounds Campground
note: Dallas County Fair Association grounds in Adel. ~80 sites with 50-amp electric, water and sewer; year-round, open to transient RVers outside event weeks. Reservable via Campspot with day-of arrivals accommodated; $15/night self-pay. Near the North Raccoon River but camping is among the fair buildings, not on the water. RV Life 3.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Don Williams Recreation Area Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Boone County Conservation Board, 5 mi S of Ogden on 150-acre Don Williams Lake. 150 electric sites (50/30/110 amp) in Campgrounds A/B/C; fits RVs to ~40 ft; restrooms, showers, dump. Open Apr 15-Oct 15. A and B reservable on mycountyparks.com (14-night max), C is FCFS. $20/night. RV Life 4.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: East Lake Park County Park and RV Park
note: Clarke County Conservation Board RV park (built 2019) at East Lake, ~1/2 mi E of Osceola off US-34. 25 sites: 16 back-in + 9 pull-through, each with water and 20/30/50-amp electric. Generous spacing, big-rig friendly. Large modern restrooms with free showers; dump station away from sites. Reservable via mycountyparks.com; season ~Apr 15-Oct 15. RV pads set back NE of the 11-acre lake behind open ground/treeline. RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Eldred Sherwood Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Hancock County Conservation Board park on a 21-acre lake near Goodell. 40 full-service sites (50/30/20-amp electric, water, sewer) plus 5 primitive; $25 full-service / $10 primitive. Reservable online via mycountyparks.com. Season May 1-Oct 15. Modern shower house, dump. RV Life 4 star, $$. --Claude

name: Featherstone Memorial Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Calhoun County Conservation Board park on the NW shore of North Twin Lake, 51 acres near Manson. ~70+ sites with 30-amp electric + water; nightly ~$25, FCFS (no reservations, self-pay envelopes at entrance). Three shower/restroom houses, two shelters. Closed Dec-Apr. RV Life unrated (0 star), $. --Claude

name: Frank A. Gotch Park
note: Humboldt County Conservation Board park (state-owned land leased to county, run by Humboldt CCB) on the Des Moines River, 4 mi S of Humboldt. ~32 water+electric sites, FCFS (no reservations), $20/night. Wooded river park with bike-trail access; satellite shows camp area set back on a bench above the river behind timber, not on the bank. RV Life 5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Gowrie City RV Park
note: Small city-run camping area at Laurel Park (by the Southeast Valley Athletic Complex / pool and the Depot Museum), Gowrie. Up to 10 water + electric hookups; self-register, FCFS (or reserve ahead via the city clerk). Flat in-town municipal park, no dump/shower noted - basic overnight stop. Thinly documented (RV Life 2.5 stars, $, no real reviews). --Claude

name: Hickory Grove Park - Breezy Bay Campground
note: Story County Conservation. Breezy Bay modern campground, 43 sites (30A/50A electric); 12 reservable (open Nov 1 for next season, 5-day advance min), 30 FCFS. Water, shower house w/ flush toilets, dump. Open Apr-Oct, ~$20/night. 98-acre lake (no-wake/fishing) but the camp loop sits north of the lake behind a treeline buffer, not on the water. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Iowa River RV Park
note: City of Belmond RV park along the Iowa River (312/408 River Ave S). 19 RV sites: 11 full-hookup (water/sewer/electric, 5 pull-through) + 8 water/electric; small tent area. Modern toilet/shower building and a dump station near the entrance. Open ~Apr 15-Oct 15. Flat ~$20/site. Reserve by calling the city; adjacent River Park has a canoe/kayak launch and rentals. River channel is screened by a continuous treeline. RV Life 4 star, $. --Claude

name: Island Park Campground (Adel)
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Adel park on the North Raccoon River, 2400 Nantucket Rd. ~32 sites with 30-amp electric (most also water); large back-in sites plus a few pull-throughs, modern shower house, dump station with rinse hose. Phone reservations accepted (not posted online), otherwise FCFS; call ahead. ~$23-25/night. RV Life 5 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Jester Park
note: Polk County Conservation Board park on the W shore of Saylorville Lake. ~142 sites: 80 electric (17 reservable) + 62 non-electric (9 reservable), plus walk-in primitive and youth areas. Large/pull-through sites, big-rig friendly. Two showerhouses (mid-Apr-mid-Oct), dump station, restrooms. Reservable Apr 1-Nov 30 via mycountyparks.com; FCFS Oct 16-Nov 30; closed Dec 1-Mar 31. Loops sit on wooded hills set back from the lake behind a treeline. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Joe Sheldon County Park
note: Humboldt County Conservation Board park ~2 mi west of Humboldt on Hwy 3, along the Des Moines River. Two camping areas: a lower campground (14 rock-pad water/electric sites) and an upper campground (28 sites, 13 pull-through, several with water/sewer/electric). 30 & 50 amp. $20/night electric, $25 full hookup. FCFS, no reservations. Season ~Apr 15-Oct 15. Modern restroom/shower house and dump station. Satellite: pull-through rows sit in an open field set well back from the river behind a continuous treeline, so not on the water. RV Life 4 stars / $. --Claude

name: Kennedy Memorial Park Campground
note: Webster County Conservation Board, 5 mi N of Fort Dodge on Brushy Creek lake. ~75 sites: 29 full-hookup, 49 electric, 25 pull-throughs; modern restrooms/showers, dump. Open Apr 15-Oct 15. Reservable on mycountyparks.com; full-hookup $30 reserved / $25 walk-up. Satellite: camp loops set back from the lake behind a treeline buffer, not on the shore. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Lake Cornelia Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Wright County Conservation Board campground on the N shore of 246-acre Lake Cornelia. 88 sites - 30 electric (~$25), 28 full-hookup water/sewer (~$30); 20/30/50-amp; 8 sites directly on the lake. Dump station, modern shower/restrooms, marina/boat dock, walking trail, WiFi. FCFS (no online reservation). No stay-length limit. Main campground Apr 15-Oct 15; lakeshore sites year-round with limited winter amenities. RV Life 3.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Lenon Mill Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Guthrie County Conservation (CCB) park on the Middle Raccoon River in Panora. 16 electric RV sites plus primitive camping; water and vault/flush restrooms (restrooms open spring-fall, weather permitting). $20/night, FCFS - no reservations accepted. Canoe/kayak access; small enough that big rigs should call ahead. RV Life 3 stars, $. --Claude

name: Linn Grove Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'creekside' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Cerro Gordo County Conservation's first park, in Rockwell along a meander of Beaver Dam Creek. 66 electric sites, back-in 12x42 gravel pads w/ 20/30/50A; shower house, dump, water fill, firewood. Open first weekend May-first weekend Oct, ~$20/night, online reservation required (credit card) on mycountyparks.com. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Little River Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Decatur County Conservation Board campground on 2,200-acre Little River Lake. 16 electric + 14 tent sites (cabins excluded). Modern shower building, potable water near all sites. FCFS - campground not reservable (cabins by phone). Hours 5:00am-10:30pm. Electric ~$15. Peninsula loop sits on the lake. RV Life 5 star, $. --Claude

name: Little Wall Lake Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Hamilton County Conservation Board park on 249-acre Little Wall Lake (a natural state-owned lake; park run by Hamilton County CCB), ~2 mi S of Jewell. 106 sites: 43 full-hookup + 29 water/electric + 6 primitive tent FCFS; 44 sites reservable on mycountyparks.com plus a group area. Open year-round; water shut off Nov 1-Apr 1. 14-day max stay. Dump station and shower building; boat ramp, jetty, kayak/canoe rentals. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Madison County Fairgrounds Campground
note: Madison County Fairgrounds (county fair board) in Winterset. Defined water/electric and full W/E/S sites open to transient RVers Apr 1-Nov 1 except during fair week; FCFS in non-fair season (call ahead). Electric $17/night, full hookup $20/night. RV Life 4 stars, $. --Claude

name: Margaret MacNider Park Campground
note: Mason City municipal campground on the Winnebago River, by the Aquatic Center. 95 sites: 20 full-hookup (W/E/S), 55 water+electric, 20 tent (water only); modern bathhouse, restrooms, laundry, dump. Open ~mid-Apr to mid-Oct. Reservable. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. The herringbone pad rows sit back from the river behind a continuous riparian treeline. --Claude

name: Marion County Park Campground
note: Marion County Conservation, SW Knoxville (RV Life mis-tags it commercial; it is county-run). 99 sites, gravel pads w/ 50A electric+water; most full-hookup (W/E/S), sites #71-76 elec/water only. Shower house, fits RVs to ~45 ft. Open 2nd Fri Apr-last Sun Oct, ~$30/night, 14-day max, reservable on mycountyparks.com. Fishing ponds, historic village on site. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. Pad rows set in open grass, small pond not at the sites. --Claude

name: Menlo City Campground
note: City of Menlo campground at 417 Sherman St. 8 leveled gravel full-hookup pads (30-amp electric + water), playground, basketball court, pavilion, library WiFi adjacent. ~$25, FCFS (no reservations), noon checkout. Sits next to the railroad tracks. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Nations Bridge Park
note: Guthrie County Conservation (CCB) park, 81 acres on the South Raccoon River between Panora and Stuart. 45 electric RV sites plus primitive camping across upper/lower campgrounds; modern showers, flush restrooms, dump station. $20/night, FCFS - no reservations accepted. Disc golf, canoe access (South Raccoon River Water Trail). Satellite shows a continuous treeline screen between the camp loop and the river, so sites are not on the water. RV Life 5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Ochee Yahola County Park
note: Worth County Conservation, W of Northwood (Worth County's oldest park, 177 ac). 4 electric (partial-hookup) drive-in RV/tent sites in a grassy area surrounded by trees; pit toilets, playground. Open ~Apr 15-Nov 1. FCFS / contact county for reservations. RV Life 3.5 stars, $. Upland wooded/prairie park, campsites not on water. --Claude

name: Pammel Park Campground
note: Madison County Conservation Board park (state-named but county-run), 4 mi SW of Winterset along the Middle River. 36 modern water+electric sites (50/30/20 amp) plus 5 tent sites; showers, dump. Reservable on mycountyparks.com, $24/night electric. Satellite: campground loops set well inland from the river behind timber. RV Life 3.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Pine Ridge Park
note: Hardin County Conservation park on the west bank of the Iowa River (also an 8-acre fishing lake). 43 electric sites; modern restrooms + showers. First-come first-served (FCFS), no advance reservations; season ~Apr-Sep. One Campendium reviewer reports 20-amp service and a 22-ft longest rig, so big-rig fit is marginal but a 23-ft Class C should fit on the larger level sites. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Prairie Bridges Park
note: City of Ackley municipal park (101 acres, six fishing ponds). ~120 sites: ~90 electric-only (30-amp) plus ~30 full-hookup (electric/water/sewer), 8 pull-throughs, big-rig friendly. Dump station, shower house. Season ~Apr-Oct; reservable via RoverPass (~$28); also carries seasonal/monthly sites but operates as a real transient RV campground. RV Life 4/5, $. --Claude

name: Quarry Springs Park Campground
note: Operated by the Colfax Park Board (city). RV campground has 40 stalls w/ 30/50A electric, water spigots, dump, modern restroom/shower; plus 27 primitive/tent sites. Right off I-80 Exit 155. Three interconnected former-quarry lakes (electric-motor only, kayak rentals). Camping ~$10/site. RV Life unrated, $. The RV pad grid sits set back from the lakes behind a wide treed/prairie strip. --Claude

name: Roberts Creek Park - East Campground
note: Marion County Conservation Board park on the county's 300-acre Roberts Creek Lake (adjacent to USACE Lake Red Rock; the campground is county-run, not federal). East campground: ~55 full-hookup sites (20/30/50-amp electric + water + sewer); modern showers, sanitary dump station, playgrounds. Reservable on mycountyparks.com; season ~Apr to late Oct; ~$25-30. Big-rig friendly. RV Life 5/5, $$. --Claude

name: Roberts Creek Park - West Campground
note: Marion County Conservation Board park (NOT the Army Corps; it fronts the county's 300-acre Roberts Creek Lake, adjacent to USACE Lake Red Rock). West campground: ~65 full-hookup sites (20/30/50-amp electric + water + sewer), many paved pads, plus 2 electric/water sites; modern showers, dump station, boat ramps. Reservable on mycountyparks.com; season ~Apr 10 to late Oct; 14-day max stay; ~$25-30. Big-rig friendly. RV Life 5/5, $$. --Claude

name: Siems Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'pond' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Kossuth County Conservation Board park ~1.75 mi SE of Whittemore, a small family campground set on borrow ponds by the East Branch Des Moines River. North and South loops with 50/30/20-amp electric + water sites; reservable online via mycountyparks (plus a walk-in site). ~$15.60-$20/night, 14-day max, opens ~Apr 15 to ~Nov 1. RV Life 4 star, $. --Claude

name: Slip Bluff Park
note: Decatur County Conservation Board park (~400 acres, 2 mi W & 1 mi N of Davis City) with a well-shaded campground; electric and non-electric sites, modern shower house (built 2009) with flush toilets, water, trash. Rigs up to 50 ft; sites set in forest, not on the 16-acre fishing lake. FCFS (no reservations). $20 electric / $15 non-electric. RV Life 5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Smith Lake Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Kossuth County Conservation Board park 3 mi north of Algona on Hwy 169. ~49 electric sites (20 & 50 amp) in two areas: a south-side full-hookup loop with sites close together, and a north-side electric+water loop with more shade. $15-20/night. Reservable on mycountyparks.com ($4 fee) or FCFS. Season ~Apr 15-Oct 31. Showers and nature center. RV Life 4.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Spring Lake Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Greene County Conservation (CCB) park, 240 acres with a 50-acre lake NE of Jefferson. 120 electric sites (50/30 amp) - 16 reservable online via mycountyparks, ~98 FCFS, plus primitive tent sites. Electric $25, primitive $10. Two shower houses + dump station (open ~May 15-Oct 15, weather dependent); swimming beach, disc golf, paddle-craft rental. RV Life 5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Stratford City Park
note: City of Stratford park campground with ~30 electric (30-amp) camping sites in an open grassy park, restroom/shower building, dump station, non-potable water and playgrounds. FCFS; call City Hall for availability. RV Life unrated / $. --Claude

name: Thayer Lake Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Union County Conservation Board, 1 mi SW of Thayer on a 10-acre lake. 8 electric campsites around the lakeside parking area plus primitive sites; boat ramp, shelter. FCFS. Rocked access road off Hwy 34. RV Life unrated (0 stars), $. --Claude

name: Thomas Mitchell Park
note: Polk County Conservation park on 197 acres along Camp Creek near Mitchellville, ~5 mi east of Des Moines off I-80. 23 electric sites (20/30/50-amp) plus 12 primitive; drive-in. Reservations required, bookable online via mycountyparks.com up to 1 yr ahead. Season Apr 1-Nov 30; electric $25, non-electric $20. Dump station, shower house, fire rings. RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Three Mile Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Union County Conservation Board park 3 mi north of Hwy 34 at Afton, on Three Mile Lake. Two campgrounds overlooking the lake, ~80-100 sites; electric sites $20, non-electric $15. Electric pads graveled, 20/30/50 amp, big-rig friendly (RVs to ~60 ft). FCFS, no campsite reservations (the 9 log cabins are reservable). Open year-round with a heated year-round shower house; dump station in the south campground. RV Life 5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Timmons Grove Park
note: Marshall County Conservation park in the Iowa River Greenbelt ~1 mi S of Albion (off Hwy 330). 25 electric sites (30-amp, incl. 2 ADA); shower house with restrooms (closed winter), boat ramp to the Iowa River. First-come first-served (FCFS) via self-registration kiosk, not reservable; ~$22. Drive-in, flat, big-rig friendly per reviews; 23-ft Class C fits. River-bottom site, occasional flood closures possible. RV Life 5/5, $$. --Claude

name: Tuttle Lake Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Emmet County Conservation Board park on 2,294-acre Tuttle Lake (IA-MN line), ~2 mi N of Dolliver. 52 electric sites, 30/50-amp, with dump station, modern restrooms/shower house, swimming beach and boat ramp. ~$25 electric/$15 non-electric, mid-Apr to mid-Oct, FCFS (no reservations). RV Life 4 star, $. --Claude

name: University 40 County Park
note: Calhoun County Conservation Board park on Cedar Creek just S of Lohrville. 8 full-hookup sites (added 2020) plus a shower house; primitive camping at the south end. ~$25 full hookup / $15 primitive, FCFS, 14-day max. Camp loop sits back from the creek behind a wooded buffer. RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Wilkinson Pioneer Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit 94f7ca6 (Add 57 IA local campgrounds, central Iowa (county conservation + city)); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Cerro Gordo County Conservation park on the Shell Rock River. ~42 electric (30-amp) sites plus non-electric (~66 total); showerhouse, dump station, water fill. Season ~first weekend May to second weekend Oct. Cashless/reservable on mycountyparks.com per the county page (older listings call it FCFS). Max length not posted; 23-ft Class C fits. RV Life ~3/5, $. --Claude

name: Winterset City Park Campground
note: City of Winterset park campground, 40 sites with 50/30/15-amp electric plus water and sewer hookups; fire rings, shower/restroom building, 5 tent sites. Rigs up to 50 ft. Season Apr 1-Nov 15. $25/night with a $5 reservation fee; reservable by phone (Parks & Rec, M-F 8-4:30) starting the first Monday of the year, else FCFS. RV Life 4.5 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Yellow Banks Park
note: Polk County Conservation park above the Des Moines River SE of Des Moines. 60-unit campground: 11 full-hookup, electric (20/30/50-amp) plus non-electric and group sites; back-in gravel pads up to 65 ft. $40 full-hookup / $25 electric / $20 non-electric. Reservable online via mycountyparks.com, 14-day max. Camp loops sit on a wooded bench set back from the river; 5-acre pond and boat ramp on site. Dump, showers. RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Ackley Creek Park
note: Floyd County Conservation Board park near Marble Rock; wooded camping loop set back from the Shell Rock River. 20 sites with electric + water, shower house, dump station, two shelters, playground, hiking trails. Reservable online via mycountyparks.com. Open ~May-Sept. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Airport Lake Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Chickasaw County Conservation Board park NW of New Hampton on a 9-acre lake. ~70 modern sites: 50-amp electric+water (sites 1-9, 31-44, 60-70, $20) and full-hookup electric/water/sewer (sites 10-30, $25), plus several primitive sites ($12); 50-amp service handles big rigs. Swimming beach, boat ramp, shower house, dump station. Open May 1-Oct 15 (weather permitting); no through traffic 10:30pm-6am. General sites are FCFS; the group camping loop (1-6 campers, $100/day) is reservable by phone 2+ weeks ahead. Veterans -$2/day. RV Life unrated (0 stars), price $. --Claude

name: Alcock Park
note: Bremer County Conservation park on the west side of Frederika straddling the Wapsipinicon River. 50+ electrical sites (20/30/50-amp) in an open camping area; non-electric $15, electric $20. No designated/numbered sites - FCFS with self-registration at the kiosk, no reservations. Shower house, modern restroom plus pit latrines, dump station, two drinking-water wells. Boat ramp and river canoe/fishing access. Open May 1-Oct 31. RV Life unrated, price $. --Claude

name: Beaver Meadows Park
note: Butler County Conservation Board park on the N edge of Parkersburg (a former state park) between Beaver Creek and Beaver Meadows Golf Course; access via the golf course entrance. 16 sites with 50-amp electric, flush toilets, showers, fill water; some sites reservable online (mycountyparks.com), others FCFS. Open ~May 1-Oct 15. Pads sit among the park grounds, set back from Beaver Creek behind a tree band. RV Life 3.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Benton City-Fry Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Benton County Conservation park on the Cedar River. 15 sites (14 electric, 1 primitive); $16/night electric. FCFS, self-register at kiosk. Season mid-Apr through end of Oct. Open-air shelter, pit toilet, playground, river access; no showers. RV Life unrated (0 stars), $. --Claude

name: Bentonsport Campground
note: Van Buren County Conservation Board park on the Des Moines River in the historic Bentonsport village. 23 gravel-pad sites with 20/30/50-amp electric; shower house, water hydrants, and dump station. Open year-round (water shut off after Oct). Reservable online via mycountyparks.com or drive-in FCFS; reserve 5+ days ahead. ~$17/night w/ electric (2026). 'River row' sites (13-23) sit closest to the river but are screened from the bank by a treeline. Boat ramp nearby. RV Life unrated (0 stars), price $. --Claude

name: Big Woods Lake Campground
note: Owned by the City of Cedar Falls, operated by Black Hawk County Conservation, on 86-acre Big Woods Lake: 55 full-hookup sites (30 & 50 amp, seasonal water, sewer, regraded pads) plus a non-modern area and 3 cabins; WiFi, dump station. Sites #2-6 & #20-25 reservable, the rest FCFS. Pad grid sits behind a continuous shoreline treeline (set back from the lake). RV Life 4.5 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Bison Ridge Campground (Mariposa Recreation Area)
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Jasper County Conservation Board. New (2024) Bison Ridge modern campground: 15 sites with electric + water, dump station, shower house. Big-rig pull-throughs plus back-ins fit a 23-ft rig. ~$25/night, 2-night minimum, reservable online via mycountyparks.com. Open ~April-Oct (closed Nov 1). RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Cedar Bend Park
note: Bremer County Conservation park ~1.5 mi NW of Waverly on the Cedar River (180 acres, largest county park): 60+ electric sites with 20/30/50-amp outlets (some gravel-pad sites), dump station, showers. No designated sites - FCFS with self-registration kiosk. Open May 1-Oct 31, ~$20 electric/$15 basic/$22 gravel-pad. Camping is in river-bottom timber, set back from the bank. RV Life 5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Cedar View Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Nashua campground on a Cedar Lake (Cedar River oxbow) peninsula: 43 RV sites + 5 tent sites, all RV sites water + 30/50-amp electric, newer pedestals, fire ring & picnic table; dump station, restrooms with showers. Open ~May 15-Oct 20, $20 water/electric. North-shore loop sits on the lakeshore. Reservable via city site. RV Life 4 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Colwell Park
note: Floyd County Conservation Board park on the Little Cedar River ~2.5 mi W of Colwell. Primitive drive-in camping ($10/night) with drive-in parking next to sites, pit toilet, pressurized water, picnic shelter, fire rings; fishing/paddling access. Open ~May-Sep. FCFS. Sites sit in timber set back behind the riverbank treeline, not on the water. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Deerwood Park
note: City of Evansdale campground on a peninsula along the Cedar River, just off I-380/US-20 exit 70. ~156 sites with tent, electric/water, and full-hookup options (tent $20, E/W $25, full $30 nightly; weekly and $400 monthly rates also offered). Dump station, shower house/restrooms, playgrounds, river access, 18-hole disc golf. Seasonal. Pads sit behind a continuous treeline screening them from the river and backwater. RV Life listed; price $. --Claude

name: Diamond Lake Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Poweshiek County Conservation park (660 acres, 90-acre lake) ~1/2 mi N of Montezuma off Hwy 63: 80 electric sites with 50-amp boxes plus 40 primitive sites; 3 shower houses, restrooms, 2 dump stations, fish-cleaning station. FCFS (no reservations taken). Several sites along the lakeshore. RV Life 4.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Eveland Access Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Mahaska County Conservation Board 44-unit campground on the Des Moines River SW of Beacon, in east and west loops. 30- and 50-amp electric sites, shower house, pit toilets, concrete boat ramp. Reservable (mycountyparks.com / 641-673-9327). The eastern loop's outer pads sit on the open sandy riverbank. RV Life 5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Fontana Park (Buchanan County)
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Buchanan County Conservation park S of Hazleton, the county's most popular campground, on Fontana Lake (Otter Creek reservoir). Lakeside loop (L02-L16) plus a Creekside loop (C01-C07); all sites electric with 50/30/20-amp on 65'x10' gravel pads. Lakeside $30/night, Creekside $20 electric/$15 non-electric, $23 reserved. Reservable online (buchanancountyparks.com). Shower house, flush toilets, dump station, nature center. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: George Wyatt City Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Rockford park on the Shell Rock River. ~24 electric sites plus no-hookup sites, gravel pads, shower house, dump, playground, disc golf. Fits big rigs. FCFS - pay cash in the envelope at the info sign, no reservations. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Greater Ottumwa Park Campground
note: City of Ottumwa campground inside 340-acre Greater Ottumwa Park: 100+ full-hookup sites (water + 50-amp electric, sewer), big-rig friendly with many pull-throughs, dump station, shower house, WiFi, stocked fishing pond. FCFS, ~$20. Open ~April-October. Pad grid sits between interior lagoons but buffered from both, and the Des Moines River is levee/road-separated. RV Life 4 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Grundy County Lake
note: Grundy County Conservation Board campground at the SW corner of Dike (completed 2008). 59 electric sites with 30/50-amp service and water at each site; modern shower house, dump station; FCFS, no reservations ($20/night). A 40-acre fishing lake is a short paved-trail walk away but the camp loop is separated from it by a road and a wide treed buffer, not on the water. RV Life 5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Halvorson Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Mitchell County Conservation Board park on the Cedar River, ~1.5 mi S of St. Ansgar. ~50 non-designated electric sites (50/30/20 amp) with water, dump station, modern showers, swim beach and boat ramp. Self-register at the entrance; FCFS. Open ~May-mid Oct. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Hannen Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Benton County CB park on a 45-acre man-made lake (Iowa's first CCB lake). ~69-83 campsites, electric, FCFS with self-register at the kiosk within 1 hr of arrival. Beach, shower houses, boat ramp/kayak launch, rustic cabin. Open mid-April to end of October. RV Life 4.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Hickory Hills Park
note: Black Hawk County Conservation park near La Porte City around 39-acre Casey Lake (camp loops sit inland in the hardwoods, not on the lake). ~69 camping units, 47 with electric on gravel pads; some full-hookup (water/sewer) sites. Water hydrants throughout, dump station at the exit, showers and flush toilets. Three group camp areas also available. Reservable online via mycountyparks.com (check-in 3pm, out 2pm); rates from ~$13. Closes Nov 1, reopens in spring; gate hours 6am-10:30pm. Note: Casey Lake drawdown/excavation work has caused periodic disruptions. RV Life unrated, price $. --Claude

name: Hoefle-Dulin Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Benton County Conservation river park on the Cedar River minutes from Vinton. 11-12 sites, 9 with electric + 2 primitive; $16/night electric. FCFS, self-register at kiosk. Season Apr 15-Oct 31. Open-air shelter, pit toilet, playground, river/kayak access; no showers. RV Life unrated (0 stars), $. --Claude

name: Howard County Fairgrounds Campground
note: Howard County Agricultural Society fairgrounds with a real public RV campground (not event-only): ~42 RV sites, water + 50-amp electric, on-site dump station, self-register. Open ~May-Oct. FCFS except the week before and week of the county fair, when it's reserved/closed to transients; long-stays (>5 days) need pre-approval, tent camping by pre-approval only. Big-rig friendly. RV Life 4 stars / $. --Claude

name: Independence RV Park & Campground
note: City of Independence Parks & Rec RV park on the Wapsipinicon River. 42 full-hookup sites (sewer/water/50-amp, picnic table, fire ring), pull-through and back-in angled pads that fit big rigs. Tent $10, RV $35/day, $150/wk, $400/mo (senior 60+ $30/$140/$365); season Mar 1-Nov 15. Showers/restrooms, dump, swimming pool, playground, WiFi. Reserve by phone (host 319-440-0472, off-season Parks & Rec 319-334-6711); some sites kept FCFS. River access for canoe/kayak/fishing but pads sit behind a continuous wooded riverbank treeline, not on the water. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Jefferson County Park
note: Jefferson County Conservation park, 254 acres on the SW edge of Fairfield. Original 24-pad electric campground (most with water) plus the newer Prairie Ridge full-hookup loop (14 sites, for self-contained rigs); two camping cabins. Electric $20 / non-electric $15. Open May 1-Oct 31. Nature center, 7 mi of trails. Campground sits in timber; the park's ponds/wetlands are separate from the sites (not waterfront). RV Life 4.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Klock's Island Park
note: City of Fayette park on an island formed by the Volga River; drive-in via bridge (not boat-in). ~30 sites with electric (110V, 30 & 50 amp) plus water hookups; big-rig friendly open grassy loops fit a 23-ft rig. Modern restroom/shower house (water on Apr-Oct), $2 dump station, 14-day stay limit. Reservable online (parkwith.us/Campspot); ~$25/night RV per city page. Volga River is paddling/tubing access only - camping loops sit back from the water behind a treeline, not riverfront. RV Life 4 stars, $. --Claude

name: Lake Belva Deer Park
note: Keokuk County CB on 260-acre Lake Belva Deer, ~5 mi NE of Sigourney. 56 modern sites with concrete pads, electric (30-amp), water and a dump station; accommodates RVs up to 45 ft. Primitive sites also available. $20/night modern, $12 primitive. FCFS, no reservations. Shower house with flush toilets. Camping loops sit in an open clearing screened from the lakeshore by a treeline (not on the water). RV Life 4.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Lake Iowa Park
note: Iowa County CB on Lake Iowa. 122 gravel sites, all with electric (mostly 30-amp; sites 117-122 are 50-amp) and water hookups throughout. FCFS, no reservations (set up early by registering an erected camper/tent). $20/night May-Sep, $10/night Oct-Apr. Two shower houses. Camping loops sit back from the lake behind a band of woods (not on the water). Big-rig friendly gravel pads. RV Life 4.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Lake Meyer Park and Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Winneshiek County Conservation park between Calmar and Fort Atkinson on 38-acre Lake Meyer. 27 electric + 8 non-electric sites; $20/night electric, $15 primitive. FCFS, no reservations. Season ~Apr-Oct. Dump station, flush toilets, showers, drinking water; nature center, electric-motor boating, hiking. RV Life unrated (0 stars), $. --Claude

name: Lake Miami Park
note: Monroe County Conservation Board park on the 135-acre Lake Miami, 5 mi E of Lovilia. Full-hookup, electric, and primitive sites; restrooms, showers, potable water, dump station. Open ~Apr 1-Nov 1. Reservable via mycountyparks.com. Camp loops sit back from the lake behind trees/grass (boat ramp + day-use area on shore), not on the water. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Lake Sugema Campgrounds
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Van Buren County CB park on 579-acre Lake Sugema between Keosauqua and Cantril. ~28-29 sites: ~11 full-hookup hard pads, ~10 water/electric, ~7 gravel electric. 21 reservable on mycountyparks.com (5 days ahead); 7 gravel sites FCFS. Hard-surface pads, ADA shower house, dump station, boat ramp. Water on ~May-Oct. RV Life 4.5 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Lelah Bradley Park Campground
note: Appanoose County Conservation Board park on the Centerville upper/lower reservoirs. 15 full-hookup (50-amp, water + sewer) sites, FCFS. Seasonal shower house, boat ramps/docks, kayak launch, trails. Camping area set back from the water. Open seasonally (closed winter). RV Life 4 star, $$. --Claude

name: Lime Creek Park (Buchanan County)
note: Buchanan County Conservation park NE of Brandon (RV Life tags it Benton/'Brandon' but it is a Buchanan CCB park). Small campground tucked in the Lime Creek wooded corridor; electric and non-electric sites, playground and shelter house in the campground. $15/night electric, $10 non-electric. FCFS, no reservations. Season Apr 15-Oct 1. Lime Creek runs through the park but camp pads are not at the creek bank (dense canopy, no per-site map) so not waterfront. RV Life unrated (0 stars), $. --Claude

name: Maynard City Park
note: City of Maynard park on the Volga River; ~21 grassy water+electric sites with some pull-throughs (big-rig friendly). Dump station; vault/restroom toilets, no showers. River/canoe access for paddling (sites sit inland near the ballfields, not on the riverbank). FCFS, no reservations; fee paid to city. Contact city office 563-637-2269. RV Life: unrated (star_rating 0), price_level 1 ($). --Claude

name: McFarlane Park
note: Black Hawk County CB on the Cedar River, 150-acre park (62-acre hunting area). 64 electrical sites; 28 of the main-campground sites are reservable (call the CB), remainder FCFS. Modern restrooms/showers, dump station, drinking water, WiFi. Camp loops sit back from the Cedar behind a wide treeline (not on the water). Open 6am-10:30pm; closes Dec 1, reopens spring. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: McGowen Recreation and Wildlife Area
note: Davis County Conservation Board, 290-acre rec/wildlife area near Bloomfield. 13 sites with water + electric (30/50-amp), 8 pull-through, accommodating 35-70 ft rigs; restrooms/showers and dump station on site; 2 modern cabins. Reservable via mycountyparks.com, $25/night (tent $10). Gravel access road, drive-in/big-rig passable. Several ponds in the area but camp loops are tree-buffered, not on the water. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Middle Amana Community Park
note: Municipal (city) park campground in Middle Amana village (Amana Colonies), camping under old oak trees. RV and tent sites with electric and drinking water, dump station and laundry on site. Sites from ~$15/night. Lily Lake (a 170-acre lotus marsh) lies south of the village but is separated from the camp area by a wide marsh/field buffer and is not RV-accessible shoreline -> not waterfront. RV Life 2 stars, $. --Claude

name: Minne Estema Park
note: Historic river park on the Cedar River ~8 mi north of Vinton; DNR-owned, operated by Benton County CB. 12 sites (10 electric, 2 primitive), FCFS, $16 electric. Open April 15-Oct 31. Open-air shelter, pit restroom, shoreline fishing and boat/river access. Sites are in riverbank timber; satellite is canopy-blind and no per-site artifact confirms pads at the water, so left not waterfront. RV Life unrated / $. --Claude

name: Morris Park
note: Van Buren County Conservation Board campground; 15 sites, modern + primitive, ~48x11 ft gravel pads (fits a 23-ft rig) with 20/30/50-amp electric, water hydrants, dump tank, shower house. Open year round (reduced Nov-Apr: electric only, no water). Reservable online (mycountyparks.com, 5+ days ahead) or FCFS drive-in; 3-night minimum on holiday weekends. Stocked pond on-site but campsites are set back in the woods, not on the water. RV Life 5 stars / $. --Claude

name: North Cedar Park
note: Bremer County Conservation park east of Plainfield on the Cedar River: 40+ electric sites (20 & 30 amp) plus tent sites, dump station, well water, flush toilets, showers; boat ramp/river access. No designated sites - FCFS with self-registration kiosk. Open May 1-Oct 31, ~$20 electric/$15 basic. Camping is in timber set back from the river (not on the bank). RV Life 3.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: North Woods Park
note: Bremer County CB park, 82 acres ~1 mi north of Sumner. Electric campsites with 20/30/50-amp service, modern restroom/shower facility, no designated sites, FCFS with self-register at kiosk (no reservations). Open May 1-Oct 31. No lake at the campground (two ponds are a short walk in the adjacent Wilson Grove Wildlife Area). RV Life unrated / $. --Claude

name: Oelwein City Park Campground
note: City of Oelwein park campground on the south edge of town beside Lake Oelwein (~24-55 acre no-wake lake). 42 electric+water sites incl. 50- and 30-amp and 3 pull-through, plus primitive tent area; modern restroom/shower house, dump station. FCFS, no reservations. Season Apr 15-Oct 15. Camp loops sit behind a wooded buffer from the lake (boat access only), not on the shore -> not waterfront. RV Life 3.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Osage Spring Park
note: City of Osage park on the Cedar River. Primitive/basic riverside camping, pull-through sites accept big rigs up to ~40 ft, toilets and drinking water (from the namesake spring); minimal hookups - confirm electric on arrival. FCFS. Open ~April-Nov. Wooded riverbank - sites not confirmed at the waterline. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Ottumwa Park Campground
note: City of Ottumwa campground in Greater Ottumwa Park (340 acres) near the Des Moines River. 100+ electric/water sites (20-amp), many pull-through and big-rig friendly (sites run a bit small; a 30-ft trailer fits site 50). Dump station, shower house/restrooms, WiFi. FCFS, no reservations. Open ~April-October. $20/night ($25 effective July 1, 2026). Sites sit in city-park green space near the river but not on the water. RV Life $$ / unrated. --Claude

name: Pioneer Ridge Nature Area
note: Wapello County Conservation Board nature area (HQ + nature center), just north of the Davis/Wapello line on Hwy 63. 9 electric/water hookup sites (gravel pad, fire ring, picnic table) plus 3 primitive sites; also cabins. Online reservation only via mycountyparks.com. Season Mar 15-Nov 1; 14-day max stay. Showers May 1-Sep 30 only; no dump station. Camp loop sits in mowed/timber ground; small stocked ponds nearby but sites not on the water. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Riverside Park Campground (Spillville)
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Spillville park campground on the Turkey River. Sites with 30/50-amp electric, picnic tables, fire rings; bathrooms, showers and dump station within walking distance. Canoe landing, disc golf, ballfield. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Riverview Park Campground
note: City of Marshalltown Parks & Rec campground in Riverview Park near the Iowa River (camping loops sit well inland of the river). 10 full-hookup (50-amp) sites, 30 electric (50/30/20 amp) sites and 17 parking pads; drive-through sites fit large RVs. Shower house, flush toilets, dump station. Reservable online; open mid-April-end of Oct. RV Life 3.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Rodgers Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Benton County CB park, 207 acres just outside Vinton on a 21-acre lake. 52 electric + 5 non-electric sites, FCFS with self-register at kiosk within 1 hr of arrival. Shower house at park shop, dump station, boat ramp/kayak launch, beach (note: water hookups/shower house at sites were out of service per the CCB page). Camping areas overlook the lake. Open mid-April to end of October. RV Life 4 stars / $. --Claude

name: Sharon Bluffs Park
note: State-owned park on the bluffs above the Chariton River E of Centerville, maintained/operated by the Appanoose County Conservation Board since 1981. 6 campsites with 30-amp electric, FCFS (no reservations); pit latrines, fill/dump station at park entrance. Open year-round. Campground sits on the wooded bluff top, well back and above the river, not on the water. RV Life 3.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Siggelkow Park
note: Black Hawk County CB, off Fairbank Rd north of Dunkerton, with canoe/kayak access to the Wapsipinicon River. Small campground with electric sites, water, dump station and drinking water; no picnic tables or showers. River is screened by woods (sites sit by the entrance road/pond, not on the water). Quiet river park; FCFS. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Split Rock Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit e16e4bf (Add 57 IA local/state campgrounds, NE Iowa (county conservation + city); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Chickasaw County CB; largest park in the system on a 7-acre lake. Camping loop with electric (50-amp) sites, $18/night with electric / $12 without. General sites FCFS; the camping loop can be reserved by calling the CB office at least two weeks ahead. Modern shower/restroom, dump, swimming beach, boat ramp. Open-grass loop fits a 23-ft rig. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Turkey River Campground (Vernon Springs Park)
note: Howard County CB at Vernon Springs Park on the Turkey River, near Cresco. 46 sites (17 pull-thru), each with water and 50/30/110-amp electric. $25/night incl. hookups. FCFS, no reservations; self-register within an hour of arrival. Modern shower/restroom, playground. Campsites are in an open field set back from the river and a small adjacent lake behind treelines (not on the water). Big-rig friendly with pull-thrus. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Twin Bridges Park
note: Small (17.5-acre) Fayette County Conservation Board park at the confluence of the south & west branches of the Volga River, 3 mi N of Maynard. Drive-in camping on the south side: ~2 electric sites plus several primitive sites in an open mowed grassy area (fits a 23-ft rig on grass). Pit toilets, shelter house, playground, dump station; no showers. Open year round. FCFS per the CCB camping page ($15 electric / $10 primitive); mycountyparks.com shows a 'Book Now' link but the CCB lists county parks as first-come. RV Life unrated (star_rating 0), price $. --Claude

name: Waubonsie Trail Park
note: City of Cantril park, 23 acres. 16 sites with water + electric (some 50-amp), two with sewer; rustic and ADA camping cabins. Sewer dump station, ADA shower house, free wifi, disc golf, covered bridges. Reservable by phone. Only water feature is a decorative 1-acre trail pond (not at the sites). RV Life 5 stars / $. --Claude

name: West Idlewild Campground
note: DNR-owned river-access tract along the Cedar River NW of Floyd, leased and operated by the Floyd County Conservation Board. 15 primitive drive-in sites, FCFS via self-registration ($10/night); vault toilet, picnic shelters, canoe ramp. Sites sit in a clearing screened from the river by a band of riverbank trees, not on the water. RV Life 5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Wildcat Bluff Recreation Area
note: Benton County Conservation Board park, ~2 mi S of Urbana on the Cedar River. 26 campsites (23 electric @ $20/night, 3 non-electric @ $10/night); season mid-April to end of October. All sites FCFS, self-register at the kiosk within 1 hr of arrival (no online reservations). Showers and pit restrooms; no dump station (nearest CCB dump is at Rodgers Park). Boat ramp, river fishing access, hiking trails, disc golf. Campsites sit on an elevated cleared bench set back from the river behind a treeline. Big-rig fit not posted; loop has drive-in pull-in pads suited to a 23-ft rig. RV Life 4 stars / $. --Claude

name: Wilder Park
note: City of Allison park on Hwy 3 just E of town; ~52 sites including 30/50-amp electric and full-hookup, with back-in and pull-through spots that fit big rigs. Drinking water, flush toilets, showers, dump station, WiFi, on-site camp hosts. Two fishing ponds in the park but campsites sit uphill/back from them behind trees, not on the water. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: 4th Pumping Plant Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit dbf58c6 (Add 51 IA local campgrounds, E Iowa / Mississippi (county conservation); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Des Moines County Conservation park on a Mississippi River backwater lake near Oakville (operator is Des Moines County, not Louisa). 46 sites: 22 electric ($25), 24 primitive ($10); central water + RV dump station, no showers, 3 outhouses, boat ramp/dock. FCFS self-register, no advance reservations; 14-day max stay. Gravel access road (a reviewer hauled a rig in for a week) but passable. RV Life unrated (0 stars), price $. --Claude

name: Bankston Park
note: Dubuque County Conservation park near Holy Cross. ~50 sites, electric (30-amp, slated to upgrade to 50-amp), max RV ~30 ft; toilets, tables, grills, playground. Open Apr-Oct. Partial online reservations via mycountyparks.com, remainder FCFS. Has a restored fishing pond but campsites sit back from it. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Bennett Park
note: Cedar County Conservation park 2 mi E of Bennett on Hwy 130. ~10-17 sites with 50/30/20-amp electric and water nearby, gravel back-in and pull-through pads; shower house w/ flush toilets (seasonal), pit toilet year-round, RV dump station. Electric $15/night. Has a small fishing pond but campground loop sits inland from it. RV Life 5-star, $. --Claude

name: Big Hollow Recreation Area
note: Des Moines County Conservation campground on 178-acre Big Hollow Lake, ~10 mi N of Burlington. 32 full-hookup sites (electric/water/sewer, $30) + 18 electric-only ($25) + 16 wooded primitive ($10); sites have room for camper plus tow/boat trailer, accommodates a 23-ft rig. Modern restroom/shower house and central dump station. Open year-round; 14-day max stay. Half the RV sites reservable up to a year ahead via mycountyparks.com, rest FCFS. RV pads sit back in trees, not on the lakeshore (satellite at 40.9535,-91.2255: campground loops separated from lake by a treeline; nearby water is small ponds). RV Life 4.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Big Spring Campground (Clayton County)
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit dbf58c6 (Add 51 IA local campgrounds, E Iowa / Mississippi (county conservation); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Clayton County Conservation primitive campground on the Turkey River, ~10 mi NW of Elkader by Big Spring trout hatchery. 7 primitive sites, $15/night, one RV or trailer per site (no length posted; small river-bottom sites). Pit toilet, fire ring, picnic table; no dump/showers. Year-round; may close at flood stage. Reservable online via mycountyparks.com (3 days advance, weekend 2-night min). RV Life unrated / $. --Claude

name: Bloody Run County Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'creekside' assessed in commit dbf58c6 (Add 51 IA local campgrounds, E Iowa / Mississippi (county conservation); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Clayton County Conservation park in a limestone-bluff valley along Bloody Run trout stream, just W of Marquette. ~19-20 primitive (no-hookup) campsites, one RV/trailer per site, quiet generators OK; FCFS, $15/night with 2-night weekend / 3-night holiday minimum. Open seasonally May-Oct (closed Nov-Apr). No dump/showers. Low valley sites can flood after heavy rain. RV Life unrated (0 star) / $. --Claude

name: Buffalo Creek County Park
note: Linn County Conservation Board (Walnut Grove Campground) near Coggon. 31 sites in two loops: 18-site A-loop reservable online via mycountyparks.com ($30/night, concrete pads, electric+water, fire ring, table, campground host) plus 13-site B-loop first-come-first-served, all electric/water. Electric service 20/30/50 amp - 50A loop fits big rigs. New (2023) shower house/restroom; dump station near park entrance. Seasonal: park roads closed to vehicles late Oct-Apr 15. RV Life 4-star / $$. --Claude

name: Central Park (Jones County)
note: Jones County Conservation park on a 25-acre lake + 7-acre pond. ~73 numbered sites: 18 modern (30A, sewer/water/wifi), 7 semi-modern (50A, water/wifi), 13 electric (30A), ~35 primitive. FCFS self-register at kiosk, no reservations; availability posted Thu-Sun in season. Water/electric on Apr 15-Oct 15 (weather dependent), primitive camping year-round; dump station and showers on site. Satellite at 42.110,-91.138: RV loops sit south of the lake behind a grass/tree buffer, not on the shoreline -- not waterfront. RV Life 4 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Chatfield Park
note: Lee County Conservation park on a 9-acre lake N of Keokuk. 14 full-hookup sites (20/30/50-amp, water, sewer) plus 10 primitive; full-hookup pads are pull-through ~60x22 with concrete, fits a 23-ft rig and larger. Open ~Apr 15-Oct 31 (gates close after first snow). Reservable online via mycountyparks.com, $26/night; 2-night weekend / 3-night holiday minimum. Dump station and showers. Campground sits up the slope back from the lake (wooded buffer), so not on the water. RV Life 4.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Coffins Grove Park
note: Delaware County Conservation Board park 4 mi NW of Manchester. 18 sites: 15 with 30-amp electric + water, 3 primitive. Drive-in loop, dump station, vault toilets, no showers; well water. FCFS with self-registration within 1 hr of setting up (not reservable). Open mid-April to mid-October. Sites sit in open mowed clearings; Coffins Grove Creek runs through a wooded buffer to the north (stream fishing). RV Life unrated (0 stars), $. --Claude

name: Eden Valley Refuge
note: Clinton County Conservation refuge near Baldwin, in a wooded valley on Bear Creek. ~28 semi-modern electric/water sites (50/30/20-amp), $25/night; clean restrooms with seasonal showers, playground. Drive-in pull-in pads on the loop; primitive/hike-in sites also exist across the road. Sites sit in the wooded valley, not on the creek. RV Life 5-star, $$. --Claude

name: F.W. Kent Park
note: Johnson County Conservation campground in F.W. Kent Park. 86 electric sites (30 & 50 amp), blacktopped pads with picnic table and fire ring, in two wooded loops (North & South); sites up to ~42 ft confirmed by big-rig reviews. $20/night, first-come-first-served (no reservations; pay online via mycountyparks.com or call the office). Open seasonally ~Apr 15-Oct 15 (campground hosts mid-May to mid-Sep); park open year-round. Shower house with flush toilets, additional vault/pit toilets, sanitary dump station, firewood for sale, playground, storm shelter. Secluded/wooded loops set back from the 27-acre lake (not lakeside). RV Life 4.5 stars, price $ (1/4). --Claude

name: Fillmore Recreation Area
note: Dubuque County Conservation 116-acre rec area NE of Cascade on Hwy 151, on Whitewater Creek. Drive-in RV/tent sites with pull-in pads; modern shower/restroom (closed ~Nov 1-May 1). Partial online reservations via mycountyparks.com (up to a year ahead), remainder FCFS. Whitewater Creek runs the park but pads sit back behind a treeline. RV Life 2-star, $$. --Claude

name: Finley's Landing Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit dbf58c6 (Add 51 IA local campgrounds, E Iowa / Mississippi (county conservation); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Dubuque County Conservation park on the Mississippi (Pool 11) ~10 mi NW of Dubuque (legacy 'city park' name; county-operated). ~24 sites, 8 electric-only plus electric/water hookups, 1 pull-through, max RV ~30 ft; shower/restroom, boat ramp, sandy swimming beach. ~30% online-reservable via mycountyparks.com, ~70% FCFS. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Flaming Prairie Park
note: Louisa County Conservation park (mailing Muscatine), ~10 mi E of Grandview near a Mississippi River backwater. ~24 electric sites (20/30/50A), some pull-through gravel pads; water Apr-mid-Oct, modern pit toilets, picnic shelter, primitive boat ramp to the river. Open year-round but low-lying -- frequently closed by Mississippi high water, so call ahead in spring/high-water periods. Satellite at 41.270,-91.118: sites are in an open mowed flat set back from the backwater behind a continuous treeline (river access is a short walk/drive, no shoreline pads) -- not waterfront. RV Life unrated / $. --Claude

name: Fountain Springs Park
note: Delaware County Conservation Board park 2 mi NE of Greeley, 239 acres on Elk Creek (stocked trout stream). Free primitive drive-in camping in an open meadow (no hookups), fire rings, picnic tables, vault toilets; open year-round, FCFS (self-register). Sites sit in the meadow set back from the creek behind a treeline. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Frenchtown County Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit dbf58c6 (Add 51 IA local campgrounds, E Iowa / Mississippi (county conservation); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Small Clayton County Conservation park 3 mi north of Guttenberg on a Mississippi backwater. 3 free primitive sites, no hookups/dump/showers, vault toilet, boat ramp; FCFS. Drive-in (cross a railroad track to enter); RVs do use it per RV Life reviews (rated ~8/10, ~15 reviews; price $) and satellite showing parked campers, but it is bare-bones and seasonal (spring-fall, closes during flooding). --Claude

name: Frieden's Park
note: Clayton County Conservation primitive campground 5.5 mi NW of Elkader along the Turkey River. 3 basic sites, $15/night, one RV or trailer per site (small canoe-access sites; no length posted). Pit toilet, fire pit, picnic table; no dump/showers; generators prohibited. Reservable online via mycountyparks.com (weekend 2-night/holiday 3-night min). RV Life unrated / $. --Claude

name: Gilbertson Campground and Education Area
