waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 94dc7f4 (Audit all 40 WI federal waterfront designations against the evidence g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Eagle River-Florence RD) on 955-acre Kentuck Lake. 31 sites; ~18 reservable online + ~13 FCFS. No hookups; pressurized drinking water, boat landing. Back-ins to ~40 ft. --Claude

name: Lac Vieux Desert Campground
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Eagle River-Florence RD) on the west shore of Lac Vieux Desert (Wisconsin River headwaters). 31 sites, all FCFS (not reservable); vault toilets, drinking water, no hookups; boat landing, picnic shelter, beach. Back-ins to ~40 ft. A real drive-in family campground, not just a boat landing. --Claude

name: Wanoka Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 94dc7f4 (Audit all 40 WI federal waterfront designations against the evidence g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Washburn RD) on 15-acre Wanoka Lake ~7 mi E of Iron River. 20 sites, no electric, drinking water + vault toilets; back-ins up to 30/35/45+ ft. Carry-in boat access + small dock. Reservable online (~60% of sites) with remainder FCFS. Open ~May 2-Oct 31. --Claude

name: Mineral Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 94dc7f4 (Audit all 40 WI federal waterfront designations against the evidence g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Great Divide RD) on 256-acre Mineral Lake ~9 mi W of Mellen. 10 sites for RVs and tents, no electric/sewer, vault toilets + potable water. FCFS (seasonal; rec.gov shows off-season closed but operates in summer). Several sites overlook the lake. --Claude

name: Namekagon Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 94dc7f4 (Audit all 40 WI federal waterfront designations against the evidence g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Great Divide RD) alongside 3,227-acre Namekagon Lake ~13 mi E of Cable. 34 sites, many accommodating 45+ ft RVs, no electric, drinking water + vault toilets. Sandy swim beach, boat landing, fishing pier. Reservable online (~20 sites) with FCFS remainder. --Claude

name: Perch Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: North Loop 10 RV back-ins within steps of NW shore
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Washburn RD) on 72-acre Perch Lake ~5.5 mi N of Drummond. 16 sites in two loops (N loop 10 sites w/ boat launch+dock; S loop 6 sites), RVs up to 30-35 ft, no electric, drinking water + vault toilets. All FCFS - not reservable. --Claude

name: Beaver Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: sites 5,7,8 lakeside w/ lake access
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Great Divide RD) on 35-acre Beaver Lake near Mellen. 10 sites (mix RV/tent; sites 2,6,9 tent-only), RV pads ~25-30 ft posted back-in with ~40-ft aprons, no electric, drinking water + vault toilets. Boat launch ~0.5 mi away. Sites 4,5,6,7,9,10 reservable online; 1,2,3,8 FCFS. --Claude

name: Lake Three Campground
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Great Divide RD) on Lake Three near Glidden/Mellen. ~7 sites, RVs/trailers up to 30 ft, no electric, vault toilets. FCFS (seasonal; rec.gov shows off-season closed but operates in summer). Several sites overlook the lake; adjacent to North Country NST. --Claude

name: Day Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 94dc7f4 (Audit all 40 WI federal waterfront designations against the evidence g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Great Divide RD) on the east shore of Day Lake ~1 mi N of Clam Lake. 52 sites (Blueberry & Heron loops), RVs up to 45 ft, no electric, drinking water + vault toilets, swim beach, fishing pier, 6 barrier-free paved sites. ~25 sites reservable online, remainder FCFS. --Claude

name: East Twin Campground
waterfront_evidence: sites 8-17 along undeveloped east shore, lake-side of loop road
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Great Divide RD) on 110-acre East Twin Lake near Clam Lake. 10 sites; sites 1-2 accommodate 40-ft back-in RVs, others up to 30 ft, no electric, drinking water + vault toilets, fishing pier. Boat ramp ~0.5 mi E. 6 sites reservable online, remainder FCFS. --Claude

name: Moose Lake Campground
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Great Divide RD) on Moose Lake. 15 sites (3 tent-only); 12 sites accommodate RVs 25-40 ft. Vault toilets, drinking water, no electric, sandy beach and boat ramp. Reservable online (some FCFS sites). --Claude

name: Black Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: ~17-20 of 29 sites lakefront within 50ft w/ carry-in access
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Great Divide RD) on Black Lake. 29 non-electric sites (3 walk-in tent-only); back-in only, up to 45+ ft. Vault toilets, drinking water; most sites lakefront. 17 reservable online + 12 FCFS. --Claude

name: Smith Rapids Campground
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Medford-Park Falls RD) on the South Fork Flambeau River by the Smith Rapids covered bridge. 11 sites; sites 1-4 tent/truck-camper only, sites 5-11 RV-capable (5: 40 ft, 6-9: 45+ ft, 10-11: 25 ft) and horse-friendly (tie stalls) but open to general drive-in RV camping. Vault toilets, drinking water, no electric. FCFS - no reservable sites. --Claude

name: Sailor Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 94dc7f4 (Audit all 40 WI federal waterfront designations against the evidence g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Medford-Park Falls RD) on 170-acre Sailor Lake, Flambeau Trail access. 25 sites (23-25 tent-only, 12 double/group); RVs up to 45+ ft. Vault toilets, drinking water, no electric. Sites 1-12/24/25 reservable online, 13-23 FCFS. --Claude

name: Stockfarm Bridge Campground
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Great Divide RD) on the East Fork Chippewa River. 7 single-family sites + 1 group site; max RV 45+ ft (site 8), site 6 to 40 ft, others 25-30 ft. Hand-pump water, vault toilets, no electric, boat launch. FCFS - all first-come. Season opens ~Thu before May fishing opener. --Claude

name: Chippewa Campground
waterfront_evidence: Beaver Loop 16-19 front Chequamegon Waters Flowage, lakeside fee
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Medford-Park Falls RD) on the NE shore of 2,714-acre Chequamegon Waters Flowage. 78 sites; electric at sites 31-49, RVs to 40+ ft, paved roads, dump station/RV fill, some loops with hot showers & flush toilets. Swimming beach, boat ramp. 41 reservable online + FCFS. --Claude

name: Spearhead Point Campground
waterfront_evidence: peninsula perimeter sites 11-18 on Mondeaux Flowage spurs, lakeside fee
note: Rustic Chequamegon NF (Medford-Park Falls RD) campground on Mondeaux Flowage. 27 sites, no hookups, vault toilets, hand-pump drinking water; some sites over 40 ft. Reservable online with some FCFS; premium ('P') sites have small docks. --Claude

name: Eastwood Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 94dc7f4 (Audit all 40 WI federal waterfront designations against the evidence g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Rustic Chequamegon NF (Medford-Park Falls RD) campground on Mondeaux Flowage. 12 sites, no hookups, 2 vault toilets, potable water; most sites over 35 ft. Mixed FCFS and reservable (5 reservable online). Open ~May 2-Oct 31. --Claude

name: West Point Campground
note: Rustic Chequamegon NF (Medford-Park Falls RD) campground on Mondeaux Flowage. 15 back-in sites (RVs accommodated on most), no hookups, 2 vault toilets, potable water. FCFS - not on recreation.gov. (Separate Picnic Point site nearby is tent-only.) --Claude

name: Grant River Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit 94dc7f4 (Audit all 40 WI federal waterfront designations against the evidence g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: USACE (Rock Island/St. Paul District) campground on the Mississippi River (Pool 11) ~2 mi south of Potosi. 73 sites, ~63 with electric (30/50 amp), 8 tent-only non-electric (sites 64-73), showers, flush toilets, dump station, boat ramp. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Highland Ridge Campground
note: USACE campground at Eau Galle Lake (150-acre reservoir) near Spring Valley. 46 sites, 35 with electric, plus 8 walk-in tent sites and a separate 10-site equestrian loop. Drive-in electric/standard loop fits RVs; showers, dump station, swimming beach. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Liberty Harbor RV Park
waterfront_evidence: Firsthand manual audit by owner (--AWH note); waterfront 'riverview' per owner: SPECIAL CASE (.
note: SPECIAL CASE (--AWH): an urban paved RV lot at the Liberty Harbor Marina / Morris Canal Basin in Jersey City - included for its unmatched NYC access (PATH/ferry/light-rail to Manhattan minutes away, plus Statue of Liberty & Liberty State Park), not as a traditional campground. It is a tightly-packed angled-stall parking lot, not a scenic campground. ~50 sites, full water + 30/50-amp electric hookups; open year-round with 24/7 self check-in. Reservable online (CampLife) or by phone. Front-row sites overlook the marina basin with open Manhattan-skyline views across the water. --Claude

name: Fountain Lake RV Park
note: RVs only (no tents), 26 full-hookup sites (30/50 amp W/E/S), 20 pull-throughs, accommodates big rigs so a 23-ft rig fits easily. Has a 99-year lot-lease membership component but explicitly rents nightly/weekly/monthly to non-members; book online. Laundry, propane, mini golf, clubhouse. Rural near Almond/Waupaca. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Antigo Lake RV Park and Campground
note: Operated by the City of Antigo Parks & Recreation at Lake Park, 815 Hudson St. ~13 paved RV sites at $25/night; 6 sites with individual water+sewer, 50-amp electric, dump station; plus rustic tent/22-ft sites. Season May 1-Oct 1; reserve online up to a year ahead, by phone, or at City Hall. Walk to downtown, ball fields, Springbrook Trail. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: DN Campground
note: Family-owned campground on the Apple River ~5 mi east of Balsam Lake with an on-site bar. Heavily seasonal (90-125 seasonal sites) but offers nightly pull-through RV sites with electric (30/50 amp) + water ~$25-40/night; online prepaid reservations. Flat river access for canoe/kayak. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Stop On Inn Motel & Campground
note: Family-owned motel + small campground in Barron on E Division Ave. ~8 full-service pull-through sites (water, sewer, 30-amp, cable, WiFi) plus electric-only sites for smaller rigs/tents; all level pull-throughs. Bathhouse, dump station, play area. Reserve by phone/email. Open year-round. No water adjacent. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Fairgrounds RV Park
note: Standalone transient RV park at the Dodge County Fairgrounds (run by the non-profit Dodge County Fair Association), N6885 High Point Rd, ~4 mi E of Beaver Dam on Hwy 33. ~50 pull-through sites on paved roads: 4 FHU 50A, 30 50A+W, 6 30A+W; dump station, restrooms/showers, WiFi. Open Apr-Oct; bookable nightly online (also cash/check), not just during events. All pull-through, fits a 23-ft rig. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Featherstone RV Park
note: Small private ATV-friendly RV park on the Tuscobia State Trail, W650 Granite Rd, Birchwood. ~18 sites (17 pull-through, accepts big rigs), full hookups (W/E/S, 30 & 50A), fire rings, bathrooms/free showers, ATV wash. $35/night; reserve by phone/in person, cash or check only. 23-ft fits easily. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Rivers Edge Campground
note: Private family campground on the Brill River, W2753 Church Rd, Birchwood (1 mi S of Long Lake dam). 19 overnight RV sites (plus 85 seasonal, 3 tent, 2 cabins) with W/E/S, 30/50A; camp store, laundry, dump station, free showers, swim pond/beach, river tubes/kayaks, ATV access. Reservable online. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Wildlife Bar & Campground
note: Wildlife Bar & Campground on Mayflower Lake, N4597 Willow Lane, Birnamwood. 200 wooded acres, ~175 sites; partial hookups (30A + water, no sewer), dump station, beach/swimming, fishing, on-site bar. Open year-round; takes overnight stays (~$30/night) plus seasonal sites. Reserve by phone/email. No standalone official website (a minor strike) but operation well-confirmed across directories. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Lost Falls Campground
note: Private mom-and-pop campground on 50 acres along the lower Black River (~800 ft of river frontage), N2974 E Sunnyvale Rd, Black River Falls. 52 campsites (plus 2 group tent areas, 7 cabins); RV sites W/E, some full hookups, 30/50A, dump station, showers; sand beach, canoe/kayak/tube rentals. Open May 1-Sep 30; reservable online or phone. 23-ft fits. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Eagle Cave Resort
note: Private resort by Wisconsin's largest onyx cave. 78 sites; water/electric (30-amp) and primitive/seasonal sites, dump station, showers, lodge with restaurant. Max RV ~35 ft so a 23-ft rig fits. Open Memorial Day-Labor Day (in-cave programs off-season). Reservable online and by phone. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Mohican RV Park (North Star Mohican Casino)
note: Standalone RV park at North Star Mohican Casino Resort (Stockbridge-Munsee Band), not a parking-lot lot. ~86 sites incl. 57 full-hookup pull-throughs, 50/30/20-amp, water/sewer, dump station, store. Big-rig friendly. Open to public transient stays; reservable online or phone; $35 weekday/$45 weekend. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Sweet Minihaha Campground
note: Private campground on 32 acres along the Sugar River near the Sugar River State Trail. ~65-132 sites incl. daily RV, seasonal RV, tent, group sites; water/sewer hookups, 30/20-amp, dump station, showers. Tube/canoe/kayak rentals with shuttle. Open May 1-Sep 30. Reservable online and by phone. Back-in RV sites fit a 23-ft rig. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: River's Edge Resort & Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit 93c2b62 (Audit all 83 WI commercial-sweep waterfront designations against the g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Private resort/campground on the Flambeau River at Bruce (has a bar & grill plus a mobile-home section, but operates daily transient RV sites). RV sites with water, 30/50-amp electric and sewer, dump station, showers; beach/boat landing, pool, tube/boat rentals. A 23-ft rig fits. Reservable by phone (also online). RV Life geoloc was ~10 km off; corrected to address. Distinct from the unrelated 'River's Edge' near Stevens Point. (RV Life 4.0 star, $). --Claude

name: Buck 'n Beam's Social House & Campground
note: Small family-owned campground & bar/grill south of Cameron near Hwy 8/53, on ATV/snowmobile trails. ~30 sites (daily, weekly, seasonal), all with water and 30/50-amp electric; tent sites too. A 23-ft rig fits. Reservable online. Real campground, not just a bar. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Hoeft's Resort & Campground
note: On Crooked Lake. 55 RV sites, water + electric hookups; nightly rates ~$36-50 with a 2-night minimum (3 nights/holidays) - genuine transient camping, not seasonal-only. Open May 1 - Oct 15. Restrooms/showers, laundry, sandy beach, boat rentals. Reserve by phone. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Six Lakes Resort & RV Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 93c2b62 (Audit all 83 WI commercial-sweep waterfront designations against the g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Private RV resort on Pokegama Lake (Chetek chain), 2535 8-7/8 Ave. ~170 sites (142 full-hookup, 28 W/E; 20/30/50 amp); nightly transient ($32-$37) plus weekly/monthly. Open May 1-Oct 1. Reservable online or phone. Marina/boat & pontoon rentals, pool, dump station. 23-ft rig fits easily. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Eagle View RV Camp
note: Small private campground at W16270 Cty Rd D, ~8.5 mi east of Chetek (Rusk County). 24 full-hookup sites (30/50 amp) plus a few overnight/pull-through sites; sites 12/13/15 are daily transient ~$40/night FHU. Off-lake (lakes nearby, not on water). Opens ~end of April. Booking by phone/email; also on Hipcamp. 23-ft fits. Coords are RV Life's approximation - verify precise pin. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Pine Harbor Campground
note: Private wooded campground at 7181 185th St near (not on) Lake Wissota. ~16-21 full-hookup RV sites (water + 20/30/50 amp), plus 5 extended-stay RV sites, 7 electric-only, primitive sites, cabins. Open ~May 1-mid-Oct. Reservable online + phone. Takes transient overnighters; 23-ft fits. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Rohr's Wilderness Tours
note: Paddling outfitter that also runs a real 47-site drive-in campground on the Wisconsin River headwaters, 5230 Razorback Rd, Conover. 25 full-hookup sites (30/50 amp, most pull-thru) plus a couple W/E sites and tent/walk-in sites. Drive-in RV pull-thrus fit a 23-ft rig. Opens ~mid-May; reservations by phone. Canoe/kayak rentals, disc golf, shower house, camp store. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Dairyland Outdoor Veterans Retreat
note: Nonprofit veterans retreat (vets-by-vets) but open to the general public - civilians help fund the mission; veterans get a discount code. 14 RV sites with water + electric, fire ring, picnic table (no sewer per a review). Cabins + primitive tent sites too. Reservable online. Address 1465 E Town Rd TT, Dairyland WI. Public can book, so not group/restricted-only. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Eagles Landing Campground
note: Standalone 45-site wooded RV park along the Yellow River, operated by St. Croix Casino Danbury (tribal-owned), just south of the casino - a real RV park with actual sites, not a casino parking lot. 35 full hookups (W/E/S, 50 amp) + 10 electric-only; big-rig friendly, max ~40 ft. Free showers, coin laundry, WiFi, shuttle to casino. Season ~late April-Oct 15. Reserve by phone or via casino. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Tom's Campground
note: 55-acre family campground ~35 mi W of Madison. ~96 sites (36 tent, 60 W/E, 12 electric-only), 16 pull-throughs, 30/50 amp; big-rig friendly. Dump station, showers, flush toilets, WiFi. Season ~April 15-Oct 31. CAVEAT: showed 'closed for the 2025 season' but takes phone reservations for the following year - verify 2026 operation by phone before relying on it. Reserve by phone/office (also Hipcamp). (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Two Lakes Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 93c2b62 (Audit all 83 WI commercial-sweep waterfront designations against the g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Washburn RD) - 67 wooded sites between Lake Owen and Bass Lake. Primitive, NO hookups, vault toilets, drinking water, dump station, swim beach. ~$25/night. Season ~May-Oct. Mix of reservable (recreation.gov) and FCFS sites. Drive-in, accommodates RVs. (RV Life 5.0 star, $). --Claude

name: Hi-Pines Campground
note: 75-acre wooded family campground ~1 mi N of downtown Eagle River at 1919 US-45 N. ~150 sites incl. 40 full hookups, 84 water/electric, 56 pull-throughs; 30/50 amp. 1-acre swim pond, water park, trails, ATV access, fiber WiFi. Season ~May 1-Oct 15. Reservable online (full payment at booking). Takes transient/overnight. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Chain O' Lakes Camping Resort
note: Family campground on Cranberry Lake (Eagle River Chain of Lakes), 3165 Campground Rd, Eagle River. ~205 total sites - mix of seasonal and transient/overnight; recently added transient RV sites. 30 amp electric + water, dump station; listed max RV up to 60 ft though some transient sites are tight for larger rigs. Heated pool, boat docks/slips/rentals. Reservable online. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Sundown Saloon and Campground
note: Bar/grill with an adjacent RV+tent campground (~31 sites) at 3508 Hwy 35, Frederic/Lewis WI. Full hookups: 30 amp electric, water, sewer; dump station. Daily/transient nightly camping plus weekly/monthly/seasonal. Phone reservations. A June 2026 reviewer confirms currently operating. A 23-ft rig fits. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Kickapoo Stump Dodger
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit 93c2b62 (Audit all 83 WI commercial-sweep waterfront designations against the g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Drive-in RV/tent campground at 388 S Railroad St, Gays Mills WI, along the Kickapoo River. ~60 RV sites; 20/30/50 amp electric, water, sewer, pull-thru sites; RV from $25/night. Reservable online. The campground is a public transient RV park (hosts weddings/events). NOTE: Lower Kickapoo River floods in spring/early summer. A 23-ft rig fits. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Happy Ours RV Park
note: 16-acre RV park at 14627 S East Mail Rd, Gordon WI with direct ATV trail access. 76 large sites, full water hookups + 30/50 amp; full-hookup sites and secluded tent sites. Open ~May 1-Sept 30. Big pull-through sites fit a 23-ft rig easily. RV Life geoloc was ~6 km off; corrected to the listed address. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Island Campground and Marina
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit 93c2b62 (Audit all 83 WI commercial-sweep waterfront designations against the g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Also branded Freedom Island Campground & Marina, on Trenton Island in the Mississippi River opposite Red Wing, MN. ~108 sites split into Upper (1-32) and Lower (33-108); partial-hookup RV + tent, marina with boat moorage. Seasonal; reserve by phone, no online booking found. Mixed nightly/monthly. Island access via causeway, passable for a 23-ft rig. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: River Road RV Campground
note: Grassy RV park on the Namekagon River near Hayward, surrounded by canoe/kayak landings. 24 full-hookup sites (W/E/S, 20/30/50 amp) plus ~10 tent sites; pull-throughs listed up to 50 ft, easily fits a 23-ft rig. Open ~April-mid October. Reserve by phone; no official website found and few online reviews. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Reel Livin' Resort and Campground
waterfront_evidence: front RV/trailer row at Lost Land Lake waterline w/ own docks
note: Resort on the south shore of Lost Land Lake (Quiet Lakes chain), ~17 mi east of Hayward. ~37 sites: 6 full-hookup overnight/transient RV sites + ~25 seasonal + tent-w/electric, plus 10 cabins; sandy beach, boat launch, bar & grill. Online booking + phone. Year-round operation. Only a handful of true transient sites but they take nightly bookings. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Callahan Lake Resort and Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 93c2b62 (Audit all 83 WI commercial-sweep waterfront designations against the g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Small resort on Callahan Lake, ~13 mi from Hayward. ~20 sites total (tent + RV), electric at all sites with central water-fill and dump station (partial hookups, not full); showerhouse, beach, boats, lodge/bar. Operator says some sites directly on the shoreline, others have lake access/views. Nightly + seasonal stays; reserve by phone/mail (also on Hipcamp). (RV Life 5.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Cedar Falls Campground
waterfront_evidence: RV pads on open bank apron of the Tomahawk River (WVIC rustic river sites)
note: Utility-run (Wisconsin Valley Improvement Co.) campground at the Cedar Falls dam where the Tomahawk River meets the 7,000-acre Willow Flowage, Hazelhurst. 42 total sites, ~30 with electric (incl. rustic sites along the Tomahawk River); boat landing on site. Season ~late April-mid October; reservable online (advised >=1 day prior), walk-in not guaranteed; monthly rates for 30+ nights. Drive-in RV access. WVIC = utility operator, so ownership=private per criteria (like Brookfield/PPL). Year-round phone 715-848-2976. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Hiles Pine Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: RV pads on open grass apron along the Pine Lake shore road
note: Private RV park on 454 ft of Pine Lake shoreline in Forest County. 20/30/50-amp sites with water; pull-thru and back-in; some 30/50-amp sites on the lakefront. Tent sites, cabins and a 30-ft rental camper also on site; seasonal + transient nightly sites. Open May 1-Oct 1. Reservable online (Firefly/RoverPass). Fits a 23-ft rig. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Forest Pond Campground
note: Small private campground in the Nicolet National Forest off Hwy 70 E, ~15 min from Eagle River. 18 tree-lined sites: 13 RV (30/50-amp electric + water, pull-throughs, dump station) and 5 walk-in tent. Panfish-stocked pond and playground. Open May 1-Oct 31. Reservable online (RoverPass/own site). Pull-throughs fit a 23-ft rig. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Top O' The Morn Resort & Campground
waterfront_evidence: front-row sites on open grass apron ~40-60m to Iron Lake w/ docks
note: Family-run lakeside resort on ~1,000 ft of Iron Lake shoreline in Bayfield County. 61 RV sites incl. full hookup, 30/50-amp, lakeside and pull-through; site descriptions say sites accommodate the biggest big rigs. 5 lakeside cabins. Open ~May 1-Oct 15. Reservable (own site/RoverPass). Easily fits a 23-ft rig. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Wildwood Campground
note: Small private RV-only campground (no tents) near Iron River. 25 sites, ~14 available for nightly/overnight (rest seasonal): 5 full-hookup and 19 electric+water, 30/50-amp; dump/limited sewer, swimming beach, boat/canoe/kayak rentals, playground, pavilion. Open May-Oct. Reserve by phone (715-372-4072) or email; not on a major online portal. Fits a 23-ft rig. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Broken Bow Campground
note: Private campground on 134-acre Broken Bow Lake (no-wake), Lac Du Flambeau area. 54 wooded sites: 1-43 electric (30-amp), 44-54 non-electric; water available, no sewer hookups. Sites under 20 ft base rate, +$6 for longer (23 ft fine). 3 beaches, free non-motorized boats. Daily/nightly rates with 2-night min on weekends; phone reservations, seasonal sites also available. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Wasko's Campground Plus
note: Private campground on Lake Minnesuing (~600 ft lake frontage, docking) - town is Lake Nebagamon but the lake is Minnesuing. 60 sites, full hookups (W/E/S) available; tent/trailer/RV. Daily, weekly, monthly and seasonal rates - takes nightly transients. On-site bar, billiards, showers/flush toilets. Reserve by phone (no online booking found). (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Heaven's Up North Family Campground
note: Private family campground in Chequamegon-Nicolet National Forest, Lakewood. 116 large sites: 8 pull-through, 19 electric-only, ~96 electric+water, 30/50-amp, dump station, WiFi; max RV length ~40 ft so a 23-ft rig fits easily. Pool, game room, ATV/snowmobile trail access. Open mid-May to mid-October. Bookable online via Campspot. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Borderline RV Park
note: Private RV park ~1.5 mi south of Land O' Lakes on US-45 along the Wisconsin River, on the WI/MI-UP border. ~30 sites, full hookups (30/50-amp electric, water, sewer), pull-throughs, big-rig friendly (23-ft no issue); WiFi, laundry, fish cleaning house. Open May-October; daily/weekly/monthly/seasonal rates, reserve by phone. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Smokey Hollow Campground
note: Large family campground at W9935 McGowan Rd, Lodi. W/E sites incl. VIP pull-thru, standard pull-thru/back-in, plus cabins/yurts/park models; central dump station, no on-site full sewer noted. Listed max RV length 75 ft (reviewer brought 38-ft Class A), so a 23-ft rig fits easily. Season ~Apr 17-Oct 18. Reservable online via Campspot. 1-million-gal man-made swimming pond on site; no natural lake/river camping. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Devil's River Campground
waterfront_evidence: official per-site PDF, sites 35-73 line the Devils River east loop
note: Private campground at 16612 County Road R, Maribel, on the Devils River by the historic 1847 Rock Mill / mill falls. ~152 grassy sites with electric (30-amp) and water hookups; central dump station, laundry, showers, swimming pond. Sites rentable daily/nightly (~$30-$45) or seasonally; open seasonally. Reservations by phone (not online). Grassy sites fit a 23-ft rig. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Central Wisconsin State Fair
note: Municipal fairgrounds park at 513 E 17th St, Marshfield, owned by the City of Marshfield (Marshfield Fairgrounds Commission); the fair is run by the Central Wisconsin State Fair Association. NOT event-only: operates a public campground for nightly/short-term RV camping ~May 1-Oct 18 (closed during the August fair and other reserved events). 100+ water/electric sites (30 & 50 amp) plus 500+ primitive spaces; dump station $5. Reserve via a Non-Fair-Time Camping Form / call ahead; no long-term camping. Flat fairground sites fit a 23-ft rig. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Wildwood Haven Resort and Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 93c2b62 (Audit all 83 WI commercial-sweep waterfront designations against the g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: On 111-acre Long Lake; W/E hookups 50/30/20 amp, sites 40+ ft long (23-ft rig fits easily), dump station, boat launch/docks/swim area, rentals. Open mid-May to mid-Oct. Reservable online. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Irvington Campground and RV Park
note: On the Red Cedar River; 45 sites (20 full hookup, 6 pull-thru, 7 tent), allstays lists 65 ft max (23-ft rig fits), restrooms/showers/laundry/dump. Paddling + shuttle on the river. Open May 1 to mid-Oct. Reservable online or by phone; seasonal sites exist but nightly offered. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Loon Lagoon RV Campground & Resort
note: 40 sites with full W/E/S hookups; open grassy park two blocks from Hwy 51 in Mercer, walkable to town, ATV-trail accessible. Tents/truck-campers welcome (23-ft rig fits). Reservation by phone (also on Hipcamp). (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Minong Wild River Trail Campground
note: MUNICIPAL - Village of Minong RV park (reservations via Washburn County Parks), not private. 20 full-hookup (W/E/S) RV sites + 5 tent sites; built 2013, expanded 2025; ATV loading ramp, coin showers, flush toilets, dump, pavilion. $45/night RV. Open May 1-Oct 15. Reservable online (7-day-advance + 2-night minimum). (RV Life 5.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Rest EZ Campground
note: 50 sites (41 full hookup W/E/S, 6 W/E, 2 tent, 1 bunkhouse), 3 drive-through sites (23-ft rig fits), flush toilets + shower house, dump stations front and back. ~3.5 mi west of Minong on WI-77. Reservation by phone. Small on-property creek for wading. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Kilby Lake Campground
note: On 50-acre no-wake Kilby Lake; ~124 wooded sites, full hookups 30/50-amp W/E/S, pull-throughs (23-ft rig fits easily), heated pool, restrooms/showers, laundry, boat launch, rentals. Open ~Apr 15-Oct 15. Reservable online + phone; seasonal sites exist but overnight offered. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Moonlite Trails Campground
note: Private drive-in campground ~8 mi N of Necedah; 50-amp + water sites, full-hookup sites available with dump station. Reservable online. Site count/season not pinned from thin official page; directories confirm operation. Near Necedah NWR. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Norm & Ann's Campground
note: ~50 sites (12 tent, 28 electric-only, 12 full-hookup), 20/30/50 amp, 14 pull-through, max RV 50 ft (23-ft rig fits), flush toilets, heated showers, dump, rentals. Open May 1-Oct 31. Reservations accepted online; no standalone official domain (minor strike) but well-confirmed across directories + reviews. ATV/snowmobile oriented. (RV Life 4.5 star, $) --Claude

name: Lakeside Fire Campground
waterfront_evidence: westernmost RV row parked at Ross Lake waterline, thin scattered fringe
note: On 52-acre Ross Lake; 200+ sites incl cabins, full-hookup pull-through & back-in (30/50 amp W/E/S) plus W/E back-ins, many large 45x60 grassy pads (23-ft rig fits easily), heated pool, mini golf, sandy beach, 1.5-acre swim pond. Open Apr 15-Oct 15. Reservable online; seasonal + large transient inventory. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Winding River Campground
note: 25-acre wooded park on the Little Yellow River; 85 sites with 30-amp + water (no sewer; dump station), spring-fed swim pond, camp store, catch-and-release pond. Open May 1-Oct 15. Overnight + seasonal; genuine overnight RV sites reservable online. Near Castle Rock Lake. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Omro RV Park
note: Explicitly a transient (no-seasonal) in-town RV park; 30 electric-only pull-through sites all 80 ft long, 30 amp, dump station + fresh water, no sewer/full hookups. Open ~May 8-Oct 23. Regular nightly stays FCFS (14-day max); the 1-week-minimum/prepay rule applies only to EAA AirVenture week. No tents/pop-ups/vans. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Hickory Oaks Campground
note: 76 sites (29 W/E 20/30/50 amp, 48 no-hookup), pull-throughs available (23-ft rig fits), dump station, sandy swim beach on a private ~4-acre spring-fed lake. Adjacent grass airstrip but a normal vehicle drives in. Open ~May 1-mid-Oct. Reservations online only. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Osh Vegas Palms Resort
note: Standalone big-rig-friendly transient RV park beside I-41 south of the EAA grounds; ~53 full-hookup sites (20/30/50 amp + water each), dump station, pull-throughs, store, WiFi. Open Mar 1-Dec 31; (23-ft rig fits with room). Reserve by phone (also Hipcamp/RVezy). (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Deep Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 93c2b62 (Audit all 83 WI commercial-sweep waterfront designations against the g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: On Deep Lake (no-wake); RV sites, cabins, tent sites, plus year-round mobile-home spots; daily RV from ~$45/night. Open ~Apr 15/May 1-Oct 15/31. Daily bookable online; seasonal by phone. Leans seasonal but daily availability exists. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Christian's Campground
note: ~24 full-hookup + 1 W/E RV sites, packed-gravel pads on grass, remote/rural ~SW of Pelican Lake village, direct ATV-trail access, playground/horseshoes/volleyball. Open May-Oct; caters to seasonal but takes nightly (book by phone/email). Gravel pull-ins on a 24-site park should fit 23 ft. No standalone official website (strike) and near-zero independent reviews - moderate confidence; confirmed operating via RoverPass/Travel Wisconsin/Oneida County. (RV Life 4.5 star, $) --Claude

name: Twin Lakes Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 93c2b62 (Audit all 83 WI commercial-sweep waterfront designations against the g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Chequamegon-Nicolet NF (Medford-Park Falls RD), Twin Lakes Recreation Area. 17 sites, no hookups, vault toilets, drinking water, $15-30/night; sites 5-11 reservable on recreation.gov (4-day min), rest FCFS. A 28-ft rig reportedly fit (23-ft fine). Missed by the earlier federal query (RV Life mis-tagged commercial). (RV Life 5.0 star, $) --Claude

name: West Bay Camping Resort
note: Private resort on Lake Thompson (~1,700 ft shoreline) S of Rhinelander. 45 full-hookup (W/E/S) 30/50A sites + 10 tent/primitive + 5 cabins; big-rig friendly to 50 ft. Pool, beach, boat launch, pier, store, laundry. Reserve by phone; also on Passport America. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: V & M Bar & Campground
note: Small private bar-and-campground at the Hwy V & M crossroads near Rice Lake. ~20 sites, mix of seasonal + nightly full-hookup; nightly $32 (weekly from $185). Restrooms, showers, WiFi. Reserve by phone/online. No size cap published - verify 23-ft fit by phone. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Alana Springs Campground
note: Private campground in the wooded hills of Richland County, est. 1955, WACO member. 35 sites, 30A electric + water (no sewer noted), 3 pull-throughs, listed big-rig friendly. Open ~Apr 1-Nov 30, nightly $22-$38. Seasonal residents hold most sites but transient/nightly sites bookable online. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: White Tail Ridge Campground & RV Park
note: Wooded private campground ('Whitetail Ridge / Backwoods Saloon') deep in maple/aspen woods near Sarona, N753 Shallow Lake Rd. Water + electric hookups (sewer likely dump-station only), free showers, flush toilets, dump station, saloon/rec hall. Large weekend + seasonal sites; ATV/snowmobile trail access. Open ~Apr 1-Oct 1, nightly from $45. Online booking. Large wooded sites fit a 23-ft rig. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Frontier Bar & Campground
note: Private bar/campground/fuel stop on US-2 at Saxon, ~4 mi S of Lake Superior (Saxon Harbor). Full-hookup level gravel pads incl. pull-throughs; 50/100A options; 24/7 gas/diesel, bar with food, showers, laundry. ~25-45 sites. Open May 15-Oct 15, nightly from $26. ATV/snowmobile trail access; nightly/weekly/extended stays. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Leon Valley Campground
note: Private 80-acre wooded park 5 min S of Sparta. 150+ sites, water/electric 30/50A + some full-hookup pull-throughs; RVs to 70 ft. Open May 1-Oct 15. Seasonal + nightly (2-night wknd / 3-night holiday min). Reservable online/phone. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Scenic View Bar & Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 93c2b62 (Audit all 83 WI commercial-sweep waterfront designations against the g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Private campground on 100-acre Poquette Lake W of Spooner. 40 sites - full/partial/primitive, water + 30A electric; sand beach. RVs to 34 ft (23-ft rig fits). Open May 1-Oct 1. Phone reservations only; substantially seasonal-occupied but takes nightly. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Stone Haven RV Park
note: Private small (~32 sites) full-hookup park (W/E/S, cable) on Hwy 70 in the town of Stone Lake; ~16 full-hookup seasonal sites, takes transient by phone reservation. No standalone website (Facebook only - minor strike, but operation confirmed via chamber/county listings + reviews). Not on the lake. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Monument Point Camping
note: Private wooded Door County park between Sturgeon Bay & Egg Harbor. 84 sites, electric-only 30A (no water/sewer), gravel pull-throughs, 35-ft max (23-ft rig fits). Open May 1-Oct 15. Phone reservations only. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: The Backyard on Amnicon Lake
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 93c2b62 (Audit all 83 WI commercial-sweep waterfront designations against the g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Private campground + bar/grill on Amnicon Lake ~20 min S of Superior. Full-hookup RV sites W/E/S 30A ($40/nt) + tent/primitive. Boat docks/landing, beach, dump station, laundry. Reservable online. Takes nightly/weekly/monthly. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Woods Edge Campground
note: Private RV park just outside Tomah on the edge of the Driftless region (former Holiday Lodge RV Park, reopened ~2018); directly on an ATV/UTV route. ~27-35 sites, all W/E with most full-hookup (sewer), 30/50A; pull-throughs to ~60-75 ft long, max RV ~60 ft. WiFi, hot showers, 24/7 laundry, dump, store. Overnight + seasonal; nightly ~$38. Open Apr 15-Oct 15. Reservable online. On-site pond with sandy beach. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Grangers Campground
note: In-town Tomah RV park (Grangers LLC), 566 N Oakwood St. 45 total spaces - 27 grass transient/pull-thru sites (30x80 ft) + 18 seasonal; ~half full hookup, rest E+W, 30A; level grass, big-rig friendly. Free hot showers, restrooms, laundry, dump + mobile sewer, cable, WiFi, playground. Adjacent to the Omaha/La Crosse River State Trail. Open Apr 1-Nov 1. Reservable online or phone. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Pine View Campground (Fort McCoy)
note: Fort McCoy Army MWR campground on Suukjak Sep Lake, open to the public (recreation area accessed off Hwy 21, no base credentials needed). 170 sites (~126 full-hookup W/E/S 30/50A, 12 electric-only, 32 tent) + 16 lakeside cabins; beach, fishing dock, dump station. Open ~May 1-Nov 30. Reservable via Army WebTrac + phone. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Tomahawk Campground & RV Park
note: Private RV park on 68 acres behind Tomahawk Speedway off Bill France Way. 75+ sites; full hookups (W/E/S), W/E, and electric-only sites available; W/E/S ~$43/night. Tent sites and cabins too. Open May 1-Sep 30; reservable online (3-night min on holiday weekends). 23-ft rig fits easily. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Terrace View Campsites
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 93c2b62 (Audit all 83 WI commercial-sweep waterfront designations against the g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Rustic wooded private campground on spring-fed Muskellunge Lake (163 acres), Lincoln County. 45 sites: 3 full hookup, 38 water + 20/30amp electric, 4 electric-only (no 50amp). 32-ft max RV length (call ahead if over 30 ft) - 23-ft rig fits. Showers, flush toilets, free dump station, 5 fishing piers, beach. Open early spring through mid-Nov. Book online or by phone. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Flatwaters Saloon & Campground
note: Family-owned saloon + campground on the Namekagon River, W4606 US Hwy 63 (between Hayward and Spooner). Wooded/open spacious sites with full water/sewer/electric hookups and dump station; RV and tent camping. River tubing rentals with free shuttle, on-site bar/food. Confirmed operating (multiple 2025 firsthand reviews). Call to reserve (no online booking). 23-ft rig fits. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: St. Croix Casino RV Park (Turtle Lake)
note: Standalone RV park at the St. Croix Casino Turtle Lake (tribal-casino operated), 100 Tamarack St. ~17-28 sites; W/E hookups (20/30/50 amp, $27/night), plus cheaper unhooked wooded/gravel sites and tent sites; dump station, showers, laundry, pool, 24-hr casino shuttle. Open ~May 1-Oct 31. Big-rig friendly (to 60 ft). Reservable by phone. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Seagull Marina and Campground
waterfront_evidence: front RV rows on open grass/sand apron ~30-50m to Lake Michigan channel
note: Waterfront marina campground on Lake Michigan at 1400 Lake St, Two Rivers. ~100 sites (82 full-hookup, 18 electric-only) with 20/30/50 amp; full/partial/primitive tiers. Showers, sand-beach access, boat launch, overnight slips. Season ~May 15-Oct 10. Mostly seasonal campers but a few short-term/transient nightly sites offered. CAVEAT: property has been listed for sale - confirm current operation before relying on it. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Potawatomi Carter Casino RV Park
note: Standalone RV park at the Potawatomi Carter Casino Hotel (Forest County Potawatomi tribal-casino operated), 618 State Hwy 32, Wabeno. ~20 designated RV sites (pull-through and back-in) with electric hookups, water, dump station, trash at each site; laundry/WiFi at casino. No camping fee - free up to 3 nights, FCFS (register inside with RV plate). Fits a 23-ft rig. (RV Life 4.5 star, $). --Claude

name: St. Croix Casino RV Park (Hertel)
note: Genuine standalone RV park (not just casino-lot overnighting): 16 pull-through sites with cement pads, full hookups (W/E/S), showers/laundry/clubhouse, adjacent to a small casino and fuel station on SR 70. FCFS. Year-round. Fits a 23-ft rig easily. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Scenic Ridge Campground
note: Mostly seasonal park but offers transient nightly RV sites in the back meadow/hill area (2-night weekend min). W/E on all nightly sites (20/30/50 amp), no sewer, dump station on site. 11 pull-throughs; max ~30 ft. Season ~mid-April to mid-October. Sandy swimming beach + boat launch on Whitewater Lake. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Point Bluff Camping Resort
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit 93c2b62 (Audit all 83 WI commercial-sweep waterfront designations against the g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Intimate riverside park (~25 sites) on the Wisconsin River; 20-amp electric at each site plus a central water source, no sewer. Takes full-size campers/RVs, gravel pads on front sites. Transient nightly reservations bookable online. On-site bar/restaurant, tube/canoe/kayak launch. Season ~late May to mid-September. 23-ft rig fits; confirm exact length on booking. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Arbor Vitae Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 93c2b62 (Audit all 83 WI commercial-sweep waterfront designations against the g); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Family lake campground on Big Arbor Vitae Lake (~1,090 ac); ~109 sites incl. 77 full hookups, 12 electric-only, 6 electric+water, plus tent sites. Sites 26-100 ft; 23-ft rig fits with full hookups. Transient nightly reservations by phone (2-night weekend / 4-night holiday min). Season ~May 1 to Oct 15. Boat rentals, rental docks, lake access via Link Creek. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: North Park RV Campground
waterfront_evidence: audited 2026-06 (WI local-government evidence-gate sweep, commit bcf7803); waterfront 'not waterfront' set by that satellite/per-site-map sweep (per-entry bucket detail in the commit message)

FCFS or reserve by calling City Hall; $35/night (2 seasonal sites also). (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Lake Emily Park

note: Portage County park (RV Life mis-tags it commercial) on 96-acre Lake Emily west of Amherst Junction. Two campgrounds (North and South); reservable online 7+ days ahead or self-register FCFS for open sites. Showers, dump station, boat ramp, swimming beach. Open May 1-Oct 31. (RV Life 5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Dells of the Eau Claire Park
waterfront_evidence: audited 2026-06 (WI local-government evidence-gate sweep, commit bcf7803); waterfront 'riverview' set by that satellite/per-site-map sweep (per-entry bucket detail in the commit message)
note: Marathon County park on the Eau Claire River (Town of Plover, near Aniwa), beside the dells/waterfalls and Ice Age Trail. 28 wooded sites, 16 with electric, gravel pads, vault toilets, central water pump; sites listed to ~60 ft. Self-register FCFS; some sites reservable online. (RV Life 5 star, $$). --Claude

note: Pepin County park on 97-acre Silver Birch Lake just south of Arkansaw (N5362 County Rd N). 20 sites with 30-amp electric (1-16 standard $20, 17-20 premium $25), pit toilets, non-potable water, boat ramp. Reservable online via Campspot; open mid-April-November. (RV Life unrated). --Claude

name: Petenwell County Park
waterfront_evidence: audited 2026-06 (WI local-government evidence-gate sweep, commit bcf7803); waterfront 'lakefront' set by that satellite/per-site-map sweep (per-entry bucket detail in the commit message)
note: Adams County park (2nd-largest county park in WI) on Lake Petenwell, 2004 Bighorn Dr, Arkdale. Large campground with electric and premium/lakefront sites, many with water hookup; heated showers, dump station, swimming area, boat launch, harbor slips. Reservable online or by phone; 2-night min; open year-round. RV $25 regular / $30 premium-lakefront. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: North Wood County Park
waterfront_evidence: audited 2026-06 (WI local-government evidence-gate sweep, commit bcf7803); waterfront 'lakefront' set by that satellite/per-site-map sweep (per-entry bucket detail in the commit message)
note: Wood County park on the Yellow River impoundments (Lake Kaunewinne 5 ac, Lake Manakiki 11 ac), 7500 Park Lane, Arpin. 99 sites: 76 electric, 23 non-electric, 5 waterfront, plus a 50-amp group site; showers, laundry, dump station. Reservable online. (RV Life 3.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Kreher Park
waterfront_evidence: audited 2026-06 (WI local-government evidence-gate sweep, commit bcf7803); waterfront 'bayfront' set by that satellite/per-site-map sweep (per-entry bucket detail in the commit message)
note: City of Ashland Parks & Rec, on Lake Superior's Chequamegon Bay. 33 RV sites with water + 30/50-amp electric (no sewer), dump station, showers, swimming beach, pavilion. Season ~May 15-Oct 15. Reservable online (Campspot); nightly/multi-day/monthly. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$) --Claude

waterfront_evidence: audited 2026-06 (WI local-government evidence-gate sweep, commit bcf7803); waterfront 'not waterfront' set by that satellite/per-site-map sweep (per-entry bucket detail in the commit message)
note: Marinette County Parks, on the Peshtigo River (Strong Falls) in the county forest. 15 non-electric (no hookup) drive-in sites with level RV/trailer spurs, dump station, vault toilets, potable water tower. Season May 1-Nov 30 (water off after Oct 15). Reservable online 184 days ahead. (RV Life unrated) --Claude

name: McClintock County Park
waterfront_evidence: audited 2026-06 (WI local-government evidence-gate sweep, commit bcf7803); waterfront 'riverfront' set by that satellite/per-site-map sweep (per-entry bucket detail in the commit message)
note: Marinette County Parks, on the Peshtigo River (McClintock Rapids) in Town of Silver Cliff; only Marinette County park with direct ATV/UTV trail access. 10 non-electric rustic drive-in sites (tent + trailer), hand-pump water, vault toilets. waterfront_evidence: audited 2026-06 (WI local-government evidence-gate sweep, commit bcf7803); waterfront 'lakeview' set by that satellite/per-site-map sweep (per-entry bucket detail in the commit message)

name: Avoca Lakeside Park & Campground
waterfront_evidence: audited 2026-06 (WI local-government evidence-gate sweep, commit bcf7803); waterfront 'not waterfront' set by that satellite/per-site-map sweep (per-entry bucket detail in the commit message)
note: Village of Avoca municipal campground on Avoca Lake (a backwater/lake off the Wisconsin River). ~105 large, well-spaced sites; all 30-amp electric, 18 sites 50-amp; water at bath houses, dump station, boat ramp, swim beach. Nightly $30 (electric) / $35 (electric+water); seasonal sites also offered. Open ~Apr 15-Nov 30. Reservable by phone (608-532-6831 weekdays, 608-532-6188 after hours). Reviews report rigs up to ~25-30 ft fit; 23-ft EKKO is fine. (RV Life 3.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Bay City Campground

Open ~Apr 15-Oct 15. Mix of seasonal and transient — daily ($45)/weekly ($300) reservations open Mar 1, monthly Feb 1; reservable sites 21-23, 34-38, 43-44. Genuinely takes overnight transients. North side was closed for construction; verify season. Operated by U.S. Army Corps of Engineers. RV Life: 4.5* / $. --Claude

name: Hapgood Pond Campground
note: Green Mountain & Finger Lakes National Forest (USFS), Manchester Ranger District, 13 mi east of Manchester. ~28 family sites in a wooded loop; reviewers report ~26 sites take RVs/trailers ~20-35 ft, so 23-ft fits. No hookups; vault toilets, drinking water. Mixed reservable (sites 9-28 on recreation.gov) and first-come-first-served. Managed swim beach on the pond. RV Life: 4.0* / $. --Claude

name: Greendale Campground
note: Green Mountain National Forest (USFS), Weston. Small remote campground along Greendale Brook, ~9-11 back-in sites. First-come-first-served only (not on recreation.gov); ~$10/night. No hookups, no drinking water, vault toilet. Spurs are short -- Forest Service guidance says best for RVs under ~24 ft, so a 23-ft rig is near the upper limit; some sites take small campers/trailers/camper vans. Open seasonally (Memorial Day-Labor Day). RV Life: 4.0* / $. --Claude

name: North Beach Campground
note: City of Burlington (Parks, Recreation & Waterfront) municipal campground on Lake Champlain. 137 sites: ~69 tent, 29 full-hookup RV, 39 water/electric trailer sites, plus lean-tos; 30/50-amp. 23-ft rigs fit (trailer drop area available). Open mid-May through Oct 15. Reservable online via Firefly Reservations. RV Life: 3.5* / $$$ --Claude

name: Lake Eden Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 7d005f5 (Audit all 33 VT waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Town of Eden municipal recreation area on Lake Eden. ~17 overnight RV sites (lower #1-8, upper #9-17) with electric/water/sewer, plus a few tent sites along the shore; beach house, playground. Open Memorial Day through early Oct. Reserve by phone/email. RV Life: unrated / $ --Claude

name: Prouty Beach & Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 7d005f5 (Audit all 33 VT waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: City of Newport (Newport Parks & Recreation) municipal campground on Lake Memphremagog. Over 75 campsites, RV (up to ~40 ft) and tent including primitive tent sites; full hookups available (50-amp water/electric/sewer per listings). Open late Apr through mid-Oct. Reservable online and by phone. RV Life: 4.5* / $$$ --Claude

name: Campbell's Bay Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'bayview' assessed in commit 7d005f5 (Audit all 33 VT waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Family-run private campground (Campbell family) on Campbell Bay, Lake Champlain in Swanton. RV sites with electric/water plus a small non-utility/tent area, lakeshore cabins, on-site store, boat rentals with gas dock. Open May 1 - Oct 1. Reserve by phone/email. RV Life: 5.0* / $$$ --Claude

name: Kenolie Village Campground
note: Private family campground, 150+ sites, big rigs to ~70 ft incl. 50A pull-throughs and 30A back-ins. Water & electric hookups; dump station. Open Apr 10-Oct 25 (2026). Mix of seasonal and nightly transient sites; tent sites are nightly-only. Reservable by phone or online (RoverPass). Within walking distance of the West River but the campground sits back from it. Source: operator rates-reservations page, Campendium. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Bald Mountain Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 7d005f5 (Audit all 33 VT waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Private family campground (est. 1968) on 160+ acres along the West River at 1760 State Forest Rd, Townshend. ~190 open and wooded sites, 62 pull-through; many sites on the West River or the beaver pond. Mix of nightly and seasonal sites plus a separate tent area and group camping. Water & electric or water-only; dump station, laundry, showers. Open May through Columbus Day. Reserve by phone or email. Source: operator reservations page. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Getaway Mountain Campground
note: Private campground on ~59 acres at 3628 Route 5 S, Ascutney. ~100 sites incl. large pull-throughs with full hookups (water/electric/sewer, 30/50A); heated pool, store, rec room, laundry. Open ~May 1-Oct 25. Mix of seasonal RVs/trailers and nightly transient sites; reservable by phone or RoverPass. Near the Connecticut River and Mount Ascutney but set back west of the river across Route 5/rail line. Source: operator/RoverPass listings, Campendium. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Country Village Campground
note: Small private wooded campground at 40 US Route 7, Leicester. 41 sites (37 RV, 5 tent); full-hookup RV sites; pool, laundry, showers, dump station. Open May 1-Oct 14. Nightly transient plus weekly/monthly rates; reserve by phone (no online booking shown). Not on water - nearest lakes/river within ~5 miles. Source: operator homepage, RoverPass, Good Sam. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Meadowcrest Campground
note: Small family-owned campground on US-2 along the Winooski River in central VT (Marshfield/Plainfield area). ~16 sites: 14 RV water/electric/sewer (some 30A, 2 with 50A) plus 2 tent sites; pull-through and spacious grassy sites. Nightly transient (~$30/night) alongside weekly/monthly/seasonal; tent ~$20/night. Free hot showers, solar-heated pool, playground, rec room. Operating for 2026; reserve by phone/email. Source: operator page. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Groton Forest Road Campground
note: Private commercial campground at 2654 VT-232, Marshfield, just N of Groton State Forest. 32 sites (RV + tent), 9 pull-throughs, max RV 40 ft; 15/30-amp electric + water, dump station, showers, laundry, store. Open May-Oct. Mixed transient + seasonal with real nightly availability (reviewer paid $41/night); reservable by phone/email and via RoverPass. Ownership changed ~2022. RV Life: 4.0* / $$ --Claude

name: Vermont Freedom Campground
note: Private adult-only (18+), clothing-optional campground on 21 acres at 218 Tousant Hill Rd, Greensboro Bend (Stannard), NEK. ~36 RV sites with 30/50-amp + water, partial hookups, dump station, showers, heated pool, hot tubs; tent camping too. Transient nightly stays (~$48) plus day passes ($30); also rents themed cabins/campers. Open May 1 - Oct 20. Booking by phone/text/email (also 802-274-3091) - no online portal. Max RV length not published. RV Life: 5.0* / $$ --Claude

name: Hazen's Notch Campground
note: Privately owned, family-operated 30+ years at 185 Green Hill Rd, Lowell (NOT the hike-in Hazen's Notch ski center). 50 sites: 30 RV (water, 30/50-amp electric, sewer; some full-hookup) + 20 tent/trailer. Dump station, hot showers, firewood. Drive-in (gravel/dirt road off Rte 58, passable); park-next-to-site. Open ~May-Oct. Transient nightly (~$30-50) plus weekly/monthly/seasonal. Reserve by phone; no online booking. Max RV length not published. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Barrewood Campground
note: Small private campground (Barrewood Campground Inc.) at 3102 VT-100, Westfield. 38 sites: ~6 full hookup, ~32 electric/water (20/30-amp), 6 pull-throughs, 5 tent-only; accepts big rigs (~55-60 ft). Camp store, showers, laundry, pool, playground. Open seasonally ~May 1 - Oct 15. Transient nightly ($44 W/E, $48 W/E/S); Passport America honored. Reservable by phone (802-744-6340 alt), not online. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Millbrook Campground
note: Small private campground at 1184 VT-100, Westfield, on Mill Brook near Jay Peak. ~34 sites in a one-way loop: RV full-hookup, tent (water+electric), plus some designated seasonal; swimming pond on-site. Primarily transient/nightly (online date-range booking, 2-night min); RV full-hookup ~$41, tent ~$29 (+9% VT tax). Max vehicle ~37 ft. Free showers/WiFi. Open ~first Sat in May - second Mon in Oct. Under new ownership 2026 (Corey Alix). RV Life: 5.0* / $$ --Claude

name: Monadnock State Park (Gilson Pond Campground)
note: Gilson Pond camping area at Monadnock SP: 41 sites total (16 tent-only, 12 platform, 5 RV/trailer up to 30 ft, 3 standard up to 25 ft, plus 5 remote hike-to). A 23-ft RV fits the RV/Trailer sites. No hookups per official reservation page. Flush toilets, showers, drinking water, small camp store. Season ~May 1-Oct 11. Reservable online/by phone via NH State Parks (ReserveAmerica). RV Life: 5.0* / $$$. --Claude

name: Greenfield State Park
note: Largest campground in the NH State Park system on Otter Lake: ~252 back-in gravel sites ranging ~20-38 ft, one camping unit per site. NO hookups. Drinking water, flush toilets, showers, camp store, beaches. ~175 sites reservable, remainder FCFS. Season ~mid-May to Columbus Day. Reservable online/by phone via NH State Parks. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Pawtuckaway State Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 32ea296 (Audit all 47 NH waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: ~190+ sites on Pawtuckaway Lake across Big Island, Horse Island, and Neals Cove loops (~55 tent-only; 100+ take pop-ups/trailers/motorhomes). Most sites fit rigs up to 24 ft; select sites up to 38 ft, so a 23-ft RV fits. NO hookups. Showers, drinking water, beach. Season ~May-Oct. Reservable online/by phone via NH State Parks; some FCFS. Note: Neals Cove access road is reportedly rough. RV Life: 3.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Bear Brook State Park
note: Beaver Pond campground at Bear Brook SP: ~101 wooded primitive sites, ~36 RV-friendly (RV spaces limited to 38 ft due to narrow roads/smaller sites). NO water/electric hookups (one site has 30-amp). Showers, vault toilets, dump station, camp store, swim beach, canoe rentals. Reservable via NH State Parks (ReserveAmerica); some FCFS. Season ~May-Oct. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Pillsbury State Park
note: Primitive 41-site campground at Pillsbury SP around Butterfield/May Pond: 14 standard drive-in sites, 6 tent-only, 4 small-popup/tent sites, 11 remote/canoe/walk-in sites. Standard sites listed up to 38 ft max, so a 23-ft rig fits the larger standard sites (some are small). NO electricity/hookups; vault toilets, drinking water; cash only on-site. Reservable via NH State Parks (ReserveAmerica). Season ~May-Oct. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: White Lake State Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 32ea296 (Audit all 47 NH waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: NH state park on White Lake (Tamworth/W. Ossipee). ~200 wooded sites across two camping areas; tents, trailers and RVs (12 tent-only sites). No hookups; dump station and communal water spigots; flush toilets, coin-op showers. Max RV ~38 ft but many sites smaller-check length when booking. Open Memorial Day weekend to ~mid-Oct; reservable via ReserveAmerica with some FCFS sites held. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Lafayette Place Campground (Franconia Notch State Park)
note: NH state park campground in Franconia Notch, west of the parkway. 97 wooded sites (88 reservable, 7 FCFS, 1 youth group); accommodates smaller rigs and pop-ups up to 28 ft-no large RVs (Cannon Mtn RV Park nearby takes 40 ft). No hookups; coin-op showers, camp store. Reservable May-Oct via ReserveAmerica, FCFS off-season. RV Life: 5.0* / $$ --Claude

name: Dry River Campground (Crawford Notch State Park)
note: NH state park campground in Crawford Notch on US-302 (Hart's Location/Bartlett), 36 wooded sites on a 31-site loop between the road and the Dry River. RVs/trailers to 40 ft but some sites only 20-30 ft-check length. No hookups; flush & vault toilets, hot showers, laundry. Reservable early May-mid Oct via ReserveAmerica, FCFS off-season. RV Life: 3.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Cannon Mountain RV Park (Franconia Notch State Park)
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 32ea296 (Audit all 47 NH waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Small 7-site state-run RV park at the Cannon Mountain tramway base on Echo Lake (Franconia Notch). Full 3-way hookups (water/sewer/electric) May-Columbus Day; off-season electric only, carry-in/carry-out. Self-contained rigs to 40 ft; reservations required year-round (ReserveAmerica); no pets. NOT the ski area-this is the adjacent RV park. RV Life: 4.0* / $$$ --Claude

name: Moose Brook State Park
note: NH state park campground in Gorham (30 Jimtown Rd), ~56-59 wooded sites on two sides of a road. Largest sites ~30 ft-OK for a 23-ft rig, not for big rigs. No hookups, no dump station; potable water spigots, full restrooms, showers. Spring-fed swimming pond on Moose Brook. Reservable Memorial Day-Columbus Day via ReserveAmerica, FCFS off-season; open ~May-late Oct. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Jericho Mountain State Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 32ea296 (Audit all 47 NH waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: NH state park campground near Berlin (298 Jericho Lake Rd) by Jericho Lake. 9 RV sites (two pull-through to 40 ft, rest back-in 24-36 ft) plus 4 tent sites, 2 Adirondack shelters and 5 cabins (RV sites are the basis for inclusion). No hookups; flush toilets, showers, laundry. Day-use beach, boat launch and 75+ mi ATV trail network. Reservable up to 11 mo via ReserveAmerica; open through late Oct. RV Life: 4.0* / $$ --Claude

name: Umbagog Lake State Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 32ea296 (Audit all 47 NH waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: 32-site drive-in base campground at the south end of Umbagog Lake (Cambridge/Errol, NH, off Rt 26). Mix of standard, pop-up/tent, tent-only sites and 2 lean-tos; electric (15-30A) and water hookups at base, dump station, camp store, flush/pit toilets and coin showers, canoe/kayak rentals. Back-in sites; max equipment length varies 10-24 ft, so a 23-ft rig fits only the larger sites. Open Memorial Day weekend through Columbus Day weekend (2026: May 22-Oct 11). Reservable via ReserveAmerica; FCFS walk-ins also welcome. A separate 35 boat/paddle-in remote sites are NOT included here. Operator: NH Division of Parks & Recreation. RV Life: 4.5* / $$$ --Claude

name: Mollidgewock State Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 32ea296 (Audit all 47 NH waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Primitive ~42-44 site campground along the Androscoggin River in the Thirteen Mile Woods Scenic Area, ~3 mi south of Errol. Mostly tent-only and pop-up/tent plus 2 lean-tos and remote canoe-to sites; 2 'standard' drive-in sites accommodate small trailers/RVs (site #029 is a 25 ft pull-through), so a 23-ft rig fits the standard sites only. NO hookups; pit toilets, potable water, fire rings; wood/ice and canoe/kayak rentals on site. Open Memorial Day weekend through Columbus Day weekend (2026: May 22-Oct 11). Reservable via ReserveAmerica; self-registration for walk-ins. Operator: NH Division of Parks & Recreation. RV Life: unrated (0*) / $ --Claude

name: Coleman State Park
note: NH State Parks campground in Stewartstown on Little Diamond Pond, Great North Woods. ~36 reservable units total (standard, pop-up/tent, tent-only, lean-to, cabins, group); drive-in standard/pop-up sites fit self-contained RVs, up to 40 ft on select pull-through sites. NO hookups; dump station, flush toilets, coin showers. All sites ATV-trail accessible. Season ~May 15-Oct 11. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica (some FCFS sites at campground). Operated by NH Division of Parks & Recreation. Limited/no cell service. RV Life: 4.0* / $$ --Claude

name: Lake Francis State Park
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 32ea296 (Audit all 47 NH waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: NH State Parks campground in Pittsburg on 2,000-acre Lake Francis, Great North Woods. 45 drive-in sites incl. 9 with water/electric (20-amp) hookups (7 of them waterfront, sites in the 13-21 block) and 9 ATV-camping sites; accommodates RVs up to ~40 ft. $35 no-hookup / $45 waterview-hookup. 14-day max stay. Season ~May 15-Oct 24. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica. Operated by NH Division of Parks & Recreation. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Campton Campground
note: WMNF (Pemigewasset Ranger District), off NH-49 ~2 mi from I-93 exit 28. 58 sites among white pines; max RV/trailer 50 ft. No hookups or dump station; coin showers ($2.50). Open mid-May to mid-October. Mix of reservable (online/phone) and FCFS sites. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Waterville Campground
note: WMNF (Pemigewasset Ranger District), Waterville Valley. 27 sites total — 6 tent-only (no vehicles), 19 sites fit RVs/trailers ~25–30 ft (30 ft cap); a 23-ft rig fits. No hookups; vault toilets, potable water. Open mid-May to mid-October, ~$24/night. Mix of reservable and FCFS sites. RV Life: 2.5* / $$. --Claude

name: White Ledge Campground
note: WMNF (Saco Ranger District), off NH-16 ~5 mi south of Conway, base of Mt. Chocorua. 28 sites (some tent-only); fits RVs. No hookups or dump station; accessible restrooms, hand-pump water. Open mid-May to mid-October, $30/night. Mix of reservable (online/phone) and FCFS sites. RV Life: 2.0* / $. --Claude

name: Tripoli Road Camping Area
note: WMNF designated roadside/dispersed camping corridor along Tripoli Rd (reached from I-93 exit 31). Drive-in numbered roadside sites managed by a concessionaire; register at the trailer on the western end. Sites range from riverside to secluded wooded; no hookups, no amenities (primitive). Open mid-May through October, FCFS only — no advance reservations; flat per-vehicle fee (~$20 weekday / $25 weekend). Site sizes vary — not all fit a 23-ft rig; choose a pull-through/larger pulloff. RV Life: unrated / $. --Claude

name: Passaconaway Campground
note: WMNF (Saco Ranger District), on the Kancamagus Highway (NH-112) ~14 mi west of Conway. 33 quiet wooded back-in sites suited to vans, truck campers, and smaller RVs/trailers — a 23-ft rig fits some sites but not the largest rigs. No hookups (no Kanc campground has them). Swift River a short distance away. Open mid-May to mid-October, $30/night. FCFS only — no reservations. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Jigger Johnson Campground
note: WMNF (Saco Ranger District), on the Kancamagus Highway (NH-112). 75 wooded sites (1–9 tent-only, rest fit RVs/trailers — large sites accommodate most rigs, only road maneuverability limits length). No hookups; the only Kanc campground with (coin) showers, flush toilets. Swift River behind the campground. Open mid-May to mid-October, $30/night. FCFS only — no reservations. RV Life: 4.0* / $. --Claude

name: Covered Bridge Campground (WMNF)
note: WMNF (Saco Ranger District) campground on Passaconaway Rd off the Kancamagus Hwy (Rt 112), Albany. 49 sites, no hookups, vault toilets, hand-pump water. Most sites max ~25 ft trailer/RV. Reservable on recreation.gov (cg 232171). Season mid-May to mid-Oct. Note: the historic Albany Covered Bridge has a 7'9" height / 3-ton limit, so larger rigs must enter via Passaconaway Rd from West Side Rd in Conway, not over the bridge. RV Life: 4.0* / $. --Claude

name: Blackberry Crossing Campground (WMNF)
note: WMNF (Saco Ranger District) campground on the Kancamagus Hwy (Rt 112) ~6 mi west of Conway, across from the Albany Covered Bridge. 26 sites, no hookups, vault toilets, hand-pump water; mostly level compacted-gravel pads (some former-CCC concrete pads). Sites fit RVs/trailers well over 23 ft (allstays lists up to ~65 ft; rec.gov site A045 35 ft). First-come, first-served only — NOT on recreation.gov. Season mid-May to mid-Oct; $30/night + $6 extra vehicle. Former 1930s CCC camp with self-guided tour. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Russell Pond Campground
note: WMNF (Pemigewasset Ranger District), near Woodstock off Tripoli Rd. 84 sites (tent to RV); max trailer 40 ft. Flush toilets, hot showers; no on-site dump station. Open mid-May to mid-October. Mix of reservable and FCFS sites (the prime pond-side sites are FCFS-only). RV Life: 3.5* / $. --Claude

name: Big Rock Campground (WMNF)
note: WMNF (Pemigewasset Ranger District) campground on the Kancamagus Hwy (Rt 112), ~7 mi east of Lincoln. 28 wooded sites (sites 21-28 tent-only), each with table, tent pad and parking turnout; no hookups, no water/electric/sewer. RVs/motorhomes up to ~40 ft on most sites (8 sites share a common lot). First-come, first-served only — NOT on recreation.gov. Season mid-May to mid-Oct; $30/night + $6 extra vehicle. RV Life: 3.5* / $. --Claude

name: Wildwood Campground (WMNF)
note: WMNF (Pemigewasset Ranger District) campground on NH Rt 112 in Easton, ~9 mi west of North Woodstock along the Wild Ammonoosuc River. 26 wooded sites, gravel pads, no hookups, vault toilets, hand-pump water; CCC picnic pavilion across the road. First-come, first-served (self-service pay) — NOT reservable on recreation.gov. FS lists max equipment length 50 ft; some reviews note tight access for larger trailers due to road/site conditions, so it suits a 23-ft rig more than big rigs. Season ~mid-May to Oct; $24/night + $6 extra vehicle. RV Life rating is low (unrated/1*) but it is a confirmed, currently-operating WMNF campground. RV Life: 1.0* / $. --Claude

name: Haystack Road Dispersed Camping (WMNF)
note: DISPERSED drive-in camping corridor on Haystack Rd (Forest Rd off US-3, ~north of the Beaver Brook Picnic Area), Jefferson/Twin Mountain in the WMNF. 11 numbered, wooded drive-in sites, free, first-come-first-served, 14-night limit, up to 3 vehicles/15 people per site. Only amenity is a fire pit — no water, toilets, or trash. Seasonal: road closed in winter, opens late spring after it dries out (mid-June in some years); check the WMNF page/Facebook for status. Road ends at the Little River and the North Twin trailhead. Sites are short/rustic — fine for a 23-ft rig but verify length on arrival; not a reservation campground. RV Life: unrated / $. --Claude

name: Sugarloaf 2 Campground
note: White Mountain NF (USFS) campground on Zealand Rd off US-302, near Jefferson/Twin Mountain. 32 rustic sites near the Ammonoosuc River; more RV-capable than Sugarloaf 1 (per-site limits ~25-30 ft, e.g. site 18 = 30 ft). No hookups, no dump station; vault toilets, hand-pump potable water. Mid-May to mid-Oct. Reservable on recreation.gov (some sites FCFS). $24/night. --Claude RV Life: 4.5* / $

name: Sugarloaf 1 Campground
note: White Mountain NF (USFS) campground on Zealand Rd off US-302, near Twin Mountain. 29 rustic sites, single loop along the Zealand River; skews more tent sites than Sugarloaf 2. No hookups, no dump station; vault toilets, hand-pump potable water. Mid-May to mid-Oct. Reservable on recreation.gov (some sites FCFS). $24/night. --Claude RV Life: 5.0* / $

name: Cold River Campground (WMNF)
note: WMNF (Saco Ranger District) campground on Rt 113 at the southern end of Evans Notch, Chatham (~15 mi N of Fryeburg ME), near the Cold River and Basin Pond. 14 sites organized on one loop; no hookups, vault toilets, hand-pump water, picnic pavilion; no showers/dump. Sites accept RVs/trailers up to ~40 ft. Mix of reservable (recreation.gov cg 232175) and FCFS sites. Season ~mid-May to mid-Oct; $30/night + $6 extra vehicle. RV Life unrated (0) but confirmed operating via FS and rec.gov. RV Life: unrated / $. --Claude

name: Zealand Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 32ea296 (Audit all 47 NH waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: White Mountain NF (USFS) campground on Zealand Rd off US-302 near Twin Mountain. 11 rustic sites in a single loop between the highway and the Ammonoosuc River; small RVs fit but the loop road is tight (no posted max length). No hookups, no dump, no showers; vault toilets, potable water. Mid-May to mid-Oct. FCFS only -- not reservable on recreation.gov. $24/night. --Claude RV Life: 0* / $$

name: Basin Campground
note: White Mountain NF (USFS) campground in the Evans Notch / Cold River area off NH-113, Chatham. 13 sites (7 standard nonelectric, 6 tent-only); standard sites cap ~30 ft, a 23-ft rig fits but no pull-throughs/not big-rig friendly. No hookups, no dump/showers; vault toilets, potable water. Mid-May to mid-Oct. Reservable on recreation.gov. Loop set back from Basin Pond (separate day-use area/boat launch). NOTE: closed for construction, expected reopen mid-2026 -- confirm via recreation.gov or Saco Ranger District before relying. --Claude RV Life: 3.0* / $$

name: Dolly Copp Campground
note: Largest WMNF (USFS) campground, ~177 sites in 13 loops on the Peabody River in Pinkham Notch off NH-16, near Gorham. Post-renovation: ~52 sites have water + 30/50-amp electric, rest non-electric, no sewer. Flush toilets, coin showers, potable water. Per-site max length varies (many fit 40-45 ft; 23-ft rig fits easily). Mid-May to mid-Oct. Reservable on recreation.gov with some FCFS sites. RIVE loop borders the river. $30/night ($14 electric). --Claude RV Life: 4.5* / $$

name: Wheelock Park Campground
note: City of Keene Parks & Recreation municipal campground. 43 sites -- mix of full-hookup, water-only, electric-only and no-hookup (dry) sites; big-rig friendly up to ~40 ft. Dump station, swimming pool (fee, late June-Aug), hot showers, laundry, playground, ballfields. Open Memorial Day weekend through mid-September. Reservable online via RecDesk or by phone. RV Life: 0.0* / $ (unrated). --Claude

name: Storrs Pond Recreation Area
note: Owned and operated by the Hanover Improvement Society (private non-profit). 17 RV sites + 12 tent sites; RV sites big-rig friendly up to 50 ft with full or partial hookups; dump station, swimming pool, hot showers, laundry. Season mid-May through Columbus Day. Reservations strongly recommended -- book online at storrspond.org or call the office. RV/site fee around $47/night (4 people). RV Life: 3.5* / $$$. --Claude

name: Sunshine Valley RV Park
note: Family-owned commercial RV park on Milan Hill Rd offering seasonal and transient sites. 28 sites (six pull-through, four back-in) plus 3 tent sites and 1 rental camper; pull-throughs accommodate large rigs. All sites have septic hookups; NO electric hookups -- generators required (7am-10pm), water via mobile tank + dump station. Free WiFi, above-ground pool, snack shack, bathhouse with showers; ATV/hiking trails adjacent. Open May 1 - Oct 31. Reservable online (Firefly). RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Saddleback Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'pond' assessed in commit 32ea296 (Audit all 47 NH waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Family-owned; 26 sites (water/electric/sewer, 20/30/50 amp, dump station, 3 pull-throughs). Big-rig friendly, accepts RVs to 50 ft. Open May 1-Oct 31. Central spring-fed swim/fishing pond. Mostly seasonal occupants but nightly ($55 RV) and weekly transient rates published on operator site; bookable via reservation-request form and RoverPass. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Epsom Valley Campground
note: Family-owned since 1974; ~64-65 sites (42 full hookup, 22 electric/water, 6 pull-thru), 30-amp, dump station, hot showers, laundry, free WiFi. Max RV 50 ft, big-rig/slide-out friendly. On the Suncook River (canoe/kayak), mini golf, swimming. Carries seasonal sites but nightly transient sites bookable online via CampLife (nightly $35-165 per The Dyrt). Open ~mid-May to mid-Oct. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Davidson's Countryside Campground
