waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverfront' assessed in commit 32ea296 (Audit all 47 NH waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Private family campground on the Pemigewasset River, ~3 mi from I-93 Exit 23, Bristol. ~99 RV sites (sources cite up to 152 total incl. tent); primitive tent, water/electric field & wooded, and water/sewer/30-amp (some 50-amp) sites. Big-rig friendly, many take slide-outs. Pool, private river beach, boat launch/rentals, store, laundry. Open ~May 15-Oct 15. Both seasonal and nightly transient; reservations by phone/email only (no online booking), 2-night minimum, 2026 nightly $48-$60. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Wolfeboro Campground
note: Family-owned since 1971; 50 wooded sites (RV sites 1-42 plus tent sites T-1-T-11). RV sites have water/electric/sewer, 20/30-amp, dump station; max trailer length 32 ft. Open ~late May (Memorial Day)-mid Oct (Columbus Day). Both nightly transient (2026 RV $45/night) and seasonal sites; 2-night weekend minimum. Phone reservations only with mailed deposit check, no online booking. Store, laundry, heated restrooms/showers. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Beaver Hollow Campground
note: Private RV resort on ~40 wooded acres, 700 Route 16, Ossipee; ~139 sites. W/E/septic, 30/50-amp, many pull-throughs; max RV 45 ft. Open mid-May-Columbus Day. Nightly transient ($70/night) and seasonal both offered; sites 101-139 are the seasonal-only area. Tent sites and rental campers too. Heated saltwater pool, store, laundry, playgrounds, gated. Frenchman's Brook runs through. Phone or online request form (no instant-book). RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Baker River Campground
note: Drive-in RV + tent campground at 56 Campground Rd, Rumney, on the Baker River; ~62 sites (21 full-hookup, 21 electric/water), 50/30/20-amp, pull-thrus, max RV 40 ft. Open ~late May (Memorial Day)-mid Oct (Columbus Day). Both nightly-transient (reservable online via Campspot) and returning seasonal campers. River beach for swimming/fishing/tubing. Operator: Modern America Campgrounds (sister property Riverbrook RV Resort is a separate campground ~10 min away). RV Life: 5.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Riverbend Family Campground
note: Family-owned campground on the Bearcamp River, 11 Jackman Pond Rd, South Tamworth; ~27-35 sites (mix seasonal and nightly transient). Full hookups (water/sewer, 30/50-amp), pull-thrus and back-ins, plus tent sites; max RV ~45-60 ft. Pool, playground, store, bathhouse, riverwalk. Open ~mid-May to mid-Oct. Nightly transient 2025 rates ~$45 full hookup / $35 partial / $25 tent; book by phone or via RoverPass. Operator's own site (riverbend-nh.com) currently serves an expired SSL cert. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: The Pastures Campground
note: Privately owned (Doug & Kim Robinson) campground on the Connecticut River at 499 NH Route 10, Orford; NH's only private campground on the Connecticut River. ~61 sites (some listings say 65) on grass, 30/50-amp, water + electric at all sites, sewer/full hookups on some; max RV 43 ft. Mix of seasonal and nightly transient (daily/weekly/seasonal rates; 2026 daily ~$40 RV / $35 tent). Boat dock, canoe/kayak/pontoon rentals, heated pool, public boat launch on adjacent property. Reservations by phone/email; no online booking. Open ~late May-early fall. RV Life: 5.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Apple Hill Campground
note: Private year-round (four-season) RV park run by the Sykes family at 647 Maple St, Bethlehem. ~30 full/partial-hookup sites, big-rig friendly with 3-way pull-thru hookups (fits a 23-ft RV); 30/50-amp service, dump station, LP/propane, hot showers, laundry, store, rec room. Nightly transient rates (~$25 no-hookup / $35 w/hookup) plus seasonal rates; reservations by phone. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Mountain Valley Campground
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'riverview' assessed in commit 32ea296 (Audit all 47 NH waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Private RV park (formerly Percy Campground) on the Upper Ammonoosuc River in Stark, run by Tim & Beth. Camper sites have water + 30/50-amp electric, picnic table, fire ring, plus dump station; new full-hookup (W/E/septic) sites and bathhouse added for 2026. Both nightly/weekly and seasonal sites; online reservations via Firefly. Open ~May 22-Oct 12. Camper sites sit in a field overlooking the river; tent sites along the river. RV Life: 5.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Rudy's Cabins and Campground
note: Private RV campground + cabins in the Great North Woods on Clarksville (Rudy's) Pond. 41 sites total: 38 seasonal/long-term, only 3 overnight transient sites (all full hookup, 30/50 amp, ~$60-65/night). Pull-through and back-in gravel sites; RVs to ~50 ft. One waterfront cabin and a few pond-edge tent sites; RV sites set back from water. Open Memorial Day-November. Fly-fishing pond, ATV-trail access. Operator: Rudy's Cabins and Campground, LLC. RV Life: 5.0* / $. --Claude

name: Shawme-Crowell State Forest
note: ~285 wooded sites at the gateway to Cape Cod, split into Area 1 (~47 sites) and Area 2 (112 sites), plus group camping and 6 yurts. Max RV length 30 ft (Area 2), so a 23-ft rig fits. No hookups; dump station, flush toilets, showers, playground, camp store. Main season late May to early Oct; reduced Thu-Sat-only operation Oct-Apr. Reservable via ReserveAmerica. Single campground despite appearing as both state and dnr rows in RV Life. MA DCR. RV Life: unrated (0*) / $. --Claude

name: Nickerson State Park
note: ~418 mostly-wooded sites in seven areas (1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 6X, 7/Primitive). No hookups; dump station, water spigots per loop, hot showers. RV/trailer length varies by loop (Area 5 ~25 ft, Area 6X up to 35 ft, larger sites elsewhere), so a 23-ft rig fits comfortably. Surrounds several kettle ponds. Season mid-April to mid-October; reservable online via ReserveAmerica (MA DCR). RV Life: 3.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Myles Standish State Forest
note: ~400 campsites across multiple pond-side loops (Fearing, Charge, Curlew, Barretts ponds). Mix of tent and RV/trailer sites; RV-friendly sites cap at 25 ft (a few only 20 ft), so a 23-ft rig fits the 25-ft sites. No hookups; dump station, hot showers at some loops. Also equestrian, group, and 3 yurt sites. Season ~April-October; reservable online via ReserveAmerica (MA DCR). RV Life: 4.0* / $. --Claude

name: Massasoit State Park
note: MA DCR. ~84-94 sites; Loop B (B22-53) takes rigs to ~40 ft, Loop A (A1-21) to ~25 ft, Loop C tent-only. ~50 sites with electric (20/30/50 amp) + water in Loops A & B; generator-free area; dump station. Reopened 2018 after major renovation; operating 2026 (~Apr 30-Oct 11). Reservable via ReserveAmerica (contract MA, parkId 32611). RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Granville State Forest
note: MA DCR. 22 sites in the Halfway Brook camping area in two loops; the 1-11 & 22 loop is largely tent-only, the 12-21 loop holds the trailer/RV-friendly sites (some 25-35 ft, but >35 ft not recommended on tight turns). No hookups, no dump station. A 23-ft rig fits the right sites - confirm per-site length on ReserveAmerica. Season ~Memorial Day to fall. Reservable via ReserveAmerica (contract MA, parkId 32607). RV Life: 0.5* / $. --Claude

name: Tolland State Forest
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakefront' assessed in commit 8f32c79 (Audit all 23 MA waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: MA DCR state forest in East Otis on a peninsula in Otis Reservoir. 93 sites: 44 standard non-electric, 29 tent-only, 14 pop-up/tent, 1 RV non-electric; a 23-ft rig fits the standard non-tent sites (avoid the tent-only sites). Max ~30-35 ft. No hookups, no dump station; 3 comfort stations w/ flush toilets + hot showers; swim beach + boat launch. Season mid-May to mid-Oct. Reservations required via ReserveAmerica (no walk-ins). RV Life: 3.5* / $. --Claude

name: Wells State Park
note: MA DCR state park in Sturbridge on Walker Pond. 60 sites: ~39 drive-in RV (gravel pull-through/back-in), ~13 tent-only, 1 group site, 4 yurts. Max RV ~35 ft. No hookups; central water spigots, flush toilets, hot showers; reportedly no dump station. Camper swim beach on the pond. Season ~mid-May to mid-Oct. Reservations required via ReserveAmerica (no walk-ins). RV Life: 5.0* / $. --Claude

name: Wompatuck State Park
note: MA DCR state park in Hingham. ~250-262 wooded sites, roughly half with electric (reported 20-amp by DCR, 30/50-amp by some reviewers); no water/sewer at sites; on-site dump station; central water spigots. Max ~35-40 ft (a 23-ft rig fits; loops/neighborhood streets are tight for big rigs). Season early May to mid-Oct. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica (no walk-ins). 2026 caveat: reservations paused 5/7-7/1/2026 for bathhouse renovation; stays from 7/2 unaffected. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: October Mountain State Forest
note: 44 campsites + 3 yurts in a three-tiered layout: Loop 1 for RV/trailers, Loop 2 wooded, Loop 3 tent + yurts. No hookups; RV dump station, hot showers, flush toilets on site. Max rig length 34 ft per ReserveAmerica, so a 23-ft RV fits easily. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica (no walk-ins). Operated by MA DCR. RV Life: 3.0* / $$. --Claude

name: DAR State Forest
note: MA DCR. 51 sites (standard non-electric, plus popup/tent and a group site) on a ridge near Upper Highland Lake off Moore Hill Rd. No hookups; dump station at HQ complex. Max trailer length ~30 ft (no pull-throughs). Reservations required, no walk-ins; season ~mid-May to October. Reservable via ReserveAmerica (contract MA, parkId 32605). RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Otter River State Forest
note: 73 campsites (numbered to 78) + 4 yurts + 3 group sites around 2-acre Beaman Pond. Sites are small/tight and a 6-ton campground-bridge limit bars heavier rigs from sites 28-78; first loops mostly tent/popup, but level RV-capable sites exist (e.g. site 33). Self-contained units 25 ft or less, so a 23-ft rig fits. No hookups; hot showers, swimming beach. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica (no walk-ins). Season roughly mid-May to mid-Oct. MA DCR. RV Life: 3.5* / $. --Claude

name: Harold Parker State Forest
note: MA DCR Lorraine Park Campground. 91 sites; 11 sites with electric + water (20/30/50 amp), rest non-hookup but with individual water spigots; on-site dump station, flush toilets, hot showers. Accommodates rigs up to ~40 ft. Season ~late May to early/mid October. Reservable via ReserveAmerica (contract MA, parkId 32608). RV Life: 4.5* / $$$. --Claude

name: Mohawk Trail State Forest
note: 46 drive-in campsites plus 6 walk-in riverside sites, 1 group site, and 6 log cabins. No hookups; dump station on site. Drive-in sites fit RVs/trailers up to ~25 ft, so a 23-ft rig fits. Season mid-May to mid-October; reservable online via ReserveAmerica. Operated by MA DCR. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Lake Dennison Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: waterfront 'lakeview' assessed in commit 8f32c79 (Audit all 23 MA waterfront designations against the gate); on-water value recorded at that commit (no separate per-entry evidence string)
note: Land is USACE (Birch Hill Dam flood-control project) but the campground is operated by MA DCR (Otter River State Forest staff), so credited state. 151 wooded sites + 1 group site, many on Lake Dennison. No water/electric hookups; on-site dump station, flush toilets, showers; pull-thru sites and big-rig access (fits a 23-ft RV). Season ~Memorial Day to mid-October. Reservable via ReserveAmerica (contract MA, parkId 32610). RV Life: 4* / $. --Claude

name: Savoy Mountain State Forest
note: 45 wooded sites in a former apple orchard plus 1 group site and 4 log cabins, on South Pond. ~6 sites accommodate RVs (fill fast); max RV length 40 ft per ReserveAmerica, so a 23-ft rig fits. No hookups; dump station and potable-water fill at the entrance, flush toilets, showers. Season mid-May to mid-Oct; reservable online via ReserveAmerica (no walk-ins). NOTE: campground scheduled to close July 31, 2026 for bathhouse construction -- confirm reopening before relying on a late-2026 stay. MA DCR. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Pearl Hill State Park
note: 50 campsites under tall pines; DCR calls them among the largest/most private in MA. Most take tents, trailers, or RVs; a few are tent-only. Official max 35 ft, but only a few sites (e.g. #5, #10) suit a larger rig and a firsthand reviewer warned wooded access roads are tight over ~18 ft, so pick a large site and expect tight maneuvering. NO hookups and NO dump station. Hot showers/full bathhouse. Open Memorial Day to Labor Day weekend. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica. MA DCR. RV Life: 5.0* / $. --Claude

name: Clarksburg State Park
note: MA DCR. 45 wooded sites on a one-way loop near Mauserts Pond; no hookups, no on-site dump station (nearest at Savoy Mountain SF). Narrow loop with tight maneuvering; only some sites take longer trailers (one site posted 23 ft max) but a 23-ft rig fits with careful site selection. Season ~mid-May to Columbus Day. Reservable via ReserveAmerica (contract MA, parkId 32604). RV Life: 3.5* / $. --Claude

name: Bonny Rigg Camping Club
note: Private campground (markets as 'Bonny Rigg Camping Club') in the Berkshires at the corner of Rt 8N & 20, Becket. ~175-206 sites, mostly seasonal members but takes nightly transient RVs — Campendium reviewers report overnight spots near the pool (~$50/night), and it's a Passport America/Hipcamp/RoverPass park open to all (not a buy-in co-op). Open ~May 1-Sep 30. 30/50-amp electric + water; sewer only at a dump station near the entrance. Sites accommodate large RVs. Reservable by phone or online; deposit required. Pool, store, laundry. RV Life: 4.0* / $$ --Claude

name: Lamb City Campground
note: Private commercial campground on ~70 wooded acres at the start of the Mohawk Trail; family-run (Blake family) since 1981. ~241-280 sites: 224 full-hookup (20/30/50-amp), 6 pull-throughs, ~30 tent sites, ~140 seasonals, plus cabins/park model. Accepts both short-term transient and seasonal guests. Max RV length 65 ft. Three swimming pools and a 5-acre stocked fishing pond (Lamb City Pond). Open May 1-Oct 15. Reservable online or by phone. RV Life: 4.0* / $$$. --Claude

name: Country Aire Campground
note: Private family campground at 1753 Mohawk Trail (Route 2), East Charlemont, in the Berkshires near the Deerfield River. Open May 1-Oct 31. Pull-through and shaded sites with full hookups (W/E/S) or water/electric; tent sites and rental cabins also. Gravel roads, grass sites; operator states no RV size limit (reviews cite rigs up to ~50 ft). Both transient (daily/weekly/monthly) and seasonal sites; nightly reservable online via Campspot. Camp store, pool, playground, dump station. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Travelers Woods of New England
note: Private family-run campground (42 yrs) on River St in Bernardston, Connecticut River Valley. 125 sites: ~85 water/electric, 7 full hookup, 40 rally-field W/E; 30/50 amp; level grassy RV + tent sites, accommodates large rigs. Open May-Oct 17. Nightly/weekly/monthly stays; reservable online or by phone. Rates from ~$40/night. Low-clearance bridge en route - use operator directions, not GPS, for tall rigs. RV Life: 4.0* / $$ --Claude

name: Historic Valley Campground
note: City of North Adams MUNICIPAL campground (ownership=local) on Windsor Lake; 100 sites (lakeside, wilderness, pull-through) for RV/camper/tent, water + electric 20/30A with some sewer; dump station, renovated bathhouses, swimming/boating/fishing. RVs up to 50 ft. Open mid-May to mid-October; nightly transient reservations with one-night deposit (phone/email/online), seasonal campers also. RV Life: 4.0* / $$ --Claude

name: The Pines Campground
note: Family-owned, drive-in transient RV campground in the North Quabbin region; large wooded sites. Nightly transient rates alongside weekly/monthly: $50/night no-hookup, $55/night 3-way (W/E/S). Tent and RV sites; full-hookup RV sites available. Locke Brook runs along the back of the property; Frog Pond on-site. Reserve by phone/email (no online booking system). Per operator site map, ~50+ numbered sites. RV Life: 4.0* / $$ --Claude

name: Charlestown Breachway Campground
waterfront_evidence: legible satellite shows rows of parked RVs along the breachway channel / Ninigret Pond shore with an open sandy apron directly to the tidal water, no buffer (saltwater barrier spit).
note: RIDEM barrier-beach campground on the east side of the breachway between Ninigret Pond and Block Island Sound. 75 RV-only sites; self-contained units required (no tents, pop-ups, or canvas-top campers; permanent holding tanks). No hookups, no on-site dump/water (use Burlingame or Fishermen's Memorial). Packed-sand parking lot, rough access road but normal-vehicle drive-in (not 4WD); back-in sites 30-40 ft, max 40 ft. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica. Open ~April-Oct. --Claude RV Life: 4.0* / $$

name: Fishermen's Memorial State Park & Campground
waterfront_evidence: loops sit inland ~700-900m from Point Judith Pond behind marsh/residential/ woods; aggregator "sites 70-74 on the pond" contradicted by geography.
note: RIDEM state campground on the Point Judith peninsula, Narragansett. 182 sites: 147 trailer/RV + 35 tent-only across 4 areas. Areas 1 & 2 full hookups (W/E/S), Area 4 water+electric (no sewer), Area 3 tent-only no utilities. RV sites to 50 ft; a 23-ft rig fits easily. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica. Season ~April 10-Oct 30. --Claude RV Life: 4.5* / $$$

name: Lake St. George State Park
waterfront_evidence: Maine BPL/reviews, sites 21-23 right on the western shoreline (30/31 lake-view).
note: 38-site Maine state park campground on Lake St. George near Liberty. No hookups; dump station, water spigots, flush toilets, hot showers. Per-site length classes on the official map run up to >35' (a 23-ft rig fits any L/X/U site), though the official guide caps practical RVs at ~24 ft due to a switchback entrance driveway -- the 23-ft EKKO is fine, larger drive-throughs are constrained by the entrance, not the sites. Season mid-May to mid-Oct. Reservable via campwithme.com; some sites FCFS. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Lamoine State Park
waterfront_evidence: Firsthand manual audit by owner (--AWH note); waterfront 'bayview' per owner: Oceanfront Maine state park campground on Frenchman Bay, ~8 mi SE of Ellsworth (near Acadia). 62 single-family drive-in sites + 2 group areas; no hookups; drinking water, flush toilets, hot showers, dump station, campground host. Per-site length classes on the official map run up to 45'; a 23-ft rig fits any M/L/X/U site. Open May 15-Oct 15. Reservable via campwithme.com; a few sites FCFS, including two near the bay. RV Life: 3.5* / $$.
note: Oceanfront Maine state park campground on Frenchman Bay, ~8 mi SE of Ellsworth (near Acadia). 62 single-family drive-in sites + 2 group areas; no hookups; drinking water, flush toilets, hot showers, dump station, campground host. Per-site length classes on the official map run up to 45'; a 23-ft rig fits any M/L/X/U site. Open May 15-Oct 15. Reservable via campwithme.com; a few sites FCFS, including two near the bay. RV Life: 3.5* / $$. --AWH

name: Mount Blue State Park
note: 136-site wooded Maine state park campground at Webb Lake near Weld (Mount Blue itself is ~8 km NE). A cluster of SE-loop sites (1-15 area) have electric & water hookups; the rest are no-hookup. Drinking water, flush toilets, hot showers, dump station, campground host. Per-site length classes on the official map go up to >35'; a 23-ft rig fits any M/L/X/U site. Season ~mid-May to October. Reservable via campwithme.com; some sites FCFS. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Rangeley Lake State Park
waterfront_evidence: lakeside sites 17/19/21/23 have water views through trees via ~50ft path, 'none directly on waterfront'.
note: 50-site Maine state park campground on a loop above the south shore of Rangeley Lake. 8 sites (8, 12, 14, 16, 18, 20, 22, 24) have water & electric hookups, the rest no-hookup. Flush toilets, hot showers, dump station, boat launch. Per-site length classes on the official map run from 20' tent sites up to >35'; a 23-ft rig fits any M/L/X/U site. Open May 1-Oct 15. Mostly reservable via campwithme.com (2-night min); some sites FCFS. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Peaks-Kenny State Park
note: 56-site Maine state park campground on a wooded knoll on the South Cove peninsula of Sebec Lake; two loops with a center exit road. 5 sites (24, 22, 55, 25, 26, 56) have electric & water hookups, the rest no-hookup. Per-site length classes on the official map run up to >35'; a 23-ft rig fits any X/U site. Open May 15-Oct 1. Mix of reservable (campwithme.com, 2-night min, 14-day max Jul/Aug) and FCFS sites. Gravel access road is potholed but passable. Sand beach + canoe/kayak rental at a separate day-use area reached by foot trail. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Aroostook State Park
note: Maine's first state park, on Echo Lake ~5 mi S of Presque Isle. 30 wooded drive-in sites (+ 2 group areas). Per the official 2026 site map, sites are length-classed S(<=20')/M(<=25')/L(<=30')/X(<=35')/U(>35'); a 23-ft rig fits M and larger. The map shows electric/water hookup sites, but per a May 2026 ranger statement at reopening, hookups and the dump station were not yet operational and water/flush toilets/showers were still under construction (vault toilets available) -- treat as primitive/no-hookup for the 2026 season. Boat launch + swimming on Echo Lake. Reservable via campwithme.com (no walk-ins); some sites FCFS. RV Life: 4* / $. --Claude

name: Crocker Pond Campground
waterfront_evidence: per-site, 5 of 7 sites directly on the pond.
note: Smallest White Mountain NF (USFS) campground: 7 primitive sites on Crocker Pond, reached via a 5.8-mi local road then a 0.5-mi dirt road off Flat Rd from West Bethel. Suited to tents and smaller RVs/trailers (a 23-ft rig fits the larger sites; no length posted). No hookups, vault toilets, hand-pump water. $18/night honor system, first-come-first-served (not reservable). Season ~mid-May to mid-Oct. RV Life: unrated / $. --Claude

name: Hastings Campground
note: White Mountain NF (USFS) campground on Rte 113 in Evans Notch, near Gilead. 24 large, flat single-loop sites described as big-rig friendly; no hookups, vault toilets, hand-pump water. ~$24/night. Reservable online with some first-come-first-served sites. Season ~late May to mid-Oct. --Claude

name: Mattawamkeag Wilderness Park
note: Town of Mattawamkeag-owned park (1,000 acres) on the Mattawamkeag River. ~50 sites incl. 6 full-hookup (W/E/S) and 8 W/E RV sites; rigs up to ~35 ft. Store, rec building, free hot showers. Reached by an ~8-mi maintained gravel/dirt access road off US-2 — operator says drive-in, reachable for most vehicles (long but not 4WD); aggregators overstate it as 'off-highway.' Reservable via ReserveAmerica. Open ~May. RV Life: 2* / $$. --Claude

name: Portage Lake Municipal Campground
waterfront_evidence: open grassy camp area meets the shore with boat launch/dock; sites on the open apron.
note: Town of Portage Lake municipal RV campground at the end of West Rd, beside the Portage Lake boat landing. 6-7 electric-hookup sites on the lake. Permit via the Town Office (207-435-4361, credit card by phone) or mailed application. Busy on weekends. RV Life: unrated / $. --Claude

name: Fort Kent Riverside Park Campground
note: Town of Fort Kent municipal RV park at Riverside Park, off Dufour St at the confluence of the Fish and St. John rivers. 14 self-contained RV sites; park also has a boat landing, pavilion, trail and playground. Reserve through the Fort Kent Police Dept (207-834-5678). RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: St. John River Campground (Madawaska)
waterfront_evidence: angled RV pull-ins on open field ~35-45m from the river across an open sandy/grass bank, no screen.
note: Town of Madawaska's St. John River Campground, 102 Public Works Ave, on the St. John River. 8 sites; register/pay at the Madawaska Police Dept before setup. $35 first night, $25 each additional (weekly/monthly rates offered). Check-in 11a-8p. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Seboomook Wilderness Campground
waterfront_evidence: RV sites on open ground along the Moosehead shoreline on the campground point; dock at tip.
note: Private campground on the NW shore of Moosehead Lake in Seboomook Twp; 45 RV/tent sites, W/E (generator) on select waterfront sites, dump station + honeywagon, bathhouse, store. Access via ~30 mi of improved dirt road (drive carefully; do not trust GPS). Reservable via Campspot. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Big Eddy Campground
waterfront_evidence: sites on open ground along the W Branch Penobscot south bank at the Big Eddy pool.
note: Operated by the Chewonki Foundation on the West Branch of the Penobscot at the Big Eddy salmon pool, just west of Baxter SP; RV sites (no hookups -- water fill + limited charging only), rustic cabins, cabin tents, tent sites. Access via the gravel Golden Road. Reservable online. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Pumpkin Patch RV Resort
note: 75+ full-hookup (20/30/50A) big-rig sites incl. 44 pull-thru, flat/grassy; many seasonal residents but takes nightly/transient guests. Adult-oriented, quiet. Open May-mid Oct. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Fogg Brook Resort
note: 91 sites with full hookups (30/50A) plus an on-site restaurant and 18-hole golf course; nightly/transient welcome (deluxe suites year-round). Open May 15-Oct 15. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Sunset Point Campground
waterfront_evidence: RV loop on a point at the Harrington tidal bay; nearest sites on open ground near the water, boat dock.
note: ~21 RV + 9 tent sites on a tidal Maine bay (Harrington) with direct ocean access and a boat dock; E/W hookups, showers, laundry. Seasonal May 15-Oct 15. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: On the Saco Family Campground
note: 1,700 ft of Saco River frontage in Brownfield; RV sites to 45 ft with full hookups plus riverside tent sites; canoe/kayak/tube rentals + shuttle, sandy-bottom river swimming. Nightly/weekly/seasonal, open May-Oct. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Potter's Place Adult RV Park
note: Adults-only (21+) park, family-run since 1967 on a 160-acre tree farm in Lebanon; 100 full-hookup big-rig sites, overnight + extended stays; 7-acre swim pond, heated pool, trails. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Whispering Pines Campground
note: Family-run since 1977 on Toddy Pond (a 10-mi lake) in East Orland; 50 sites with W/E/S hookups, beach access. Open Memorial Day-Sept 30. No standalone official site (book by phone). RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Sleeping Bear Campground & Cabins
note: Small owner-run campground (8 sites, 1 pull-thru) in the Lincoln Lakes region, village of Lee; W/E, RV + tent + cabins; lake, beach and boat ramp nearby. Open mid-May-late Oct. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Arndt's Aroostook River Lodge & Campground
note: On the bank of the Aroostook River 4 mi from Presque Isle; 55 RV sites (full/partial hookup, big-rig, many pull-thru) + 20 tent sites, in-ground pool, canoe/kayak rentals. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Shin Pond Village Campground
note: Family resort 15 mi from Baxter SP's north gate (Mount Chase); 25 W/E RV sites + brookside tent sites and cottages on/near Lower Shin Pond; general store, restaurant. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Paul Bunyan Campground
note: 88 full/partial-hookup (30/50A) big-rig sites, many pull-thru, on Union St in Bangor; heated pool, paddleboat pond, playground. Open May 1-Oct 15. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Katahdin Shadows Campground
note: 105 sites (40+ full-hookup big-rig, 40+ tent, 11 cabins) in Medway at the gateway to the North Maine Woods; pool, store, restaurant, arcade, dump station. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: South Arm Campground
waterfront_evidence: RV rows on the open peninsula reaching the Lower Richardson Lake shore.
note: On a peninsula on Lower Richardson Lake (Andover); 65 wooded W/E sites (RVs to 60 ft), about half right on the lake, plus 35 tent sites; sandy beach, 40-slip marina, boat/canoe rentals, store. Open ~late May-late Sept; no cell service. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Keenes Lake Family Campground
waterfront_evidence: rows of RV sites along the Keenes Lake shore.
note: On the shore of Keenes Lake in Calais (10 mi from the border); 96 full/partial-hookup big-rig sites (to 50A), beach, paddle-craft rentals. Open May-Oct. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Desert of Maine Campground
note: Campground at the Desert of Maine attraction in Freeport, 8 min from L.L. Bean; 25 full/partial-hookup big-rig sites (also small RVs/tents), pool, store. Open early May-mid Oct. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Christies Campground & Cottages
waterfront_evidence: RV sites on a point into Sebasticook Lake, shore rows near the water, boat launch/docks.
note: On Lake Sebasticook in Newport; RV-focused (no tent sites), full-hookup sites, boat launch + docks, hot showers, laundry. Open ~May-late Nov. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Jonesport Campground
waterfront_evidence: RV sites on the open point at Jonesport harbor.
note: Small seasonal oceanfront campground (~20 sites, RVs to ~45 ft) on Jonesport harbor; no hookups, self-contained rigs only (portable toilet on site). Book by phone. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Moosehead Family Campground
note: 26 RV/camper sites (W/E 30/50A) + tent sites in Greenville Jct, ~1 mi from Moosehead Lake; big-rig pull-thrus, though a few sites fit only ~20 ft -- several take up to 45 ft. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Stony Brook Recreation and Camping
note: On the Androscoggin River in Hanover, nearest full-service campground to Grafton Notch SP; 20 full-hookup + 20 W/E + 10 no-hookup sites, premium pull-thrus fit any size rig; pool, mini-golf. Open year-round. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Birch Point Campground & Cottages
note: On Pleasant Lake just off I-95 in Island Falls; 41 full/partial-hookup big-rig sites + cottages, lodge with bowling/arcade; salmon & trout fishing. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Grand Isle Campground
note: 21 W/E sites in the St. John Valley (Grand Isle), a short walk from the town boat landing on the St. John River; RV/trailer/tent, showers, ATV-trail access. Seasonal mid-May-end Sept. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Apple Valley Campground
note: Family RV park (no tent sites) in the hills of Acton; full/partial-hookup big-rig sites beside an on-site fishing pond; pool, rec hall, store. Open ~May 15-Oct 12. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: River Run Canoe & Campground
note: Wooded campground on the Saco River in Brownfield; drive-in RV/tent sites (dump station, LP gas), 3 private swimming beaches, canoe rentals + shuttle. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Oceanfront Camping @ Reach Knolls
waterfront_evidence: camp loops with sites on open ground at the Eggemoggin Reach shore; operator confirms waterfront sites 8/10/12.
note: Oceanfront on Eggemoggin Reach at the mouth of the Benjamin River (Brooklin); 40 large sites, RVs to 40 ft, 20-30A electric, dump station, pit toilets, well-kept gravel roads. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Hanging Rock State Park
waterfront_evidence: loops in forest ~300m NW of the 12-ac park lake, continuous treeline; lake-through-trees at best
note: 73-site family campground in two loops near the 12-acre park lake. No hookups; water hydrants and bathhouses with hot showers. Reservable online, unreserved sites FCFS. --Claude

name: Lake Norman State Park
waterfront_evidence: loops in wooded center of peninsula, Lake Norman arms ~250m+ on multiple sides with continuous forest buffer
note: 44-site campground on a wooded peninsula. Sites 1-32 paved back-in (no hookups), sites 34-44 full hookups (30/50-amp electric, water, sewer); large rigs OK. Bathhouse, dump station. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Morrow Mountain State Park
waterfront_evidence: family loops in dense forest, Lake Tillery downhill east, no shoreline pads
note: 106-site family campground in three loops near Lake Tillery; Loop C has 22 electric sites, rest non-electric. Dump station, hot showers. Reservable online, open year-round. --Claude

name: Pilot Mountain State Park
waterfront_evidence: wooded loops on mountain slope, no lake/river present
note: ~42-site family campground in the Mountain Section; primitive gravel back-in sites, no hookups or dump station, community water and two bathhouses. Reservable online, unreserved FCFS. Season mid-March-November. --Claude

name: South Mountains State Park
waterfront_evidence: single loop set back in forest above the Jacob Fork River drainage, band of trees between
note: Drive-to family campground ~0.5 mi from the Jacob Fork parking area; 18 back-in sites (17 non-electric + 1 50-amp electric pull-through), driveways 15-50 ft. Bathhouse with hot showers. Reservable online. Separate equestrian campground excluded. --Claude

name: Cliffs of the Neuse State Park
waterfront_evidence: loop uphill/inland in woods, 11-ac park lake ~250m+ SE, separated by forest
note: Wooded family campground (~35 sites) near the park office; 12 full-hookup sites (30/50-amp electric, water, sewer), rest standard. Dump station, bathhouse with hot showers. Open year-round, reservable online. --Claude

name: Jones Lake State Park
waterfront_evidence: loop set back in pine woods, lake shore developed as day-use complex, loop not on shore
note: 20-site family loop accessed by gravel road; 6 full-hookup sites, most on raised gravel pads, rigs to ~45 ft. Sites set back ~0.5 mi from the lake. Reservable online, FCFS if unreserved. --Claude

name: Goose Creek State Park
waterfront_evidence: loops in cleared forest inland, creek/wetland channel ~150-250m W through continuous woods
note: 22 drive-in full-hookup RV sites (electric/water/sewer), RVs to ~40 ft, no pull-throughs. Bathhouse near loop entrance. Separate walk-in tent sites and cabins excluded. Reservable online. --Claude

name: New River State Park
waterfront_evidence: drive-to loop in forest by visitor center, New River South Fork ~250m+ S through dense woods
note: US-221 Access: 20-site drive-in loop for tents/trailers/RVs (sites to 33+ ft) with 50-amp electric, some full-hookup. Dump station (fee), water, flush toilets, showers. Other accesses are walk-in/paddle-in only. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Raven Rock State Park
waterfront_evidence: loop fully ringed by forest, well inland from the Cape Fear River
note: Moccasin Branch Campground (opened ~2023): 9 drive-in full-hookup RV sites (50/30/20-amp, water, sewer, long driveways) plus 15 drive-to tent sites, 6 cabins, bathhouse with hot showers. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Standing Indian Campground
waterfront_evidence: canopy-blind, rec.gov all Standard Nonelectric no shoreline flag, headwaters streams
note: USFS Nantahala NF (Nantahala RD). 85+ wooded sites in five loops at the Nantahala River headwaters (~3,400 ft). No hookups, max RV 50 ft. Mix of FCFS and reservable-online sites. Open ~Apr 1-Oct 30; flush toilets, showers, water. --Claude

name: Cheoah Point Campground
waterfront_evidence: loop set back ~50m+ behind continuous forest buffer from Lake Santeetlah; rec.gov "campsites overlook the lake", no shoreline flag; peninsula = view not access
note: USFS Nantahala NF (Cheoah RD) on a peninsula of Lake Santeetlah. 23 sites: 17 nonelectric + 6 with 15/30/50-amp electric (sites 18-23; no water/sewer). Electric sites take larger rigs; nonelectric ~22 ft. RV/cabin sites reservable online, tent sites FCFS. Open ~Apr 15-Oct 31. Flush toilets, hot showers, boat ramp. --Claude

name: Jackrabbit Mountain Campground
waterfront_evidence: wooded peninsula loops behind treeline, beach via trail; FS "most provide views of Lake Chatuge"
note: USFS Nantahala NF (Tusquitee RD) on a peninsula of Lake Chatuge near Hayesville. ~100 wooded sites in three loops, no hookups, RV dump station. Reservable online (Loop A May-Sep, B/C May-Oct). Swimming, boat ramp, bike/hike trails. --Claude

name: Tsali Campground
waterfront_evidence: clearings inland, Fontana arm ~250m NW
note: USFS Nantahala NF (Cheoah RD) near a Fontana Lake arm. 42 sites, FCFS (pay at on-site fee station, no online reservations). No hookups; flush toilets, hot showers, water. Many sites fit small/mid RVs; a sharp interior curve hampers big rigs. Open ~Apr 15-Oct 31. Popular mountain-bike/horse TRAIL hub. --Claude

name: Van Hook Glade Campground
waterfront_evidence: ridge above small Cliffside Lake day-use, trail across US-64
note: USFS Nantahala NF (Nantahala RD) on US-64 in Cullasaja Gorge near Highlands. 18 sites, reservable online, no hookups. Larger sites take ~24-26 ft (a 23-ft rig fits those); a sharp loop curve hampers big rigs. Flush toilets, hot showers. Open ~Apr 1-Oct 31. Trail down to Cliffside Lake. --Claude

name: North Mills River Campground
waterfront_evidence: leaf-off satellite shows loop hugging the North Mills River bend, sites along the open bank within a pad-depth; FS "river runs through the campground"
note: USFS Pisgah NF (Pisgah RD) on the North Mills River. 26 sites; 1-13 open year-round, 14-26 seasonal. One electric RV site (014), rest nonelectric. Reservable online Mar 20-Nov 15; winter walk-up FCFS with limited services (pit toilets, no water). Operating in 2026. --Claude

name: Sunburst Campground
waterfront_evidence: small loop on a narrow bench, West Fork Pigeon River ~20-40m off the east edge across open ground but loop road + riparian strip between pads and channel; no riverfront site flag
note: USFS Pisgah NF (Pisgah RD), former logging camp on NC-215 by the West Fork Pigeon River. 9-10 nonelectric sites, reservable online. No potable water or showers; vault/flush toilets. Small RVs only (short undeveloped pull-ins; a 23-ft rig fits, big rigs do not). Open ~Mar 23-Nov 15. --Claude

name: Cedar Point Campground
waterfront_evidence: loop set back in woods, continuous salt marsh + treed strip to the White Oak River, pads 100m+ from open water
note: USFS Croatan NF at the junction of the White Oak River and Bogue Sound. 40 sites all with 20/30/50-amp electric; bathhouse with hot showers, dump station, shallow-draft boat launch. 32 reservable online, 4 FCFS. Big-rig accessible (sites to ~39 ft). Year-round. --Claude

name: Flanners Beach / Neuse River Campground
waterfront_evidence: loops on a wooded bluff/bench ~50-80m back from the wide Neuse estuary, treed strip + day-use beach complex between camp and shore; rec.gov "on a bluff overlooking the river"
note: USFS Croatan NF, Neuse River Recreation Area, on a bluff above the Neuse River estuary. 40 sites; 24 with 20/30/50-amp electric (incl. 2 doubles), showerhouse, dump station. Mix of reservable-online and FCFS sites. Large RVs OK. Open seasonally (spring-fall). --Claude

name: West Morris Mountain Campground
waterfront_evidence: inland Uwharrie ridge clearing
note: USFS Uwharrie NF (Uwharrie RD). 14 individual + 2 group sites for tents or RVs (1 pull-through; sites to ~35 ft), each with table, grill, lantern post and pad. No hookups; two vault toilets. FCFS (pay at on-site fee tube). Year-round. --Claude

name: Yates Place Camp
waterfront_evidence: inland field/forest camp
note: USFS Uwharrie NF (Uwharrie RD). Free primitive FCFS camping at an old homesite/open field by the Uwharrie National Recreation Trail; undesignated drive-up sites used by self-contained RVs and trailers (not equestrian-only). Vault toilet, tables, grills, fire rings; no potable water. Year-round. --Claude

name: Deep Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: canopy-blind, creek-adjacent sites are walk-to tent-only, no flag confirming a drive-in RV pad on the creek
note: Great Smoky Mountains NP (NPS) near Bryson City. 92 sites: ~50 take tents or RVs, 42 are walk-in tent-only. RVs/trailers to 26 ft; no hookups (flush toilets, water, no showers). Reservable online, not FCFS. Open ~early May-late Oct. --Claude

name: Byrd's Branch Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.2890,-80.8769: RV loop ~80m from Byrd's Branch behind continuous treeline; pond ~250m N - no site on water
note: Small family-owned campground in the Elkin foothills; 14 RV sites (back-in along a creek that runs through the property, plus wide pull-throughs that take big rigs) + 4 tent sites. Full hookups, 30/50 amp, store, bathhouse, laundry. Open year-round. On the Mountains-to-Sea Trail; near area wineries. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Turkey Quarter Campground & RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.2333,-77.2139: two farm ponds but pads set back across open grass/lot from central pond; no RV pad at shoreline
note: Small rural RV/tent campground ~20 min from New Bern; 19 RV sites, 10 full hookups, 30/50 amp, dump station, showers, laundry. Two small ponds with a boat ramp. Open year-round. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Hominy Valley RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.5000,-82.7326: RV loops surrounded by fields/woods, no water adjacent
note: Small inexpensive full-hookup RV park in Candler ~8 mi from Asheville; ~9 self-service sites (one water/electric only), 50 amp, full hookups. No restrooms/pool/laundry on site. Open year-round. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: River Country Campground & RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.5258,-81.3349: south arc of RV oval loop within ~40-50m of the New River across open unused grass apron, thin riparian fringe only
note: Riverside campground on the New River surrounded by New River State Park; 61 sites (38 full hookup, 13 water/electric), 20/30/50 amp, takes big rigs with pull-throughs, bathhouse, store, canoe/tube/fishing. Seasonal, roughly Apr 15-Nov 1. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Simple Times RV Park & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.9311,-79.4851: linear RV/MH park on hillside, no water adjacent
note: Family-owned campground on 30+ acres in Snow Camp; spacious gravel-pad sites with full hookups, 30/50 amp, WiFi/cable. ~12 mi from Burlington. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Yates RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.2421,-81.8470: RV loop with pool in residential/wooded area, no water adjacent
note: Small 22-acre RV resort in Forest City; 10 sites (9 back-in 35x50 ft, 1 pull-through), cement pads, full hookups, 30/50 amp, fenced dog park, nature trails. Open year-round. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: The Little Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.1668,-81.9408: development along a road/hollow, no significant water adjacent, creek unconfirmed
note: Tiny Blue Ridge campground near Banner Elk at ~3,500 ft; 4 full-hookup RV sites, 30/50 amp, water, sewer, WiFi, laundry. Near Grandfather Mountain and Elk River Falls. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Highlands Riverbend RV Resort and Cabins
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.1610,-83.3128: RV pad rows directly along east bank of the Cullasaja River, open apron within ~20-40m
note: Riverfront/creekfront RV resort on the Cullasaja River and Ellijay Creek near Franklin; 18 sites, full hookups, 30/50 amp, gravel pads, kayak/tube rentals, clubhouse. Seasonal, ~April-November. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Hillbilly Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.5178,-83.0973: RV pads in wooded lot; Campbell Creek a thin tree-screened drainage, no pad on open bank; creekside/view is name/marketing only
note: 30 full-hookup RV/pop-up sites (no tents) in central Maggie Valley, walkable to restaurants; Campbell Creek runs through the property with some creek-view sites. Laundry, showers, store. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Jordan Dam RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.6263,-79.0774: on-site fishing pond with RV pads along E/S shore on open grass apron to the waterline
note: Family-owned full-hookup RV park just off US-1, ~0.5 mi from Jordan Lake; 45 gravel sites 30/50-amp. Small on-site fishing pond, year-round, big-rig friendly. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Country Woods RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.1352,-83.3983: hillside wooded RV/MH rows in US-441 valley, no water near sites
note: 72 full-hookup shaded hillside RV sites (RVs only) near Franklin. Seasonal: Apr 1-Oct 31. Free WiFi, cable, laundry, showers. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Tumbling Waters Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.3767,-83.6295 canopy-heavy: RV pads in wooded clearings; creek tree-screened, no pad on open bank resolvable, no per-site map -> default down
note: Quiet creek-side campground near Bryson City/Deep Creek; 32 gravel sites, 5 pull-thrus, full hookups, 30-amp, fits big rigs. On-site trout pond. Seasonal: Mar 1-Dec 1. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Cotton Patch Goldmine & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.4349,-80.1876: RVs in open gravel/grass lot; panning creek to SW, no pad fronting open water
note: Gem/gold-mining-themed campground; 26 full-hookup sites (30/50-amp), RVs up to 50 ft, year-round, on-site store. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Goodwin's RV Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.6374,-79.0805: small wooded RV clearing; Jordan Lake ~0.5 mi away, no water at sites
note: Full-hookup RV park near Jordan Lake, ~30 min from Durham; 20 sites 50-amp, water, sewer, cable, rigs up to 50 ft. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: JCO Campground at Lee Baysden Pond
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.8296,-77.6695: open grassy camping field/peninsula runs unobstructed to the 22-acre Lee Baysden Pond edge; operator confirms RV sites on the water
note: Juniper Creek Outfitters campground on a 22-acre pond; RV sites 30/50-amp, pull-thrus, big-rig, plus tent sites and cabins. Boat ramps, fishing, kayak/canoe rentals, trails. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Andy's Trout Farm
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.9978,-83.4541: grid of RV pads on peninsula at main pond NE shore, pads at the waterline on open ground
note: Historic trout farm (est. 1965) on 325 acres; ~10 full-hookup RV spots (30/50-amp) plus cabins. Trout fishing, gem mining, bath house. RV Life: 5* / $. --Claude

name: Double L Farms RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.6635,-79.0514: RV rows in farm field, no water near
note: Working 46-acre farm 2 min off I-95 near Lumberton; 26 full-hookup transient RV sites among pastures (general RV, not equestrian). Free WiFi, bathhouse. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: The Dotmond RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.5148,-79.1757: wooded RV loops along highway, no water adjacent (Hyco Lake well away)
note: Renovated historic schoolhouse property off NC-57; 12 spacious 60-ft gravel sites with concrete patios, 30/50-amp, sewer, filtered water. Year-round; near VIR and Hyco Lake. WiFi, laundry, bathhouse, dog park. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Peek of the Mountains Campground & RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.5068,-81.9842: RV loop in hillside clearing, no water present
note: Small new mountain park (~12-13 gravel sites) with water/power/septic hookups and a free dump station; pull-through sites. Bookable nightly via Campspot but carries a sizable seasonal/full-timer base. RV Life: 5* / $. --Claude

name: Nantahala Ridge Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.3364,-83.5649: forested ridge sites, no water adjacent
note: Small ridge campground (~5 sites) near Bryson City/Nantahala; full hookups 50/30/20-amp, water, septic, WiFi; sites for vans up to big rigs. Reservable online. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Herds Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.3583,-79.2832: RV pads in clearing; farm ponds 150m+ away behind treelines
note: New RV park on a retired farm near Southern Pines/Pinehurst; full hookups 30/50-amp, large 35x55 ft gravel pads, gated, dog park, laundry, WiFi. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Camp Springs Bluegrass Park & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.2891,-79.4799: open-field sites; pond at far SW corner screened by treeline, not pad-adjacent
note: Bluegrass festival venue that also takes general RV camping; ~57 power+water sites (30/50-amp) plus dry-camping fields, bathhouse, dump station, fishing pond; rigs under 45 ft. Bookable via Hipcamp. RV Life: 5* / $. --Claude

name: Ho-Hum Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.9962,-83.7563: RV sites east of road uphill/inland; Lake Chatuge fingers west of road, separated
note: RV-only park near Hayesville/Lake Chatuge; 87 full-hookup sites 30/50-amp, max RV ~38 ft, water/showers/dump. Seasonal Apr 1-Oct 31; takes short-term stays. RV Life: 5* / $. --Claude

name: Mayberry Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.4535,-80.6365: RV pad rows on open grass apron at the shoreline of both on-site ponds, no buffer
note: Large year-round park minutes from downtown Mount Airy; 106 full-hookup sites 50/30-amp with cable/internet, level gravel pads, larger rigs OK, laundry, playground, two on-site ponds. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Camping World Racing Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.3591,-80.6900: speedway camping lots; small decorative ponds but no RV pads on water
note: Large RV resort at Charlotte Motor Speedway; ~380 gravel full-hookup sites 30/50-amp, pull-throughs for big rigs, bathhouse, laundry, WiFi. Open year-round for daily transient camping on non-event days. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Pride RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite: RV rows along US-276; Jonathan Creek not adjacent to RV pads
note: Full-service mountain resort on Jonathan Creek near Maggie Valley; ~140-150 full-hookup sites 30/50-amp, pull-throughs, rigs to ~50 ft; pool, hot tub, mini golf, fishing ponds. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Spring Hill RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.90,-79.17: RV cluster in woods/fields, no water near
note: Wooded full-hookup RV park near Chapel Hill/Durham; pull-throughs, big rigs OK, year-round, no age restriction. Quiet shaded sites. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Murphy / Peace Valley KOA Holiday
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.123,-83.989: Valley River wraps S/E of loops but RV pads interior, screened by continuous riverbank treeline
note: KOA Holiday in SW NC mountains, ~1 mi from Harrah's Cherokee Valley River casino. Full-hookup RV sites plus patio sites and cabins; pool, bathhouse. Valley River runs through the campground. Big-rig friendly. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Red Gates RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.341,-82.401: 2-acre lake with sandy swim beach on NW shore; RV pads set back S behind treed strip, no pad-front apron
note: Small 20-site full-hookup RV park (30/50A) on rolling meadowland 5 min from Hendersonville; 2-acre stocked lake on the property; fire pits, shuffleboard. Max RV ~45 ft. Seasonal: Apr 1-Nov 1. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Farm Country Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.779,-77.140: RV rows in open field; small pond by buildings not adjacent to RV pads
note: Family-owned rural campground (est. 2012) near Williamston; ~35-40 large sites, many pull-throughs with full hookups, big rigs OK. Walking/horse trails, store, propane, year-round. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Holly Ridge Family Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.260,-80.714: RV sites in wooded ravine, Yadkin River off-site downhill, not site-adjacent
note: Yadkin Valley campground near Boonville; ~62-80 sites full hookups (30/50A), pull-throughs, big-rig friendly. Pool, store, mini-golf; canoe/kayak rental toward the Yadkin River. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: River Valley Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.5197,-83.2818: RV pad rows along Raven Fork river edge, westernmost pads ~1 pad-depth back behind thin riverbank fringe
note: RV campground on Big Cove Rd outside Cherokee, S of the Smokies' southern entrance; concrete full-hookup and E/W sites along the Raven Fork River, pull-throughs, max RV ~45 ft, big rigs OK. Pool. Seasonal ~Mar-Oct. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Tranter's Creek Resort & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.5660,-77.1005: easternmost RV rows ~20-30m from Tranter's Creek behind continuous wooded bank strip; visible through bare trees but real treeline buffer -> view
note: Coastal-plain resort on Tranter's Creek W of Washington; full-hookup sites (20/30/50A), pull-throughs, big-rig friendly, year-round. ~3000 ft of creek frontage with boat launch and fishing; pool, store. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Persimmon Creek RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.0327,-84.2073: RV loop rings a central farm pond with open grass apron, closest pads ~20-30m on open ground
note: Small creekside RV park (~19-25 sites) on Persimmon Creek 12 mi west of Murphy. 30/50-amp full hookups, pull-through and back-in plus creekside RV/tent sites; bass and trout ponds. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Cullasaja River RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.1407,-83.2983: long rows of RV pads line both banks of the Cullasaja River at the water's edge, narrow grass apron

name: Tanglewood Park RV Campground
