note: Forsyth County-operated RV campground in 1,100-acre Tanglewood Park along the Yadkin River near Clemmons. 44 full-hookup sites (W/S/30-50A, cable, WiFi), RVs to 55 ft. Reservable online or by phone; season Mar 1-Dec 5. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Oak Hollow Family Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.0110,-80.0080: RV row at the head of an Oak Hollow Lake cove with an open grass/beach apron (~40m, no screen) to the waterline
note: City of High Point campground on Oak Hollow Lake in 1,550-acre Oak Hollow Park. 103 full-hookup RV sites (30/50A, W/S, most cable/WiFi) plus 13 tent platforms. Reservable via Campspot or phone; some FCFS. Year-round, gated. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Lake Reidsville Recreation Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 36.2918,-79.6835: camper sites on an open grassy bank above Lake Reidsville on the SW point, ~30-40m open apron with only a thin tree fringe
note: City of Reidsville campground on Lake Reidsville. 46 wooded sites; ~28 full-hookup, rest water/electric. Reservations by phone; ~8 lakefront sites fill fast. Hot showers, fishing piers, no swimming. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Contentnea Creekside RV & Trail Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.3678,-77.4368: RV loop on the south bank of Contentnea Creek ~30-50m back behind a continuous wooded riparian buffer; no open apron
note: Town of Grifton-operated RV park along Contentnea Creek. ~10 full-hookup sites (E/W/S), RVs to ~70 ft; restrooms with showers, dump station, walking trails. Year-round. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Aiken State Park
waterfront_evidence: SC per-site map (aiken_state_park_53): RV loop 001-025 in forest; Children's/Main Pond small CCC ponds, no pads fronting water; canopy-blind satellite, no shoreline pads
note: 25-site campground: 19 sites with water + 30/50-amp electric, 6 full-hookup (sewer, #20-25); packed-sand pads, some fit RVs to 35 ft. Flush toilets, hot showers, dump station. Reservable online (2-night min). Pine/hardwood forest near the South Fork Edisto River and small park lakes. --Claude

name: Andrew Jackson State Park
waterfront_evidence: SC per-site map: sites 16-19 nearest park lake but separated by forest strip + trail; satellite shows loop set back in canopy SE of lake, no pads at waterline; pin moved off field onto loop
note: 25-site campground on a single loop beside the park's 18-acre lake. 22 sites water + 30-amp, 3 full hookups; 5 paved sites to 36 ft, 20 gravel to 30 ft. Hot showers, dump station. Reservable online (2-night min), year-round. --Claude

name: Barnwell State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.3303,-81.3017: loop set back ~150-200m east of CCC fishing lake behind continuous treeline; only day-use clearing/dock at shore
note: 25 drive-in sites in a single wooded loop; water + electric, sites 18-25 add 50-amp full hookups (sewer). 4 sites to 36 ft, rest to 28 ft. Dump station, hot showers, playground. Reservable online (2-night min) + walk-ins. CCC-built park with three small fishing lakes. --Claude

name: Chester State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.6806,-81.2525: single oval loop ~700-900m inland NW of the 160-acre lake through forest; no water adjacency
note: 25 packed-gravel sites with water + 50-amp electric (no sewer); RVs to ~33 ft, 3 pull-throughs; hot showers, dump station. Reservable online/phone (2-night min), year-round. 160-acre park lake with piers and jon-boat rentals; loop sits back from the lake in pine forest. --Claude

name: Croft State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.8645,-81.8385: loops on wooded hillside set back/above Lake Craig behind continuous treeline; lakeside structures are day-use dock/ramp; reviews 'views through trees' (screened)
note: Standard developed campground (separate from the 48-stall equestrian camp). 50 packed-gravel sites with water + electric in two loops above Lake Craig: inland 1-25 (30-amp), lakeside 26-50 (20/30/50-amp). No sewer; dump station. Some sites to 40 ft. Reservable online (2-night min); showers, playground. --Claude

name: Givhans Ferry State Park
waterfront_evidence: SC per-site map (Givhans-Ferry): RV sites 1-25 loop set back S of the Edisto behind the bluff-edge River House/picnic area; river fronted only by day-use/bluff-trail strip
note: 25 drive-in sites (water & 20/30/50-amp electric, 6 with sewer), RVs to ~40 ft; sites 26-35 are walk-in tent/hammock only. Campground on a bluff above the Edisto River. Reservable online (2-night min), year-round. --Claude

name: Hickory Knob State Resort Park
waterfront_evidence: Hickory Knob per-site map (24-SCP-4990): campground on a Strom Thurmond Lake peninsula, sites 1-44 ringing the shoreline with driveways fronting water; reviews cite sites 6-26 waterfront elevated ~10ft (apron); satellite confirms peninsula loop surrounded by lake
note: 43 packed-gravel sites with water + 30-amp electric (no sewer); dump station, hot-shower bathhouse. Big-rig friendly (directories list to 45 ft, some pull-throughs; internal roads rough). Reservable online (2-night min), year-round. On Lake Thurmond (Strom Thurmond Reservoir). --Claude

name: Keowee-Toxaway State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.9326,-82.8857: developed RV/tent loop in forest NW of Hwy 11, no water adjacent; official map: only primitive sites are shoreline (hike/paddle-in)
note: 10 paved RV sites with water + 50-amp electric (RVs to 40 ft; winding park roads harder for larger rigs), plus a tent area (~10-14 water/20-amp sites). 3 backcountry sites are hike/paddle-in (excluded). Reservable online (2-night min). --Claude

name: Kings Mountain State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.1500,-81.3465: camping loop set back ~150m+ from Lake Crawford behind continuous treeline; lake-edge is separate parking/dock area
note: Family campground: 115 standard sites with water + electric (30/50 amp); gravel pads, several to 40 ft. Dump station, hot showers. Reservable online (2-night min). Plus 6 hammock/tent sites. Separate equestrian camp not included. --Claude

name: Lee State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.2114,-80.2027: canopy sandy loop, no water adjacent; Lynches River ~0.5km west, not adjacent
note: Family campground: 25 standard drive-in sites with water/electric; sites 15,17,19,21,23,25 add sewer. RVs to 30-36 ft. Hot showers, dump station. Reservable online (2-night min), year-round. Separate 23-site equestrian camp is a distinct loop. --Claude

name: Oconee State Park
waterfront_evidence: SC per-site map: Lake Loop standard RV sites (16-18, 24-34) run along Campground Lake's west shore with a camp pier; canopy-blind satellite defers to per-site map authority
note: 149 sites: ~110 standard water/electric (20/30 amp), 24 full-hookup, 15 walk-in tent; 46 pull-thru. Max RV 50 ft. Reservable online (2-night min); dump station, bathhouses, laundry. Year-round (some closures Dec-Mar). 20-acre Campground Lake (pier, swim area, boat rentals) + 12-acre lake. --Claude

name: Poinsett State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.8053,-80.5476: cleared loop ~80-120m N of Levi Mill Lake across open ground with bathhouse/beach day-use strip at shore; water visible/unscreened across clearing - view not access
note: 50-site campground: 24 back-in RV sites with water + 20/30-amp electric (some to 40 ft), plus 26 water-only tent sites. Hot showers, dump station. Reservable online (2-night min), year-round. High Hills of Santee near 10-acre Levi Mill Lake (fishing, johnboat rentals). --Claude

name: Sesquicentennial State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.0894,-80.9078: camping loops set well back ~200m+ from park lake; lake frontage is day-use complex (parking, splash pad, beach)
note: State park near Columbia. ~84 RV/tent sites in two loops, all with water + electric (sites 1-9 full hookup). Max RV 50 ft. Dump station, hot showers. Reservable online (2-night min); Loop 1 seasonal. 30-acre park lake for paddling/fishing. --Claude

name: Table Rock State Park
waterfront_evidence: SC state-park map: camping loops set back from Pinnacle Lake & Lake Oolenoy; lakeshore fronted by cabins/lodge/day-use; canopy satellite defers to map
note: 94 standard sites in two areas: 69-site Mountain Laurel campground near the West Gate/Pinnacle Lake (main RV loop, rigs to 40 ft) and a 25-site area near White Oaks. All sites water + electric, no full-hookup; dump station, hot showers, laundry, store. Reservable online (2-night min). Roads narrow/steep. --Claude

name: Twin Lakes Campground, Hartwell Lake
waterfront_evidence: campground occupies a Hartwell peninsula, south/SE RV loops run to the shoreline with an open tan-clay drawdown apron + private docks between pads and the waterline; rec.gov photos confirm lakefront sites; pin moved ~400m N onto the campground
note: USACE (Savannah District) campground on the SC side of Hartwell Lake near Pendleton; 102 sites, all with electric and water (sites 81 & 101 are RV Electric), max RV to ~62 ft. Reservable online. 5 comfort stations w/ showers, 2 dump stations, swim beach. Many sites reported lakefront. --Claude

name: Woods Ferry Campground
waterfront_evidence: loop set back in trees east of the Broad River, only a boat-ramp clearing at the water (5 standard + 12 equestrian sites; the standard sites qualify)
note: USFS Sumter NF (Enoree RD) recreation area on the east bank of the Broad River. 17 sites total - 5 standard non-equestrian (11,13,14,15 + double 09) plus 12 equestrian; reservable online, ~$7/night. No hookups or dump station; water spigots, vault toilets. Gravel spurs vary; some fit a standard RV. Drive-in via gravel forest road. --Claude

name: Elmwood Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: free FCFS open-field primitive campground, no water adjacent
note: USFS Francis Marion NF open-field primitive campground off Rutledge Rd (SC-857) north of McClellanville. ~30 FCFS sites in an open field (fire pits, tables, grills), potable water, no hookups, vault facilities; free, 14-day limit, year-round. Accommodates large RVs (a 40-ft Class A reported); dirt-road drive-in passable by normal vehicle. FCFS - not on recreation.gov. --Claude

name: Betty's RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.4012,-81.8523: RV loops along US-1, no water in frame
note: Small family-owned park (~15 sites) on Hwy 125 (Atomic Rd) east of Beech Island, near Augusta GA. Full hookups (30/50 amp, water, sewer), dump station; RVs to 40 ft. Owners on-site; call for availability. RV Life: 5* / $. --Claude

name: Turtle Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.0754,-81.7335: RV sites along I-85, no creek/shoreline at sites
note: Small family-owned year-round park (~15 sites) off I-85 near Gaffney/Cowpens. Full hookups (30/50 amp, water, septic), free internet; RVs to 45 ft; no tent sites. Passport America. RV Life: 5* / $$. --Claude

name: Briarcliffe RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.7955,-78.7507: RV rows inland of a marina parking/building strip; ICW ~150m+ away behind developed marina, no shoreline pads
note: Large 289-site RV resort on the Intracoastal Waterway in Myrtle Beach; full hookups, big rigs OK, self-contained RVs only (no tents). Year-round; nightly transient rates. Olympic-size pool, walking distance to Barefoot Landing. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Cherokee RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.1214,-81.5004: RV sites by field; small pond ~200m east across field+treeline, no waterfront pads
note: 37 full-hookup sites (30/50 amp), RV-only, 45 ft max, year-round. Off I-85 Exit 102. Laundry, propane, pet-friendly. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: River Bottom Farms Family Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.6691,-81.1986: RV pads in open fields; small ponds nearby but no RV sites on water, no adjacent river
note: 70 RV/camping sites on 43 acres of farmland ~28 mi SW of Columbia. Full hookups 30/50 amp, pull-throughs, big-rig accessible. Pool, playground, laundry. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Palmetto Cove RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.0641,-82.6441: RV oval loop in fields; wooded ravine/treeline between loop and any river, no riverfront pads
note: 146 full-hookup sites (30/50 amp), RV-only (no tents, no bathhouses), mostly level grass. Volunteer-run but open to public with online reservations; season ~Apr-mid-Nov. Mountain views between Caesars Head and Table Rock; private river frontage and trails. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Edmund RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.8585,-81.2057: RV sites among trees by highway/industrial; clay-pit pond not adjacent
note: 125 sites (100 pull-throughs); 50 full hookups + 75 E/W, 50 amp, RVs to 60 ft. Year-round. Laundry, dump station, WiFi. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Moon Landing RV Park & Marina
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.2461,-81.9894: RV units on pads at Lake Greenwood waterline along peninsula shore with private docks; RV-only big-rig park, operator markets nightly waterfront sites
note: Family-owned RV park and marina on ~1/4 mi of Lake Greenwood shoreline (30 acres), Cross Hill. Year-round. Two boat ramps, 50 boat slips, dry storage, store, gas dock. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Tanager Woods RV Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.1812,-80.3274: RV sites in a forest clearing, no water near
note: Small 12-site full-hookup park (30/50 amp), 10 pull-throughs, RVs to 40 ft, year-round, pet-friendly. Dorchester/Ridgeville area. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: New Green Acres RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.8837,-80.7160: RV/MH park W of I-95, only a pool; no natural water
note: 106 sites (101 pull-throughs, 70 full hookups), 20/30/50 amp, extra-long sites for big rigs. Off I-95 Exit 53, Walterboro. Pool, playground, laundry, dump station. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: The Barnyard RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.9766,-81.1554: RV rows amid residential/commercial; no natural water near
note: 129 sites (82 pull-through), paved concrete pads/roads, full hookups 30/50-amp, cable, WiFi. Big-rig friendly (to 85 ft). Year-round; laundry, showers, dump, RV storage. By the Barnyard Flea Market off I-20/I-26. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Bass Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.4467,-79.3663: 7-acre pond NE separated from RV loop by wide continuous treeline; no pad fronts water
note: 60 pull-through sites, 43 full hookups, 20/30/50-amp; big rigs (to ~70 ft). 7-acre stocked fishing lake, bathhouse, laundry, dog park. At I-95 Exit 193. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Jolly Acres RV Park and Storage
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.1795,-80.5136: small fishing pond SW set back behind buffer; no shoreline frontage
note: 31 sites (12 pull-through), 19 full hookups + 12 E/W, 20/30/50-amp. Country setting off I-95, ~45 min from Charleston; laundry, playground, dump, on-site fishing. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Swamp Fox Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.1585,-79.8736: RV park at I-95 interchange amid fields/residential; no natural water
note: ~60-70 sites, large full-hookup back-in/pull-through pads, 30/50-amp, RVs to ~60 ft; pool, rec hall, playground, laundry, RV wash/storage. Off I-95 in Florence. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Sweetwater Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.6255,-80.9005: RV rows on peninsula with open grassy apron (~20-40m, no treeline screen) running to lake arm on E within distance bound
note: ~25-50 RV sites with W/E/S overlooking a 40-acre spring-fed lake; FCFS, no reservations (self-pay), long- and short-term. At I-26 Exit 139. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Aiken RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.6476,-81.6753: RV park E of airport; no natural water near
note: ~50 sites (9 pull-through), 47 full hookups, 20/30/50-amp; bookable nightly via Campspot (also monthly). 1 mi off I-20 Exit 22, 10 min from downtown Aiken; showers, laundry, WiFi, playground. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Tuck in the Wood Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.3651,-80.5848: sites in dense woods; ponds E separated by wide wooded belt; no shoreline frontage
note: 80 RV/tent sites, 30/50-amp, some full hookups, WiFi; year-round, FCFS (no reservations), snowbird monthly rates. Wooded St. Helena Island setting near Beaufort and Hunting Island SP; bathhouse, laundry, rec hall, stocked fishing pond. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Sandy Oak RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.7326,-81.5648: RV sites on open sandy ground; no natural water (swim area constructed)
note: 30 sites (28 full hookup, 2 E/W), 50-amp, pull-through; RVs to ~55 ft. Family-owned, quiet, convenient to I-20; bathhouse, laundry, swim area, playground. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Broad River Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.2176,-81.2132: RV loops in wooded clearings along SC-215; Broad River not in frame, no water at pads
note: Full-hookup sites with metered 20/30/50-amp, very large pads fitting any size camper; WiFi, laundry, bathhouse, nature trails. RV loops on both sides of SC-215. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Mr. Z's RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.9766,-81.3581: in-town park off US-1 surrounded by lots/pine; no adjacent water
note: Small family-owned RV park ~6 mi from Lake Murray; 19 full-hookup sites (6 pull-through), 30/50-amp, pool, laundry, WiFi. Daily/weekly/monthly; year-round. RV Life: 4* / $. --Claude

name: Lake Marion Resort and Marina
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.4701,-80.4382: RV pads in open grassy field, Lake Marion cove visible NE/E but shoreline treeline screens it and pads set back; open-ground floor = lakeview
note: Lakefront RV resort/marina on Lake Marion; ~52 full-hookup sites (30/50-amp), nightly and monthly; sites for rigs over 38 ft. Pool, bathhouse, boat launch, fishing dock; also cabins. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Cedar Pond Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.8000,-81.4322: camp clearing N of pond with continuous treeline buffer along the pond's north shore; RV cluster set back behind trees, no pad fronting open water
note: 32 sites (6 pull-through, 26 full-hookup), 20/30/50-amp, 40-ft max; year-round. On a 79-acre pond with sandy swim beach plus a pool; kayak/canoe/pedal-boat rentals, fishing. Also cabins, tent sites. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Saluda River Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.1760,-81.6974: RV pads uphill screened by treeline from a narrow boat-slip channel; pool/clubhouse between; Saluda River well south, not at pads
note: Rustic riverside campground on the Saluda River ~9 mi from Newberry; full-hookup RV sites (20/30/50-amp) with 4 pull-throughs, 38-ft max, big rigs OK; plus primitive/tent sites. Dump, showers, pool ($5), river fishing/paddling. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: All Seasons Family Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.2680,-81.9812: RV loop in forest clearing, no water in frame; Lake Greenwood well away
note: Small quiet campground ~25 min from Greenwood near Lake Greenwood SP; full-hookup sites (water/50-amp/sewer), gravel pads, any size self-contained rig; year-round. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: King's RV Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.5850,-82.5449: RV/MH loop in forest, no adjacent water
note: Small full-hookup RV park near Belton; ~$35/night, online reservations; no WiFi/cable. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Creekside Campground at Table Rock
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.0238,-82.7123: RV pads front a grassy loop in a forest clearing; small creek in a wooded draw to the east, no open water apron at pads
note: Campground near Table Rock State Park in the Blue Ridge foothills; RV and tent sites, dump station, 24/7 laundry, bathhouse; bookable nightly via Campspot, year-round. A small creek runs through the campsite. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Clemson Hills RV Club
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.7196,-82.8851: RV loops on hillside; small on-site pond in a basin separated from the lower loop by the access road/slope, no pad on pond edge; Lake Hartwell not adjacent
note: RV park ~4 mi from Clemson/Seneca on 30 acres with concrete pads, full hookups, mountain views, clubhouse, trails; bookable nightly via Campspot, no membership required despite 'Club' name. ~0.5 mi to Lake Hartwell access. RV Life: 4* / $. --Claude

name: Allison Creek Park
waterfront_evidence: original pin on a subdivision construction site ~600m SW; real park at Campspot coord on Lake Wylie. Newly-established 33 full-hookup RV + 16 tent sites, no waterfront-site designation; base imagery predates the campground (interior woods W of the boat-ramp/day-use peninsula); no shoreline RV pads confirmable - default down
note: York County park on Lake Wylie. 33 paved full-hookup RV sites (water/sewer/electric 50/30/20 amp) plus 16 primitive tent sites. Year-round, 14-night max; reservable via Campspot. RV Life: 5* / $$$. --Claude

name: Chau Ram County Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.6813,-83.1459: campsite loop on a bench ~100-150m east of the Chauga River, separated by continuous forested slope/gorge; RA brochure designates no riverfront sites
note: Oconee County park at the confluence of Ramsey Creek and the Chauga River. 26 RV sites with water and electric plus 8 tent sites; bathhouses with hot showers. Campground roads narrow - 23-ft generally OK. Season Mar-mid-Nov; reservable via ReserveAmerica. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Ebenezer Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.0223,-81.0431: camp loops buffered from Lake Wylie by a large day-use parking lot + swim beach to the N and a treeline plus private lakefront homes to the E; no RV pad at the shore
note: York County park on Lake Wylie north of Rock Hill. 79 full-hookup RV sites (water/sewer/electric 50/30/20 amp) plus 5 tent sites. Year-round, 14-night max; reservable via Campspot. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: High Falls County Park
waterfront_evidence: ReserveAmerica/Oconee County brochure: 91 campsites, 10 waterfront, all electric + water; satellite at 34.7968,-82.9294 shows camp loops on a peninsula reaching the Lake Keowee cove with shoreline sites and docks
note: Oconee County park on Lake Keowee. 91 campsites (electric 20/30 amp + water), 10 of them waterfront (2-night min on waterfront sites); bathhouses, dump station. Camping closed mid-Nov-Feb. Reservable via ReserveAmerica. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Lynches River County Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.0344,-79.7886: developed core set back ~150m+ from the Lynches River behind a continuous wooded swamp floodplain; river screened, no riverfront RV sites
note: Florence County Parks & Rec park on the Lynches Scenic River between Florence and Lake City. 40 RV/tent sites with water, electric (30/50 amp) and sewer; hot showers, laundry; FCFS (cabins separately reservable). RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Mile Creek Park
waterfront_evidence: legible satellite at 34.8527,-82.8853: RV pad loops directly above Lake Keowee coves with open grass aprons to the waterline and swim docks; pin was at the entrance ~400m N. Multiple shoreline pad loops confirmed
note: Pickens County park on Lake Keowee near Six Mile. 69 campsites, all with water and electric; more than two-thirds have direct shoreline access. Three heated bathhouses, boat ramps, 150-ft dock, store. Year-round. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Pleasant Ridge County Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 35.0859,-82.4799: small interior 4-acre fishing lake; camp/retreat sites scattered in forest with no pads at the lakeshore
note: Greenville County Parks, Rec & Tourism park (former state park) on SC-11, 272 acres with a 4-acre fishing lake and trails. Campground has tent and RV sites with electric hookups and a dump station; reservable through Greenville County Rec. Unrated on RV Life. RV Life: unrated / $. --Claude

name: Mill Creek County Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.7328,-80.5240: rural park of open fields and woods; small ponds/creek not adjacent to the campsites; no lake or river fronts the camp area
note: Sumter County Recreation & Parks campground west of Pinewood, adjacent to Manchester State Forest and a Palmetto Trail access. 126 sites - 30-amp ($20) and 50-amp ($25) with water, renovated bathhouses, fishing pier. A general campground that also offers 20 horse stables. RV Life: unrated. --Claude

name: Bruton Branch Primitive Campground (Pickwick Landing State Park)
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. Satellite canopy-blind; multiple firsthand guides (campendium/outdoorsy/dyrt) confirm 14 of the RV/trailer sites sit on the bank of Pickwick Lake, RV-accessible up to 50ft
note: Primitive drive-in campground on the north side of Pickwick Lake near Savannah, operated by TN State Parks as a satellite of Pickwick Landing. 33 sites, 28 fit trailers/RVs up to 50 ft; sites 4-9 NOT suitable for RV/trailer (tent only). No site hookups; bathhouse w/ flush toilets & showers, untreated well water, playground, beach, boat launch. Reservable online; 14-night max stay; ~$16/night. Open year-round. RV Life: unrated (0*) / $ --Claude

name: T.O. Fuller State Park Campground
note: TN State Parks campground at 1500 W Mitchell Rd, SW Memphis (~10 mi S of downtown). 45-site single loop (~10 pull-through, rest back-in), some sites fit large RVs; 30/50-amp electric + water at each site, no sewer; two dump stations, bathhouse w/ showers, laundry, playground. Reservable online up to 1 year ahead; 14-night max stay. Year-round, currently operating. RV Life: 3.5* / $$ --Claude

name: South Cumberland State Park - Foster Falls Campground
note: 26 rustic drive-in campsites, the only vehicle-camping area in South Cumberland SP. Gravel parking pad, table, fire ring/grill per site; vault toilet, water, heated showers. No hookups; no gas generators. Vehicle limit 25 ft (sources note total tow combo should stay under ~30 ft, narrow turns/limited turnaround - tight for a 23-ft rig). Open year-round, reservation required (reserve.tnstateparks.com). Operator: TN State Parks. RV Life: 5.0* / $$ --Claude

name: Tims Ford State Park - Fairview Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. Satellite at 35.199,-86.159: peninsula loops with outer-ring pads on open sand/dirt apron to Tims Ford Lake at high-water edge; firsthand descriptions confirm lakeshore RV sites
note: 82 sites with 20/30/50-amp electric and water hookups; 31 sites add sewer. Big-rig friendly (RVs up to ~65 ft). Dump station at entrance, two heated bathhouses, picnic table + fire ring/grill per site. On Tims Ford Lake ~8 mi from main entrance. Reservable online (reserve.tnstateparks.com). Operator: TN State Parks. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Tims Ford State Park - Main Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakeview [coord_fix 35.2105,-86.2502]. Pin was at the visitor-center complex; real loops sit on a peninsula at 35.2105,-86.2502 where satellite shows pads behind a treed slope/screen down to the lake (water visible, no open walkable apron); descriptions call the main-cg waterfront 'limited'
note: 52 sites with water and 30-amp electric; 4 sites add sewer. RVs up to 40 ft (two ADA sites to 70 ft). Dump station near entrance, heated bathhouse, picnic table + fire ring/grill per site. Open year-round, reservation only (reserve.tnstateparks.com). Operator: TN State Parks. RV Life: 4.0* / $$ --Claude

name: Hiwassee/Ocoee Scenic River State Park - Gee Creek Campground
note: Gee Creek Campground: 47 drive-in sites on A and B loops, concrete pads 40-108 ft (some north-side Loop A sites need leveling). No hookups; potable water pumps, central bathhouse with hot showers, table/fire ring/grill per site. Seasonal (approx Apr 1-Oct 31), reservable online (reserve.tnstateparks.com). NOTE: park announced infrastructure renovation closing the campground fall 2026, reopening anticipated spring 2027. Operator: TN State Parks. RV Life: 4.0* / $ --Claude

name: David Crockett State Park
note: Tennessee State Parks campground in Lawrenceburg along Shoal Creek. Two campgrounds, 100+ sites: Campground 1 (45 W/E sites + 10 primitive, along Shoal Creek, seasonal Mar-Nov, max RV ~55 ft) and Campground 2 (paved/concrete pads, water+electric, year-round, max RV ~50 ft). No individual sewer; dump station. 30/50-amp electric. Reservable online up to 1 year ahead ($5 fee). State-operated. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Chickasaw State Park
note: Tennessee State Parks. RV campground has 53 paved sites with water + 20/30/50-amp electric; ~26 sites also have sewer (full hookups). Paved pull-throughs; max RV ~60 ft; dump station. Separate tent and wrangler (equestrian) campgrounds exist. Reservable online ($5 fee). RV Life: 4.0* / $$ --Claude

name: Old Stone Fort State Park
note: Tennessee State Parks (state archaeological park). ~50 heavily wooded campsites with water + 30/50-amp electric (no sewer); hard-surface pads. Max RV ~40-50 ft. Dump station year-round; bathhouse closed Dec-Feb. Two-week stay limit. Reservable online ($5 fee). Accessed via Country Club Drive off US-41. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Henry Horton State Park
note: Tennessee State Parks. Campground South: 56 RV sites with paved pads, water + 20/30/50-amp electric; plus 19 tent and 9 primitive sites. Bathhouses, WiFi, pool, camp store. Along the Duck River ~40 mi south of Nashville. Reservable online up to 1 yr out ($5 fee). RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Fort Pillow State Historic Park
note: Tennessee State Parks (state historic park). Family Campground: 15 RV-capable sites with water + 20/30/50-amp electric (renovated, ~11 concrete pads with 50-amp), some pads to 60 ft; plus 9 primitive tent sites. Wooded, heated bathhouses, dump station at entrance. Drive-in; reviews confirm 28-33 ft rigs fit. Reservable online up to 1 yr out ($5 fee). Separate 1-mile hike-in backcountry site is NOT this campground. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Fall Creek Falls State Park
note: Year-round state-park campground off Village Camp Rd; ~228 developed sites across loops A-E, all with water and 20/30/50-amp electric, 107 full sewer. Mix of RV and tent sites; pull-throughs, some sites to 65 ft. Reservable online up to 1 yr ahead. Separate backcountry/tent areas. Operated by Tennessee State Parks. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Mousetail Landing State Park
note: Tennessee State Parks main campground on the east bank of the Tennessee River (3 Campground Rd, Linden). 25 sites; 19 with water + 20/30/50-amp electric (4 primitive), modern bathhouse, laundromat, dump station, WiFi at sites 1-13. Mix of tent and RV sites; fits a 23-ft rig. Reservable online up to 1 yr ahead ($5 fee). Separate Spring Creek primitive campground (21 tent sites) on the river is tent-only. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Natchez Trace State Park, Pin Oak Lake Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. Satellite: RV pads on shoreline loops with open grass apron to Pin Oak Lake waterline (~20-30m)
note: Tennessee State Parks campground on Pin Oak Lake. 77 sites with 50/30/20-amp electric, water and sewer (full hookups); two bathhouses, dump station, swimming beach, boat dock. Accommodates large RVs (well over 23 ft). Open year-round; reservable online via reserve.tnstateparks.com. Currently undergoing site upgrades (separate Pin Oak Lodge/restaurant closed for rebuild). RV Life: 5.0* / $$ --Claude

name: Natchez Trace State Park, Cub Lake Campground
note: Tennessee State Parks campground on Cub Lake. Cub Lake Camp 1: 23 sites, all with water and 20/30-amp electric (sites 8, 9, 11, 12 are 50-amp), one bathhouse, dump station nearby (no sewer); max RV/trailer 25 ft (fits a 23-ft rig). Open seasonally Apr 1-Oct 31; reservable online via reserve.tnstateparks.com. (Adjacent primitive Cub Lake Camp 2, water-only, 20-ft max, is closed for renovations.) RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Rock Island State Park
note: TN State Park. Main campground: 50 drive-in RV/trailer sites (#1-50), all with 20/30/50-amp electric and water; 8 full-hookup sites with sewer (#1-4, #31-34). Max RV length ~45 ft. Year-round; dump station, 2 bathhouses, Wi-Fi. Reservable online up to 12 months out (max stay 14 nights). A separate 10-site Riverside area is rustic/tent-oriented and seasonal (closed Nov 2-Mar 15). State-operated. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Cumberland Mountain State Park
note: TN State Park around Byrd Lake. 145 tent+RV drive-in campsites across 5 camping areas; Loop/Area 1 has 16 full-hookup RV sites (electric/water/sewer) in a treed setting, ~123 sites with electric+water. 30/50-amp and 110V service. Reservable online via reserve.tnstateparks.com. Accessible sites/bathhouses in Areas 1, 2, 3, 5. State-operated. Fits a 23-ft RV. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Edgar Evins State Park
waterfront_evidence: -> lakeview. official/TN parks: wooden platform sites built over slopes/ravines with tree-top views overlooking Center Hill Lake; sites sit atop steep slope (climb-down, not walk-across) per canopy-blind satellite at 36.0814,-85.8324
note: 60 tent/trailer campsites on distinctive wooden platforms (reinforced w/ concrete & steel) built on the slopes above Center Hill Lake; each has water + 20/30/50-amp electric, picnic table, grill, fire ring. Rated for rigs up to 33 ft, a few select sites to 40 ft. 3 bathhouses, central dump station. ~20 sites + 1 bathhouse open year-round (rest seasonal). Reservable online; $5 non-refundable reservation fee. State-operated. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Cedars of Lebanon State Park
note: 117 drive-up campsites across 3 camping areas in a dense cedar forest; all sites have water + 20/30/50-amp electric, picnic table, grill. 3 bathhouses (one per loop), dump station. Max RV ~38 ft (Area 1 ~45 sites has tighter curves; Areas 2-3 more wooded/private). Camp store & laundromat open year-round. Reservable online; $5 non-refundable reservation fee. State-operated. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Roan Mountain State Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> riverfront. satellite at 36.16,-82.10 illegible (dim/low-res canopy); TN parks + multiple sources confirm lower-section RV sites are riverside 'along the Doe River' (named riverside RV sites) — defer to authority, cannot downgrade canopy
note: ~106 sites: 86 RV sites (water + 30/50-amp electric, no sewer) plus tent and group sites; big-rig friendly (50+ ft) so fits 23 ft easily; dump station near check-in; year-round (some bathhouses close ~Nov-mid-Apr), 14-day max stay; reservable online via TN State Parks. State park; high elevation (~2,950 ft). Lower riverside RV loop open and bookable; upper loops across the Doe River bridge closed since Hurricane Helene (2024), reopening years out. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Panther Creek State Park Campground
note: 50-site single paved loop. All sites water + 20/30/50-amp electric; 8 full-hookup (sewer) sites; max RV ~40 ft; 2 bathhouses w/ hot showers, dump station, laundry, camp store; year-round, 14-day max stay; reservable online via TN State Parks ($5 fee). State park on Cherokee Lake but campground set back inland - not within sight of the lake. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Big Ridge State Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakeview [coord_fix 36.2425,-83.9300]. satellite at 36.2425,-83.93: lake-side loop pads ~30-50m from Norris Lake arm across grass with a continuous shoreline treeline fringe; reviews say 'view of lake from campsite'/'short walk' — open view holds but treeline+setback prevents on-water
note: 50 combined RV/tent sites; water + 30/50-amp electric, no sewer, dump station on-site; most sites up to ~35 ft (mostly back-ins, ~1 pull-through), fits 23 ft easily; 3 bathhouses (2 close Nov-Mar) w/ hot showers; year-round (winter rates), 14-day max (28 days Dec-Feb); reservable online via TN State Parks. State park (TDEC) on Big Ridge Lake (dammed arm of Norris Lake). RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Bledsoe Creek State Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront [coord_fix 36.3782,-86.3573]. TN parks ranger names waterfront RV sites #55-57 that 'hug the lakeshore' with easy bank-fishing access on Bledsoe Creek embayment of Old Hickory Lake; canopy-screened satellite at 36.3782,-86.3573 confirms loops reach the embayment — defer to per-site authority
note: 68 standard campsites (58 paved, 10 gravel) all with water + electric (30/50-amp) hookups; fire rings, grills, picnic tables. Dump station, bathhouse w/ showers, laundry, playground, boat ramp. Open year-round (14-night max stay; 28 days Dec 1-Feb 28/29). Reservable online up to 1 year ahead; $5 non-refundable reservation fee. Operated by Tennessee State Parks (TDEC). Construction ongoing through Summer 2026 (weekday daytime noise). RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Paris Landing State Park Campground
note: 45 campsites with water + electric (30/50-amp, no sewer) plus 18 primitive sites (63 total); also has cabins. Max RV length ~38 ft. Dump station (year-round), two bathhouses, laundry, camp store. Hardwood forest setting. Open year-round (14-night max; 28 days Dec 1-Feb 28/29). Reservable online up to 1 year ahead; $5 non-refundable reservation fee. Operated by Tennessee State Parks (TDEC). RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Reelfoot Lake State Park - Airpark Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront [coord_fix 36.4708,-89.3435]. satellite at 36.4708,-89.3435: campground sites at edge of Reelfoot cypress-swamp; pads transition directly into standing-cypress lake water, well within distance bound
note: Airpark/North campground (3120 Hwy 213, north/east side; distinct from the South campground at 6812 Hwy 21 W). 14 RV sites with electric (30-amp) + water hookups (no sewer) plus ~10 primitive tent sites. Drive-in. Picnic table + grill each site; bathhouse w/ showers, dump station, laundry, playground. Max RV length reported 35-66 ft (sources conflict). Reservable online up to 1 year ahead; $5 reservation fee. Operated by Tennessee State Parks (TDEC). RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Warriors' Path State Park Campground
note: 134 total sites on Patrick Henry Reservoir (Holston River). Main Campground: 94 year-round sites with water + electric (some 50-amp), trailers up to 40 ft, plus 40 seasonal summer overflow sites; tables, grills, fire rings, heated showers, dump station. Moody Bluff loop adds full-hookup (water/electric/sewer) pull-through sites for larger rigs. Reservable online up to 1 yr ahead; $5 reservation fee. Operated by Tennessee State Parks (TDEC). RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Indian Mountain State Park
note: 47 paved-pad sites for RVs and tents; all sites water + electric (20/30/50 amp), 5 with sewer; picnic table, grill, fire ring per site; level pads fit large rigs (well over 23 ft); modern bathhouse + dump station. Reservable online up to 1 yr ahead ($5 non-refundable reservation fee). Open year-round; water/bathhouse winterized Nov 1-Mar 31. Operated by Tennessee State Parks (TDEC). Separate primitive hike-in Rabbit Road tent area is not this campground. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Tumbling Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> creekside. USFS Cherokee NF page: '8 campsites along the banks of Tumbling Creek' (agency authority); satellite at 35.0174,-84.4664 canopy-blind but shows creek-line clearings
note: Primitive 8-site FCFS campground along Tumbling Creek near Ocoee #3 Lake, Cherokee NF (Ocoee/Big Frog Mountain region). Free, no fee, no reservations (not on recreation.gov). Mostly small RVs/vans/cars, but the first couple of sites fit medium RVs/trailers with room to turn around (23-ft feasible). No designated pads, no hookups, no toilets/water/tables; max 5 people & 2 vehicles per site; bear-proof food storage required. Access via gravel FR 45/FR 221; easiest approach is paved Grassy Creek Rd off Hwy 68 then ~2 mi on FR 221 — passable by a normal vehicle. Open year-round. FCFS — not on recreation.gov. RV Life: 0* / $. --Claude

name: Parksville Lake RV Campground
note: 17 RV sites (16 bookable 01-16 plus a 03A/03B), all 30-amp standard-electric; gravel back-in pads with long driveways (site 12 ~122 ft, site 01 ~65 ft) that fit a 23-ft rig and larger; central bathhouse with flush toilets/hot showers, water hydrants, dump station. Separate tent/group area across Hwy 30. ~$20/night, season roughly late Feb-mid Jan. Reservable online with some first-come availability. Operated by Cherokee National Forest (Ocoee/Hiwassee Ranger District). RV Life: 4.5* / $ --Claude

name: Chilhowee Campground
note: USFS Cherokee NF (Ocoee Ranger District) campground atop Chilhowee Mtn. 62 reservable sites across loops A-F: RV electric, RV nonelectric, and tent-only; RV sites accommodate large rigs (electric 55-75 ft, nonelectric up to 80 ft). 30/50-amp electric at electric sites; dump station at end of E Loop; water hydrants throughout; bathhouses with flush toilets/showers. Season ~mid-April to late Oct (overflow/day-use year-round). Reservable online. Access via paved FR 77/Oswald Dome Rd (7.5 mi climb) -- FS warns NOT to follow GPS up the unpaved Benton Springs Rd. 7-acre McKamy Lake offers day-use swim beach and fishing. RV Life: 4.5* / $ --Claude

name: State Line Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> riverfront. USFS Cherokee NF page: campground 'adjacent to Tellico River... sites located along the water' (agency authority); satellite at 35.2615,-84.0806 canopy-blind Tellico River corridor
note: Primitive Cherokee NF campground adjacent to the Tellico River at the TN/NC state line, ~17 mi up FR 210 (Tellico River Rd) from Tellico Plains. FS states each site 'can accommodate two 26 foot camping trailers and four cars' - drive-in, RV-capable. Vault toilets, no potable water; bear food storage. FCFS only (no reservations); pay on-site (Scan-and-Pay/QR or cash). Single $10 / double $20, open year-round. Operated by Cherokee National Forest (Tellico River Ranger District). RV Life: 0* / $ --Claude

name: Big Oak Cove Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> riverfront. USFS Cherokee NF page: 11 sites 'on the Tellico River... just feet away from the water' (agency authority); satellite at 35.2643,-84.0870 canopy-blind Tellico River corridor
note: USFS Cherokee NF, Tellico Plains Ranger District. ~11-12 small primitive sites (gravel pads) on the Tellico River along paved Tellico River Rd (FR210). Picnic table, fire ring, lantern post, vault toilets; NO potable water, NO hookups. RV + tent (small rigs; sibling sites on this road fit ~25-26 ft). FCFS only -- no reservations on recreation.gov; pay on-site via Scan & Pay QR, ~$10/night. Seasonal, roughly mid-spring to fall (2025 open date May 22). Currently operating per 2025/26 USFS schedule. RV Life: 0* / $ (unrated). --Claude

name: Davis Branch Campground
note: USFS Cherokee NF, Tellico Plains Ranger District. 6 small primitive sites on the Tellico River along paved Tellico River Rd (FR210). Picnic table, fire ring/grill, lantern post, vault toilets; NO potable water, NO hookups, no cell. RV + tent; firsthand report of a 25-ft Class C in Site 6 (sites fairly level). FCFS only -- no reservations; pay on-site via Scan & Pay QR, ~$6-10/night. Primitive, reported open year-round. Currently operating. RV Life: 0* / $ (unrated). --Claude

name: North River Campground
note: Small (11-site) primitive Cherokee NF campground along North River, ~3 mi up gravel FR 217 off FR 210 from Tellico Plains. Drive-in; FS notes 'Sites 2 and 3 are ideal for mid-sized campers and trailers' and sites 4-6 for mid/smaller rigs (firsthand report of a 25-ft truck camper; low branches a caveat for tall rigs). Vault toilets, no potable water; max 5 people / 2 vehicles per site; bear food storage. FCFS only, $8/night, open year-round. Operated by Cherokee National Forest (Tellico River Ranger District). RV Life: 0* / $ --Claude

name: Jake Best Campground
note: Primitive USFS campground on Citico Creek, Cherokee NF (Tellico Ranger District). 7 sites, standard nonelectric -- no hookups, no potable water (bring your own), vault toilet. Each site has picnic table, fire ring/grill, lantern post. Firsthand report: gravel road passable by normal vehicles (no 4WD), sites fairly level, Site 2 best for RVs. $6/site FCFS -- no advance reservations; pay on-site by cash/check or Recreation.gov Scan-and-Pay QR. Open roughly March-November (2025: Mar 6-Nov 17). Max 5 people / 2 vehicles per site; horses not allowed. RV Life: 0* / $ (unrated). --Claude

name: Cades Cove Campground
note: ~159 sites (B and C loops) plus group sites; max trailer 35 ft, max motorhome 40 ft (per-site driveway caps on rec.gov), so 23-ft rigs fit at many sites. No hookups (flush toilets + drinking water only; B loop generators 8a-8p, C loop generator-free). Open year-round; reservation required via recreation.gov (6 months ahead), ~$30/night. Operated by NPS (Great Smoky Mountains National Park). RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Look Rock Campground
note: GSMNP campground (reopened); 68 sites incl 10 with electric+water hookups (30/50-amp) -- the first Smokies campground to offer hookups -- plus dump station; remaining 58 sites no hookups. No showers. Reservation-required online only, no FCFS. Seasonal (~May-late Oct). Bear food-storage rules enforced. RV Life: 3.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Cosby Campground
note: GSMNP campground, 157 sites across loops A/B (138 tent-only, ~14 mixed tent/RV, 9 RV-only, 3 group); RV sites cap at 25 ft (some RV-only to 30 ft). No hookups; dump station; no showers or camp store. Reservation-only online (site-specific), no cash at office. Seasonal (~mid-Apr to end of Oct). RV Life: 5.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Moonhill Bridge Campground
note: Free TVA primitive/dispersed lakeside camping near Moonhill Bridge on Douglas Lake; roughly 6-15 sites on both sides of Chestnut Hill Rd, some by the gravel boat ramp. Gravel access, no hookups, no water/electric, fire rings only. FCFS, no reservations; 14-night max. Seasonal (reported open Mar-Oct). RV Life: 0.0* / $ --Claude

name: Ragland Bottom Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. rec.gov: 'LAKE' loop sites 01-30 incl Standard Electric (RV) sites; satellite at 35.97650,-85.72320: loop on Center Hill Lake peninsula with pads on open sandy shoreline apron within ~30-40m of waterline
note: USACE (Center Hill Lake, Nashville District). 57 sites: 40 water+electric RV sites on the LAKE/MIDDLE loops (max length 51-72 ft, fits a 23-ft rig) + 16 non-electric tent sites (HILL loop). No sewer; dump station, showers. Reservable online; season ~April-November; credit/debit only. RV Life: 4.5* / $. --Claude

name: Paint Creek
waterfront_evidence: -> riverview. canopy-blind satellite at 35.978,-82.845; USFS Cherokee NF page: sites along Paint Creek with 'streamside views' but 'well screened with native vegetation' (vegetated buffer = view, not open apron)
note: Cherokee NF campground along Paint Creek; 20 sites (sites 13/14/16 tent-only -> 17 RV-capable), pads ~50x25 ft so a 23-ft rig fits. No hookups; vault toilets. FCFS scan-and-pay (not advance-reservable), $10/night. Narrow/steep access road -- fine for 23 ft, not big rigs. Season ~May 5-Oct 24. RV Life: 5.0* / $. --Claude

name: Floating Mill Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront [coord_fix 36.0450,-85.7650]. satellite at 36.046,-85.765: RV row on north shore of Center Hill Lake with open grass/beach apron to waterline beside swim area (pads ~15-25m, no treeline screen)
note: USACE (Center Hill Lake, Nashville District). ~105 sites across two loops (TCLP, FMIP); most water+electric 30/50-amp RV sites (max length 16-48 ft; larger sites fit a 23-ft rig, some sub-23-ft sites do not), pull-throughs at 19-22/38-41/52, plus stair-access tent-only lakeshore sites. No sewer. Reservable online; season ~mid-April to early November. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Poole Knobs Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakeview. satellite at 36.0579,-86.5214: western loop pads set back ~25-35m behind a treed shore band on Percy Priest Lake; lake visible/close but no open on-water apron
note: USACE (J. Percy Priest Lake, Nashville District). 78 sites (loop PKNO) + 1 group site: mix of water+electric, non-electric, and tent-only sites; gravel spurs 18-40 ft (larger sites fit a 23-ft rig). No sewer; dump station, flush toilets, showers. Reservable online; season ~May-September. RV Life: 4.0* / $. --Claude

name: Long Branch Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> riverview. satellite at 36.1002,-85.8318: angled pad rows along Caney Fork River set back ~30m behind a treed bank; overlook river but no open shoreline apron
note: USACE (Caney Fork River tailwater below Center Hill Dam, Nashville District). 60 sites: 52 water + 50-amp electric (loop LBRA) + 9-site group loop (GLOO); max length 45-75 ft, easily fits a 23-ft rig. Flush toilets, showers, laundry, dump station, boat ramp. Reservable online; season ~April-October; credit card only. RV Life: 4.5* / $. --Claude

name: Anderson Road Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakeview [coord_fix 36.1071,-86.6073]. current pin in residential area; corrected to rec.gov site centroid; satellite at 36.1064,-86.6090: loop pads set back behind continuous shoreline treeline on Percy Priest Lake
note: USACE (J. Percy Priest Lake, Nashville District); distinct from the adjacent Anderson Road day-use area. 37 sites: ~10 electric/RV sites (15/30/50-amp, max ~45 ft, fits a 23-ft rig) + ~26 primitive/tent sites (some non-electric driveways cap at 20 ft). No sewer; dump station, showers, laundry, boat ramp. Reservable online; short season ~mid-May to early September. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Seven Points Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakeview [coord_fix 36.1331,-86.5731]. pin offset into woods; corrected to rec.gov site centroid; satellite at 36.1330,-86.5746: westernmost sites set back ~30-50m behind shoreline vegetation on Percy Priest Lake, water visible but treed buffer
note: USACE (J. Percy Priest Lake, Nashville District). 59-60 standard sites + 2 group shelters; all standard sites 50-amp electric + water, paved back-in pads, RVs to ~45 ft (select pull-throughs longer). No sewer; dump station, flush toilets, hot showers, laundry, boat ramp, swim beach. Reservable online; season ~April to late October. RV Life: 4.5* / $. --Claude

name: Cedar Creek Campground (Old Hickory Lake)
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront [coord_fix 36.2780,-86.5106]. current pin in residential/marina area; corrected to rec.gov site centroid; satellite at 36.2792,-86.5107: north-loop pad spurs at the Old Hickory Lake shoreline edge with open apron (within ~15-25m)
note: USACE (Old Hickory Lake, Cumberland River, Nashville District) -- on Old Hickory Lake, not Percy Priest. ~60 sites in wooded wagon-wheel/cul-de-sac loops, one-way roads; water + electric 30/50-amp, RVs to ~41-43 ft (tight but fits a 23-ft rig). No sewer; laundry, hot showers, playground, adjacent boat launch. Reservable online; season ~mid-April to mid-October. RV Life: 4.5* / $. --Claude

name: Harpeth River Bridge Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> riverfront. satellite at 36.2843,-87.1447: northern row of angled pads along Harpeth River with open grassy apron and only scattered (gappy) bank trees, within ~25-40m distance bound
note: USACE (Cheatham Lake project, Harpeth River near the Cumberland confluence, Nashville District). 14 sites, each water + 50-amp electric on concrete pads, max length up to 50 ft, fits a 23-ft rig. No sewer; playground, pavilion, boat ramp. Reservation-only online (no walk-ups); season April 15 - October 30. RV Life: 4.5* / $. --Claude

name: Cages Bend Campground
note: USACE Nashville District, Old Hickory Lake (Cumberland River). 43 sites, all with 50-amp electric + water hookups on gravel pads; site lengths well over 23 ft (~38-40 ft typical). Flush toilets, hot showers, laundry, dump station, boat ramp, two fishing docks. Reservation-only via recreation.gov (no walk-ins). Season ~April-October. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Lock A Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> riverview. satellite at 36.3149,-87.189: campground loop along Cumberland River; RV sites overlook river over a thin/scattered bank treeline, open grassy sites, continuous tree screen prevents riverfront
note: USACE Nashville District, Cheatham Lake (Cumberland River). 45 sites; ~38 RV/standard electric + 7 tent-only sites, all 50-amp electric (RV sites well over 23 ft). Modern restrooms, hot showers, dump station, boat launch, swimming area, playground. Reservation-only via recreation.gov (no walk-up sites). Season ~April-October. RV Life: 5* / $. --Claude

name: Salt Lick Creek Campground
note: USACE Nashville District, Cordell Hull Lake (Cumberland River). 150 family sites + 1 group shelter, all with electric hookups; one wheeled unit per site. Flush toilets, showers, laundry, dump station, boat ramp, playground, visitor center. Sites organized in LAKE/WOOD/STRE/SLIC loops. Reservable via recreation.gov. Season ~April-October. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Big South Fork NRRA -- Bandy Creek Campground
note: Year-round NPS developed campground; 181 sites -- 96 trailer sites with water + electric (30/50-amp), 49 tent sites, 2 group loops; no sewer at sites, dump station near kiosk. Large sites (many 25 ft+, some pull-throughs to ~60 ft). Reservable online (recreation.gov) up to 180 days out; fees ~$20-$32. Showers, swimming pool (summer), camp store/stables on site. RV Life: 5.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Boswell Landing
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. satellite at 36.51905,-88.02454: LBL lake-access sites on Kentucky Lake peninsula tip with open sandy beach apron running directly to waterline on multiple sides
note: LBL NRA self-service campground (USFS) on the Tennessee side near Dover; ~16-19 primitive sites, gravel pads, paved 5-mi access road. No hookups; vault toilets, fire rings, dumpster, boat ramp. FCFS - pay by fee envelope at the iron ranger (not on recreation.gov). Open year-round, 14-day max. RV Life: 0* (unrated) / $. --Claude

name: Obey River Park
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. satellite at 36.5305,-85.1695: Dale Hollow Lake loops with RV sites on the peninsula fronting an open grassy/sandy apron to the shoreline
note: USACE Nashville District, Dale Hollow Lake. 123 single + 4 group sites; mix of paved RV sites, gravel RV/tent sites, and tent-only sites. Most have electric, many with water; some 50-amp; no sewer. RV sites well over 23 ft. Swimming beach, boat launch, courtesy float, playgrounds. Reservable via recreation.gov; 14-day stay limit. Season ~mid-April to mid-October. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Dale Hollow Damsite
note: USACE Nashville District, Dale Hollow Lake project (below the dam, along the Obey River; no direct lake access). 72 single sites (+3 group, 1 picnic shelter). 50-amp electric + water on all sites except site 37; no sewer. Mix of RV/tent sites with paved spurs; RV sites well over 23 ft. Reservable online via recreation.gov; 2-night weekend / 3-night holiday minimum. RV Life: 4.5* / $. --Claude

name: Gatlin Point
note: LBL NRA self-service campground (USFS) at the south end on a Lake Barkley embayment near Dover; ~18-19 primitive sites, gravel pads, gravel-road access. No hookups (drinking water in campground, none at sites); vault toilets, dumpster, boat ramp. FCFS - fee envelope at the iron ranger (not on recreation.gov). Note: first entrance is steep and some aprons sit at an awkward angle for trailers. RV Life: 0* (unrated) / $. --Claude

name: Willow Grove Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront [coord_fix 36.5878,-85.3448]. satellite at 36.5878,-85.3448: herringbone pads on Dale Hollow Lake peninsula with open grass/sand apron to shore within a site-depth (measured to high-water edge); original pin was at interior road junction
note: USACE Nashville District, Dale Hollow Lake. 81 single sites (+1 group, 1 picnic shelter). Sites ~1-49 standard electric (50-amp) + water on paved pads; sites 55-81 tent-only (no hookups). No sewer. Standard sites up to ~42 ft (well over 23 ft). Swim beach, playground, Accordion Bluff trailhead. Reservable online via recreation.gov; 14-day stay limit. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Bumpus Mills Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakeview [coord_fix 36.6163,-87.8860]. satellite at 36.6163,-87.8860: single loop on Lake Barkley point; pads behind a continuous treeline screen to water, lower sites overlook water through trees; original pin was 700m off in forest
note: USACE Nashville District, Lake Barkley (Cumberland River section, Stewart County). Compact 15-site campground, all RV electric sites with water + electric (30-50 amp); no sewer; sites fit 23-ft+ rigs. Hot showers, dump station, boat ramp, nearby public marina. Seasonal: ~early May to early September. Reservable online via recreation.gov. RV Life: 4.5* / $. --Claude

name: Pickwick Dam Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakeview [coord_fix 35.0656,-88.2643]. satellite at 35.0656,-88.2643: wooded sites set back behind a road + open sandy beach strip; front sites overlook water across open beach (road/beach buffer disqualifies on-water); original pin was in forest north of sites
note: Rebuilt drive-in campground below Pickwick Dam on TVA land, operated by Go Camp Tennessee (Vista Recreation, private concessionaire). ~92-93 sites in 3 paved-entry loops, all with electric (20/30/50-amp) + water, no sewer (dump station on-site); 3 bathhouses w/ showers, general store. Plus ~8-9 primitive tent sites. Reservable online via Campspot, 2-night minimum. Season Mar 15 - Nov 15. RV Life: 4.5* / $$. --Claude

name: Barton Springs Campground
note: Drive-in RV campground on the south shore of Normandy Lake (TVA easement), operated by Go Camp Tennessee (private concessionaire). 128 sites - gravel pull-through and back-in, all with electric (20/30/50-amp) + water, no sewer (dump station); bathhouse, swim beach, boat ramp, pavilion. RV max ~40 ft. Reservable online via Campspot, 2-night minimum. Season Mar 15 - Nov 15. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Cedar Point Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. satellite at 35.4748,-86.2275: Normandy Lake peninsula camp; west/SW pads sit on an open unused sand/grass apron running to the shore within a site-depth
note: Drive-in RV campground on Normandy Lake (TVA land) near Manchester, operated by Go Camp Tennessee (private concessionaire); distinct from Barton Springs on the same reservoir. 53 sites: 45 with water + electric (37 at 20/30/50-amp), 8 primitive, no sewer (dump station). Swim beach, boat ramp, general store, pavilion. Reservable online via Campspot, 2-night minimum (not on recreation.gov). Season ~Mar 15 - Nov 15. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Notchy Creek Campground
note: Drive-in RV campground on a Tellico Lake peninsula near Vonore, operated by the Tellico Reservoir Development Agency (TRDA, an independent TN state agency on former TVA land). ~50 RV sites + a few primitive tent sites, gravel and fairly level; every RV site has electric (30/50-amp) + water, no sewer (dump station). 2 bathhouses, swim beach, store, boat ramp. Reservable online via Campspot, gated entry. Season ~mid-Mar to mid-Nov. RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Fooshee Pass Campground
note: TVA campground on Watts Bar Lake run by concessionaire Tennessee Valley Hospitality LLC (Go Camp Tennessee). ~54 graveled sites, all with water + 30-amp electric (no sewer); gravel pads, big-rig friendly with pull-through sites. Boat ramp, swim area, bathhouse, dump station. Season approx. mid-March to mid-November. Reservable online via Campspot. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Hornsby Hollow Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. satellite at 35.6905,-84.7050: Watts Bar Lake peninsula camp with RVs visible; NE/perimeter pads on an open unused sand/grass apron to the shoreline within a site-depth
note: Privately operated (Klinker Management LLC) campground on TVA's Watts Bar Lake; family-run since 2006, charter member of TVA Camp-Right program. 109 sites with water + electric (30/50 amp; a subset are 30/20-amp only), fire pit + grill + picnic table each, 3 bathhouses, free wifi, boat ramp, playground, watercraft rentals. Reservable online via Campspot or by phone. RV Life: 4.5* / $$$. --Claude

name: Melton Hill Dam Campground
note: TVA campground on Melton Hill Lake (Clinch River) at Melton Hill Dam, run by concessionaire Tennessee Valley Hospitality LLC (Go Camp Tennessee). 61 sites: 9 full-hookup (electric/water/sewer, 4 ADA), 35 electric+water, 15 tent-only/no-hookup. Big-rig friendly with pull-throughs; dump station, camp store, bathhouses, boat ramp. 2-night minimum. Season approx. mid-March to mid-November. Reservable online via Campspot. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Douglas Tailwater Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> riverfront. satellite at 35.95773,-83.54878: RV pads in a riverside loop with open grass/gravel apron directly to the tailwater river edge within distance bound
note: Drive-in campground below Douglas Dam on the French Broad River tailwater. ~58-61 sites with electric and water plus 2 ADA and a few primitive tent sites; flush toilets, heated showers, dump station, boat ramp, fishing pier. Season approx Mar 15-Nov 15; reservable online via Campspot, 2-night minimum. Run by Go Camp Tennessee (Vista Recreation) in partnership with TVA. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Douglas Headwater Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. official page: 21 RV sites mostly waterfront left of boat ramp; satellite at 35.96000,-83.53600 shows peninsula RV pads with open sandy apron to the high-water edge within bound (reservoir at low pool)
note: Drive-in campground above Douglas Dam on Douglas Lake (headwater/reservoir). ~60 sites: 54 with electric and water, 2 ADA, 4 primitive tent; 3 bathhouses, boat ramp, swimming beach, picnic area. Season approx Mar 15-Nov 15; reservable online via Campspot, 2-night minimum. Run by Go Camp Tennessee (Vista Recreation) in partnership with TVA. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Cherokee Dam Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakeview. satellite at 36.15420,-83.51430: RV pads on an open grassy slope set back ~50m+ across grass with scattered trees from the Cherokee Lake cove; open unscreened view but beyond on-water bound
note: TVA campground on the south side of Cherokee Dam, run by a private concessionaire (Go Camp Tennessee / Vista Recreation). 44 sites: 39 with electric+water hookups, 5 primitive; no sewer (fill/dump). Longest RV reported ~30 ft; perimeter/waterfront sites roomiest. Seasonal, roughly mid-March to mid-November. Reservable online via Campspot, 2-night minimum. RV Life: 5.0* / $$ --Claude

name: Loyston Point Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. satellite at 36.26680,-83.96650: Norris Lake peninsula RV pads with open sandy/grassy apron directly to the shoreline within distance bound
note: TVA campground on Norris Lake (TVA easement), run by private concessionaire Vista Recreation (Go Camp Tennessee). 64-65 sites: 59 with electric hookups, plus tent sites and 3 cabins; fill/dump station, ADA swimming beach. Max RV length 42 ft (paved interior roads; approach road tight per a reviewer). Seasonal, roughly mid-March to mid-November. Reservable online via Campspot. RV Life: 4.5* / $$$ --Claude

name: Watauga Dam Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> riverview [coord_fix 36.3337,-82.1262]. pin was on the facility/day-use complex; campground loop is NW along the Watauga tailwater; satellite dark/leaf-off shows a riparian tree fringe along most of the bank with pads set back (operator 'right on river' = prose only); riverview floor on open ground
note: TVA campground operated by concessionaire Tennessee Valley Hospitality LLC (Go Camp Tennessee partner) on the Watauga River tailwater below Watauga Dam. 29 RV sites, all with electric + water, 3 with full sewer; no tent sites. Big rigs fit (firsthand report of a 40-ft Class C parked on site). Open mid-March to mid-November; 2-night minimum, 14-night max. Reservable online (Campspot) and by phone. RV Life: 5.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Chester Frost Park
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. satellite at 35.18180,-85.14050: peninsula RV pads (RVs visible) with open sandy/grassy apron directly to Chickamauga Lake shoreline within bound
note: Hamilton County park on a Chickamauga Lake peninsula. 228 campsites (~188 RV with water + 50/30-amp electric; no sewer except yurts); a limited number fit RVs up to 65 ft. Year-round; reservable online. Designated waterfront, inland, and tent sites. RV Life: 4.0* / $$. --Claude

name: Winchester Parks and Recreation Campground
note: City of Winchester municipal campground next to Tims Ford Lake and the community ball fields (1895 Phillip Fulmer Pkwy). 28 sites with 20/30/50-amp electric and water, some full-hookup; tents OK. Seasonal Apr 1-Oct 31. Reservable by phone or city website. RV Life: 1.5* / $ (thin reviews, public park). --Claude

name: Wiseman Park
note: Metro Lynchburg/Moore County municipal campground - a paved-and-grass lot behind the downtown square, a short walk from the Jack Daniel's Distillery. ~20 sites: a handful of 50/30-amp full-hookup spots plus several 15-amp sites; water at select sites; ~40-ft max. FCFS, pay at the courthouse (walking distance) or by mail. Access runs through the town square - tight during festivals but passable for a 23-ft rig. Currently operating. RV Life: 4.5* / $. --Claude

name: Beech Bend Campground (Decatur County Park)
note: Decatur County park on the Tennessee River (Kentucky Lake), 5 mi E of Parsons, 11 acres, open year-round. 74 sites (tent + RV), 30/50-amp electric and water hookups, dump station, boat ramp, playground. Accommodates rigs to ~25 ft; reviewer cautions not for big rigs. Reservable by phone/text (cash or check). RV Life: 0* (unrated) / $. --Claude

name: Poland Creek Campground & Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. satellite at 35.82670,-84.10010: peninsula campground on Fort Loudoun Lake; multiple RV pads on west and south edges with open grass apron to the waterline within ~15-25m
note: City of Louisville campground on Fort Loudoun Lake (leased from TVA), bordered by Poland Creek. 24 electric sites (16 on the shoreline, 8 set back) + 8 tent sites, gravel pads, 50-amp available, boat launch, swimming area, playground. Season April-October. Reservable via the city; note that electric sites are often rented for the full season early in the year, limiting short-term electric availability. RV Life: 0* (unrated) / $. --Claude

name: Riley Creek Campground (Roane County)
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. satellite at 35.83000,-84.52680: open peninsula on Watts Bar Lake; RV sites along the NW point and east edge sit on open tan/grass apron running to the high-water shoreline within the distance bound, no treeline buffer
note: Roane County Parks & Rec campground on Watts Bar Lake (TVA land, TVA Camp-Right gold-level). ~45 sites, water + electric hookups (mostly 30-amp, some 50-amp), 6 pull-through, accepts big rigs (~to 40 ft), boat launch, two swimming areas. Season April-October; first-come, first-served (no reservations). RV Life: 4* / $$. --Claude

name: Meadow Park Lake Campground
note: City of Crossville (Leisure Services) campground at Meadow Park Lake. 8 RV sites total: 6 electric/water, 2 full hookup (electric/water/sewer); 5 primitive tent sites. 30-amp service, dump station, showers. Open Apr 1-Nov 30, 7-night max stay. FCFS/by phone reservation (931-788-2034) — not online. RV Life: 3.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Milan City Park Campground
note: City of Milan campground inside 160-acre City Park. ~5 paved RV sites (1-5) near entrance with back-in/pull-through, 20/30/50-amp power + water; sites 6-9 gravel overflow (~50x15 ft) by the RC course. Sewer dump station on site (no per-site sewer). Max reported RV 32 ft. $25/night, 14-night max. Reservations by phone (731-238-3064), 48-hr advance, M-F office hours. RV Life: 4.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Cherokee Park
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. satellite at 36.25850,-83.27330: RV row along the east edge fronts an open tan/grass bank to Cherokee Lake within ~20-30m, no separating buffer
note: Hamblen County government park on Cherokee Lake; ~85 campsites mixing primitive tent and water/electric RV hookup sites, incl. premium 'Prime Lake Access' RV hookup sites. Year-round (gate hrs seasonal). Reservable by phone (423-586-0325); not on a public online portal. Public boat dock + Hamblen Marina on site. RV Life: 3.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Anderson County Park
waterfront_evidence: -> lakeview. satellite at 36.28760,-84.03011: RV loop on Norris Lake set back ~60-80m behind a developed day-use swim beach and parking strip; open sightline earns lakeview, used-beach buffer bars on-water
note: Anderson County (TN) government park on Norris Lake; 40 short-term water + 30/50-amp/110V electric RV/tent sites (roomy gravel, max RV ~33 ft per firsthand review), plus separate long-term and group areas. Season Mar 1–Dec 1. Reservable online via LetsBonfire. Boat ramps, swim area, nature trails. RV Life: 4.0* / $ --Claude

name: Sullivan County Observation Knob Park
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. satellite at 36.5806,-82.0440: peninsula loops on South Holston Lake have RV pads on open drawdown apron at high-water shoreline
note: Sullivan County park on TVA land at South Holston Lake, operated by the county since 1975. ~175 sites with 30-amp electric + water (no breaker at pedestal, surge protector advised), two RV pump-out stations, boat ramp, fishing pier. $25-$35/night. Open seasonally late March through Oct 31. Reservable online via Staylist or by phone; not on recreation.gov. Drive-in. RV Life: 3.5* / $$ --Claude

name: Around Pond RV Park
note: Private RV park off Van Hill Rd, Greeneville; full-hookup sites incl. pull-throughs, large rigs fit (allstays lists 100-ft max). Reservations available. Operating, well-reviewed. RV Life 4★/$$, 92 reviews. --Claude

name: Bean Pot Campground
note: Family-owned since 1978, Crossville. Full-service RV sites with level gravel pads, room for larger rigs (30/50A, water, sewer); seasonal pool, laundry, dump station. Reservable online (ResNexus) or by phone. RV Life 4★/$$, 168 reviews. --Claude

name: Blue Moon RV Park
note: Small quiet park just off I-75 in Athens (formerly Over-Niter RV Park), ~halfway Knoxville–Chattanooga. 16 full-hookup gravel sites incl. 10 pull-throughs (50A); nightly/weekly/extended rates, reservations accepted. Bathhouse, laundry, WiFi. RV Life 4★/$$, 87 reviews. --Claude

name: Bradley's Pit BBQ & Grill RV Sites
note: Restaurant in Sweetwater with 6 level gravel RV sites (30/50A; 2 with water), $20/night, room for RVs/buses/semis. Also a Harvest Hosts location. Small but a real drive-in nightly RV stop. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 3 reviews; specifics confirmed on operator page. --Claude

name: Brooks Corner Campground & RV Park
note: Behind R.M. Brooks Store near historic Rugby; 10 full-service sites (7x30A/3x50A), bathhouse, WiFi, $10-30/night, reservations accepted. Adjacent to a Big South Fork trailhead, ~1 mi from Clear Fork River. RV Life 5★/$$, 2 reviews; specifics confirmed on operator listing. --Claude

name: Buchanan Resort
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront [coord_fix 36.4148,-88.1082]. satellite at 36.4148,-88.1082: RV loop on lake peninsula has pads on open apron at Kentucky Lake waterline (marina point); pin was on cottage/marina, moved to RV loop
note: Kentucky Lake fishing/RV resort est. 1946, Springville; open mid-March–mid-Nov. Three campgrounds with gravel full/partial-hookup sites for RVs up to 40 ft; nightly/monthly/seasonal. 350-slip marina, restaurant, store, pool. RV Life 4★/$$, 3 reviews; nightly camping confirmed on operator site. --Claude

name: Cherokee Trails Campground and Stables
note: Small private campground at base of Holston Mountain on Morrell Creek, adjacent to Cherokee NF (offers equestrian sites but is a general drive-in campground). ~22 gravel full-hookup sites (30A, W, S), open Mar-Nov, cash/check only. Access roads narrow/tight; a 30-ft trailer reportedly fit but no pull-throughs for big rigs. Reserve by phone. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 3 reviews. --Claude

name: Chyttle's Farm & Campground
note: Small private farm campground near Danville/Stewart, booked via Hipcamp. 10 full-hookup RV sites + 5 water/electric, fire rings, dump station; creek runs through property. Danville public boat ramp ~1.5 mi (Kentucky Lake access). Active FB/Hipcamp listings confirm operating. RV Life 5★/$, 1 review (thin). --Claude

name: Coops Creek RV Campground
note: Small private 10-slip full-hookup RV park (W/S/E, 30/50A, WiFi) in downtown Dunlap, adjacent to Coke Ovens Historical Park with a foot path to Coops Creek. Reserve by phone/email. Operating per Dunlap Mercantile listing and active FB page. RV Life 5★/$$, 2 reviews (thin). --Claude

name: Crazy Horse Recreational Park
waterfront_evidence: -> riverview. satellite at 35.4593,-87.7754: main RV row set back ~150m across field from Buffalo River; near-bridge sites on open grass overlook river, bank partly treed
note: Family-owned campground on the Buffalo River, Waynesboro; canoe/kayak outfitter + ~50 sites (full and water/electric 30A, plus primitive), dump station, showers. Open seasonally Apr-Oct. Reservable via RoverPass; updated Nov 2024 (operating). RV Life 4★/$$, 1 review. --Claude

name: Deer Run RV Resort
note: Well-established 200-acre RV resort on the Cumberland Plateau, Crossville. 87 oversized full-hookup big-rig-friendly sites (30/50A), pool, dog park, 25-acre private Good Neighbor Lake (fishing/swimming/beach). Reservable online via Campspot. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 123 reviews. --Claude

name: Doe Valley Campground
note: Private campground on 110 acres of former farmland near Mountain City, adjacent to Doe Mountain Recreation Area (ATV/trails). 70-ft full-hookup pull-through RV sites (20/30/50A, W/S, WiFi). Reservable online via Firefly Reservations. Watauga Lake ~10 mi away (no on-site water). RV Life 5★/$$, 9 reviews. --Claude

name: Fall Creek Marina and Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront [coord_fix 36.2879,-83.2227]. satellite at 36.2879,-83.2227: RV pads ring marina peninsula on Cherokee Lake with open shore/beach aprons at waterline; original pin sat ~150m NE in an empty field
note: Private marina campground on Cherokee Lake (mile marker 22), Russellville. Big-rig-friendly full/partial hookup sites (30/50A metered), swim beach, camp store, dump station, showers. Official site lists both lakefront and interior camping. Reserve by phone. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 1 review. --Claude

name: Grand View Cabins and RV Park
note: Family-run private RV park on the Cumberland Plateau near Altamont; ~20 gravel full-hookup pull-thru sites (30/50A), 4 cabins, max RV ~34 ft, plus a small fishing pond. Reviews note sites are level and long enough for any rig. Reservable (RoverPass)/call. RV Life 5★/$$, 16 reviews. --Claude

name: Green Acres
note: Small private RV park at 6403 S York Hwy, Clarkrange; 21 full-hookup sites (water, 30/50A electric, sewer), backs to a working cattle farm with on-site coffee shop. Nightly/weekly/monthly; reservable via own site or phone. No on-site water. RV Life 5★/$$, 1 review (confirmed operating). --Claude

name: Green Acres RV Park
note: Large full-service private RV park at 215 Ziffle Circle, Savannah (~6 mi from Pickwick Dam, inland — not waterfront); ~50 full-hookup sites (50/30/20A, W, S) incl. 12 pull-thrus, saltwater pool, laundry. Reservable online/phone. RV Life 5★/$$, 169 reviews. --Claude

name: Greenlee of Fall Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. satellite at 36.2870,-83.2205: front RV rows sit on open grass/sand apron running to Cherokee Lake shore beach within site-depth
note: Private campground on a 140-acre peninsula on Cherokee Lake at 5601 Fall Creek Dock Rd, Russellville; primitive to full-hookup sites (30/50A), 2 boat ramps, beach, 1 rental cottage. Overnight + seasonal; reservations accepted, 3-day min on holidays. RV Life 4★/$$, 6 reviews. --Claude

name: Hatchie Cove Campground
note: Small family-run private RV park at 2553 Marvin Chapel Rd, Brownsville, off I-40; full hookups (50A, W, S), fits up to a 36-ft Class A per reviews. Open year-round; reservations required (booked via parkwith.us). RV Life 4.5★/$$, 48 reviews. Name refers to the nearby Hatchie River area, not on-site water. --Claude

name: Indian Boundary Campground
