note: USFS Cherokee National Forest campground (Tellico Ranger District) on 96-acre Indian Boundary Lake, 2 mi off the Cherohala Skyway; 87 electric sites across loops A-D, swim beach, boat launch, 3.2-mi lake trail. Campground road rated for vehicles up to 26 ft (fits a 23-ft rig). Reservable on recreation.gov. RV Life 4.5★/$, 34 reviews. --Claude

name: Indian Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> riverview. satellite at 36.2376,-85.7936: RV pads cluster by road on open grass field running ~150m down to a sandy beach on the Cumberland (Cordell Hull) bend; water visible across open ground but pads beyond on-water distance bound
note: Private campground on Cordell Hull Lake at 4 Park Ln, Buffalo Valley (near Granville); 53 sites incl. electric-only and ~33 elec+water (30/50A), 5 pull-thrus, max RV ~40-50 ft, plus boat-in sites and a boat ramp with direct lake access. Reservable/call. RV Life 5★/$$, 4 reviews. --Claude

name: Lakeview RV Resort
note: Private family-owned RV resort next to Boone Lake on Hwy 11E, Bluff City; full-hookup sites, cabins, year-round. Reservable online/by phone. RV Life 4★/$$, 167 reviews. --Claude

name: Lazy Acres RV Park
note: Private RV park at 7502 Jackson Bend Rd, Lenoir City; 66 sites incl 18 pull-throughs, full hookups (20/30/50A), year-round. Transient nightly. RV Life 4★/$$, 80 reviews. --Claude

name: Little Eagle RV Park
note: Private RV park at 14652 Hwy 79, Buchanan, on Kentucky Lake; full-hookup sites accommodate up to 50ft. Reservable nightly via Campspot. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 9 reviews. --Claude

name: Made in the Shade RV Park
note: Private RV park at 6878 Granville Hwy, Granville, along the Cumberland River near Cordell Hull Lake; 32 full-hookup sites, big-rig friendly with pull-throughs. Reservable nightly via Campspot. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 26 reviews. --Claude

name: Maple Hill RV Campground
note: Private RV park & cabins at 1386 N York Hwy, Jamestown (Cumberland Plateau). Nightly/weekly/monthly; reservable via Campspot. RV Life 5★/$$, 30 reviews. --Claude

name: Marion County Park and Lake
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. satellite at 35.0318,-85.5602: angled pull-through RV pad rows run down a finger of Marion County Lake to the shoreline with RVs parked at an open grass edge ~15-25m from waterline
note: Marion County government park at 9696 US-41, Jasper, on county-owned Marion County Lake; ~60 big-rig-friendly full-hookup + tent sites, gravel/grass, boat ramp. $20/night, reserve by calling office. RV Life 4★/$$, 80 reviews. --Claude

name: McMillan Farms
note: Private farm retreat at 495 McMillan Rd, Mosheim; general campground (offers horse/hiking trails, not equestrian-only) with 7 full-hookup back-in sites for RVs of any size, bathhouse, on-site fishing ponds. Gated, reservation required (email/phone). RV Life 4.5★/$$, 9 reviews; confirmed operating. --Claude

name: Mountain Glen RV Park & Campground
note: Private RV park at 6182 Brockdell Rd, Pikeville (Cumberland Plateau); all pull-through full-hookup (30/50A) sites, year-round, daily/weekly/monthly. Reservable by phone/RoverPass. RV Life 4★/$$, 60 reviews. --Claude

name: Mountain Pass Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> riverfront. satellite at 36.5459,-83.5802: single row of RV pads lines the Powell River bank with open grass apron directly to water
note: Private RV campground on the Powell River near Cumberland Gap, 575 Benfield Lane, Harrogate. ~56 gravel full-hookup sites (30/50A), big-rig pull-throughs; daily rental or yearly lease. Reservable by phone/email. River access, boat launch. RV Life 4★/$$, 13 reviews; operating per Campendium. --Claude

name: Nashville I-24 Campground
note: Private Good Sam RV park, 1130 Rocky Fork Rd, Smyrna (~30 min from Nashville). 100+ sites of all sizes, full hookups (30/50A), pull-throughs, dump, laundry, pool, 3 cabins; open year-round, reservable online/phone. RV Life 4★/$$, 208 reviews. --Claude

name: Old Mountain Campground
note: Family-run private campground at base of Viking Mtn, 1074 Old Mountain Rd, Greeneville. ~26 RV sites (8x 50A, 18x 30A) full hookups, pool, offers horse trails (general CG, not equestrian-only); open year-round, FCFS/by phone. Confirmed operating via operator site. RV Life 4★/$$, 1 review. --Claude

name: Outer Haven Campground
note: Family-run private campground, 4111 Chavis Rd, Cosby, ~6 mi from GSMNP Cosby entrance. 17 graveled RV sites (1-10 full hookup, 11-17 W/E); site pads 55-75 ft long, fit a 23-ft rig easily. Booked nightly via Airbnb; operating. RV Life 4★/$$, 1 review; tent + glamping also on-site. --Claude

name: Parker's Ridge RV Park
note: Private RV park on 17 acres, 460 Murphy Rd, Wildersville (I-40 exit 108). Pull-through full-hookup sites (30/50A), camp store, walking trails; overlooks Parker's Crossroads battlefield. Reservable. Some reviews note tight spacing for big rigs but fine for a 23-ft. RV Life 4★/$$, 3 reviews. --Claude

name: Possum Creek RV Park & Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. satellite at 35.3008,-85.0883: SW row of RV pads at the Chickamauga Lake (Possum Creek embayment) shoreline with open grass/dirt apron to water
note: Privately operated RV park on the Possum Creek embayment of Chickamauga Lake (TN River), 1845 Lee Pike, Soddy-Daisy. 44 full-hookup RV lots + 9 tent sites, swimming, boat ramp; open year-round, reservable by phone. Some full-time residents present but takes nightly reservations; Jan-2026 firsthand review confirms operating. RV Life unrated/$. --Claude

name: R&D Campground
note: Private mountainside campground, 900 Mining Town Rd, Mountain City, at the Doe Mountain ATV/trail system (drive-in, normal-vehicle accessible). 19 total sites: 1-9 W/E RV sites ($40/day), 10-19 primitive tent. 2 bathhouses, pavilion; open year-round, by phone. RV Life 4★/$$, 7 reviews. --Claude

name: R&R RV Campground
note: Private RV campground on US-127 in the Sequatchie Valley, 23876 US-127, Dunlap; near Fall Creek Falls SP. 17 gravel sites, full hookups (30/50A), pull-throughs; tent camping too. Reservable. RV Life 5★/$$, 24 reviews. --Claude

name: Raccoon Valley RV Park at Volunteer Park
note: Private RV park, 908 Raccoon Valley Rd, Heiskell (I-75 exit 117), north of Knoxville. Former Escapees Raccoon Valley park, sold and now run as Volunteer Park, open to the general public for nightly transient stays (booked online). 64 full-hookup sites, 38 pull-throughs (20/30/50A), pool, laundry, dog park; open year-round, RVs only. RV Life 4★/$$, 195 reviews. --Claude

name: Skull Island Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. satellite at 35.2416,-85.0728: campground occupies a small island in Chickamauga Lake, pads within ~30m of open shoreline on all sides; vista-rec PIP map corroborates
note: Privately operated (Go Camp Tennessee / Vista Rec) on a TVA lakeshore easement island on Chickamauga Lake; 68 sites with 50/30/20-amp, beach, camp store. Reservable online via Campspot (2-night minimum). RV Life 4.5★/$$, 22 reviews. --Claude

name: Smoker Holler RV Resort
note: Private full-hookup RV park (33 sites, 30/50-amp, water/sewer) in Wears Valley on Hwy 321 between Sevierville and Townsend; rigs to 60 ft, reservable by phone/Campspot. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 13 reviews. --Claude

name: Smooth Rapids
note: Owner-operated RV camping + kayak outfitter/grill on the Barren Fork River in downtown McMinnville; ~25 sites with 30/50-amp + water, dump station, shower house. Reservable; confirmed currently operating via operator site. CAVEAT: reviewers note a narrow, steep entrance road tricky for larger RVs. RV Life 4★/$, 1 review. --Claude

name: Spring Lake RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: -> lakefront. satellite at 35.9927,-84.9297: RV pads ring the private 3-acre lake on east/west shores, pulled to the shoreline with open grass apron
note: Private full-hookup RV resort near Crossville; 63 sites (30/50-amp, water/sewer, concrete patios), 40 of them around a 3-acre catch-and-release fishing lake; rigs to 75 ft. Reservable online/phone. RV Life 5★/$$, 267 reviews. --Claude

name: Swiss Haven RV Campground
note: Small private full-hookup RV park near Hohenwald (300 Gay Rd); 16 sites, 50-amp (5 also 20/30-amp), 3 pull-throughs. Official site, currently operating; reservations by phone while booking engine upgraded. Thinly reviewed (RV Life 4★/$$, 4 reviews) but operator page confirms a transient nightly RV park. --Claude

name: Tanbark Campground
note: Family-owned (since 1982) 120-acre RV park ~9 mi south of Dickson; 38 paved full-hookup sites (30/50-amp, many pull-through), big-rig friendly to 60 ft. Reservable by phone and via RoverPass; well established and operating. RV Life 4★/$$, 103 reviews. --Claude

name: Tennessee Country Campground and RV Park
note: Private transient RV park at I-75 Exit 56 in Niota (McMinn Co), midway Chattanooga-Knoxville. ~22-50 sites, 20/30/50amp, full hookups, year-round, reservations by phone. $30/night. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 24 reviews. --Claude

name: Union City RV Park
note: Small private RV park (~12-14 gravel full-hookup sites, 50amp) off US-51/Reelfoot Ave in Union City; year-round, takes nightly and monthly stays (nightly transient confirmed on operator site), book by phone. Thinly reviewed (RV Life 4.5★/$$, 4 reviews) but operating with active website. --Claude

name: Whispering Oaks Campground
note: Family-owned private campground off I-24 Exit 105 in Manchester. ~70 sites, full hookups 30/50amp, back-in and pull-through, pool/playground, online booking. RV Life 4★/$$, 41 reviews. --Claude

name: Whispering Pines RV Park
note: Private RVs-only park off US-70 near I-40 Exit 76 in Jackson. ~30 full-hookup pull-through sites, accepts big rigs, year-round, book by phone. No official website found but confirmed operating via multiple current directories. RV Life 4★/$$, 9 reviews. --Claude

name: Windrock Gap Campground
note: Private drive-in campground at foot of Windrock Mountain (Oliver Springs), 300 Hoskins Gap Rd; ~33 RV sites (7x 50amp, 25x 30amp) water+electric, dump station, bathhouse, online booking. Popular as OHV-trail-access base but a general drive-in RV CG. $40/night. RV Life 5★/$$, 2 reviews. --Claude

name: Windy Rivers Campground
waterfront_evidence: -> riverfront. satellite at 36.4347,-82.8976: SE pads on loop perimeter front the Holston River across open unobstructed grass (~50m or less); reviews report ~60ft from water
note: Private campground on the Holston River in Rogersville (Hawkins Co); river access with onsite boat launch, fishing. Reviews report RV sites as close as ~60 ft from the water. Book by phone/Facebook. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 4 reviews. --Claude

name: Amicalola Falls State Park & Lodge
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.5698,-84.2438: campground loops in forested mountain terrain, no lake/river at pads (falls are a gorge creek away)
note: GA state park (concessionaire-managed by North Georgia Mountains Authority/Coral Hospitality). Small drive-in campground, ~24 sites with electric+water hookups (20/30/50 amp), gravel pads, bathhouse, laundry; no on-site dump station. Sites fit RVs up to ~40 ft per some sources (others cite ~24 ft) — 23-ft EKKO fits. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5★/$$$, 57 reviews. --Claude

name: Black Rock Mountain State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.9061,-83.4076: mountaintop campground under dense canopy, no water body adjacent (park lake is well away)
note: GA's highest state park (~3,640 ft). 44 tent/trailer/RV sites with electric (Hickory Grove area), plus walk-in and backcountry sites (excluded). Bathhouse, laundry, dump station. Park warns of steep, winding mountain access roads but developed sites accept trailers/RVs. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 69 reviews. --Claude

name: Bobby Brown Park
waterfront_evidence: pin was day-use parking ~700m off; satellite at 33.9869,-82.5928 shows campground peninsula on Clarks Hill Lake but RV rows set back behind store/buildings/treeline from shore (docks+swim area on water); pads not on water
note: Formerly Bobby Brown State Outdoor Recreation Area; GA DNR lease ended and the park is now operated by Elbert County Government (ownership local, NOT a state park; reserve via county site, not ReserveAmerica). ~55 drive-in campsites plus yurts/cabin on the north end of Clarks Hill (J. Strom Thurmond) Lake; boat ramps. Reservations required via county. RV Life 3★/$$, 10 reviews. --Claude

name: Chattahoochee Bend State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.4224,-84.9818: campground loop in woods with small interior pond; Chattahoochee River is ~600m+ west, no riverfront pads
note: 37 tent/trailer/RV sites in two loops (30/50-amp electric+water; several full-hookup host sites), set in a meadow a short walk from the Chattahoochee River. Riverfront platform sites and walk-in/paddle-in sites are tent-only (excluded). Boat ramp. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica; daily ParkPass applies. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 85 reviews. --Claude

name: Cloudland Canyon State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.8339,-85.4917: plateau-rim campground in forest, no lake/river at pads (canyon is a gorge below)
note: Large, popular canyon-rim park. 72-75 tent/trailer/RV sites with electric (West Rim and East Rim campgrounds), plus walk-in, yurt, cottage, backcountry, pioneer options (excluded). Bathhouses, dump station. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 294 reviews. --Claude

name: Crooked River State Park
waterfront_evidence: pin was in adjacent subdivision; satellite shows SP campground loop set back ~100-200m in maritime forest behind a treeline from the Crooked River tidal channel; no riverfront pads
note: Coastal park near St. Marys (gateway to Cumberland Island ferry). 62-63 tent/trailer/RV sites with electric, set in maritime forest of palmettos and live oaks; boat ramp and dock. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica; daily ParkPass applies. RV Life 5★/$$, 342 reviews. --Claude

name: Don Carter State Park
waterfront_evidence: pin was entrance/group area; satellite shows camping loops under canopy set back behind wooded buffer from Lake Lanier (lakeside units are cabins); no shoreline RV pads
note: GA's only state park on Lake Lanier. 44 developed sites with electric (15-50 amp), water and sewer in a gated RV loop; plus a tents/hammocks loop and primitive sites (excluded). Two boat ramps, courtesy docks, rentals. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica; daily ParkPass applies. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 103 reviews. --Claude

name: F. D. Roosevelt State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.8432,-84.8305: RV cluster ~40-60m above a park lake's north shore, separated by a thin continuous wooded fringe; water visible/close but treeline screens pads, so view-only not on-water
note: Largest GA state park. ~115 tent/trailer/RV sites with electric; some sites sit along the 15-acre Lake Delano. Bathhouses, dump station, pool/beach (Liberty Bell Pool). Reservable online via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 259 reviews. --Claude

name: Florence Marina State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.0858,-85.0432: open-grass/lawn campground loop with RV sites and an unobstructed open apron running to Lake Walter F. George (Eufaula) shoreline; RVs parked at lakeside edge
note: GA state park on Lake Walter F. George (Lake Eufaula) at the north end. 41-43 drive-in tent/trailer/RV sites with electric (some water/sewer); dump station, boat ramp, marina. Reservable via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 154 reviews. --Claude

name: Fort Mountain State Park
waterfront_evidence: canopy-blind satellite over ridge-top park; only a small 17-acre lake with a day-use sand swim beach, campsites are in the wooded campground not on the lakeshore; no per-site shoreline evidence, default down
note: Mountaintop GA state park near Chatsworth. 70 drive-in tent/trailer/RV sites with electric, plus walk-in/platform/backcountry (excluded). 17-acre lake with beach. Reservable via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 113 reviews. --Claude

name: Fort Yargo State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite around Lake Marbury: RV campground loops are wooded and set back behind a treed shore; the lakefront sites are walk-in tent-only, yurts/cabins occupy the immediate shore - no RV pads on open water
note: GA state park near Winder on 260-acre Marbury Creek Reservoir. ~47 drive-in tent/trailer/RV sites with water/electric, rigs to 50 ft; plus 12 walk-in tent sites (excluded). Beach, boat ramp. Reservable via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 117 reviews. --Claude

name: General Coffee State Park
waterfront_evidence: campground in heavily forested area along the narrow Seventeen-Mile River/cypress swamp; canopy-blind satellite, no per-site evidence of open shoreline RV pads; default down
note: GA state park near Nicholls. 50 drive-in tent/trailer/RV sites with electric plus 10 no-hookup sites. Small 4-acre lake. Reservable via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 87 reviews. --Claude

name: George T. Bagby State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 31.6670,-85.0598: new campground loop on Lake Walter F. George with RV sites on the outer (west) edge fronting an open grass/sand apron to the shoreline; adjacent swim beach
note: GA state park on Lake Walter F. George near Fort Gaines. Brand-new 32-site campground (opened Dec 2025) with full hookups (water/sewer/30-50A electric); pool, comfort station, laundry. Reservable via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 5★/$$, 3 reviews (new). --Claude

name: Georgia Veterans Memorial State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 31.951,-83.913: RV campground loops overlook Lake Blackshear but a continuous vegetated treed shore screen separates the pads from the water; sites set back behind treeline -> view not access
note: GA state park on Lake Blackshear near Cordele (resort operated by NGMA/Coral Hospitality). ~77-83 drive-in tent/trailer/RV sites with water/electric (some cable), in pine forest by the lake; dump station, boat ramps. Reservable via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5★/$$$, 165 reviews. --Claude

name: Hamburg State Park
waterfront_evidence: leaf-off satellite at 33.2085,-82.7890: campground loop on a peninsula in Hamburg Lake with RV pads at the shoreline (peninsula-tip sites within a pad-depth of water, only scattered trees); agency: 'shaded sites along the edge of Hamburg Lake'
note: GA state park near Mitchell on 225-acre Hamburg Lake (Little Ogeechee River). 30-32 drive-in tent/trailer/RV sites with water/electric along the lake edge; dump station, hot showers. Reservable via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 5★/$$, 56 reviews. --Claude

name: Hard Labor Creek State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at Lake Rutledge SW shore: RV campground loops are wooded and set back from the lake behind a treed shoreline; lake access is via a separate day-use sand swim beach, no RV pads on open water
note: GA state park near Rutledge. 51 drive-in tent/trailer/RV sites (13 with sewer, many electric) plus tent/pop-up and 11 horse sites (excluded). Two lakes: Lake Rutledge (274 ac, boat ramp) and Lake Brantley. Reservable via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 134 reviews. --Claude

name: High Falls State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.1828,-84.0186 + GA park map: Lakeside Camping Area RV/yurt pads on High Falls Lake shore with open grassy apron to waterline; camper dock at loop
note: GA DNR state park on a 650-acre lake on the Towaliga River. 106 electric/water tent-trailer-RV sites split between the Lake Campground (23 sites, rigs to 25 ft) and the larger River Camping Area (89 sites, rigs to 50 ft) plus 6 yurts; reservable via ReserveAmerica. 23-ft rig fits either area. Dump station, 2 boat ramps. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 258 reviews. --Claude

name: Indian Springs State Park
waterfront_evidence: GA DNR park map: campground (sites 1-52, 201-224) sits inland NW of McIntosh Lake in forest; only lakeside features are cottages+dock (don't count)
note: GA DNR state park (one of the oldest in the nation) on the 105-acre Chief McIntosh Lake with Sandy Creek. ~76 electric tent-trailer-RV sites (campground on the north side of the lake) plus cottages/yurts; reservable via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 122 reviews. --Claude

name: James H. "Sloppy" Floyd State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.434,-85.343: camp loop set back in forest ~150m+ west of lower lake behind continuous treeline; lakeshore clearing is day-use
note: GA DNR state park with two stocked lakes near the Chattahoochee NF. 24 electric tent-trailer-RV sites plus 4 backcountry sites and 4 cottages; reservable via ReserveAmerica. Small campground, fits 23-ft rigs. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 40 reviews. --Claude

name: Kolomoki Mounds State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 31.4685,-84.9310: peninsula camp loop with RV pads (parked RVs) and open grass apron running to Lake Yahola shoreline
note: GA DNR state historic park (Native American mounds) on Lake Kolomoki. 25 large 30-amp electric+water tent-trailer-RV sites, back-in and pull-through, some sites long; reservable via ReserveAmerica. Dump station, boat ramp. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 57 reviews. --Claude

name: Little Ocmulgee State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite swept lake peninsula/north arm/lodge/golf: lake-adjacent features are day-use beach, cabins, lodge, golf; no RV campground confirmed on shore so default down
note: GA DNR state park (operated by North Georgia Mountains Authority/Coral Hospitality) next to a ~265-acre CCC-built lake. 54 tent-trailer-RV sites with 30/50-amp; some full-hookup (sewer), rest water/electric, some cable; pull-through and back-in, fits larger rigs. Reservable via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5★/$$$, 83 reviews. --Claude

name: Magnolia Springs State Park
waterfront_evidence: GA park map + satellite: Tent/RV camping (sites 1-29) set back SE of Magnolia Lake behind boat-ramp/forest; lakeside blue squares are cottages
note: GA DNR state park built around a large natural spring (7M gal/day) with a small lake; site of Civil War Camp Lawton. 26 electric tent-trailer-RV sites plus 3 walk-in tent sites and 9 cottages; reservable via ReserveAmerica. Fishing dock, boardwalk. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 70 reviews. --Claude

name: Mistletoe State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.6505,-82.3712 + GA park map: peninsula campground loops surrounded by Clarks Hill Lake (No Wake Zone) with RV pads and sandy apron at shoreline
note: GA DNR state park on a peninsula in Clarks Hill Lake (J. Strom Thurmond, ~72,000 acres). ~93 tent-trailer-RV sites (some seasonal) all with electric/water, select full-hookup, plus walk-in/backcountry sites and cottages; reservable via ReserveAmerica. Boat launch in campground, dump station, seasonal beach. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 133 reviews. --Claude

name: Moccasin Creek State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite + GA park sitemap: camp loop set back behind fish hatchery; easternmost sites (16-21) overlook Lake Burton across open day-use lawn (developed strip blocks on-water, open view holds)
note: GA DNR state park on the west shore of Lake Burton (~2,775 acres) where Moccasin Creek (trout stream) enters. ~53 tent-trailer-RV sites; flat terrain, sites close together; reservable via ReserveAmerica. Boat dock/ramp, ADA fishing pier. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 64 reviews. --Claude

name: Red Top Mountain State Park
waterfront_evidence: Official park trail map: RV campsites occupy Lake Allatoona peninsula fingers off Park Marina Rd; satellite at 34.1565,-84.6965 shows peninsula points with open sandy shore aprons at the waterline (canopy hides individual pads, per-site map is authority); current pin was SE of the campground
note: GA state park on a Lake Allatoona peninsula near Acworth/Cartersville. ~92 tent/trailer/RV campsites w/ electric hookups, reservable online via ReserveAmerica. Also 20 cottages + yurts (not counted). Campground road narrow/one-way with some tight, sloped sites — workable for a 23-ft rig but pick sites carefully. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 142 reviews. --Claude

name: Reed Bingham State Park
waterfront_evidence: Official park trail map: RV/Tent camping set back behind the day-use/beach strip on the lake's SW shore, not fronting water; satellite confirms campground inland from the lake
note: GA state park on a 375-acre lake near Adel. 46 tent/trailer/RV campsites in two loops (Loop 1 water+electric w/ dump station; Loop 2 newer full-hookup w/ sewer), pull-through options, reservable online via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 369 reviews. --Claude

name: Richard B. Russell State Park
waterfront_evidence: Official park trail map: RV sites 1-20 line a Lake Richard B. Russell shoreline peninsula, several on the lakeshore with boat docks; current pin was in open water, fixed to the camping peninsula (canopy hides pads, per-site map is authority)
note: GA state park on Lake Richard B. Russell near Elberton. 27 tent/trailer/RV campsites w/ 30-amp electric + water, some with boat docks, reservable online via ReserveAmerica. (RV Life lists 48; official park page says 27.) RV Life 4.5★/$$, 27 reviews. --Claude

name: Stephen C. Foster State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.8258,-82.3598: RV loops in pine forest set back ~300m from the Suwannee Canal boat basin; surrounding Okefenokee is wet prairie, no RV-accessible open shoreline
note: GA state park inside the Okefenokee NWR (gateway to the swamp) near Fargo; $5 refuge fee, gates lock at night. Renovated campground reopened Aug 2025 with two loops — one full-hookup RV loop, one tent loop; ~64 sites, reservable online via ReserveAmerica. Currently operating. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 87 reviews. --Claude

name: Tallulah Gorge State Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.7445,-83.3955: Terrora campground loops set back in woods east of US-441; the Tallulah Falls Lake frontage is the day-use beach, campsites not on the lakeshore
note: Terrora campground, ~50-52 tent/trailer/RV sites, all water+electric hookups; flush toilets, showers, dump station. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica. Drive-in, fits 23-ft rigs. Sandy beach on Tallulah Falls Lake at the adjacent Terrora day-use area. RV Life 4.0★/$$, 129 reviews. --Claude

name: Tugaloo State Park
waterfront_evidence: Official park trail map: Tent/RV sites 1-20 line the camping peninsula's shoreline points on Lake Hartwell; satellite at 34.4990,-83.0758 shows pads with open shore aprons at the waterline; current pin was on a neighboring residential peninsula
note: On a wooded peninsula in Lake Hartwell; 105 tent/trailer/RV sites (some seasonal) plus 11 walk-in tent sites, cottages, yurts. Water+electric hookups; reservable online via ReserveAmerica. Drive-in, fits 23-ft rigs. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 83 reviews. --Claude

name: Unicoi State Park and Lodge
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.7270,-83.7185 + park description: campground on elevated terrain above Smith/Unicoi Lake with a steep grade (bluff/bench), set back from the lake; no shoreline RV sites
note: Managed by North Georgia Mountains Authority/Coral Hospitality. ~48-51 full-hookup RV sites (water/electric/sewer, 30/50-amp) plus tent sites near 53-acre Unicoi Lake; dump station, laundry. Reservable online. RV sites accommodate up to ~30 ft, fits 23-ft EKKO. RV Life 4.5★/$$$, 148 reviews. --Claude

name: Victoria Bryant State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.2960,-83.1575 (partly cloud) + park info: Piedmont park with two small stocked fishing ponds only, campground in woods not fronting a pond; default-down, no lake/river
note: 27 tent/trailer/RV sites with electric hookups plus 1 tent/RV combo; flush toilets, showers, dump station. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica. Drive-in, fits 23-ft rigs. A stream and two small fishing ponds in the park; campground not on the ponds. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 60 reviews. --Claude

name: Vogel State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.7635,-83.9230: campground loops sit inland in the valley SW of Lake Trahlyta; lake fronted by developed day-use beach/dam, not by RV sites
note: 90 tent/trailer/RV sites with electric (95 W/E), plus 18 walk-in sites; flush toilets, showers, laundry, dump station. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica. Mountainous terrain — not for large RVs, but 23-ft EKKO fits; pick appropriate sites. 22-acre Lake Trahlyta drained for dam repairs (as of 2026) but campground fully open. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 159 reviews. --Claude

name: Watson Mill Bridge State Park
waterfront_evidence: TheDyrt reviews + park info: RV/trailer loop is ~3/4 mi from the bridge/creek, set back in woods; only primitive (non-RV) sites front Big Cloud Creek
note: 21 tent/trailer/RV sites with electric hookups (separate from the equestrian campground); flush toilets, showers, dump station. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica. Drive-in, fits 23-ft rigs — enter from Hwy 22 (covered bridge has 9-ft clearance/3-ton limit; do NOT route an RV over it). On the South Fork Broad River below the historic covered bridge/millpond. RV Life 5.0★/$$, 45 reviews. --Claude

name: Bussey Point Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov campsites: 14 drive-in Loop A/B sites are heavily wooded with no shoreline flag; the on-water sites are boat-in only (don't count); satellite canopy-blind at peninsula entrance
note: USACE J. Strom Thurmond (Clarks Hill) Lake, Savannah District. 14 primitive drive-in campsites, no water/electric (hand-pump well water, vault toilets); $6/night, reservable on recreation.gov. Sites large enough for horse trailers with some pull-throughs, so a 23-ft rig fits. On a ~3,000-acre peninsula with boat ramp and natural beaches; equestrian/mtn-bike trails (general campground that also serves horse users). Final ~0.5 mi maintained dirt road, normal-vehicle passable. Unstaffed. RV Life 0★ (unrated), 0 reviews. --Claude

name: Charlie Elliott Wildlife Center Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.4599,-83.7267: loop sits in a forest clearing; nearest pond ~150-200m away through trees, no shoreline pads
note: Georgia DNR Wildlife Resources Division (state, not USFS — RV Life mis-tagged). 20 campsites total: 14 full-size RV sites with electric/water/full sewer + WiFi, plus 6 tent-only sites. Reservable online via Go Outdoors Georgia (walk-ins if available). Within the 6,400-acre Clybel WMA / Marben Public Fishing Area (22 ponds). RV Life 5★, 2 reviews. --Claude

name: Cotton Hill Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 31.6750,-85.0638 (pin was ~1km off): south/east loop pads on the peninsula sit within ~20-30m of the Walter F. George cove with open grass apron to the waterline
note: USACE Walter F. George Lake (Lake Eufaula), Mobile District, near Fort Gaines. 101 sites — 91 with electric & water, 10 primitive; flush toilets, showers, dump station, boat ramp, fish-cleaning station, laundry. Reservable on recreation.gov (2-night weekend / 3-night holiday minimums). Gate closed 10pm–7am. RV Life 5★, 75 reviews. --Claude

name: Deep Hole Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov overview: 'Campsites 5 & 9 are directly next to the River' (Toccoa River); satellite shows the loop's south arm adjacent to the river channel
note: USFS Chattahoochee NF, Blue Ridge Ranger District, on the Toccoa River south of Blue Ridge off GA Hwy 60. 9 sites in one loop, no hookups (vault toilets, no water/electric/dump); 30-ft max RV so a 23-ft rig fits (no pull-throughs). Reservable on recreation.gov, ~$15/night. Toccoa River canoe-trail put-in. RV Life 4.5★, 3 reviews. --Claude

name: DeSoto Falls Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov: both loops have 'creek sites' along Frogtown Creek and amenities list 'water front sites'; satellite canopy-blind so deferring to agency per-site authority
note: USFS Chattahoochee NF, Chattooga River Ranger District, on US-129 north of Cleveland. 23 sites in upper & lower loops, standard non-electric + tent-only (no hookups/dump); campground max ~40-ft RV / 30-ft trailer so a 23-ft rig fits (one group site capped at 18 ft). Reservable on recreation.gov. Creek sites in each loop; DeSoto Falls trail on-site. RV Life 5★, 4 reviews. --Claude

name: Dockery Lake Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov: sites 3,5,7,9 flagged '(lake site)' on 6-acre Dockery Lake; satellite at corrected coord (pin was ~1km S) shows loop adjacent to lake, low-res so deferring to agency per-site authority
note: USFS Chattahoochee NF, Blue Ridge RD. 11 standard nonelectric sites on Dockery Lake, 12 mi N of Dahlonega. No hookups/dump; flush toilet + water Apr 1-Oct 31. Reservable on recreation.gov. Access is a twisty gravel FS road; firsthand reviews confirm a 23-ft RV fits though turns are tight. RV Life 3.5★, 4 reviews. --Claude

name: Doll Mountain Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov sites 19/34/35/37 carry Lakefront proximity but are TENT ONLY (15ft max vehicle, no RVs); RV/drive-in sites are heavily wooded and set back from Carters Lake
note: USACE Mobile District, Carters Lake. ~68 sites, majority RV with electric (some water) hookups; flush toilets, showers, dump station, boat ramp. Reservable on recreation.gov. Heavily wooded peninsula campground. RV Life 4.5★, 67 reviews. --Claude

name: Harris Branch Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.6035,-84.6238: developed beach+parking day-use tip sits behind a treeline; primitive/FCFS area, no on-water RV pads visible
note: USACE Mobile District, Carters Lake. ~10 sites, FCFS only (not on recreation.gov); RVs/trailers up to 40 ft, some sites with electric; swim beach, restrooms. Confirmed operating via USACE Carters Lake camping page. RV Life 0★ (unrated), 0 reviews. --Claude

name: Hickey Gap Campground
waterfront_evidence: canopy-blind satellite at 34.8941,-84.6721; only USFS prose 'alongside Mill Creek trout stream', no per-site map/legible imagery/photo confirming pads at water -> default down
note: USFS Chattahoochee NF, Conasauga RD (Cohutta WMA), on Mill Creek E of Crandall. 5 sites, FCFS, free, no fee/reservation; tent pad, fire ring, table, vault toilet, no potable water. Access via ~6 mi maintained gravel road; road/campground limited to RVs under 24 ft (a 23-ft rig fits). Open year-round. RV Life 0★ (unrated), 0 reviews. --Claude

name: Holiday Campground
waterfront_evidence: leaf-off satellite at 33.0150,-85.1875: West Point Lake peninsula loops sit behind a continuous wooded band + drawdown shoreline; rec.gov has no per-site shoreline flag
note: USACE Mobile District, West Point Lake (W shore). ~112 sites + 2 group areas on a forested peninsula; electric & water hookups, showers, dump station, courts, playground; paved back-in/pull-through pads 25-78 ft. Reservable on recreation.gov. RV Life 4.5★, 44 reviews. --Claude

name: Jacks River Fields Campground
waterfront_evidence: illegible low-res satellite at 34.8635,-84.5202; only USFS prose 'South Fork Jacks River flows alongside', no per-site waterfront attribute -> default down
note: USFS Chattahoochee NF, Conasauga RD, on the South Fork of the Jacks River. 7 sites, FCFS only (pay on-site, not reservable); no water/electric/dump, vault toilet. Trailers over 25 ft not recommended (23-ft fits). Access is 15+ mi of steep narrow winding gravel, passable by standard vehicle. Also accommodates horses/mules. Jan-Mar accessible only from Blue Ridge side. RV Life 0★ (unrated), 0 reviews. --Claude

name: Lake Conasauga Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov Loop Lower Site 19 = Lakefront proximity AND drive-in (RV 21ft/trailer 25ft); lower loop on the 17-acre lake; canopy-blind satellite shows lake at immediate N shore
note: USFS Chattahoochee NF, Conasauga RD; Georgia's highest lake (~3150 ft) in the Cohuttas. ~30 sites in Lower Loop (near lake) + Upper Loop; no electric/dump/showers, flush toilets + water hydrants. Reservable on recreation.gov. Max RV/trailer ~24 ft (23-ft fits); access is 15+ mi of graveled mountain road. RV Life 0★ (unrated), 0 reviews. --Claude

name: Lake Conasauga Overflow Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov: overflow loop set ~1 mile from Lake Conasauga, no Lakefront sites (all Standard Nonelectric)
note: USFS Chattahoochee NF, Conasauga RD overflow area: 6 standard nonelectric sites, no water/electric/dump, ~$8/night. FCFS only — not on recreation.gov (book the main Lake Conasauga cg instead). Drive-in but access is a 15+ mi steep, narrow, winding gravel mountain road (FR 68 for trailers); trailers over ~25 ft not advised — the 23-ft EKKO can make it but it's a slow rough haul. Same road serves the reservable main cg (24-ft cap). RV Life 4★, 1 review. Seasonal (~Apr/May-Oct). --Claude

name: Lake Rabun Beach Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: USFS: Lake Rabun lies directly across the road from Loop 1 entrance (road buffer); heavily wooded loops; rec.gov no per-site shoreline flag -> default down
note: USFS Chattahoochee NF (Chattooga River RD), concessionaire-run (FIND Outdoors). ~80 sites in 2 loops; Loop 2 has 21 sites with electric + water, Loop 1 nonelectric. Hot showers, dump station, boat ramp. Reservable on recreation.gov plus some FCFS. Drive-in, paved access. RV Life 4★, 13 reviews. --Claude

name: Lake Russell Recreation Area Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.4952,-83.4969: canopy-blind loop on a wooded point above a Lake Russell cove; no shoreline RV pads resolvable and no per-site map confirms them; default down
note: USFS Chattahoochee NF on 100-acre Lake Russell. 42 sites on two paved loops (Loop A 16, Loop B 26), no hookups, water hydrants, flush toilets/showers, dump station. Reservation-only via recreation.gov, ~$24/night. Firsthand reports confirm 23-36 ft rigs fit (uneven — leveling needed). RV Life 3★, 2 reviews. --Claude

name: Lake Sinclair Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.2042,-83.4014: camp loop set back ~300m in continuous forest from the Lake Sinclair cove (SE corner)
note: USFS Oconee NF on Lake Sinclair (Eatonton). ~33 sites; Loop A has 5 sites with water + 30/50A electric, rest nonelectric. Bath house, showers, dump station, boat ramp, swim beach. Reservable via recreation.gov. Reported max RV ~24 ft (fine for 23-ft EKKO but at the limit). RV Life 3★, 1 review. --Claude

name: Lake Winfield Scott Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.7338,-83.9743: north/south loops sit in woods ~200m south of the 18-acre lake (lake's only shore contact is the dam/day-use); sites inland
note: USFS Chattahoochee NF (Blue Ridge RD) on 18-acre Lake Winfield Scott near Suches. 31 sites in two loops (North Loop open yearlong); water+electric on sites 12-15, rest standard. Reservation-only via recreation.gov, ~$26-39/night. Reviews report 40 ft+ rigs fit. Open and operating. RV Life 3.5★, 6 reviews. --Claude

name: Low Gap Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.7510,-83.7830: small NF creek campground, imagery illegible (canopy/regrowth), no creek or pads resolvable and no per-site map confirms creekside RV sites; default down
note: USFS Chattahoochee NF (Chattooga River RD) on Low Gap Creek near Helen. 13 standard nonelectric sites, vault toilet, dump station, seasonal hand-pump well (bring your own water). Reservation-only via recreation.gov, ~$12/night. Drive-in via Upper Chattahoochee River Rd (dirt, ~4.5 mi); road is long/bumpy/narrow and hard for larger RVs — fine for the 23-ft EKKO, not big rigs. RV Life 0★ (unrated), 0 reviews; confirmed operating via rec.gov listing. Seasonal (~mid-Mar-Sep). --Claude

name: McKaskey Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.1901,-84.7180: RV loop set back ~100m+ behind a continuous treeline plus a wide drawdown flat from the Allatoona cove; no confirmed shoreline pads
note: USACE Mobile District, Allatoona Lake (concessionaire-operated). 51 sites, 32 with 50A electric + water, some primitive; back-in pads 15-35 ft (23 ft fits). Flush restrooms, hot showers, dump station, laundry, swim beach, boat ramp. Reservable on recreation.gov plus some FCFS, ~$18-28/night. Seasonal (~early May-early Sep). RV Life 3.5★, 17 reviews. --Claude

name: Oconee River Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.7212,-83.2903: open RV pad/clearing directly on the Oconee River bank with unobstructed short access (RV visible at waterline)
note: USFS Oconee NF (Oconee RD) on the Oconee River near Watkinsville/Scull Shoals. 5-6 sites, tables/grills/tent pads, no hookups, no drinking water; paved main road, drive-in. FCFS honor-system, ~$5/night (scan-and-pay) — not reservable on recreation.gov. Campendium firsthand reports confirm RV use (a 25-ft truck camper, a 21-ft Class B on pull-through site 1; some sites ~30 ft). 23-ft EKKO fits. Year-round, confirmed open. --Claude

name: Pocket Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.5857,-85.0834: spring/creek 'Pocket' area; camp clearing/loop with no significant water body adjacent
note: USFS Chattahoochee NF, Armuchee/Cohutta RD. 26 non-electric sites, single loop, paved parking spurs + gravel tent pads, flush toilets, drinking water, no hookups/showers/dump. Paved access road; sites take tents up to 28-ft trailers (23-ft EKKO fits). Reservable on recreation.gov. RV Life 4★, 1 review. --Claude

name: River Junction - Lake Seminole
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.7495,-84.8384: shoreline loop ~30-50m from Lake Seminole but a continuous treeline screens the RV pads from the open water; water visible past the fringe
note: USACE Mobile District, Lake Seminole. 11 sites, hard-packed level gravel, each with water + 50-amp electric, picnic table/fire ring; restrooms, showers, dump, boat ramp. FCFS — pay at electronic kiosk, not reservable on recreation.gov. Max ~40 ft. Sites overlook the lake through trees. RV Life 3.5★, 3 reviews. --Claude

name: Sandy Bottoms Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: FS/multi-source: 13-site USFS campground 'resting on the shore of Tallulah River', half the sites have direct water access, onsite boat launch; satellite at 34.96322,-83.55900 shows loop immediately against the river channel
note: USFS Chattahoochee NF, Chattooga River RD, on the Tallulah River via FS-70 (gravel). 13-14 non-electric sites, vault toilet, hand-pump water; no hookups/dump. Reservation-only on recreation.gov. Sites take ~35-ft vehicle / 28-ft trailer (23-ft EKKO fits). RV Life 0★ (unrated), 0 reviews; confirmed via recreation.gov + USFS page. --Claude

name: Sarah's Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: canopy-blind satellite at 34.92607,-83.26297 (illegible pads); only general prose 'some sites alongside Sarah's Creek', no per-site map confirming RV-drivable creekside sites; default down
note: USFS Chattahoochee NF, Chattooga River RD, off Warwoman Rd via FS-156 (gravel, tight/narrow). 22 non-electric sites, 2 vault toilets, no hookups/water. Reservation-only on recreation.gov since 2025. Max RV ~26 ft (23-ft EKKO fits; narrow access). RV Life 0★ (unrated), 0 reviews; confirmed via recreation.gov + USFS page. --Claude

name: Shoal Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: canopy-blind satellite at 34.16737,-84.01331 (wooded Lake Lanier peninsula, no resolvable pads); named waterfront favorites are tent sites (7-9,12,32) and RV site #1 noted set back/elevated; RV sites overlook lake on water-surrounded peninsula but no per-site map confirms RV pads at waterline
note: USACE Lake Lanier land leased to and operated by Lanier Islands (concessionaire) — federal. ~47-104 wooded and lakefront sites, with/without 50-amp + water hookups, showers, laundry, dump, boat ramp, swim area; year-round, transient nightly. Max RV 40 ft. Reservable via Campspot, not recreation.gov. RV Life 3.5★, 39 reviews. --Claude

name: Sweetwater Campground
waterfront_evidence: original pin was 9km off in rural forest; real campground at end of Fields Chapel Rd; rec.gov: 'Lake front sites are also available' (105 W/E RV sites); satellite at 34.1962,-84.5785 shows camp loops on peninsula running down to the Allatoona shore
note: USACE Lake Allatoona (Mobile District), run by a private contractor. 151 sites: 118 w/ 50-amp electric+water, plus tent-only non-electric and 2 group areas; flush toilets, showers, dump, swim beach, fishing dock. Pads vary 23-75 ft (23-ft EKKO fits). Reservable on recreation.gov. RV Life 4★, 55 reviews. --Claude

name: Tallulah River
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site: 'Campsite 5 is next to the river', site #11 'first site not on the river' (sites 1-10 riverside), long pull-in drives for trailers; canopy-blind satellite at 34.92759,-83.54323 with river along the loop
note: USFS Chattahoochee NF, near Clayton. 17 standard nonelectric sites on the Tallulah River; vault toilets, no hookups. Reservation-only via recreation.gov (no FCFS). Max RV 30 ft / trailer 40 ft, so a 23-ft rig fits, though roads are narrow (no big-rig/pull-through). Camp host mid-Apr to mid-Oct confirms operating. RV Life 2★, 2 reviews. --Claude

name: Upper Stamp Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: original pin 6.5km off; real campground (80 Chitwood Cove Rd) on Allatoona peninsula; allatoonalake.org/multi-source: 'Sites 7-14 face the water directly, lakefront sites available' (18 W/E RV sites, swim beach, fishing dock); satellite at 34.1431,-84.7028 shows loop on water-surrounded peninsula
note: USACE Mobile District, Allatoona Lake (Etowah River arm); run by a private concessionaire (still federal). 20 sites, 18 with 50-amp electric + water, paved back-in pads up to ~33 ft; flush toilets, showers, dump station, boat ramp, swim beach. Reservable on recreation.gov, 2-night wknd min; seasonal (~mid-May to early Sep). RV Life 5★, 3 reviews. --Claude

name: Van Pugh South Campground
waterfront_evidence: original pin landed on a lakefront residential subdivision; real COE campground on a narrow wooded Lake Lanier peninsula (rec.gov electric-site coords 34.184-34.185,-83.989--83.993); 'most campsites have direct access to the lake' (37 standard electric RV sites); satellite shows narrow peninsula surrounded by water
note: USACE Mobile District, Lake Sidney Lanier (Flowery Branch, Gaines Ferry Rd peninsula). 37 sites with 30-amp electric + water plus 18 primitive tent sites; flush toilets, showers, laundry, dump station, boat ramp. RVs over 26 ft not permitted (narrow roads) — 23-ft EKKO fits. Reservation-only via recreation.gov. RV Life 5★, 14 reviews. --Claude

name: Victoria Campground
waterfront_evidence: official USACE Victoria Campground map (cdn.recreation.gov 71471_VICT.pdf): '* Premium/Waterfront Campsites' marked (sites 13*,14*,17*,19*,21*-26*,46*-56*) on the Allatoona shore, all sites have water + 50amp electric (RV); pin at 34.14941,-84.61597 matches campground address
note: USACE Mobile District, Allatoona Lake (Woodstock). 74 reservable sites, paved pads 31-80 ft (back-in + pull-through), electric/water; flush toilets, showers, dump station, boat ramp, swim beach. Reservable via recreation.gov. Seasonal (~early May to early Oct). RV Life 4.5★, 100 reviews. --Claude

name: Watsadler Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.3433,-82.8400: multiple RVs on pads on the peninsula tip with open sandy apron running directly to the waterline (~15-25m)
note: USACE Savannah District, Hartwell Lake (near Hartwell Dam). 51 sites, all 50-amp electric + water; flush toilets, showers, dump station, boat ramp w/ courtesy dock. Max RV ~50 ft (select sites). Year-round: sites 1-23 winter (Dec 1-Mar 31), all 51 Apr 1-Nov 30. Reservable via recreation.gov. RV Life 4.5★, 84 reviews. --Claude

name: Whitetail Ridge Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.0218,-85.1917: canopy loop set back behind continuous treeline ~100m+ from West Point Lake arms; rec.gov has no per-site shoreline flag ('waterfront facility' is marketing prose only)
note: USACE Mobile District, West Point Lake (western shore, near LaGrange). 58 sites, all electric + water, paved roads with large gravel pads up to ~35 ft (some longer); hot showers, laundry, dump station. Reservable via recreation.gov. Season ~early Mar-Nov. RV Life 4.5★, 51 reviews. --Claude

name: Woodring Campground
waterfront_evidence: canopy-blind satellite at 34.6179,-84.6422 (loops on a forested peninsula, pads unresolvable); rec.gov notes steep shoreline limiting bank access and only 'scenic lake views', no per-site shoreline RV sites confirmed
note: USACE Carters Lake (Mobile District; operated by FIND Outdoors), heavily-wooded campground on the lake's north shore. 42 sites, 31 with water + electric (30/50 amp); max RV ~40 ft on larger sites. Reservable online. Flush toilets, showers, dump station, beach, boat ramp. Seasonal (~Apr-Oct). RV Life 4.5★, 21 reviews. --Claude

name: A.H. Stephens State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.564,-82.897: RV loop sits inland NW of the small park lake, separated by dense forest ~250m; lakeshore has only a dock and cottages, no RV pads
note: Georgia DNR state historic park (RV Life mis-bucketed commercial). ~25-site tent/trailer/RV campground (full hookup, back-in + pull-through, level gravel), rigs to ~50 ft so 23-ft fits; separate equestrian camp not counted. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica. RV Life 5★/$$, 101 reviews. --Claude

name: Americus KOA
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.003,-84.111: RV rows among farm fields, no water body anywhere near the sites
note: KOA Journey on the Brickyard Plantation golf course; 48 full-hookup sites, mostly long pull-throughs, big-rig friendly so 23-ft fits easily. Currently operating (2025-26 reviews, active KOA reservation page). Reservable online. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 105 reviews. --Claude

name: Beaver Run RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.334,-81.956: RV pads lined along the pond's south shore with an open grass apron to the waterline, scattered non-screening trees, within distance bound
note: Owner-operated commercial RV park just off I-16 Exit 111; nightly/weekly/monthly ($45/night RV), transient-dominant with a few long-term residents. Large spaced sites, pull-thru + back-in; 23-ft fits. Book direct by phone/email. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 102 reviews. --Claude

name: Big Hart Campground
waterfront_evidence: TripAdvisor review: sites 25-26 'right on the lake', boats anchored at sites on Clarks Hill Lake; canopy-blind satellite at 33.615,-82.513 cannot downgrade
note: On USACE J. Strom Thurmond (Clarks Hill) Lake land but operated by McDuffie County under a 2020 10-yr lease (no longer on recreation.gov) — credit the local operator. 32 sites, mostly pull-through, RV max ~60 ft so 23-ft fits. Reservable online via RMSCloud. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 41 reviews. --Claude

name: Blanton Creek Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.743,-85.108: RVs in open clearing at peninsula shoreline on Lake Harding; GA Power/review authority names lakefront sites 3 & 9 with boat docks
note: Georgia Power utility recreation area on Lake Harding (Bartletts Ferry Lake, Chattahoochee) — NOT West Point Lake as aggregators misfile it. 51 sites, electric+water (no sewer; tent sites 16-23). RV sites fit 23-ft (rigs to 33-60 ft reported). Seasonal ~Mar-Oct, reservable via Georgia Power's gplakes portal. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 29 reviews. --Claude

name: Boss's RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.178,-82.397: RV rows along highway among fields, no water body near the sites
note: Commercial RV park, mixed-use (long-term residents + traveler sites) but reliably takes transient overnighters (one-night pass-through, Masters/coastal-route stopover). Pull-throughs to 80 ft so 23-ft fits easily. Booking by call/text owner. Live operator site. RV Life 4★/$$, 40 reviews. --Claude

name: Buck Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.380,-84.442: small inland RV park in rural residential area near Buena Vista, no creek/lake/pond adjacent to the sites
note: Privately owned (owners host onsite), est. 2015. ~20 full-hookup (W/E 30/50A/sewer) RV/travel-trailer sites at 3610 Dr Deryl Hart Rd, Buena Vista. Laundry, showers, WiFi; pets ok. Takes overnighters; from ~$36/night. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 7 reviews. --Claude

name: Callaway Plantation
waterfront_evidence: original pin was on the airport runway; camp is the 10-site RV area at the plantation buildings off Lexington Rd (satellite at 33.774,-82.812), no water nearby
note: Operated by the City of Washington, GA (Callaway Historic Site, a 56-acre city-owned open-air museum at 2160 Lexington Rd) — ownership local. ~7-10 full-hookup RV sites (2 pull-thru) in a wooded area behind the plantation; day/week/14-day stays (~$35/night). Free WiFi. RV Life 5★/$, 6 reviews. --Claude

name: Camp South RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.3707,-82.1809: RV loops sit in pine woods; only nearby water is a small farm/horse-arena pond ~150m S, no pads on it
note: Family-owned farm RV park, 18903 Kermit Rd, Metter — 1 mi off I-16 Exit 98. ~15 tree-lined sites, bathhouse, laundry, playground, dog park, trails, on-site fishing pond. Takes nightly reservations by phone. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 13 reviews. --Claude

name: Camp Sylvania
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.7393,-81.6249: RV rows set in woods; small amenity pond ~150m+ E screened by continuous treeline, no pads on water
note: Private RV park (aka Savannah River RV Park), 101 Stockyard Rd, Sylvania. 25 full-hookup sites; nightly + long-term, booked via Campspot. Operator describes it as bordered by the Savannah River. RV Life 5★/$, 1 review; confirmed operating via active Campspot booking page. --Claude

name: Catfish Creek RV Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.0118,-81.9156: two small man-made amenity ponds; RV sites are by buildings/road to the NW, not pads on the ponds
note: Private RV park, 12532 US Hwy 301 N, Glennville (~10 mi from Claxton). ~10 large level pull-thru big-rig-friendly sites (30/50A full hookup, double-wide entry ramps), free WiFi, dump station, laundry; catch-out fishing pond within walking distance. Takes overnighters. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 4 reviews. --Claude

name: Cecil Bay RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 31.0458,-83.3995: gridded RV park beside I-75, no lake/creek/river adjacent to any pad
note: Private RV park, 1787 Old Coffee Rd, Cecil — I-75 Exit 32 north of Valdosta. 100 shady level pull-thru sites (40x65), 30/50A full hookup, clubhouse, on-site fishing ponds; takes overnighters. RV Life 4★/$$, 103 reviews. --Claude

name: Cedar Creek RV & Outdoor Center
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.1335,-85.3080: RV loop outer row overlooks Big Cedar Creek across open grass but a continuous wooded riparian bank screens the immediate water (no walk-across apron) -> view not on-water
note: Private RV/outdoor center along Big Cedar Creek, 6770 Cave Spring Rd SW, Cave Spring. 62 full-hookup RV sites (+ tent/group sites), RVs up to 65 ft; on-site canoe/kayak/tube rentals with shuttle. Creek-side sites have more tree cover. Booked via Campspot. RV Life 4★/$$, 55 reviews. --Claude

name: Chehaw Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: pin was park entrance/buildings on Philema Rd; actual RV campground is angled pad rows in pine uplands at 31.6285,-84.1310 (~1.3km NE), no lake/creek at pads (Muckalee Creek ~600m W through woods)
note: Run by the Chehaw Park Authority, an interlocal authority of the City of Albany, Dougherty County, and Lee County (created 1979) — local government, not private. 800-acre park with zoo. ~40 RV sites w/ full hookups ($33) plus regular-hookup sites ($25), tent sites, cabins; dump station. Reservable online (FareHarbor) or by phone. RV Life 4★/$$, 57 reviews. --Claude

name: Cherokee Campground
waterfront_evidence: operator confirms wooded mountain lots not on a river, ~5mi from Chattahoochee tubing in Helen; canopy/cloud satellite shows forested cabin-area, no water at sites
note: Private RV park ~5 mi from Helen. 38 wooded graveled/level sites, 20/30/50-amp full hookups, 8 pull-throughs, cable/WiFi; bathhouse + laundry; open year-round, transient daily rates (~$27). Reservations by phone/email. No on-property water (Lake Burton a few miles off). RV Life 4.5★/$$, 39 reviews. --Claude

name: City Limits RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 31.7683,-84.8067: RV park beside US-82 next to ballfields, no lake/creek/river adjacent to pads
note: Locally owned private park ~1 mi from downtown Cuthbert, opened 2019. ~41 concrete-pad sites, full hookups, accepts RVs to 50 ft (23-ft fits easily); covered commons, laundry; daily/weekly/monthly rates, transient nightly bookable via Campspot. RV Life 4★/$$, 14 reviews. --Claude

name: Cross Creek Camp Ground and Cabins
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.911,-83.386: small private pond on E side; cabins/RVs set back in trees around it, no RV pads resolvable at shoreline; canopy-heavy; site touts fishing pond/dock but no per-site map confirms shoreline RV sites
note: Private campground in Mountain City (Rabun Co), Blue Ridge mountains. ~27-28 sites plus cabins; full hookups, dump station, bathhouse, laundry, WiFi. A creek runs through the property. Transient nightly, reservable online via Campspot. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 35 reviews. --Claude

name: Crossroads Travel Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.474,-83.746: RV/MH park with pool amid commercial/cemetery/woods; no water within 700m frame
note: Private RV park in Perry, minutes from I-75 Exit 136. ~64-75 sites, paved pull-throughs sized for big rigs, 30/50-amp full hookups, cable/WiFi; pool, dog park, laundry. Transient nightly (weekly/monthly also). No on-property water. RV Life 4★/$$, 78 reviews. --Claude

name: Currahee RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.535,-83.351: RV pads in open grassy field off highway; no water nearby
note: Private RV park ~10 min from downtown Toccoa near Currahee Mountain, open year-round. 38 sites: lettered A-G permanent, 11-29 concrete pull-throughs, gravel pull-throughs, plus back-in gravel sites with wood decks overlooking a creek. Full 20/30/50-amp hookups, bathhouse, laundry. Transient nightly (~$34) by phone; monthly also offered. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 68 reviews. --Claude

name: Dreamland RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 31.439,-82.873: MH/RV park with loop roads in open ground; no water nearby
note: Private RV park at 20 Sugarsand Rd, Douglas (Dreamland Parks). ~25 RV sites on 30 wooded/open acres, full hookups (30/50 amp, water, septic), wifi, big-rig pull-thrus, laundry. Transient: nightly/weekly/monthly (also some permanent). Open year-round; daily reservations via operator. RV Life 5★/$, 4 reviews. --Claude

name: Exchange Club RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 31.549,-84.223: large open field/ballfield by highway interchange; no water nearby
note: RV park run by the Exchange Club of Albany at its fairgrounds, 810 S Westover Blvd. Open to public for transient stays year-round (daily/weekly/monthly, no time limit). 112 grassy sites under pines, 20/30/50 amp, water, some sewer, free wifi; 40 ft max. Not a members-only lodge. RV Life 4.5★/$, 4 reviews. --Claude

name: Feathered Camp'N Acres
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.714,-82.593: rural building/field amid pine plantation and farm fields; no water nearby
note: Small private RV park at 316 Aonia Rd, Washington (Aonia community). 7 full-hookup sites incl 4 pull-thrus, 30 amp, wifi, picnic/fire pit; accepts big rigs. Transient, open year-round. Thinly reviewed but confirmed operating via multiple directories. RV Life 4★/$, 5 reviews. --Claude

name: Flint River RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.904,-84.583: RV park sits at US-27 interchange ~400m+ S of Flint River with railroad and woods between; pads not on the river despite name
note: Private RV park at 801 W Shotwell St, Bainbridge, on the Flint River next to Bainbridge Boat Basin Park. 85 sites, full hookups (30/50 amp), pull-thrus fitting big rigs, pool, bathhouse, laundry, propane. Transient; reservable via Campspot. RV Life 4★/$$, 50 reviews. --Claude

name: Forest Glen Mobile Home & RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.255,-84.092: MH/RV park alongside I-75 in woods; no water nearby
note: Mixed mobile-home + transient RV park at 218 Glade Rd, Jackson, just off I-75 (exit ~201). RV side: 83 full-hookup sites (30/50 amp), 41 pull-thrus, big-rig accessible, 50 ft max, free wifi; takes nightly travelers. Some interstate noise. RV Life 4★/$$, 46 reviews. --Claude

name: Frank Gross Campground
waterfront_evidence: USFS Chattahoochee NF page: campground on the banks of Rock Creek, sites 2 & 5 directly adjacent to creek; satellite canopy-blind so agency per-site authority governs
note: USFS (Chattahoochee NF) campground on Rock Creek off Rock Creek Rd, near Morganton (RV Life mis-bucketed as commercial). 8 primitive sites, no hookups/water, $8/night, vault toilets. Reservable on recreation.gov; open Apr 1-Oct 31. Drive-in; sites fit 23 ft (site 1=40 ft, site 5=33 ft, site 3=27 ft). Popular trout fishing. --Claude

name: Georgia International Horse Park RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.6716,-83.9411: RV area by event buildings/road; South River ~150m+ S through forest, no shoreline pads
note: City of Conyers-owned 1996 Olympic equestrian venue (local government). 140 full-service RV sites (water/electric/sewer) near the stable/Walker Arena complexes; showers in fee. Takes transient nightly stays for non-event guests but capacity limited by show schedules — call ahead. General campground, not horse-only. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 2 reviews. --Claude

name: Hester's Ferry Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.9333,-82.5320: peninsula loop pads (one with parked RV) on open sandy apron straight to Strom Thurmond waterline, no buffer
note: USACE Savannah District campground on J. Strom Thurmond (Clarks Hill) Lake at Fishing Creek. 16 RV sites with water + electric (30-amp, some 50-amp), no sewer (dump station); separate loop of 10 primitive sites. Reservable online (ReserveAmerica), ~$18-37/night. Federal land. RV Life 5★/$$, 4 reviews. --Claude

name: Hiawassee RV Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.9635,-83.7933: RV lot S of US-76, set back; Lake Chatuge ~250m N across highway and forest
note: Private RV park at 2068 US-76, Hiawassee (near Lake Chatuge but no on-site water frontage). ~140 full-hookup sites (30/50-amp water/sewer), seasonal heated pool, rec hall, laundry; nightly transient ~$50 plus monthly. Open to public, currently operating. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 3 reviews. --Claude

name: Hidden Forest RV Club
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.0921,-82.3102: rural fields/forest with scattered RVs, no water body present
note: Private RV park (not a membership club despite the name) on 48 wooded acres at 760 Robert Poole Rd, Lyons. Big-rig-friendly 65x45 full-hookup pull-through lots (30/50/20-amp, water, sewer); nightly transient from ~$45. Ohoopee River ~10 min away (not on water). Open to public, operating. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 8 reviews. --Claude

name: High Shoals Falls RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.8284,-83.7309: small roadside lot, no significant water body near pads
note: Private boutique RV park (formerly Appalachian Campground) at 1504 Moody Hollow Rd, Hiawassee, on the headwaters of the Hiwassee River. 16 riverfront sites with private wood decks, 4 pull-through sites, plus 3 cabins; big-rig friendly, full hookups, online booking. Under new management 2024, currently operating. RV Life 5★/$$, 11 reviews. --Claude

name: Hippie Hollow Campground

waterfront_evidence: satellite at 31.4275,-81.6065: developed boat-basin RV park, riverfront full-hookup rows sit behind a vegetated tree fringe/island fronting the basin channel with floating docks for access (marina-park case); river overlook but no open apron at waterline
note: Operated by Glynn County Recreation & Parks. ~56 campsites incl. full-hookup and riverfront full-hookup RV sites with concrete pads, on the Altamaha River; boat ramps, camp store, bath houses. Reservations via Park Store/phone (no online booking). RV Life 2.5★/$$, 8 reviews. --Claude

name: Andersonville City Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.1952,-84.1426: in-town park surrounded by residential lots, no lake or river in frame
note: Operated by the City of Andersonville. ~25 sites, paved pads, full hookups (30-amp), bath house; wooded setting beside the historic village, near Andersonville NHS. Reservable; nightly/weekly/monthly rates. RV Life 4★/$$, 47 reviews. --Claude

name: Arrowhead Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.8205,-83.8005: RV pads/loops run to the Lake Tobesofkee shoreline along entire eastern and southern edge, multiple pads with spur docks at the waterline
note: Macon-Bibb County Lake Tobesofkee Recreation Area (Arrowhead Park). Full-hookup pull-through and W/E sites, spacious level pads, dump station, beach. Reservable by phone up to 60 days out; $22-25/night, weekend 2-night minimum. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 91 reviews. --Claude

name: Bainbridge Riverview Campground (Earle May Boat Basin)
waterfront_evidence: operator page: sites 3,4,7 'sit on high bank above the Flint River' with river views/kayak access, most sites canopied; satellite at 30.8972,-84.6002 shows loop set back behind a continuous riverbank treeline on a high bank (bench above river, not walk-across apron)
note: Operated by the City of Bainbridge at Earle May Boat Basin Recreation Area on the Flint River. 12 shaded sites (back-in and pull-through), water/electric, restrooms/showers, dump station; sites 3,4,7 have river views/access. Online reservations required (parkwith.us), pick site number; $35/night. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 6 reviews. --Claude

name: Barrington County Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 31.4712,-81.6070: campground loop/parking sits ~40-60m up the bank behind a continuous treeline screen along the Altamaha; river access only via two docks, no sites on open shoreline
note: Operated by McIntosh County. Recently renovated and now open (county page) after a 2024 construction closure; formalized tent and RV sites (back-in and pull-through) with water, electric, concrete pad, fire ring; boat ramp, dock on Harpers Lake off the Altamaha River. Reservable via Firefly. RV Life 0★ (unrated) — confirmed operating/specs via McIntosh County official page. --Claude

name: Ben Hill Landing County Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 31.7744,-83.0086: primitive county landing on a wooded bluff above the Ocmulgee River, continuous treeline along the bank, sites set back inland among trees with no open shoreline frontage
note: Operated by Ben Hill County on the Ocmulgee River. 35 sites total: 15 with water/electric (mostly 30-amp, some 50-amp) plus 20 primitive; boat launch. $20/night W/E, $15 primitive. RV Life 0★ (unrated) — county/state-tourism listing confirms drive-in RV W/E sites and operation. --Claude

name: Broad River Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.9760,-82.6300: campground loop under dense canopy on a Clarks Hill Lake peninsula; shore is a day-use boat ramp/dock area, no RV pads resolvable at waterline; gafreshwatercamping page has no per-site map
note: Lincoln County Parks & Rec campground on Clarks Hill/J. Strom Thurmond Lake (USACE-built, now county-managed) at the Broad River/Savannah River confluence; booked via Georgia Freshwater Campgrounds concessionaire (county land run by concessionaire = local). 31 sites (22 single, 7 double, 2 triple), all water/electric hookups, bathhouse, dump station, boat ramp. Reservable online (Firefly). Remained open after Hurricane Helene. RV Life 4★/$$, 3 reviews. --Claude

name: Brush Creek Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.2065,-85.082: Lake Wedowee river-arm; loops/sites set back among trees with a continuous treeline/bank buffer along the water, no pads at waterline; franklingeorgia page has no per-site map
note: Heard County recreation area on West Point Lake. 34 campsites, 30-amp electric + water (no sewer), dump station, bathhouses, boat ramp/direct lake access, ballfields/playground. Large sites (30+ yds apart). $25/night. Confirmed operating (county page + 2025 reviews). RV Life 0★ (unrated)/$, 0 reviews. --Claude

name: Cain's Creekside RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 31.0095,-83.379: rows of RV pads directly along the edges of two ponds with open grass apron to the water (east and center ponds)
note: Private RV park in Lowndes County at 6143 US Hwy 41 N, Hahira (sold by original owners Feb 2023). ~30 sites, 20/30/50-amp full hookups, bathhouse; reviewer parked a 42-ft 5th wheel so 23-ft fine. Some long-term/monthly but takes nightly/transient. Reservable (Campspot/Hipcamp). RV Life 4★/$$, 125 reviews. --Claude

name: Chatuge Woods Campground
waterfront_evidence: pin was on Young Harris College campus (mis-pin); real campground at 34.9830,-83.8055 on a Lake Chatuge peninsula, satellite shows numerous RVs at the shoreline with open ground to the water
note: Towns County-operated 30-acre campground directly on Lake Chatuge (2307 Red Banks Dr, Young Harris). 100+ sites, all water/electric (30-amp), 2 dump stations, 2 bathhouses, picnic shelter, adjacent boat ramp. Open Apr-Oct; reservable. Many sites have Blue Ridge views. RV Life 5★ but only 2 reviews (RV Life price level looks anomalous for a county park). --Claude

name: Clark Creek North Campground
waterfront_evidence: official USACE Clark Creek North campground map (allatoonalake.org PDF): legend '*Premium/Waterfront Sites', nearly all sites *1-*24 flagged waterfront on the Allatoona Lake peninsula shore; canopy/low-pool satellite
note: USACE (Lake Allatoona) campground via Glade Rd, Acworth; run by a private contractor for the Corps. 24 premium sites, all 50-amp electric + water, hot showers, laundry, dump station. Pads ~25-40 ft (back-in) — fits 23 ft. Reservable on recreation.gov plus some FCFS. Open mid-May to early Sept. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 34 reviews. --Claude

name: Clark Creek South Campground
waterfront_evidence: official Bartow County Clark Creek South campground map: peninsula loop fully ringed by Allatoona Lake, RV sites (1-5, 9-14, 32-40) front the shoreline; canopy/low-pool satellite
note: Operated by Bartow County Parks & Recreation on USACE Lake Allatoona land (county runs camping, books via county system, not recreation.gov). 40 sites: 18 electric/water ($34) + 22 primitive ($22), max ~40 ft. Bathhouse, dump station, boat ramp, swim beach. Season Mar 1-Oct 31; reservable online, 2-night min Fri/Sat. RV Life 4★/$$, 19 reviews. --Claude

name: Diamond Lakes Regional Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: official Augusta Diamond Lakes Campground layout: all 26 sites set back on green land across a footpath/buffer from the two park ponds, no pads front the water; imagery predates 2018 campground
note: Operated by Augusta-Richmond County (Recreation/Parks) in 286-acre Diamond Lakes Regional Park. 25 full-hookup sites (50-amp, 2 pull-thru), accepts RVs up to 45 ft. Reservable via ReserveAmerica or county site; 30-day max. Park has 5 small fishing ponds; Spirit Creek along south border. RV Life 4★/$$, 47 reviews. --Claude

name: Euharlee Creek Campground at Frankie Harris Park
waterfront_evidence: new 26-site paved campground not yet in satellite imagery; only marketing 'creek access' prose (forbidden), no per-site map; visible park-to-Euharlee-Creek interface is wooded with a treeline buffer along the bank; default down
note: City of Euharlee-run campground at Frankie Harris Park, 112 Covered Bridge Rd. 26 paved back-in full-hookup sites (30/50-amp, water, sewer, WiFi), accepts larger RVs. Reservable via Campspot; $55-65/night. General store, bathhouse, laundry; on Euharlee Creek near the historic covered bridge. RV Life 4.5★/$$$, 24 reviews. --Claude

name: Evans County Public Fishing Area Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.1305,-81.7925: RV sites along entrance road on east shore, set back ~50-80m behind a treeline from the lake with a dock/pier at the shore; lake visible but sites screened from open shore; pin was on the dam
note: Owned/operated by Georgia DNR Wildlife Resources (Public Fishing Area), not a county park — reclassified state. 14 full-size RV/tent sites with electric+water (5 premium with full sewer + pull-thru) plus 8 tent-only; reservable via Go Outdoors Georgia. 372-acre PFA with 3 lakes, event center off unpaved Area Line Rd. RV Life 5★/$$, 28 reviews. --Claude

name: Falling Rocks Campground & RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 31.940,-82.371: RV/cabin development set back from the Altamaha River behind a continuous treeline buffer; only an interior pond near the sites, no RV pads on the river
note: Operated by Appling County Board of Commissioners near the Altamaha River, ~2 mi from Plant Hatch. 14 RV sites with electric+water (20/30/50-amp), 4 tent sites; tables, showers, laundry, playground. $21/night (weekly/monthly also). Mix of paddling visitors and Plant Hatch outage workers but takes nightly transients; book via county clerk. RV Life 0★ (unrated)/$ — confirmed via Appling County page + allstays. --Claude

name: Gatewood Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.176,-84.724 (Lake Allatoona): pin on brushy peninsula; 58-pad campground not resolvable in imagery and no shoreline RV apron confirmed; lake is fragmented coves, sites/buildings set well back; default down
note: Bartow County Parks & Recreation campground on a Lake Allatoona peninsula, 224 Bartow Beach Rd SE. ~58 powered sites (water, concrete pad, table, grill) + 12 primitive; per official map sites range 20-40 ft so 23-ft rigs fit (reviews warn of narrow, hilly loop roads). Bathhouse, boat ramp, swim beach; reservable via county. Seasonal (~Apr-Oct). RV Life 4.5★/$$, 10 reviews. --Claude

name: Jim R. Miller Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.902,-84.591: Cobb County event park (ballfields, amphitheater, parking lots); only a small treelined pond; RV camping is event-parking, not on water
note: Cobb County Parks & Rec event-center park (2245 Callaway Rd SW, Marietta). 44 full-hookup RV sites (water/sewer/electric), flat grassy terrain, bathhouse. FCFS drive-up — pick a site and pay at the office; camping runs alongside park events but general overnight RV camping is available. 23-ft rig fits easily. RV Life 4★/$, 7 reviews. --Claude

name: John Tanner Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.602,-85.168: RV sites set back in woods ~150-250m from the lake; the lakeshore here is a day-use swim beach, no RV pads on open shore
note: Carroll County park (former John Tanner State Park, county-run since 2010; 354 Tanner Beach Rd, Carrollton). 31-32 gravel RV sites, water/electric/cable, pull-thru + back-in, pads 15x50 to 15x40 (fits 23 ft). Reservable by phone. Two lakes (12 & 16 acre) with large sand beach. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 42 reviews. --Claude

name: Lakeside RV Camps
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.354,-82.164: small private pond to the north; RV development set back south across open fields with the pond screened; no confirmed shoreline RV pads
note: Private family-run park (Boatright family farm, opened to RV camping 2017; 19751 Eden Church Rd, Cobbtown). ~9-19 full-hookup sites overlooking Boatright Lake; takes nightly/weekly transient plus monthly. Currently operating. RV Life 0★ (unrated)/$, 0 reviews — confirmed operating + nightly transient + full hookups from operator site; max RV length not published. --Claude

name: Little Tallapoosa Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.645,-85.051 (lake on west): the lakeside developed area is a day-use beach/pavilion; the 23-site RV loop is not resolvable on the water and official page does not place sites lakeside; default down
note: Carroll County park (256 acres, 1930 Hwy 113 N, Carrollton). 23 full-hookup RV sites (30/50 amp, water/sewer), paved level pads, several pull-thrus, plus 32 electric tent/pop-up sites. Reservable by phone; 14-night max. Park bisected by the Little Tallapoosa River; creek and two fishing ponds. RV Life 5★/$$, 129 reviews. --Claude

name: Lock & Dam Park (Coosa River Campground)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.199,-85.256: campground loop with sites overlooking the Coosa River but a vegetated bank/treeline buffer runs along the shore; nearest sites ~20-40m back behind the bank, not an open apron
note: Rome-Floyd County Parks & Recreation Authority (regional authority; 181 Lock & Dam Rd SW, Rome). 31-33 RV sites on open terrain adjacent to the Coosa River, full hookups (power/water, select sites direct sewer), RVs up to 48 ft, bathhouse/laundry. Year-round; boat ramp, trading post. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 37 reviews. --Claude

name: McIntosh Reserve Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.4424,-84.9626: primitive open-field RV camping on Chattahoochee floodplain, but a continuous riparian treeline screens the dispersed field sites from the river; sites set back
note: Carroll County park (527+ acres, 1046 W McIntosh Cir, Whitesburg) with frontage on the Chattahoochee River. 50+ primitive drive-in campsites — no hookups, no dump station; self-contained RVs/pull-behinds allowed, generators OK. $20/night, 5-night max; register in person/by phone (largely FCFS, group sites reserved). Open ground fits a 23-ft rig. RV Life 4★/$, 2 reviews. --Claude

name: Oconee Springs Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.2879,-83.1993: RV loop set back behind a developed marina strip (boat ramp/docks/buildings) on Lake Sinclair; sites overlook lake across open lawn but immediate shore is developed, not a camper apron
note: Putnam County (GA)-owned 12-acre park on Lake Sinclair, run as a commercial campground/marina by a concessionaire (county land, so local). RV sites with 30/50-amp electric, water, dump station; beach, marina, boat ramp, camp store. Reservable (1-night deposit), 14-day max. ~$65/night. RV Life 4.5★/$$$, 3 reviews. --Claude

name: Old Hwy 41 #3 Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site data: sites 39/40/41 'Proximity to Water: Lakefront' on Lake Allatoona; satellite at 34.0935,-84.7108 shows shoreline camp loop (canopy-partial); pin was offset ~500m S
note: US Army Corps of Engineers campground on Allatoona Lake (Acworth/Emerson). 44 sites (37 single, 6 double), all 50-amp electric + water; premium sites open to the lakeshore. Showers, laundry. Reservable on recreation.gov plus some FCFS. Open ~mid-May to early Sep. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 26 reviews. --Claude

name: Paynes Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov per-site data: sites 17/26/01/34/22 all 'Lakefront' (37 of 44 waterfront) on Tugaloo arm of Lake Hartwell; original pin landed on I-85; loop at 34.475,-82.978 (canopy-blind satellite, per-site map authoritative)
note: Hart County (GA)-operated since Jan 2021 (formerly USACE) on Hartwell Lake's Tugaloo arm. 44 sites with full water + 50-amp electric, ~37 waterfront historically; dump station, swim beach, boat ramp, SORBA bike trail. Reservable online. Open Mar 1 - Oct 31. RV Life 4★/$$, 36 reviews. --Claude

name: Poteete Creek Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 34.9483,-84.0947: RV pads (white campers) sit at the red-clay/grass shoreline of Lake Nottely with open unobstructed aprons to the water on the lower-right loop
note: Union County (GA) park on Lake Nottely, 13 mi N of Blairsville. ~88 sites (RV + tent), power + water at most sites, central dump station, swim beach, boat ramp, pavilion, bathhouse. Reservations recommended. Open Apr 1 - Oct 15. RV Life 4★/$$, 3 reviews. --Claude

name: Pyne Road Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: Troup County official: waterfront sites are even-numbered, 'some right on the water's edge'; firsthand reviews place sites 8 and 13 lakeside on West Point Lake; satellite of wooded loop canopy/low-detail (illegible, does not downgrade)
note: Troup County (GA) Recreation park on West Point Lake, LaGrange. 22 tent/RV sites - some primitive, some with electric + water, some on the water's edge (even-numbered); boat ramp w/ 24-hr attendant, dump station, flush toilets, trails. Open Mar-Nov, 14-day max. RV Life 0★ (unrated)/$ — operation confirmed via Troup County Rec + visitor sources. --Claude

name: Raysville Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 33.6335,-82.4675: campground shore on Clarks Hill/Thurmond Lake has open grassy/sandy camper frontage to the water plus lakefront wooded sites; operator confirms many sites on the water; original pin was ~700m SW in woods
