note: McDuffie County (GA)-operated (formerly USACE) on the Little River arm of Clarks Hill / J. Strom Thurmond Lake, ~9 mi from Thomson. All 55 sites 50/30/15-amp electric + water; bathhouses, dump station, boat ramp. Reservable online year-round, 30-day max. RV Life 5★/$$, 6 reviews. --Claude

name: Riverbend County Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.2439,-84.9109: loop sites on open peninsula sit back from Walter F. George/Chattahoochee shore behind a brushy buffer band; water visible across open ground but no clean waterline apron
note: Stewart County park on the Chattahoochee River near Lumpkin. ~10 primitive drive-in sites, no hookups (dry camping); pit toilets, picnic table + fire ring per site. FCFS, $5/night honor-system drop box. Drive-in accessible (long gravel drive off US-27); firsthand reviews report RVs up to ~25 ft fit. RV Life 0★ (unrated), 0 reviews; confirmed via Campendium firsthand reviews. --Claude

name: Salacoa Creek Park
waterfront_evidence: Gordon County official park map: numbered RV campground (sites 1-35) is the inland loop ~0.5mi NE of Salacoa Lake; satellite at 34.5319,-84.7591 confirms pin on that inland loop, lake well to south
note: Gordon County park at 388 Park Dr NE, Ranger, on a 126-acre lake. 32 RV sites with 30-amp electric, water, picnic table, fire ring, deck + dump station; also 25 primitive sites. Reservable (county rec1.com booking), $28/night, 14-day max. Seasonal: first weekend Apr to first weekend Nov. RV Life 3.5★/$, 7 reviews. --Claude

name: Sprewell Bluff Park, River Ridge Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.8512,-84.4765: camp clearing/road strip sits behind a continuous treeline screening it from the Flint River (~60-80m, no open apron, water not visible across open ground)
note: Upson County park on the Flint River, ~10 mi W of Thomaston (700 Sprewell Bluff Rd). River Ridge Campground has 6 RV sites with 30/50-amp electric + water, fire ring, picnic table (most back-in, overlooking the river); also cabins/primitive sites + bathhouse. Reservable via ReserveAmerica; $5 park entry. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 9 reviews. --Claude

name: Tally Valley County Park
waterfront_evidence: pin lands on industrial complex on Hwy 78, not a campground; could not resolve camp loops in surrounding woods on satellite; n-georgia.com directory says 'in the quiet woods, fishing nearby,' not lakeside; no water adjacency anywhere near
note: Haralson County park at 3025 Hwy 78 E, Tallapoosa, in the woods. ~40 RV sites with full/water+electric hookups (30-amp) + pull-thrus, bathhouse, dump station, pool; gold panning on site. Call to reserve, $16/night + tax (cash/check). Seasonal: Apr 15 to Dec 1. RV Life 4★/$$$, 1 review. --Claude

name: Traders Hill County Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.7807,-82.0267: RV campground loops set back in forest ~250m+ west of the St. Marys River channel; only a sandy boat-ramp access at the river, sites inland
note: Charlton County recreation area on the St. Marys River near Folkston, by the Okefenokee NWR. ~7 RV sites with electric + water hookups (plus 7 primitive tent sites); bathhouse, boat ramp, fishing pier. Drive-in, flat open/lightly-wooded sites. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 5 reviews. --Claude

name: Whitewater Creek County Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 32.3511,-84.0701: RV loops set back in trees behind an open day-use/picnic field; nearest open-water channels of the Whitewater Creek marsh impoundment are >100m across that used field, water at the loops is marsh vegetation
note: Macon County park (482 acres) at 165 Whitewater Rd, Oglethorpe, on Whitewater Creek. 29 RV sites — 16 full-hookup + 13 electric-only (all with electric+water), 50-amp, sewer, dump station; bathhouse, boat launch. Open year-round; reservable by phone. RV Life 4.5★/$$, 5 reviews. --Claude

name: Stephen Foster Folk Culture Center State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.3332,-82.7717: RV/camping loops set back several hundred m N of the Suwannee River behind continuous forest; river only reaches frame edge
note: 45 full-hookup sites (W/E), pull-throughs to ~100 ft. Reservable online via ReserveFlorida. RV Life 4.5★/$$.

name: Silver Springs State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.1971,-82.0354: heavily wooded camping loops, no water anywhere in ~700m frame; Silver River head spring ~1 mi away
note: 59 sites in Fort King & Sharpes Ferry loops, all W/E (12 full hookup), max RV ~50 ft. Reservable online via ReserveFlorida. RV Life 5★/$$.

name: Manatee Springs State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.4870,-82.9762: camping loops (incl. Magnolia) set back behind a wide continuous forest/wetland band from the Suwannee River and spring run; only a boardwalk reaches the water
note: ~80 sites across Magnolia 1/2 and Hickory loops, max RV ~40 ft. Reservable online via ReserveFlorida. RV Life 4★/$$.

name: Florida Caverns State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.8198,-85.2426: RV loop on upland; spring run/stream SW of loop screened by forest, no pads on a waterline; Blue Hole is a short walk
note: Blue Hole family campground: 38 sites (32 full-hookup RV + 3 walk-in tent), max RV ~32-40 ft. Separate equestrian camp is NOT this loop. Reservable online via ReserveFlorida. RV Life 4.5★/$$.

name: O'Leno State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.9132,-82.5829: camping loops set back ~150-200m behind a continuous treeline from the Santa Fe River channel; river not visible from loop, vegetation buffer noted in lead
note: Magnolia (~27) and Dogwood (~26) loops, 30/50-amp W/E, max RV ~50 ft (narrow roads). Reservable online via ReserveFlorida. RV Life 4★/$$. Coords pinned to Magnolia loop (river-closest).

name: Blackwater River State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.7095,-86.8765 + reviews: 30 FHU stoned pads set back ~150m behind forest; white-sand river beaches (incl. by sites 16/21) reached by on-site nature trails, not pads on the water
note: 30 gravel full-hookup sites in two loops, max RV ~50 ft. Reservable online via ReserveFlorida. RV Life 5★/$$.

name: Falling Waters State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.7309,-85.5329: RV loop on upland pine ridge; Falling Waters Lake ~0.4 mi S at frame bottom, separated by continuous forest
note: Pine Ridge campground, 24 sites all W/E (sites 1-7,16-21 add sewer), max RV ~40 ft, 5 pull-throughs. Reservable online via ReserveFlorida. RV Life 4.5★/$$.

name: Fred Gannon Rocky Bayou State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.5005,-86.4268 + reviews: original pin was residential/highway; campground loop fronts Rocky Bayou with sites 29-36 confirmed bayou views, but a continuous shoreline treeline screens pads (no open apron at waterline) -> bayview not bayfront
note: ~42 sites on the north shore of Rocky Bayou, max RV ~50 ft, 4 ADA. Reservable online via ReserveFlorida. RV Life 5★/$. Coord approximate — refine loop on satellite.

name: Ochlockonee River State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.00,-84.48: campground sits in pine flatwoods ~200-300m NW of the Ochlockonee River behind a continuous treed buffer; no shoreline RV pads
note: Single Loop A, 30 full-facility 50-amp sites, max RV ~40 ft. Reservable online via ReserveFlorida. RV Life 4.5★/$$.

name: Torreya State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.57,-84.95: small wooded campground loop on the bluff, no water near loop; Apalachicola River ~150ft below down a treed bluff (climb-down, not walk-across)
note: Weeping Ridge Loop, 30 sites (1-16 30-amp, 17-30 50-amp) all W/E, plus yurt/cabin; big-rig capable despite winding entrance. Reservable online via ReserveFlorida. RV Life 4.5★/$.

name: Ruth B. Kirby Gilchrist Blue Springs State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.83,-82.68: RV loop sits inland in pine woods ~200m+ from the spring run, behind the day-use core; no shoreline RV sites
note: ~23 sites; 16-17 RV/tent sites with 30A electric + water, no sewer/dump, driveways ~40 ft. Distinct from Orange City's Blue Spring SP. Reservable via ReserveAmerica (parkId 284900). Spring/swim closed for construction as of late 2025; camping open. RV Life 3★/$$.

name: Santos Trailhead & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.10,-82.09: campground loop in dry uplands, no water body anywhere near
note: On the Marjorie Harris Carr Cross Florida Greenway (state Office of Greenways & Trails). 23 sites, W/E 30/50A, dump station, showers; big-rig capable. Reservable via ReserveAmerica (parkId 282840). RV Life 4.5★/$$.

name: Indian Lake State Forest
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.27,-82.05: RV sites set back in pine woods ~150m N of Indian Lake behind a treed buffer; sandy lake shore has no RV pads at the waterline
note: Florida Forest Service. 13 sites; sites 6-11 RV/Trailer with W/E hookups, plus tent sites + host. Reservable via Florida State Forests ReserveAmerica (parkId 1121200). RV Life 5★/$.

name: Sand Pond Campground (Pine Log State Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.43,-85.88: campsites ring the N/NW shore of Sand Pond with an open grassy/sandy apron running unobstructed to the waterline within a site-depth; FDACS confirms sites on the pond shores
note: Florida Forest Service (Florida's first state forest). 20 sites all W/E (30/50A), no sewer; dump station, showers, fishing pier. Max length ~23-42 ft. Reservable via Florida State Forests ReserveAmerica (parkId 1120116). RV Life 4.5★/$$.

name: River Road Campground (Lake George State Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.13,-81.50: small sandy loop in scrub flatwoods, no adjacent water; St. Johns/Lake George ~0.75mi away; ReserveAmerica flags all sites Near Water: no
note: Florida Forest Service. 10 drive-in sites, pads ~12x60 ft (tents or RVs), no hookups/dump; communal pump + portalet, bear lockers, gate-code access. Reservable via Florida State Forests ReserveAmerica (parkId 1120152), ~$9/night. RV Life unrated. May flood after heavy rain.

name: Bear Lake Campground (Blackwater River State Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.86,-86.83: RV pads/loops set back ~100m+ behind a treeline; the Bear Lake frontage is a day-use parking lot/boat ramp, not RV sites
note: Florida Forest Service. 39 sites; 31 RV/tent with 30/50A electric + water, 8 tent-only; max lengths 25-93 ft. Boat ramp (electric motors only). Reservable via Florida State Forests ReserveAmerica (parkId 1120101). RV Life 4.5★/$$.

name: Krul Campground (Blackwater River State Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.8623,-86.8522: Krul Lake shore is a day-use swim beach + dock + parking lot; RV camp loops set back uphill in forest, no pads at waterline
note: Florida Forest Service. 43 RV/tent sites, all 30/50A electric + water; max lengths 29-68 ft. Swimming dock + pier. No pets. Camping reservation required via Florida State Forests ReserveAmerica (parkId 1120113); day passes not valid for camping entry. Separate from Bear Lake (~1.3 mi away). RV Life 4.5★/$.

name: Hurricane Lake North Campground (Blackwater River State Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.9445,-86.7546: peninsula loop, RV pads with open unused grassy/sandy apron (~30-40m) to Hurricane Lake shore within bound; ReserveAmerica flags sites 012-016 'Near Water'
note: Florida Forest Service. ~17-18 RV/tent sites, 30/50A electric + water (a few no water); max lengths to 75 ft. Boat ramp; alligators (no swimming). Reservable via Florida State Forests ReserveAmerica (parkId 1120105). RV Life 4.5★/$$.

name: Karick Lake North Campground (Blackwater River State Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.8975,-86.6404: RV sites front an open grassy apron with scattered (non-screening) trees running to Karick Lake within ~30-50m; ReserveAmerica flags ~8/15 sites waterfront
note: Florida Forest Service. ~15 RV/tent sites, all 30/50A electric + water; max lengths 30-60 ft. Boat ramp + pier. Reservable via Florida State Forests ReserveAmerica (parkId 1120109). RV Life mislabeled this 'Karick Lake Upper'/'North Karick Lake'. RV Life 4.5★/$.

name: Karick Lake South Campground (Blackwater River State Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.8939,-86.6446: wooded peninsula loop sits behind a pine fringe along the shore (continuous-ish treed screen, no clean open apron); water visible/overlooked through trees so at least view
note: Florida Forest Service. ~14 RV/tent sites, all 30/50A electric + water; max lengths 38-65 ft. Boat ramp + pier (electric motors only). South end of Karick Lake, separate from Karick Lake North. Reservable via Florida State Forests ReserveAmerica (parkId 1120110).

name: Coldwater Equestrian Campground (Blackwater River State Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.8469,-86.9811: equestrian camp with stables/open paddocks, no water in frame; Coldwater Creek not adjacent to pads
note: Florida Forest Service. General drive-in RV campground that also offers equestrian facilities (horses only in corrals/trails, not at sites — non-horse RVers welcome). ~54-55 RV/tent sites + 1 tent-only, all 30/50A electric + water; max lengths 24-60 ft. Reservable via Florida State Forests ReserveAmerica (parkId 1120104). RV Life 4.5★/$$.

name: High Bluff Campground (Lake Talquin State Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.4617,-84.4973: dense forest atop bluff, fishing pier between camp and Lake Talquin; per-site map (lead) shows all 19 sites on interior loops, reservation system lists 'No waterfront sites'
note: Florida Forest Service. 19 tent/RV sites, driveways 24-36 ft (fits 23 ft), primitive (no hookups), vault restroom, fishing pier, boat ramp. Reservable via Florida State Forests ReserveAmerica (parkId 1120121), ~$9-16/night. RV Life 4.5★/$.

name: Cash Creek Campground (Tate's Hell State Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.8161,-84.8469: small loop set ~300-400m back in pine flatwoods, Cash Creek tidal channel and shared boat ramp/dock far to the NW across road and woods
note: Florida Forest Service. 13 RV/tent sites (10 electric 30/50A + water, 3 non-electric); max length 50 ft electric / 25 ft non-electric. Showers, pavilion, boat ramp + dock; recently upgraded (paved drive-in). Reservable via Florida State Forests ReserveAmerica (parkId 1121250). RV Life 4★/$.

name: County Line OHV Campground (Tate's Hell State Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.0113,-84.6163: upland pine flatwoods/scrub OHV area, no water anywhere in frame
note: Florida Forest Service. General drive-in RV/tent camping allowed (OHV permit applies only to riding trails, not camping). 3 sites (#19/20/21), drive-in, max lengths 72-76 ft, no hookups; vault restroom. Access roads rough but passable. Reservable via Florida State Forests ReserveAmerica (parkId 1120128). RV Life unrated.

name: Escribano Point Bayside Campground (Escribano Point WMA)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.5162,-87.0069: open sandy camp clearing with structure and dock directly on East Bay shore, vehicle/dirt road leading in, unobstructed apron to waterline
note: FWC (Escribano Point Tract, Yellow River Marsh WMA). 12 primitive sites, FWC says 'suitable for tents, campers, or small RVs'; some fit a 23-ft rig (firsthand up to ~40 ft reached it). No hookups. Last ~4 mi is unpaved clay with soft spots — passable by careful 2WD in dry weather, not 4WD-required; use the hard-packed red road by Choctaw Field, ignore Google Maps routing. Free FWC permit/reservation required (WebReserv or phone). RV Life 5★/$.

name: Myakka River State Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.2452,-82.3141: campground loops sit inland in forest/burned-field, set back well over 200m from Upper Myakka Lake/river; no shoreline RV sites
note: Large FL State Park drive-in campground with 90 sites across three loops (Big Flats sites 1-26 and Old Prairie 27-48 are water/electric; Palmetto Ridge 49-90 has 42 full-hookup gravel pads). Max RV length 50 ft. Reservable online up to 11 months ahead. RV Life 4.5★/$$.

name: Livingston Creek Campground (Lake Wales Ridge State Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.70587,-81.44571: two small loops with pavilion set in forest with open field to south; Livingston Creek drainage screened by treeline and distant, no shoreline RV frontage
note: Florida Forest Service hunt-camp-style campground on the Arbuckle tract; 7 back-in RV/tent sites (pads 12x60 ft, Class B/C up to 32 ft), each with picnic table and fire ring. Drive-in via paved Rucks Dairy Rd; open year-round. Reservable online (ReserveAmerica/FLFS). No potable water - bring your own. RV Life unrated, $.

name: Okaloacoochee Slough State Forest - Overflow Camp
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 26.54311,-81.29368: small roadside camp clearings amid wet-prairie/slough; no open-water body with apron, slough is vegetated marsh
note: Florida Forest Service state forest. 6 drive-in gravel sites (max equipment 40 ft) off Keri Road, no hookups, generators OK; $9.09/night, reservable online up to 11 months out. RV Life 4★/$.

name: Okaloacoochee Slough State Forest - Panther Pond Primitive Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 26.55361,-81.29676: camp clearing along forest road; 'Panther Pond' to NE is a vegetated marsh/cypress depression not open water, no RV pad on shoreline apron
note: Florida Forest Service state forest. 16 primitive sites (15 tent-only + site 16 RV/tent to 50 ft), drive-in via Keri Road, no hookups, fire rings/picnic tables/portable restroom; $9.09/night, reservable online. RV-fitting site available. RV Life 4★/$.

name: Dinner Island Ranch WMA - Hammock Camp
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 26.49940,-81.13083: open grassy field camp with scattered pads; small farm ponds 150m+ away across field, no shoreline frontage
note: FWC Wildlife Management Area. Hammock Camp: 17 sites under an oak hammock, 10 for tents/campers/small RVs (1 ADA), vault toilet, no other amenities; free permit required (reserve online or by phone, up to 14 days out). Gravel access road off CR 833 (short, washboardy, can get muddy after heavy rain). Best dry season Nov-Apr. RV Life 4.5★.

name: Dinner Island Ranch WMA - Kowechobe Camp
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 26.49791,-81.12956: open pasture/prairie camp with scattered pads; small pond near road junction distant from camp field, no RV site on water
note: FWC Wildlife Management Area. Kowechobe Camp: 20 sites in an open field (40x50 ft each), tents/campers/RVs; primitive, no amenities; free permit (reserve online or by phone). Easy trailer access in dry season (Nov-Apr); may flood and require 4x4 in summer. RV Life 4.5★.

name: Big Scrub Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.0507,-81.7561: campground loops in dry scrub forest, no water within 700m frame
note: USFS Ocala NF campground in the Big Scrub uplands; 46 sites (38 standard, 8 tent-only), no hookups, tents/campers/RVs welcome, showers/restrooms/potable water. Reservable online. $20/night. 14-day stay limit. Seasonal gate hours; verify open status.

name: Lake Dorr Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.0125,-81.6400: FS camping loop set ~350m+ west of Lake Dorr behind continuous forest; lake not in 700m camp frame; shoreline is a separate boat-ramp/swim beach
note: USFS Ocala NF campground on the NW shore of 1,760-acre Lake Dorr; 34 standard non-electric sites for tents/RVs, no hookups, boat ramp, flush toilets/showers/potable water. Reservable online. 14-day stay limit.

name: Lake Delancy West Campground
waterfront_evidence: rec.gov 10207758: dispersed tent/small-trailer sites in pine forest, no per-site shoreline flag; satellite at 29.4269,-81.7894 shows lake as low marsh/dry bed with no RV pads at a waterline
note: USFS Ocala NF OHV staging campground on the W shore of Lake Delancy; 30 sites (tent and small-trailer) along the pines, vault toilets, fire rings, bear lockers, no potable water. Reservable online; $6-10/night. Access to Ocala North OHV trail system and Florida Trail.

name: Wright Lake Campground (Apalachicola National Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.9994,-85.0018: RV loops sit south of Wright Lake behind a continuous treeline; only a small day-use/swim clearing touches the lake, no shoreline RV pads; rec.gov 251908 lists no waterfront sites
note: USFS drive-in campground near Sumatra; 18 nonelectric sites + 1 electric host site, accommodates any size RV, bathhouse with flush toilets and hot showers. Reservable online. Open Oct 1-May 31. RV Life 3.5★.

name: Camel Lake Campground (Apalachicola National Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.2771,-84.9867: RV loop overlooks Camel Lake across an open day-use beach + paved parking strip (~120-150m, lake unscreened) - water visible across open ground but developed day-use strip + distance bar on-water
note: USFS drive-in campground south of Bristol; 10 sites (5 with electric) on ~20x45 ft aprons, suitable for tents or RVs, bathhouse with warm showers and flush toilets, sandy swim beach at the day-use area. Reservable online. RV Life 4.5★.

name: Hickory Landing Campground (Apalachicola National Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.9888,-85.0132: RV sites in pine-forest clearings set ~60-120m back from Owl Creek behind a continuous cypress/hardwood creek-bank buffer; creekside clearing is the boat-ramp/day-use, not RV pads
note: USFS drive-in campground near Sumatra; 10 sites with picnic tables and grills among cypress stands on Owl Creek, suitable for tents or RVs (no hookups), boat ramp with trailer parking. No potable water, no restrooms. FCFS $3/night - not on recreation.gov. RV Life unrated.

name: Mack Landing Campground (Apalachicola National Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.0942,-84.6456: sandy RV sites set ~60-100m back from the Ochlockonee River behind a continuous wooded riverbank; riverside is the boat-ramp/day-use, no open apron from pads to water
note: USFS drive-in campground ~10 mi NW of Sopchoppy on a ridge above the Ochlockonee River; ~10 sites for tents/RVs (no hookups), picnic tables, fire rings, well water, vault toilets, concrete boat ramp. Camp host Oct-Mar. FCFS $3/night - not on recreation.gov. RV Life unrated.

name: Whitehead Landing Campground (Apalachicola National Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.1653,-84.6752: dense hardwood canopy over the landing, narrow tree-lined Ochlockonee tributary; pads unresolvable; FS page says sites are 'under the trees' (prose only, no per-site map/rec.gov flag) so no qualifying shoreline-pad confirmation -> default down
note: USFS drive-in campground on the Ochlockonee River; ~6 sites with picnic tables, metal fire rings, pit toilets, non-potable water spigots, concrete boat ramp. Suitable for tents or RVs (no hookups). FCFS $3/night year-round - not on recreation.gov. RV Life unrated.

name: Porter Lake Campground (Apalachicola National Forest)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.1770,-84.6772: total canopy over the dirt-track landing along a narrow tree-lined river; pads unresolvable, no per-site map/rec.gov flag; reviews/'lake access' prose is forbidden as sole basis -> default down
note: USFS primitive drive-in campground on Porter Lake near the Ochlockonee River basin; a few sites with fire pits, vault toilet, water spigot. Accessible by maintained dirt road (E) / paved road (W); a reviewer fit a 23-ft Class C. FCFS, free; closed during hunting season - not on recreation.gov. RV Life unrated.

name: Ocean Pond Campground (Osceola National Forest)
waterfront_evidence: legible satellite at 30.2394,-82.4360: RV pads sit ~40-60m back from Ocean Pond behind a continuous marsh/cypress treeline screen; rec.gov has no per-site shoreline flag; FS 'lakeside access' prose forbidden as sole basis -> real vegetated buffer, not waterfront
note: USFS campground on the north shore of Ocean Pond near Olustee; 64 sites in Electric (30/50-amp + water), Water-only, and Primitive sections, large parking slips for most RVs, bathhouses with flush toilets and hot showers, boat ramp, swim beach, dump station. Reservable online. RV Life 4★.

name: Long Pine Key Campground
waterfront_evidence: legible satellite at 25.3979,-80.6570: campground loops set back in pinelands; nearest pads ~60-100m+ from the marshy Long Pine Key pond behind a brush fringe and perimeter road -> not waterfront
note: Everglades NP drive-in campground ~7 mi inside the main (Homestead) entrance off the park road; 108 drive-up sites, no hookups, flush toilets/showers/dump station. Open seasonally Nov-May. Reservable through the Flamingo Adventures / Everglades Guest Services concessioner (NOT recreation.gov), with some FCFS sites. RVs up to ~35-45 ft. RV Life 4.5★/$$.

name: Bear Island Campground (Big Cypress National Preserve)
waterfront_evidence: legible satellite at 26.18215,-81.24777: small loop on a dry hammock surrounded by open cypress prairie/marsh sheetflow, no defined waterbody with RV sites on it -> not waterfront
note: Big Cypress backcountry-unit campground at the end of ~20 mi of graded crushed-limestone Turner River Rd (passable by a normal vehicle/camper, no 4WD; washboard/dusty when dry). 40 primitive sites (1-12 year-round; 13-40 Aug 15-Apr 15), no water/hookups, pit toilets, RVs up to 50 ft. $10/night. Reservable online. Note: reported closed as of spring 2026 due to the Hilux Fire - verify reopening before relying on it.

name: Lake Eaton Campground
waterfront_evidence: FS page confirms coords 29.2532,-81.8648 and campground 'cradled by the Big Scrub' with only boat-ramp/pier lake access; satellite shows the loop set back in sand-pine scrub several hundred meters from Lake Eaton's shore (canopy-blind on pads but lake is clearly distant) -> not waterfront
note: USFS Ocala NF campground on 308-acre Lake Eaton; 14 standard non-electric primitive sites, no hookups, boat ramp/pier. Reservable online. Seasonal (roughly Oct-May/Jun). Access via FR 44 is unimproved/rutted with dips, but firsthand reports note 30+ ft rigs camp here; 14-day stay limit.

name: The Glades RV Resort, Golf & Marina
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 26.7777,-81.2288: RV loops sit inland among golf fairways; rim canal/marina well away, no pads fronting water
note: All-ages RV resort, golf course and marina on a Lake Okeechobee rim canal at Moore Haven; full-hookup sites, on-site marina/boat access to the lake. Takes nightly transient stays. RV Life 4.5 star / $$.

name: Okeechobee Landings RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 26.7524,-80.9137: large MH/RV resort ~1mi from Lake Okeechobee; only a small interior decorative pond, sites set back
note: All-ages RV resort at Clewiston, about 1 mi from Lake Okeechobee (not on the lake); full-hookup sites, pool, clubhouse. Takes nightly transient stays. RV Life 4.5 star / $$.

name: Fisheating Creek Outpost
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 26.9374,-81.3181: RV loops inland; Fisheating Creek ~150-250m SW behind dense forest, only tent/canoe area fronts creek
note: Private concessionaire (Cape Leisure) campground in Fisheating Creek WMA at Palmdale; drive-in RV and tent sites, canoe/kayak outfitter on Fisheating Creek. Reservable online. RV Life 4.5 star / $$.

name: Wandering Oaks RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.6824,-87.0537: RV/MH sites in trees off road; only a dry retention pond nearby, no natural water body
note: Family/pet-friendly RV park at 6436 Robie Rd, Milton. Takes daily, weekly, and monthly stays; accommodates Class A/C, fifth wheels, and toy haulers (big-rig friendly). Reservable online via own site. RV Life 5.0 star / $$.

name: River Landing RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.4398,-84.9861: identifiable RV pull-through rows set back ~150m from Apalachicola slough across open grass; immediate frontage is pole barns + boat ramp, water visible
note: Small (10-site, more planned) full-hookup park at 12319 N Central St, Bristol; 30/50-amp, sewer, level rocked pads, $40-60/night. Takes short-term and extended stays (big-rig friendly). Reservable online via Campspot. Described as across from the Apalachicola River. RV Life 5.0 star / $$.

name: Lake Victor RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.9472,-85.8943: RV/MH units overlook Lake Victor through a wooded shore edge with private docks; visible but no open-apron waterline pads
note: 24-site full-hookup park at 1338 Dancy Landing, Westville; big-rig pull-throughs, 50-amp, pool. Open all year, transient. Full-hookup sites adjacent to a fishing lake (Lake Victor). Reservable online via RoverPass. RV Life 5.0 star / $$.

name: Simple Livin Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.9695,-86.3049: cleared RV pad rows by road/ballfields; only a small retention pond, no lake/river adjacent
note: Full-hookup RV park at 37 Bear Bay Flats Rd, Laurel Hill; 30/50-amp, sites accommodate 34-77 ft (big-rig friendly), $30-35/night. Takes nightly/weekly/monthly. No water on site. Reservations by phone. RV Life 5.0 star / $$.

name: In The Garden RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.0533,-83.1612: RV park by highway; only a small cypress/stormwater pond nearby, sites not on it
note: 14 full-hookup sites (30/50-amp) at 895 E Main St (US-27), Mayo; flat with scattered trees, in town. Suwannee River is ~1 mi away, not on site. No standalone reservation site - book by phone/Facebook. RV Life 5.0 star / $.

name: Hitchin Post RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.7484,-85.3905: RV pads line the highway at top, set well back behind woods/open field from the center pond and small lake; no pad on water
note: General RV park (not equestrian-only) at 2492 Dilmore Rd, Cottondale; big-rig sites up to 100 ft, full 20/30/50-amp. Takes overnight and extended stays; ~$45/night, military/senior discounts. Has a private lake and two ponds for fishing. Reservable online. RV Life 4.5 star / $$.

name: Suwannee River Rendezvous Resort & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.0855,-83.0961: dense RV rows sit ~200m+ back from Suwannee River behind a wide forest band; riverbank holds only scattered cabins/lots, no RV pads
note: Riverside RV resort on the Suwannee River at Mayo, with RV sites, cabins, tent sites, pool complex and a natural spring; takes transient/nightly guests (daily RV rates ~$54-80). Reservable online via Campspot. Two areas: Primrose Park (family) and Key West Village (21+). Large sites (40x80+). RV Life 4.5 star / $$.

name: Rocky's Convenience Store & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.0857,-83.6572: RV loop sits beside a small screened retention pond with treeline between pads and water; no open apron to water
note: Roadside RV campground on US-98 at Perry (Taylor County), attached to Rocky's convenience store/cafe; ~37-40 grassy pull-through and back-in sites with full hookups (50/30/20 amp), dump station, laundry, bathhouse, plus 2 primitive tent areas. Popular overnight/scalloping-season stopover; appears FCFS/call-ahead (no online booking confirmed). Official rockyscampground.com SSL cert expired at time of vetting. RV Life 4.5 star / $$.

name: Sunset Isle RV & Yacht Club
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.8458,-84.6684: SW fan-loop RV pads back directly onto Apalachicola Bay tidal marsh with open grassy/sandy apron to the water edge, no treeline/road buffer
note: RV & yacht resort on Timber Island, Carrabelle, on Apalachicola Bay/Gulf with free boat slips, pier, pool, clubhouse. Mixed-use community (some RV/home lots for sale) but confirmed to accept transient/nightly travelers. 39 sites, ~27 full hookups, 50-amp, up to 50 ft. Reservation by request form/phone (no online booking). RV Life 4.5 star / $$.

name: Ingram's Marina
waterfront_evidence: satellite at corrected 30.4220,-84.6240: RV sites sit in interior loops uphill behind the marina store/buildings and treeline; Lake Talquin shore is covered boat docks, not RV pads
note: Marina, RV park and campground on Lake Talquin (354 Lois Lane, Quincy). ~50 RV sites (30/50-amp full hookups, pull-thrus) plus tent sites, RVs to ~40 ft; boat ramp, slips, fish-cleaning station, store, bait. Transient nightly stays (~$42.50/night) reservable online via CampgroundOnline. RV Life 4.5 star / $$.

name: The Captain's Hideaway
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.9015,-83.6294: pin sits in a rural residential lot subdivision with no visible campground and only minor wooded ponds, no significant water near; default down
note: Boutique 4-site RV campground at 15400 Fiddlers Way, Perry (Taylor County, Big Bend). Oversized secluded full-hookup sites with WiFi and a common firepit; max length ~38 ft, tent camping available; takes reservations. Small but a real drive-in RV campground with strong firsthand reviews. No standalone official website (Facebook is the operating page). RV Life 4.5 star / $$.

name: Gulf Breeze RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at corrected 30.4038,-86.9780 (5862 Gulf Breeze Pkwy): inland RV park on N side of US-98 with rows of pads, no adjacent water; Santa Rosa Sound is well south across the highway
note: Small 18-site full-hookup RV park at 5862 Gulf Breeze Pkwy (US-98), Gulf Breeze, set back from Santa Rosa Sound near Pensacola Beach / Navarre Beach. Transient guests accepted; reservations online via ResNexus. RV Life 4.5 star / $$.

name: River's Edge RV Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.6768,-86.7490: RV pad rows sit ~100m+ back from Yellow River behind forest and a cleared boat-ramp area; treeline screens pads from the river, no riverfront pad
note: 79-site full-hookup RV campground at 4001 Log Lake Rd, Holt, on the Yellow River (Okaloosa County). Big-rig friendly, large sites. Boat ramp on the river. Daily/weekly/monthly rates; reserve by phone. Also a Harvest Hosts location. RV Life 4 star / $$.

name: Dead Lakes RV Park & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.1381,-85.1997: RV row on east edge of on-property fishing pond with open grass apron to waterline; pads at pond edge
note: Private RV park at 482 Gary Rowell Rd, Wewahitchka, on the Dead Lakes (distinct from the state-run Dead Lakes Recreation Area). Phase 1 open. Back-in sites 50x24 ft, water/electric (some sewer), Starlink WiFi. Two on-property lakes, fishing piers, boat ramps to Dead Lakes and Apalachicola River, kayak rentals. Transient; reserve online or by phone. RV Life 4 star / $$.

name: Drifters RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.5303,-87.2793: RV lot in highway/commercial corridor, no water adjacent (only a distant retention pond)
note: Veteran-owned 21-site full-hookup RV park at 9110 Untreiner Ave, Pensacola. Large back-in sites 18 ft wide x 75 ft long; accepts big rigs. Transient (stays under 180 days), military/first-responder/Good Sam discounts. Reserve online. RV Life 4 star / $$.

name: Unhitched Leisure Lakes RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.5354,-87.3758: RV pads on west/south rim of on-site fishing pond, open grass apron at waterline, sites abut pond
note: 60-site RV park on 23.5 acres at 5565 W Nine Mile Rd, Pensacola (Unhitched-managed, not a membership park). Pull-through 30/50 amp sites, on-site fishing lake/pond with kayak beach, nature trails. Accepts travelers and long-term guests; reserve online. Also two tiny-home cabin rentals. RV Life 4 star / $$.

name: Cedar Lakes RV Park & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.5903,-86.9742: RV strip set back ~80-120m from small spring/pond behind open field+treeline; river further, no pads on water
note: Small quiet 25-site full-hookup RV park at 4312 Coachman Rd, Milton (Santa Rosa County). Shady wooded sites; small spring with a free paddle boat on an island. No showers or WiFi. Reserve by phone (no online booking found). RV Life 4 star / $$.

name: White Springs RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.3200,-82.7923: RV park in pine plantation/woods, no water adjacent; Suwannee River well away
note: Transient/nightly RV park off I-75 near White Springs in the Suwannee River Valley; big pull-through sites with full, partial, and boondock hookup options. Open 7 days/week; reserve by phone or online. RV Life 5 stars/$$.

name: Lake in the Forest RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.1459,-81.8360: sites on peninsula set back across open grass; marsh/aquatic-vegetation band at shore and Half Moon Lake open water >50m beyond, open line of sight
note: Transient/nightly RV resort on Half Moon Lake in the Ocala National Forest; 30/50-amp full-hookup sites plus cabins, heated pool, rec center, boat slips, lake fishing. Also takes seasonal/snowbird stays. Reserve online via ReserveAmerica or by phone (no walk-ins). RV Life 5 stars/$$.

name: Buddy's Lakeside Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.0342,-82.6012: RV/MH pads fronted by continuous marsh/lily-pad band to Lake Rousseau open water; water visible across vegetated buffer, no pads on shore
note: On Lake Rousseau near Inglis/Crystal River; under new ownership, newly renovated (also branded Big Bass Village). Transient RV sites alongside some permanent units, plus cabins; boat ramp, store, laundry, 30/50-amp full hookups. Bass-fishing destination. RV Life 5 stars/$. Confirm transient availability when booking.

name: The Hideaway
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.555,-82.965: RV/upland developed area ~300m+ from Suwannee River behind continuous wooded wetland (river access via boardwalk, per lead); no pads on bank, no view; pin was in pasture N of campground
note: Old-Florida campground on the Suwannee River near Old Town; 30/50-amp full-hookup RV sites plus primitive and glamping sites, pool, clubhouse, store. 1,500' boardwalk through wetlands to the river. Daily/weekly/monthly/annual rates; book online via CampLife. RV Life 4.5 stars/$$.

name: Strawberry Fields For RV'ers
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.48903,-82.85588: large open RV lot east of road, no water anywhere in frame
note: Transient/nightly RV park in Chiefland near Manatee Springs and Cedar Key; big-rig friendly spacious 30/50-amp full-hookup sites, bathhouse, laundry, community room, pickleball/shuffleboard/bocce. Nightly/weekly/monthly/seasonal rates; Harvest Hosts and Good Sam listed. RV Life 4.5 stars/$$.

name: Kelly's Countryside RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.64536,-81.87044: small roadside RV park in pine/oak, no water nearby
note: Transient/nightly RV park in Callahan, ~30 min from Jacksonville and 25 mi from Amelia Island; ~40 shady/sunny pull-thru 50-amp sites plus cabins and tent sites. Pool, store, rec room, laundry, on-site fishing. Daily ~$40; reserve by phone or online. RV Life 4.5 stars/$$.

name: Sportsman's Cove Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.44997,-82.21366: MH/RV rows set ~100m+ back from Orange Lake behind a continuous marsh/wetland band; 'waterside' is marketing, RV pads not at shore
note: On the shore of Orange Lake midway between Ocala and Gainesville. 49 full-hookup sites (30/50 amp) incl. pull-through and waterside sites; all dirt/grass (no concrete pads). Nightly, short-term, seasonal, and annual sites; restrooms, showers, dump, laundry. On-site boat ramp, rental slips, kayak rentals, fishing. RV Life 4.5 stars/$$; Good Sam 8/9.5/7.5.

name: Lake Waldena Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.19654,-81.93083: MH/RV development screened from Lake Waldena (west) by a continuous treeline/woods buffer; no pads at shore
note: On the shore of Lake Waldena near the Ocala National Forest (also 'The Retreat at Lake Waldena'). 104 sites, 103 full-hookup w/ concrete pads, fire rings; 50 ft max, big rigs welcome. Nightly from $50; weekly/monthly/seasonal also. Some manufactured-home lots but transient nightly RV sites confirmed. Fishing, playground. RV Life 4.5 stars/$$.

name: Astor Landing Campground & Marina
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.1215,-81.5089: RV campground loop is set back inland around an interior pond; the St. Johns River frontage is the marina (boat slips/docks) plus a treed buffer, not RV pads. RV sites ring the pond (S/E rows) on an open grassy apron to the waterline -> pond. 'Waterfront camping' refers to the pond; the river is not in clear sightline from the loop. (Re-audit per AWH spot-check; prior riverfront call misread the marina point/MH row.)
note: On the St. Johns River in Astor, surrounded by Ocala National Forest. Full-hookup pull-through and level back-in sites up to 50 ft, 30/50 amp; bath house, laundry. Daily/weekly/monthly. Marina with boat ramp, wet slips, storage; fishing. Advertises waterfront camping. RV Life 4.5 stars/$$. RV sites ring an interior pond (the advertised "waterfront"), set back from the St. Johns River behind the marina + treeline; not river-frontage. --Claude

name: Ocklawaha Canoe Outpost & Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.37332,-81.90478: campsite cluster set back in woods; Ocklawaha River ~250m+ NE across continuous forest, RV pads not at river
note: On the Ocklawaha River at Fort McCoy. 17 full-hookup RV sites (30/50 amp), picnic table and fire ring each; large open RV area; bathhouse. RV $40/night, $240/week. Canoe/kayak launches and fishing docks inside the campground. Also rents cabins/tent sites and runs paddle trips, but real drive-in RV sites confirmed. Coord from RV Life (not independently re-pinned). RV Life 4.5 stars/$$.

name: Kelly's RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.31907,-82.72633: RV sites in pine forest along highway, no water in frame
note: Family-run park on U.S. 41 near White Springs, ~1 mile from the Suwannee River (no on-site frontage), easy I-10/I-75 access. 20 acres of oversized sites; 28 pull-through big-rig sites; all 30/50 amp water/sewer, many paved. Daily $40, weekly $280, monthly. Heated pool, laundry, WiFi, propane, clubhouse; cabins and tent sites too. RV Life 4.5 stars/$$.

name: Casey Jones RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.11676,-82.65980: RV park near I-10 interchange in cleared/wooded land, no water nearby
note: Convenient overnight/transient park just off I-75 in Lake City. Full-hookup pull-through sites, mostly grass and fairly level, 30 amp; cable, WiFi, 24-hr laundry, bathhouse, propane; after-hours pull-up sites for late arrivals. Nightly from $27. RV Life 4 stars/$$.

name: Lake City RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.2431,-82.6404: small internal fishing pond at NW corner; RV pads set back behind treeline/road, not on pond apron
note: Private RV park at the I-75/I-10 crossroads in Lake City. Nightly/transient (from ~$65) plus weekly/monthly; reservable online via Campspot. Full-hookup sites, laundry, restrooms/showers, cable & WiFi. On-site stocked fishing pond. RV Life 4 stars/$$.

name: Sun Retreats Ocala Orange Lake
waterfront_evidence: pin was ~1km S in pasture; real resort RV rows at 29.4205,-82.2030 are inland behind golf course + marsh fringe, Orange Lake open water well NE
note: Sun Outdoors (Sun Communities) gated RV resort at 18545 NW 45th Ave Rd, Citra, near Orange Lake. Offers nightly/transient RV camping (back-in & pull-through, full 30/50-amp hookups) bookable online via Campspot, plus longer stays and rentals; ~20% off 7+ nights. 9-hole golf, pool, restaurant, rec hall, pickleball, boating/kayaking. RV Life 4 stars/$$.

name: Gulf Coast RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: pin in forest; real RV park at 29.0205,-82.6655 sits inland in pine forest, ~600m S of Withlacoochee River and N of barge canal, no water adjacent
note: Private RV park at 10885 N Suncoast Blvd (US-19/98), Inglis, ~12 min N of Crystal River between the Withlacoochee River and the Cross Florida Barge Canal, ~10 min to the Gulf. Nightly/transient from ~$68 (Passport America half-price), reservable online via Campspot; full hookups (20/30/50-amp), oversized lots, pull-throughs, two bathhouses w/ laundry, pool. RV Life 4 stars/$$.

name: Futter's 4 Oaks RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.3201,-82.7936: RV park ringed by forest/pine plantation, no water body anywhere near
note: Small (31-site) family-run park off I-75 at 2264 NW Thunder St, White Springs; managed by Craig and Suzy Futter. Level pull-through full-hookup sites (30/50-amp, water, sewer), bathhouse, clubhouse/library, WiFi, laundry. Convenient I-75 overnight stop; near Stephen Foster Folk Culture Center State Park and the Suwannee River. RV Life 4 stars/$$.

name: Arrowhead Campsites & MHP
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.2047,-82.1872: in-town MHP/RV park near I-75/US-441, no water near
note: No-frills budget campground at 1720 NW 38th Ave, Ocala, with a mix of RV, tent, and mobile-home sites. Despite 'MHP' in the name it has genuine transient nightly RV sites - a dated Nov-2025 firsthand review reports a 33-ft Class A staying one night in site 19 (~$40/night, CASH ONLY); grass pads, shady oaks, full hookups (30/50-amp, water, sewer), pool, laundry. Currently operating. RV Life 4 stars/$$.

name: Cherry Blossom RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.4576,-81.5198: RV park W of US-17 in forest/clearing; Crescent Lake far N beyond highway+residential, no pads on water
note: Gated owner-occupied RV resort at 2359 US Hwy 17, Crescent City. 80+ big-rig sites, full hookups (30 & 50 amp), free cable/WiFi, pull-throughs; heated pool, pickleball, dog park, clubhouse, laundry, propane. Child/pet-friendly (lodge cabins adults-only). Reservable nightly online via Campspot. RV Life 4 stars/$$.

name: Ichetucknee Family Canoe and Cabins
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.9868,-82.7649: scattered sites in dense woods set back from road; Ichetucknee River corridor screened by continuous forest, not visible/accessible from sites
note: Canoe/tube livery with a real campground 1/4 mi past Ichetucknee Springs north entrance, 8587 SW Elim Church Rd, Fort White. Full RV hookups (water/electric) plus primitive sites; shady sites with picnic table, grill, fire ring; bathhouse, store, game room. Rents tubes/canoes/kayaks for the Ichetucknee & Santa Fe Rivers. Reservable by phone. RV Life 4 stars/$$.

name: Goethe Trailhead Ranch
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.1980,-82.5780: RV/equestrian sites in open pasture by Goethe SF, no water body present
note: General RV campground at 9171 SE CR 337, Dunnellon, near Goethe State Forest trails (has equestrian stalls/arena but is a full RV park, not equestrian-only). 73 full-hookup large RV sites (40x60), 30/50 amp, bathhouse, pool, dog park; rental cabins. Reservation only, 2-night minimum; $75/night. Opened 2007. RV Life 4 stars/$$.

name: Chiefland RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.4901,-82.8648: in-town RV park along US-19, no water anywhere near
note: In-town RV park at 600 NW 11th Ave, Chiefland, next to the Farmers Flea Market; near Manatee Springs. 60 sites, back-ins and pull-throughs up to 45 ft, 20-ft concrete slabs, city water/sewer, 30/50 amp, WiFi, dump station, laundry. Transient stays accepted (~$249/week). RV Life 4 stars/$.

name: Welaka Lodge & Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.4753,-81.6737: RV/MH loop sits inland behind a developed waterfront residential/cabin+dock strip and treeline on the St. Johns River; pads not at shore
note: Riverside resort on the St. Johns River in Welaka with 23 RV-only big-rig sites, full hookups (20/30/50 amp). Pool, on-site restaurant/bar, boat ramp, fishing dock, boat/kayak rentals. Nightly/weekly/monthly; reservable online via Campspot. RV Life 4 stars/$$.

name: St. Johns River Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.1691,-81.5191: small RV park set ~1/4 mi inland from the river/marina, separated by woods and a road
note: RV campground at 1520 State Road 40, Astor, about 1/4 mile from the St. Johns River (bass fishing). Full RV hookups plus rental cabins; rec room, laundry, showers. Daily/weekly/monthly; reservable by phone. RV Life 4 stars/$$.

name: Georgetown Marina, Lodge & RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.3821,-81.6278: RV rows set back from the St. Johns/Lake George behind a waterfront cabin/residential strip and treeline; pads uphill, marina park
note: Full-service marina + RV park at 1533 CR 309, Georgetown, near Marker 72 on the St. Johns River at the NE corner of Lake George. 33 RV sites with full hookups (30/50 amp, cable, concrete patio); also 7 cabins. Boat launch, fuel, pump-out, bait/tackle store, laundry/showers. $45/night, $225/week, $450/month plus electric. RV Life 4 stars/$$.

name: Homebound RV Parks-Bushnell
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.6018,-82.1803: RV park in a forest clearing; nearest ponds are distant in adjacent woods, not adjoining sites
note: Private all-ages RV park off I-75 (Exit 309); daily/weekly/monthly stays, online booking via Campspot. Formerly Sumter Oaks (Escapees club), now independent; mix of transient and long-term sites, 70 ft max. RV Life 4 star / $$. --Claude

name: Tampa South RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.6962,-82.4589: eastern RV/park-model row sits directly on the Little Manatee River bank with seawall and docks, pads within a pad-depth of the waterline
note: All-ages RV resort on the Little Manatee River near Tampa Bay (Ruskin); ~100 sites (some park models), full hookups, rigs to ~50 ft, docks/boat access. Nightly/weekly/monthly/seasonal stays. RV Life 4.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Rainbow Chase RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.2470,-81.5891: RV park set well back from surrounding ponds by roads and open fields; no pad-to-water adjacency
note: Private all-ages RV resort off Lake Wilson Rd, Davenport (near Disney); ~160 sites with dedicated short-term pull-through slabs plus longer-term sites. Full hookups, big-rig access. RV Life 4 star / $$. --Claude

name: Eras RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.5791,-81.8018: RV loops separated from the Peace River by a wide continuous forested buffer (~150-250m); only a small interior pond, no shoreline pads
note: New private all-ages RV resort at the Little Charlie Creek/Peace River confluence near Wauchula; paver-patio sites, pool, pickleball, dog park. Daily/weekly short-term and seasonal stays, online reservations. RV Life 4 star / $$. --Claude

name: Ponderosa RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.3068,-81.3662: interior pond ringed by continuous treeline; RV pads face loop road, no open shoreline apron
note: Private all-ages RV park on Fortune Rd off Hwy 192, Kissimmee (near Disney/Universal); ~200 sites, stays 1 day-6 months. Full hookups, concrete patios, pull-through big-rig sites, 45 ft max. RV Life 4.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Cody's Catfish Pond and RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.5448,-82.5852: RV pads on NE/E shore sit at the catch-pond edge across open grass apron, within ~15-25m, no treeline screen; lead confirms premium pond sites
note: Family-owned 49-acre all-ages RV park 2 mi from Weeki Wachee Springs; nightly transient + seasonal/monthly; full-hookup RV and tent sites; on-site catch-and-release catfish pond with canoes/kayaks. Book by phone (no online booking). RV Life 4 star / $$. --Claude

name: Johnston Springs RV Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.1625,-81.4395: two small on-site ponds are tree-ringed; RV pads scattered among trees, no pad on an open shoreline apron
note: Family-owned 50+ acre all-ages RV campground near Kissimmee (former Merry D); nightly transient (full-hookup) reservable online; large/spacious sites; two stocked catch-and-release ponds, dog park, clubhouse. RV Life 4.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: LeLynn R.V. Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.1632,-81.8045: westernmost site row fronts Little Lake Agnes across an open unused mowed-grass apron (~30-40m), no treeline screen; lead confirms lakefront row
note: All-ages 360-site full-hookup resort on Little Lake Agnes near Polk City off I-4; concrete pads, boat ramp, pool. Snowbird-heavy but has overnight/short-term pull-thru sites; transient stays year-round. Reserve by phone. --Claude

name: Avalon RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.9265,-82.7322: two interior decorative/retention ponds in the MH section; dedicated RV section to the south does not front them
note: All-ages year-round RV resort on US-19 in Clearwater (RHP); full hookups, pool. Nightly/weekly/monthly stays reservable by phone; mix of transient and long-term, tight sites. --Claude

name: Hammondell Campsites
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.9861,-81.6610: on-site ponds are small/treelined; the larger lake to the east belongs to a separate residential subdivision across the road, no RV pads with open apron to water
note: 163-site RV park (transient RV section within a mobile-home community) in Winter Haven; families welcome up to 2-week stay, big-rig friendly, full hookups; daily/weekly/monthly. Reserve by phone. RV Life 5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Camp Central RV Parks - North
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.8508,-81.5859: small interior pond among units facing loop roads; marsh/sinkhole feature is across the highway; no shoreline RV pads
note: All-ages transient/seasonal mix off US-27 south of SR 60; ~150 full-hookup sites, pull-throughs, heated pool; nightly and monthly stays, reserve by phone or Hipcamp. Passport America. RV Life 4.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Fiddlers Green RV Ranch
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.9712,-81.6478: round pond ringed by continuous emergent-vegetation/marsh buffer; RV sites sit ~150m south, no open apron to open water; lead confirms pond is tent/dock only
note: 26-site full-hookup RV ranch (2 pull-thrus) bordering Ocala National Forest near Altoona; daily/weekly/monthly, max RV 45 ft. Optional equine stalls but RVers without horses welcome. Reserve by phone/email. RV Life 4 star / $$. --Claude

name: Little Flamingo RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.2185,-82.2639: RV pads in lower loop front two small on-site ponds with open grass apron within a pad-depth, no buffer
note: Private 57-site full-hookup all-ages RV park near Wesley Chapel/Tampa; daily/weekly/monthly, ~45'x15' lots fit big rigs, on-site ponds and pool. Reservable by phone/online. RV Life 4 star / $$. --Claude

name: Florida Sand Music Ranch
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.4351,-82.2748: camping/venue area is inland; nearest lake ~300m+ NW through dense woods, no RV sites on water
note: Formerly Sertoma Youth Ranch; 80-acre wooded event/camping ranch between Brooksville and Dade City. W/E sites $35, primitive $20, dump station. FCFS (groups of 20+ reserve). Hot showers, RV storage; max 50 ft, pull-thrus. Confirm event calendar - festivals occasionally rent the whole ranch and close general camping. --Claude

name: Cedars Lake RV Park & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.7682,-82.6328: RV/MH units on west and south edge of small interior pond (Cedars Lake) with open ground to water within a pad-depth
note: Small wooded RV park around a small interior pond in Old Homosassa; mix of part/full-time residents but takes transient overnighters. Daily ~$35, weekly/monthly. Book by phone or RoverPass (official cedarslakervpark.com appears down). 50-amp full hookups, rigs to 50 ft. RV Life 4 star / $$. --Claude

name: Lake Toho RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.2022,-81.3756: two rows of sites flank a navigable Lake Toho canal, pads at the canal waterline/seawall both sides with narrow open apron
note: All-year RV resort on West Lake Tohopekaliga near St. Cloud/Kissimmee; full hookups. Has short-term/transient RV sites adjacent to a long-term/seasonal park plus a mobile-home section. RVs to ~36 ft. Boat slips/ramp on Lake Toho. $100 deposit holds a site; rates by phone. RV Life 4 star / $. --Claude

name: Lazy Dazy Retreat
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.1237,-81.9716: RV/MH park rows along highway with no water body adjacent or nearby
note: All-ages full-hookup RV park on US-98 N north of Lakeland; 86 sites (30 pull-thru), 34 ft max. Nightly stays reservable online (Roverpass) plus weekly/monthly. RV Life 5 star; reviews thin. Official site has an expired SSL cert. --Claude

name: Bud's RV Park and Marina
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.8946,-81.3736: a row of RVs on open lawn fronts the Tiger Lake canal/cove within a pad-depth, open grass apron to the water
note: Transient + seasonal mix on Tiger Lake (Kissimmee chain) near Lake Wales; 31 full-hookup gravel/concrete pads, big-rig friendly (to ~50 ft), 50-slip marina + boat ramp. Daily $40/weekly $250, reserve by phone/form. RV Life 5 star / $. --Claude

name: Masonic RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.6673,-82.3579: lake south shore is occupied by clubhouse/day-use building+lawn; RV sites are scattered inland among woods set back behind that used strip and treeline
note: RV park within the 204-acre Masonic Park & Youth Camp near Wimauma, open to non-Masons; 110+ full-hookup sites with step-out pads. Daily $60/weekly $360/monthly $550; reserve via online form + deposit. RV Life 5 star / $. --Claude

name: Southern Oaks RV Resort
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.0166,-82.0132: MH/RV park inland off US-441; only a small retention pond at park's NE edge, pads not on water
note: AGE-RESTRICTED: 55+ only. Big-rig-friendly RV resort near The Villages (Summerfield); mix of back-in, pull-through, and park-model sites. Genuine transient nightly rates (~$55-65, Passport America 50% off) alongside weekly/monthly/annual. Heated pool, pickleball, full hookups. RV Life 4.5 star / $$.

name: International RV Park & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.1587,-81.0976: RV loops set in forest beside I-95; nearest small pond is tree-screened, pads not on water
note: AGE-RESTRICTED: 55+ only. 194-site park on W. International Speedway Blvd in Daytona Beach, open year-round. Markets as 'Seasonal 55+ RV Living' but explicitly offers daily/weekly/monthly rates and welcomes short-term guests; big-rig & pet friendly, curved pull-through sites with small concrete pads, full hookups, bathhouse, pools, clubhouse. Caveat: seasonal-leaning - confirm short-stay availability. RV Life 4 star / $$.

name: Floridian RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.4578,-82.6560: small RV pad cluster at highway interchange amid forest/fields and a power substation; no water nearby
note: AGE-RESTRICTED: adults-only (18+, no minors). At 890 N Hathaway Ave, Bronson, convenient to Gainesville and Gulf-coast springs. 30 large level sites, asphalt/hard-gravel pads up to 70 ft, 30/50 amp, sewer, clubhouse with restrooms/laundry. Accepts nightly transient (~$45) plus weekly/monthly. RV Life 4 star / $$. Note: official site had a TLS cert mismatch when checked.

name: Lynch's Landing RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.5989,-81.6019: RV rows along interior road set back behind a treeline/clubhouse buffer from St. Johns River; units near the dock area overlook open water but no pad has an unobstructed on-water apron
note: AGE-RESTRICTED: adults-only for renters (kids may visit but not stay as renters). Transient RV park on the St. Johns River ~5 mi south of Palatka; 37 full-hookup sites (30/50A), sand/grass mostly-level pads, laundry, two river docks, boat ramp. Nightly ~$35 (30A)/$45 (50A); weekly/monthly discounts. Big-rig friendly; under renovation/new ownership as of 2026. Official site uses a self-signed/cert-mismatched HTTPS. RV Life 4 star / $$.

name: Scottish Traveler RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.9007,-82.7461: dense RV/MH park amid residential/commercial in Pinellas Park; nearest small retention pond is across the highway by a plaza, pads not on water
note: AGE-RESTRICTED: adults-only (18+, no minors). Full-hookup park near Largo, 7 mi from Gulf beaches (formerly Lee's Travel Park); some full-timers but takes transient nightly/weekly/monthly stays. Max RV ~30 ft. RV Life 5 star (few reviews). Reserve by phone.

name: Travel World RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.8846,-82.7170: packed RV grid in industrial zone beside I-275 interchange; scattered retention ponds not adjacent to pads
note: AGE-RESTRICTED: adults-only (21+, no minors). Locally owned RV park at 12400 US-19 N, Clearwater; daily/weekly/monthly (~$50-55/nt), max RV 37 ft, heated pool. Mix of transient and long-term; reserve by phone (Facebook-only, no standalone site). RV Life 4 star / $$.

name: Coe Landing County Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.4547,-84.4814: RV pads in wooded loop on a Lake Talquin point, set back ~40-70m behind a continuous treeline; only the NW boat ramp/parking touches water, so pads overlook water through trees but are not on an open apron
note: Leon County park on Lake Talquin; 20 water/electric sites (no sewer, dump station on site), boat ramp + fishing pier. Reservable online via Leon County's portal. RV Life $$, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: Williams Landing Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.4483,-84.5212: camp clearing/parking set ~100m inland behind a continuous treeline from Lake Talquin; concrete boat ramp on the water is the only open-water feature, sites screened by woods
note: Leon County park on Lake Talquin; 10 water-only sites (no electric), boat ramp, fishing pier, bathhouse with showers. Sloped pull-throughs need leveling. Reservable online via Leon County's portal. RV Life $, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: Pat Thomas Park at Hopkins Landing
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.4164,-84.6356: pine-shaded RV loop on a Lake Talquin point with a dock on the SW shore; nearest pads sit behind a treeline/marsh fringe, set back from open water, not on an unobstructed apron
note: Gadsden County park at Hopkins Landing on Lake Talquin; water/electric hookup sites plus primitive sites, boat ramp, fishing pier, playground, restrooms/showers. Book via Gadsden County Parks & Rec (phone) - no online portal confirmed. RV Life $, 5 stars. --Claude

name: Myron B. Hodge City Park & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.0500,-84.4987: RV/camp area sits east of US-319; Sopchoppy River ~250m west behind a wide wooded floodplain buffer, no riverbank sites
note: City of Sopchoppy park on the Sopchoppy River; 26 RV sites with 30-amp electric, water and sewer, plus primitive sites, $20/night. Boat ramp, fishing piers. Book by phone. RV Life $, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: Shell Mound Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.2083,-83.0632: loop on a wooded hammock screened by shrub/treeline from surrounding tidal salt marsh; open tidal creek/boat basin is distant and reached via the developed ramp strip, no pads on open water
note: Levy County park at the edge of the Lower Suwannee NWR near Cedar Key; FCFS walk-in only, cash only, 7-day max, office check-in 7:30am-6:30pm. ~28 sites with 30-amp electric/water, dump station. Big-rig friendly. Not on recreation.gov. RV Life $$, 4 stars. --Claude

name: Escambia County Equestrian Center
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.5148,-87.3678: equestrian facility (arenas/barns) with RV parking along the perimeter; only small stormwater retention ponds, no lake/river adjacent
note: County-run equine event park at 7750 Mobile Hwy, Pensacola, offering 75 public RV sites (35 full-hookup, 30/50-amp, water+sewer), dump station; reserve by phone >=24hr ahead, contracted events get priority, cash/check only. Not equestrian-only - open to general RVers. RV Life $$, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: Wilderness Landing Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.8035,-86.7675: primitive sandy camping loop in pine woods; Blackwater River ~150m east behind a continuous treeline, no sites on the bank
note: Free FCFS Okaloosa County primitive park on the Blackwater River near Baker; hard-packed sand/grass drive-in sites, no hookups, bathhouse + boat ramp, 14-day limit; rough but passable gravel/dirt access road. 29-36 ft RVs reported. RV Life $, 5 stars. --Claude

name: Lake Stone Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.9665,-87.2945: RV pads on a peninsula loop with open grass aprons running to the Lake Stone waterline on three sides (S/SW/E fingers), RVs visible at the shore edge within a pad-depth of the water
note: Escambia County FCFS lakeside campground on Lake Stone near Century; 77 sites with water/30-50amp electric, 2 dump stations, boat ramp (electric motors only) and fishing pier; pay-at-office, cash only, big-rig friendly (37-ft reported), waterfront sites 14-night max. RV Life $, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: Gibson Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.4378,-83.0938: open picnic/parking ground set ~120m back from Suwannee River; continuous treed bank/bench, boat ramp drops down a graded slope - no RV pad on open apron to water
note: Hamilton County park on the Suwannee River near Jasper; 13 sites (10 with 30A/water, 3 with 15A) at $22/night, primitive tent $5.50; boat ramp, Florida Trail trailhead, Suwannee River Wilderness Trail access. Reserve by phone - not online. RV Life $, unrated. --Claude

name: Neals Landing Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.9752,-85.0077: camp loops on open ground but separated from Chattahoochee/Lk Seminole by continuous treed bank fringe; water visible across thin fringe, boat-ramp clearing is day-use not RV sites - view not access
note: Free FCFS Jackson County park on the Chattahoochee River arm of Lake Seminole near Bascom; open camping loops (no fixed/numbered hookup sites, no fee, no hookups), restrooms/showers, USACE-owned single-lane boat ramp. Wide loops fit a 23-ft rig. RV Life $, unrated. --Claude

name: Newport Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: canopy-dense satellite at 30.1996,-84.1745 + official county map (lead): St. Marks River reached by WALKWAY from west loop, sites set back behind treed buffer - no open apron, screened
note: Wakulla County campground on the St. Marks River near the Newport Bridge (Crawfordville); 29 sites (10 full-hookup, ~9 water/electric incl. two pull-throughs, rest dry tent), bathhouse, dump station, boat ramp, kayak launch, river observation deck. FCFS/self-pay. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Cotton Lake Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.7843,-87.3138: small primitive clearing with vehicles ~40m from Escambia oxbow behind a treed/cypress bank fringe; scattered trees don't fully screen so water visible - view not open apron
note: NWFWMD campground on the Escambia River (Cotton Lake oxbow) in the Escambia River WMA near Molino; 7 sites (1-4 take RVs/trailers, 5-7 tent-only), no hookups - picnic tables, pedestal grills, fire rings, portable toilet, boat ramp; maintained dirt/gravel access. Camping permit required; reserve free via NWFWMD CampLife. Confirm a 23-ft rig fits sites 1-4 on arrival. --Claude

name: Magnolia Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.6340,-81.5500: camp loops in oak hammock east of road; Lake Apopka shore fronted by developed day-use strip (pavilion/parking/dock) + treed buffer - sites set back, no lake sightline
note: Orange County park on Lake Apopka; 18 RV/tent sites under live oaks, water+electric, on-site dump (no sewer at sites). A few sites tent-only; firsthand review backed in a 27-ft rig. Reservable online via Orange County WebTrac (up to 45 days out). RV Life $, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: Moss Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.3836,-81.1901 + official map (lead): camp loops inland in oak hammock; the lakeside clearing/loop is the day-use boat ramp, not RV sites - camp not on Lake Mary Jane shore
note: Orange County park between Lake Mary Jane and Lake Hart; 54 improved RV/tent sites + 5 group sites, water+electric, max RV length 45 ft. Reservable online via Orange County WebTrac. RV Life $$, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: Trimble Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.7683,-81.6563: small peninsula ringed by Lk Beauclair/Carlton; NE/E shore loop has cleared pads with adjacent docks at the waterline; corroborates multiple sources reporting sites 7-13 lakefront
note: Orange County park on a peninsula between Lake Beauclair and Lake Carlton (Harris Chain); 15 RV/tent sites, water+electric, max RV length 35 ft. Small and popular, book early. Reservable online via Orange County WebTrac. RV Life $, 5 stars. --Claude

name: Lake Monroe Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.8410,-81.3226: gridded RV loop on interior higher ground; developed boat basin/marina to SW separated by treed strip, St. Johns/Lk Monroe across highway - sites not on water
note: Volusia County park on Lake Monroe / St. Johns River; ~25 sites, water+electric, dump station, hot showers. Reservations by phone only (no online channel). RV ~$40/night; 7-day / 30-day-per-year stay cap. RV Life $$, 4 stars. --Claude

name: Withlacoochee River Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.3503,-82.1287: fields/pond and forest at pin; Withlacoochee River is the wooded waterway well E, RV loop inland with portage to river per lead
note: Pasco County park (NOT the unrelated private Withlacoochee River RV Park on SR-575); 15 RV sites (#1-15) with 20/30-amp electric+water, plus tent/cabin and a separate hike-in primitive area. Equestrian sites are in the adjacent Green Swamp preserve, not this campground. No posted RV length cap; spacious shaded sites. Reservable online via Pasco County CivicRec. RV Life $$, 4 stars. --Claude

name: Bull Creek Campground and Marina
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.4230,-81.4346: RV pads on open cleared sandy apron directly fronting Dead Lake by the ramp/docks; open walkable shore
note: Flagler County campground on Dead Lake (connects to Crescent Lake); 25 RV sites on level concrete pads, full hookups (50/30/20-amp, sewer, non-potable water -- bring drinking water), bath house, marina/boat ramp. Reservable online via Flagler County CivicRec (up to 1 yr out). Rates $25 summer-fall / $35 winter-spring incl tax. RV Life $$, 4 stars. --Claude

name: Gornto Springs Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.7800,-82.9403: open grassy camping clearings with dock running to the Suwannee riverbank; unobstructed apron to water
note: Dixie County park on the Suwannee River (at Gornto Spring); 8 camp/RV sites, water + 30-amp hookups on the RV sites. First-come first-served (not bookable in advance despite a Campspot listing). Fees: county residents RV $10/night, non-residents $20/night. RV Life $$, unrated; confirmed via Campspot RV-site category and firsthand reviews. --Claude

name: Hart Springs Park & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.6737,-82.9510: developed spring/day-use on river; RV loop set back inland under oak canopy away from spring and Suwannee per ReserveAmerica/lead
note: Gilchrist County park at Hart Springs on the Suwannee River; 71 full-hookup sites (30/50-amp, water/electric/sewer) under oak canopy, sites take RVs up to 60 ft. Pool, splash pad, store, laundry, showers. Reservable online via ReserveAmerica. RV Life $$, unrated. --Claude

name: Jacksonville Equestrian Center RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 30.2385,-81.9104: equestrian complex with gravel RV lot; no water anywhere near
note: City of Jacksonville facility (Better Jacksonville Plan, opened 2004; day-to-day run by NE Florida Equestrian Society concessionaire). ~70 full-service pull-through big-rig sites (30/50A); open to non-equestrian transient campers year-round subject to availability (book the Non-Event option); a reviewer parked a 32-ft rig. Reservable online via RoverPass plus the official form. RV Life $$$, 5 stars. --Claude

name: Bill Frederick Park at Turkey Lake
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.5078,-81.4793: RV/park loops set back inland behind treeline/marsh fringe along Turkey Lake; open central grounds give partial lake sightline, no shoreline pads
note: City of Orlando (Families, Parks and Recreation) campground at Turkey Lake; 36 RV sites (10 full-hookup + ~25 electric/water), concrete back-in pads 37x10 up to 65x20 ft, max length 45 ft. Reservable online ~11 months out via the city portal. RV Life $$, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: Kelly Park / Rock Springs
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.7585,-81.5046: open grassy/forested camping loop inland; Rock Springs Run ~0.3+ mi N, no water at sites
note: Orange County park at Rock Springs (Apopka); 26 single-family sites + 2 group on a hard-pack shell-rock loop, water+electric, dump station. Firsthand report: sites 35-70 ft deep, Site #17 a pull-through double, RVs welcome. Reservations required online via Orange County WebTrac (account needed). RV Life $$, 5 stars. --Claude

name: Otter Springs Park & Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.6422,-82.9406: RV loops in forest around developed spring area; Suwannee ~0.5 mi W, sites not on water
note: Gilchrist County land leased to the nonprofit ForVets, Inc. concessionaire (still local); 636 acres near the Suwannee River with 100+ full-hookup sites (30/50A, max 60 ft), tent sites, 4 cabins. Reopened after a multi-year wastewater closure (operator booking site live as of mid-2026). Reservable via operator site/phone up to 1 yr out; not on rec.gov. RV Life $$, 4 stars. Recommend a phone confirmation that it is currently open. --Claude

name: John Prince Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 26.603,-80.084: gridded RV loops set back ~80-150m from Lake Osborne across open ground with a shore treeline; water visible across open span but beyond on-water bound
note: Palm Beach County Parks & Rec campground on Lake Osborne; ~266 RV/tent sites, paved roads with pull-throughs, water + 30/50-amp electric, many full-hookup. Restrooms/showers, laundry, dump station. Reservable by phone up to 90 days out (no online booking). RV Life $$$, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: C.B. Smith Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 26.020,-80.318: NW-shore RV loop pads sit directly on the recreational lake with open grass apron to the waterline, no buffer
note: Broward County Parks campground; 83 paved full-hookup RV sites, 30/50-amp, grills + picnic tables. Heated pool, clubhouse. Reserve by phone/email to park office (no online booking); tri-county resident discount. RV Life $$$, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: South Bay RV Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 26.682,-80.729: RV pads ring a small interior retention pond behind the Okeechobee levee; Lake Okeechobee/rim canal is far across the levee embankment
note: Palm Beach County Parks campground at the base of the Lake Okeechobee levee in South Bay (100 Levee Rd). 72 paved full-hookup RV sites (water/30-50-amp/sewer), grills, picnic tables. Boat ramp to Lake Okeechobee across the street; Okeechobee Scenic Trail atop the levee. Reservable online and by phone. RV Life $$, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: Topeekeegee Yugnee (T.Y.) Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 26.034,-80.169: westernmost RV row fronts the park lake's east shore with an open grass apron within ~30-50m, only scattered shoreline trees (no continuous screen)
note: Broward County Parks campground (Whispering Pines) in 138-acre T.Y. Park, Hollywood. 60 paved full-hookup RV sites, 30/50-amp, hot showers, laundry, dump station. Reserve by phone to park office (no online booking). RV Life $$$, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: Turtle Beach Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.220,-82.515: RV sites set back from Gulf behind a dune strip plus a developed beach-parking complex; Blackburn Bay screened by a residential/marina strip - no open water frontage
note: Sarasota County beachfront campground on Siesta Key (8862 Midnight Pass Rd). 39 full-hookup sites (20/30/50-amp, water, sewer, cable, WiFi), crushed-shell pads; accommodates RVs up to ~38 ft. Direct Turtle Beach (Gulf) access; kayak launch on Blackburn Bay side. No pets. Reservable online up to 12 months out. RV Life $$$, 5 stars. --Claude

name: Savannas Recreation Area and Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.388,-80.310: RV loop's east sites are separated from the Savannas marsh/lake by a continuous scrub/treeline screen; open water not adjacent or in clear sightline
note: St. Lucie County Parks campground in the 550-acre Savannas Preserve, Fort Pierce. Full-hookup RV sites (water/sewer/electric), hot showers, laundry. Reserve by phone or in person up to a year out (not site-specific, not online). RV Life $$, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: E.G. Simmons Conservation Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.7415,-82.4690: U-shaped peninsula loop has RV pads lining both sides of the interior Tampa Bay tidal waterway with open grass apron within ~15-30m; pin was on the entrance road to the south
note: Hillsborough County conservation park on Tampa Bay near Ruskin. 110 campsites, all with electric (30-amp; some long sites up to 160 ft have 50-amp) + water, picnic tables, fire pits; 2 dump stations, showers. 700 ft of Tampa Bay beach, fishing pier, kayak launch. FCFS - first-come first-served, no advance reservations. RV Life $, 4 stars. --Claude

name: Torry Island Campground & Marina
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 26.705,-80.719: RV pads line the canal-finger peninsulas at the water's edge with docks (Lake Okeechobee rim canal), pads directly on water
note: City of Belle Glade-operated campground on Torry Island in Lake Okeechobee (640-acre island, SE shore). 375 sites, big-rig friendly with 30/50-amp full hookups, plus marina, 6-lane boat ramp, bait shop. Seasonal/snowbird popular but accepts transient daily/nightly stays ($35 W/E, $40-55 full hookup). Reservable online via CampLife. RV Life $$, 4 stars. --Claude

name: Hardee Lakes County Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.6265,-81.9730: lakefront RV loop along upper-lake SW shore with open grassy apron to waterline (sites 37-60), docks at shore
note: Hardee County park on reclaimed phosphate-mine lakes. 60 RV sites with water/electric in two loops (inland sites 10-36; lakefront loop sites 37-60), plus a separate 15-site primitive tent loop. Drive-in. Reservable online via FireFly up to 1 year out; lakefront sites book up Jan-Apr. RV Life $$, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: Lithia Springs Conservation Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.8645,-82.2270: campground loops in dense oak canopy; Alafia River is a narrow forest-screened blackwater channel, no resolvable open RV-site-on-water; lead notes 'heavily wooded' riverbank (treeline buffer)
note: Hillsborough County conservation park near the Alafia River with a swimming spring (separate fee). 44-45 campsites, most RV-suitable, two loops with bathhouses, water/electric (30-amp all, 50-amp on 15); sites range ~14 ft to 100+ ft, most 40-80 ft. FCFS, no reservations (call ahead); $24/night. RV Life $$, 3.5 stars. --Claude

name: Edward Medard Conservation Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.9285,-82.1630: camping loops sit ~200m+ inland from Medard Reservoir behind continuous forest; only the boat ramp is at water
note: Hillsborough County conservation park on Medard Reservoir (built 1970 for Alafia River flood control). 43 drive-in RV/tent sites with water/electric (30/50-amp), shell surfacing, dump station, boat ramp; loop B is winding with overhead trees. Documented rigs up to ~29 ft. FCFS, no reservations; $24/night. RV Life $$, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: Donald MacDonald Park and Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.8173,-80.5052: sites scattered in forest set back from tree-lined St. Sebastian River; lead (Florida Rambler) confirms no sites directly on river, access via boat ramp/dock
note: Indian River County park on the St. Sebastian River. Small: 31 sites on 37 acres, only 5 with water/electric for RVs (rest primitive tent). County steers RVs over 30 ft to the Fairgrounds Campground, so RV sites fit a 23-ft rig. Drive-in; boat launch, boardwalk, hot showers. Reservable online via county park page; 2-week max stay. RV Life $$, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: Pioneer Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.5004,-81.7999: RV/site grid set ~200m+ back from Peace River with forest between; sites not on water
note: Hardee County 115-acre park along the Peace River at US-17/SR-64 in Zolfo Springs. ~62-65 sites with water/electric (some sewer), plus 25 no-hookup sites; larger rigs in a pull-through parking-lot-style loop, smaller in wooded areas. Drive-in. Reservable online via FireFly; Passport America park. Museum, wildlife refuge, boat ramp. RV Life $$, 3.5 stars. --Claude

name: Brownville Park
