waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.3004,-81.8414: Peace River is a narrow forest-lined channel; small camping clearing by the boat ramp, no resolvable open RV pads on the wooded riverbank
note: DeSoto County-managed 75-acre park on the Peace River ~5 mi N of Arcadia (RV Life mis-tagged commercial; county-operated). RV sites with full hookups on concrete pads up to ~30 ft; interior sites primitive. Drive-in; dump station, showers, boat ramp. Reservable online via FireFly. Peace River paddling/fossil hunting. RV Life $$, 4 stars. --Claude

name: Coleman Landing at Shady Oaks Recreation Area
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.8259,-81.2178: easternmost loop RV sites line the canal to Lake Kissimmee with open grass apron to the waterline
note: Polk County campground on Lake Kissimmee, opened 2015. 54 grass-pad RV/tent sites with water + electric (20/30/50 amp) partial hookups, plus 6 primitive sites; bathhouse, dump station, three boat ramps. FCFS - Polk County does not accept campground reservations; 28-day max in peak season. RV Life $, 4 stars. --Claude

name: Saddle Creek Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 28.0625,-81.8839: campsites sit on grassy peninsulas/causeways between phosphate-pit lakes with open grass running to water on both sides, well within distance bound
note: Polk County campground at 750-acre Saddle Creek Park, Lakeland; loop in an oak hammock near the back of the park among former-phosphate-pit lakes. Partial-hookup RV + tent sites (20/30/50 amp + water), dump station, showers; ~54 RV sites. Max RV ~30 ft. 14-day max. Reopened Oct 2024 after hurricane closures. RV Life $$, 1.5 stars (low rating = dated reviews of aging bathhouse, not closure). --Claude

name: Lake Arbuckle Park and Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.7214,-81.4188: RV pads in cleared center ~80-120m from Lake Arbuckle behind a cypress/marsh treeline; boat ramp/dock at shore but no pad on open shoreline
note: Polk County park on Lake Arbuckle near Frostproof. 38 drive-in RV sites with water/electric hookups (plus separate primitive tent area), each with table, fire ring, grill; bathhouse, dump station, boat ramp. FCFS - not reservable online, cash payment; 14-day max. Sites large, shaded by cypress/live oak; one report documents a 28-ft RV. RV Life $, 4 stars. --Claude

name: Markham Park
waterfront_evidence: original pin at canal bridge is wrong; camping loops sit interior among day-use lakes near 26.1285,-80.3540; could not resolve RV pads at any lake shoreline, loops set back in grass/trees -> default down
note: Broward County Parks RV campground in 669-acre Markham Park & Target Range, on the edge of the Everglades Conservation Area. 88 full-hookup sites (water/electric to 50A/sewer) in lettered loops; paved/gravel/grass pads, back-in and pull-through; accommodates large rigs (40+ ft). Reservations by phone or email to the park office only (not online via WebTrac). Year-round. RV Life $$$, 4 stars. --Claude

name: Easterlin Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 26.1706,-80.1610: heavily canopied loops; interior kidney lake ringed by trees and separated from camp loops, no RV pads on shoreline; I-95 canal on east edge
note: Broward County Parks campground in Easterlin Regional Park, a heavily wooded inland park in Oakland Park. 45 paved full-hookup RV sites (water, sewer, 20/30/50-amp) plus 6 partial-hookup; big-rig capable (up to 40 ft, tight loop turns). A/C restrooms, hot showers, laundry, WiFi. Reservations by phone/email to the park office (not the online WebTrac system). RV Life $$$, 4 stars. --Claude

name: Pahokee Campground & Marina
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 26.8240,-80.6688: RV pads strung along open grassy levee directly on Lake Okeechobee shore, unobstructed open-grass apron to waterline, no buffer; marina basin adjacent to NE
note: City-of-Pahokee municipal campground on the water side of the Herbert Hoover Dike on Lake Okeechobee (former Lake Okeechobee KOA, now city-operated). 125 sites, ~60 with full hookups (30/50-amp, water, sewer); level hard-packed limestone pads. Lakeside pool. Reservable online via the city site's Marina Reservations tab; walk-ins welcome. Currently operating. RV Life $$, 2.5 stars. --Claude

name: Istokpoga Canal Campground (SFWMD)
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.4015,-81.1610: gravel loop/lot set back ~40-60m from Istokpoga Canal with a vegetated treeline along the canal bank; no pad at canal frontage -> default down
note: SFWMD gated free campground in the Kissimmee River Public Use Area, on the Istokpoga Canal near Lorida. ~12 lightly-graveled drive-in sites plus ~6 spots in the treeline; tents or any size RV (Big Rig Friendly). Each site has table, fire ring, grill; potable water and restrooms, no showers. Free, but a free SFWMD Special Use License reservation is required in advance (reservable online or by phone); gate combination emailed after booking. 7-8 night max. The Dyrt 4.5/5 (6 reviews). --Claude

name: Phipps Park Campground
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 27.1135,-80.2815: SW/west row of RV pads on the peninsula sits on open grass apron running to the St. Lucie Canal waterline, no buffer; confirms operator's canal-side 'waterfront' row
note: Martin County 55-acre conservation park & campground on the Okeechobee Waterway (St. Lucie Canal) at the St. Lucie Lock & Dam, Stuart - a distinct facility adjacent to (not the same as) the federal St. Lucie South Campground. 99 RV sites: 58 waterfront full-service + 41 interior full-hookup, 30/50-amp water/sewer, table + fire pit. Reservable online via FireFly (24-hr advance min for gate code). 90-day max within 300 days. RV Life $$$, 4.5 stars. --Claude

name: Clay County Fairgrounds RV Park
waterfront_evidence: satellite at 29.9809,-81.7445: county fairgrounds RV loop in the NW corner of the grounds; only stormwater/retention ponds on site, with a treed buffer between the loop and the nearest pond, RV pads not on an open apron -> not waterfront
note: County-operated RV park at the Clay County Fairgrounds (2463 SR-16 W, Green Cove Springs); 48 full-hookup sites (30/50-amp, water, sewer), gated/coded entry, dump station, laundry, big-rig friendly. Open year-round for transient stays (30-day max), closed during the spring fair (so NOT event-only camping). Reserve by phone/email through the county fairgrounds office or via Hipcamp. RV-only park with no kid amenities. RV Life 8.3*/35 reviews; Good Sam listed. Operated through the county fairgrounds office (Fairgrounds@claycountygov.com), hence ownership=local. --Claude
